title: #GIRLBOSSGOD
description: Join God on a quest to regain her place as the world’s greatest #influencer. Expect devilishly catchy pop songs, shocking behind-the-scenes revelations and Papal product placements in this tongue-in-cheek look at celebrity, popularity and reality tv. But is she losing faith in herself? Will Jesus ever call her back? Should she finally see a doctor about that burning bush? Alison Arnopp is an award-winning Irish performer with credits ranging from the Royal Shakespeare Company to London’s West End. If you’re into pop culture, banging tunes and blasphemy, get yourself a front-row seat to this irreverent high-energy romp.

title: #Hysterical
description: Following a ★★★★ sell out run of WIP "#Hysteria: A History of Human Sexuality with Callaghans Questions" - writer/performer Callaghan returns for their debut hour #Hysterical - a satirical, educational comedy show exploring the historical views and problems surrounding sexuality and gender. A one person powerhouse of character driven comedy, #Hysterical opens as “The Governess” prepares the audience to be “good little wives”. Their future husband, “The Duke”, a rapscallion, then takes the audience through the history of male sexuality. All culminating in burlesque strip, #Hysterical combines Callaghan’s specialties of scripted comedy, burlesque and educational insight. Developed with the support of Soho Theatre Labs, #Hysteria sold out at Brighton, Camden & Lambeth Fringes and sold at 80% capacity during previews for its limited 2025 Edinburgh run. ★★★★ “This show is delightful” - Fringe Fan “Everything that needs to be said... but said hilariously” - Wombshine

title: 'Title Goes Here' An Improv Comedy
description: ‘Title Goes Here’ An Improv Comedy is a fast-paced, laugh-out-loud night of unscripted chaos where no two shows are the same! Touring nationwide with a core cast of top-notch comedians, each performance features a different local guest actor, keeping the comedy fresh, unpredictable, and full of surprises. From audience-driven scenes to bizarre games and on-the-spot songs, it’s comedy made up right before your eyes — and it’s never happening that way again!

title: (The) All true adventures of... (Work In Progress)
description: Do you choose your own adventure, or does adventure choose you? That’s the question Impish Scribe’s asking.. and answering! Buckle up for escapades, anecdotes and a renewed passion for life, as sassy, softly spoken Scribe bids for a quiet life but repeatedly gets ambushed by chaos, coincidence and questionable calls. Because adventure isn’t something you look for. Sometimes it sneaks up on you, taps your shoulders and bangs on about “quests”. Book now for hilarious true stories, unexpected turns and proof quiet ones are never as sensible as they seem.

title: 1 Hour to Turn Your Life Around – All Your Problems Solved by Comedians
description: Think you have it bad? Not for long, and believe us, we've seen worse. Bring us your insurmountable secrets and we guarantee no problem or scenario too complicated, silly or gross for us to take on. We want to solve it for you and we must. Relationships, work, feuds with the local council? This is your moment to have award winning comedians take on your secrets and scenarios to make a fool proof plan which will have you leaving with a new lease of life. Or even ownership if leasing is no longer your thing. They say the best time to plant a tree is yesterday, the second best time is now. We don't know anything about that, but this is a show for humans... not trees.

title: 10 Things They Hate About Me
description: When it comes to relationships, Shinanne Higgins is all about the D...disappointment! She's a 40-year-old, single lady on a journey of reflection. Her eggs are hanging on like contestants in Gladiators...even her cultural references are old! The show is an hilarious account of the trials and tribulations of dating in a Tinder reality, after growing up in a kissing-in-the-disco world. Inevitably she begins to ask - what's wrong with me? So begins the introspective, belly-laugh funny list of "red flags" that expose Shinanne's weaknesses in the modern dating era. Shinanne is a rising star of the Irish comedy scene and her show sold out at Edinburgh Fringe in both 2024 and 2025.

description: Hot on the heels of Camden Fringe and off-West End success, 1816 returns to the stage. It’s Byron and Shelley, as you've never seen them before. A Friends meets Frankenstein comedy-drama musical, from the same company that launched SIX: 1816 is the ultimate origin story of the first modern vampire novel and Frankenstein, where a group of five Romantics get trapped for a summer they'd rather forget in their storm-swept villa by Lake Geneva... Trapped indoors by torrential rain, legendary writers Byron, Percy and Mary Shelley and their friends, Polidori and Claire search for inspiration by the gloomy Lake Geneva. Driven by a desire for escapism, they challenge one another to create the best ghost story. But things fall apart the longer they are stuck together. Polidori’s diary is brought to life in the 1816: The Year Without A Summer musical.

title: 2ShoulderPads: GALAXY TRAIN
description: A musical that sold out its entire run at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe and won the Spirit of the Fringe Award. Based on classic Japanese literature, this poetic work uses an extreme minimal costume concept: male performers wear only “two shoulder pads.” There is nothing obscene—laughter at the wiggling bottoms soon gives way to imagination, and by the final scene many are moved to tears. Exploring universal themes of life, death and parting, the show blends humour, live music and kabuki-inspired physicality into a joyful theatrical experience for audiences of all ages.

title: 3 by King of More
description: Three is one. One is Three. Come and See! From the Adelaide Fringe 2025 award winning artist, King of More, comes a brand-new show about the wonder, symbolism and mystery of the number 3. Number 3 has ancient, magical and mundane meanings and these various aspects are brought into this unique show. Everything in threes is welcome - from threesomes to Holy Trinity. Three wise men meet three Fates. The maid, the mother and the crone all knocking on one door. This joyful, intriguing, and adamantly not-educational show will sprint through our body, mind and soul facets to unite and question. With a mystery visit from the Hag Kuaka testing your hope. All in service of embracing the uncertainty and the unknown. Come and let us not know together. King of More is queer immersive performer and an esoteric jester, originally from Bosnia and Herzegovina. Using humour, music, and interactive storytelling, he creates unique, thought-provoking experiences inspiring people to live 5% more present.

description: Who is responsible for the evil in this world? You didn’t choose your family, upbringing or culture. No one had a choice. Not even Hitler. Well, then who is to blame? The universe has played a sick joke on us. If you hear the call, come join me for this psychedelic ritual as we attempt to reach the other side and summon... something. Born out of an accidental exorcism during a shamanic ceremony, 7-7-7 is a one-man ritual-performance that confronts the illusion of free will and our relationship with a Higher Power. It asks what drives people to commit acts of unspeakable evil and if we even have any choice in who we are and what we do. Part confessional story-telling, part seance, this dark comedy blends philosophical inquiry, theological absurdism and the theatricality of ritual. Quick and unpredictable 7-7-7 keeps the audience on their toes as it lightly dances between genres, liminal spaces and through the dark night of the soul.

description: The future of working life, today! We here at Corporate Management Solutions are delighted to invite you - yes, you - to attend one of our exclusive, behind-the-scenes presentations of the proper workplace of the not-too-distant future. Do you want the inside track to your success in the upcoming corporate utopia? Are you desperate to go above and beyond for a business that cares deeply about pretending to care about you? Perhaps a lazy, lollygagging loser wanting to turn your fortunes around? Or just in need of passing an hour in an exciting fashion? Whether you're any of the above or none at all, get your tickets ASAP for a uniquely entertaining and engaging experience you won't soon forget! Written and Performed by Tom Pagett, originally developed at the University of Chichester, before being redeveloped with the help of Lighthouse Poole's Sanctuary scheme.

title: A Drop of Brandy With Friends
description: A Drop of Brandy with Friends is back for its fourth year at Brighton Fringe. Hosted by the one and only Sister Brandy Bex, this is where comedy gets real, bold, and unforgettable. Each show brings you two to three top-notch acts—sometimes polished, sometimes delightfully obscure, but always side-splitting. It’s not just a show; it’s a staple. Brandy’s banter, razor-sharp wit, and her handpicked line-up make this a must-see event. Expect laughter, unexpected gems, and a night you’ll talk about long after the Fringe. Don’t miss this comedy institution!

title: A Dublin Story
description: On a Dublin night out, Cat loses her friends, her phone and her sense of direction, and finds herself wide awake to the world again. A chance meeting with a busker leads to a fleeting connection, and a reminder of what it feels like to want things. Funny, tender and honest, this solo show explores heartbreak, risk and the magic of being alive at 5am.

description: Welcome to A Gathering of Witches—Brighton’s most magical poetry and music night! We celebrate the creative talent of local artists, and inspire people to connect to their personal power, nature, and spirituality within a supportive community. Join us for a special edition of our show at the Brighton Fringe, where we will be inviting local witches to the stage for an exclusive set of words and music. Prepare for an enchanting evening of spellbinding performances—and of course, a spell or two! Featuring performances from Delphine - ocean witch songstress, The Coven Choir - Brighton’s witchiest community choir, and sprigs of heather - poet, witch, and musician. Doors at 7pm for a 7.15pm start. The show will last around one hour. Make sure you follow @agatheringofwitches on Instagram for all event updates!

title: A Head Full Of Bees
description: What if you secretly think you might be demonic and doomed to hellfire? How do you reconcile that with creative ambition, a dedication to Motherhood and a wisp of hope that all is not lost? Hot mess hairdresser Lola has a secret; she's been faking it, all of it. Sanity, self-esteem, capable Mother and Salon owner. Truth is, she's been thumping against the walls of life, ALL her life and she's wondering, "What the f**k is wrong with me? No one seems to know, except the formidable Mama Patsy; everything! But Lola is determined to figure it out. With the dubious "help" of her Shame Parrot and Media Maven Moda Monde' she faces her past to find an answer. But what is it THE answer? It's Sesame Street meets The Exorcist with original music and songs. A solo comedy with music about "learning to love your weird" .

title: A History Mystery
description: A History Mystery is an exciting, interactive children’s theatre adventure for ages 6–10, designed to make history fun and engaging! This unique production blends storytelling, puzzles, and audience participation as young detectives uncover fascinating secrets from the past. When the mischievous Tick Tock Taker—a villainous time-thief—starts stealing moments from history, the gang must leap through time to restore the past and protect our future. Packed with laughter, surprises, and hands-on fun, this show invites kids to join the quest to save history itself. Perfect for curious minds and families looking for an unforgettable experience!

title: A Kate Bush Cornucopia
description: Back for a third year after consecutive sell-outs, A Kate Bush Cornucopia returns. Created and performed by Paul Diello, this high-energy tribute celebrates the music of Kate Bush with flair, heart, and serious musical punch. Featuring bold three-part harmonies, the show reimagines Kate Bush classics in fresh and unexpected ways. This year brings something extra, with a special guest appearance from The Marella Quartet, adding sweeping string arrangements and a new sense of drama to the performance. An interactive Kate Bush quiz is woven into the night, keeping things fun and unpredictable. Joyful, theatrical, and unapologetically devoted, A Kate Bush Cornucopia delivers a night of music that’s loud, immersive, and unforgettable.

title: A Letter To My Parents
description: Viral sensation Henning Nilsen recalls the emotional journey from leaving his Finance job to making his challenging Edinburgh Fringe debut. What makes an accountant from Norway quit his stable job and move to the UK to pursue a career in stand-up comedy? In this brutally candid show, Henning attempts to answer these questions. Henning is an award-winning stand-up comedian making waves in the UK. He's a viral sensation with over 100 million online views and has in a short time gained a large following through his dry humour videos. He regularly performs at the top comedy clubs in the UK and has toured internationally in the US, Europe and Asia. His first solo show '1 And Done' had sold out shows at the Edinburgh and Brighton Fringe in 2025. Don’t miss this one and only chance to catch a rising star on the UK comedy scene at Brighton’s biggest arts festival! Free Entry – Reserve your tickets now to guarantee your seat in advance.

title: A Man Went Into A Shop To Buy A Face
description: Have you ever started a joke and forgot where it was going? Try pausing it for 41 years... Jimmy Ennis, host of Brighton's longest running new material night "Cool-Aid Comedy" brings you his debut full hour show!! Over a family meal in Dudley, a five-year-old started a classic setup but never delivered the goods. He ate his dinner, grew up, and kept the world waiting. Now, the silence breaks... This is a stand-up comedy show about the longest set-up in history. The meal is long gone, but will the punchline finally be served? Will the 41 year old delay be worth it? Come for the nostalgia, stay to see if he finally buys that face. As well as regular host of Cool-Aid Comedy, Jimmy has performed at various clubs around the country, including locally Komedia, The Forge & The Comedy Rooms. Komedia New Comedy Act Semi-Finalist 2023, Leicester Square New Comedian Quarter Finalist 2023, Best Decorated Easter Egg Oakham Primary School 1988.

title: A Marriage of Inconvenience
description: Join best friends and rising comedy stars Harsh and Abi for a split bill that explores the intersection of Type-A ambition, queer identity, and the chaotic search for love. United by their nerdy neuroses and a spectacular track record of romantic failures, the pair present A Marriage of Inconvenience: a show about the logistical nightmare of modern dating, and their pact to enter a sexless lavender marriage if they're still single by 40. Until then, they're saying "I do" to the next best thing: awkwardly sharing the stage for half an hour each.

title: A Scallywag Party Spectacular with Chainska Brassika
description: Welcome to the Scallywag Party, a home for all lovers of good times and upbeat live music. Headlining our fringe spectacular is the fantastic high energy, brass driven 9 piece, Chainska Brassika plus more acts TBA. Our acts are always eclectic, positive and dance inducing and our aim is to introduce you to the very best independent artists from the UK festival circuit and this Scallywag Party Spectacular will not disappoint. We’re so pleased to have been invited back to Caravanserai on this first Saturday of the festival and we plan to pull out all the stops and create a truly spectacular scallywag party not to be missed. Alongside amazing live music expect DJ’s, face painting, walkabouts, laughter, tomfoolery and togetherness. Scallywag Party have over a decade of experience running festivals, stages and events, championing grass roots acts and building a light hearted, live music loving community. Come join the family and get involved, WE bring the music but YOU make the party!

title: A Simply Beastly Murder
description: I say! There’s been a murder! The Famous Five grew up and grew apart. When Dick, now a hardened PI, gets in over his head, he turns to his sister Anne for help. But this isn’t Kirrin Island anymore. Do they have what it takes to solve the crime? A British Film Noir, full of gangsters, intrigue, and lashings of tinned pineapple. With additional voices by Alistair McGowan. Nominated for the Funny Women Awards. Directed by James Stone. Supported by Fenton Arts Trust and Barnsley Civic. Rachel E. Thorn writes for BBC Radio (Dead Ringers, The Now Show). She’s been nominated for the Kenneth Branagh Award, the Female Pilot Club, the Key Mellor Fellowship and the BBC’s Alfred Bradley Award.

title: A tribute to Ozzy - A classical guitar recital
description: Matthew Sear: A Tribute to Ozzy. From Ozzy to Bach – A Guitar Journey You are very warmly invited to my guitar recital at the Brighton Fringe. I’m a classical guitarist and composer, and my work has featured on everything from Netflix UFO documentaries, too BBC Radio 3, to the Concert Platform. Most people know me for classical music — but my whole journey began at age 13, when I was lent a cassette of Ozzy Osbourne: Randy Rhoads Tribute. Ozzy’s music, and especially Randy Rhoads’ extraordinary guitar playing, first drew me toward the world of classical music. With Ozzy’s recent passing, I want to celebrate that connection — performing music that inspired his guitarists, the influences they shared (from The Beatles to Bach), and my own reworkings of iconic Black Sabbath and Ozzy pieces arranged for classical guitar. If you love discovering how heavy metal and classical music inspire one another, I think this will be a recital you’ll truly enjoy.
description_html:
Matthew Sear: A Tribute to Ozzy. From Ozzy to Bach – A Guitar Journey

You are very warmly invited to my guitar recital at the Brighton Fringe.

I’m a classical guitarist and composer, and my work has featured on everything from Netflix UFO documentaries, too BBC Radio 3, to the Concert Platform. Most people know me for classical music — but my whole journey began at age 13, when I was lent a cassette of Ozzy Osbourne: Randy Rhoads Tribute. Ozzy’s music, and especially Randy Rhoads’ extraordinary guitar playing, first drew me toward the world of classical music.

With Ozzy’s recent passing, I want to celebrate that connection — performing music that inspired his guitarists, the influences they shared (from The Beatles to Bach), and my own reworkings of iconic Black Sabbath and Ozzy pieces arranged for classical guitar.

If you love discovering how heavy metal and classical music inspire one another, I think this will be a recital you’ll truly enjoy.

title: Aaand We’re Back!
description: ...again! After 2025's sold out shows in the Brighton Fringe, "Aaand We're Back" ...is back! Two stand-ups and one sit-down in a comedy triple-header of sarcastic observations, filthy one-liners and chronic hilarity. Basically, it's a 50 minute show with three stand-up comedians. Are they funny? Yes. Is there some sort of theme? Kind of. Jim has Long Covid which gives him some new material to try out, but he'll need to sit down. Kerry’s back to Brighty after going full circle Downunder on the international comedy circuit. And Rich... had a cigarette break. It’s only a loose theme. Kerry Herbert - “Razor-sharp wit” Latest 7 - Funny Women Brighton winner & national semi-finalist Jim Holland - “Genuine charm” – The Scotsman - “Delectably quirky and inventive” – Fringe Review Rich Perry - So You Think You’re Funny finalist - Chortle Student Comedy Award finalist - Writer for BBC Radio 4 Extra’s Newsjack

title: Aalex Mandel-Dallal - Where was I?
description: Moving at the speed of an overthinking mind, this Work-in-Progress show blends sharp observation with playful absurdity, turning self-exploration into shared relief. This isn’t a guide to fixing your life; it’s a celebration of trying very hard, knowing better, and doing it regardless. Warm, high-energy and deeply relatable, this is a comedy show for anyone who’s ever thought, “I (don’t) got this” Expect an abundance of misunderstandings, a rap, and a whirlwind of overcomplication that somehow lands right here, the moment you’ve chosen to see her. 2026 Musical Comedy Awards Semi-Finalist 2026 Chortle Hotshots Finalist 2025 Funny Women Stage Award Semi-finalist. 2026 Chortle Hotshots Finalist And in 2024&2025 a Leicester Square New Comedian Quarter finalist. ★★★★ “a charming humility that brings the audience together in laughter” - The Student.

title: Acaprov: The Improvised A Cappella Musical
description: Imagine a new kind of musical: beatboxers, rappers, actors, singers, dancers and body percussionists coming together completely a cappella to improvise a fresh, heart-warming show, that you’ll never forget. Every performance is unique. Acaprov take a location and title from the audience before spontaneously creating a non-stop musical that will make you laugh, cry, dance in your seat, and sing all the way home! Everything seen and heard is 100% invented in the moment and entirely from the minds, mouths and bodies of the performers - completely acappella. Joy - Heart - Harmony.

title: Actors Showcase: Pick of the Fringe Mondays
description: Actors Showcase: A Night of Unforgettable Talent Step into a world of enticing performances at the Actors Showcase, your weekly "Pick of the Fringe" event! Every Monday, we bring you an exciting selection from the Actor’s Brighton Fringe programme, featuring some of the best shows across drag, comedy, music, LGBTQ+ theatre, and more. Experience diverse performances, incredible talent, and unforgettable moments as our artists push boundaries and captivate audiences. Whether you're in the mood for laughter, drama, or something entirely unique, the Actors Showcase promises a thrilling start to your week. Don’t miss the chance to catch a little bit of everything at one dazzling event! All donations collected will be split equally among the performers and contribute to our theatre renovation project. Your generosity is greatly appreciated!

title: Adam Bromley: Hyper-Connected
description: Adam Bromley: Hyper-Connected is a comedy show about one manic man navigating an increasingly deranged hyper-online world. Adam grew up in the analogue age when people had to remember facts and basic courtesy – now it’s Chat GPT and online hate mobs. Join him on this madcap journey through his own over-stimulated brain and world that is too connected for its own good. Smart phones have taken over the world, AI will be next and we’ll be too busy doom scrolling to notice.

title: Adam Hopkins: Sorry, What? (Work In Progress)
description: How growing up with a profoundly deaf brother gave Adam Hopkins a fascination with language and communication and helped him deal with the language barrier when he moved to Shanghai, China, for a job as a journalist. Hopkins performs at London's top clubs such as Top Secret, Backyard, and The Bill Murray and opened for Kyle Kinane as part of Kinane's 2025 UK tour. He has been featured by Comedy Central and his stand-up special, Taipei, is available now on YouTube.

title: Adam Vincent - Cigarettes & Regret Baby!
description: I talk about working two jobs, having teenagers who are richer than me, love and not being able to escape family. I smile while talking about these things. While I'm hardly old* If you want youthful energy or hot political takes, this isn't for you. If you shimmy after sleeping straight through the night then I'm your man. *I did recently catch myself flossing with an eyebrow hair so make of that what you will. Nominated for Best Show at Buxton Fringe “Vincent is undeniably one of the more talented comedians I have seen at the Fringe.” Broadway Baby

title: Addiction – It's A Family Matter. Who Cares?
description: Bringing to life the dilemmas and uncertainty experienced by family members in the face of their loved one’s struggle with addiction, through dramatic monologue, autobiography and poetry. This presentation shows the ways addiction reaches into and disrupts the lives of all those related to the addicted person, who may be their child, parent, sibling, partner. Sources range from Amy Winehouse’s father Mitch and footballer Peter Shilton’s wife Steph. As well as current auto-fiction writers Julie Myerson and Douglas Stuart, 20th century autobiographical writer Caitlin Thomas, wife of poet Dylan Thomas, & earlier classical authors such as Anne Brontë. Post show Q&A, with time for discussion of the issues raised and to consider how the creative arts can be used to publicise the plight of the family affected by alcohol, drug, gambling and other addictions. Addiction–It’s a Family Matter. Who Cares? is a collaboration between psychologist Jim Orford & poet/writer Christine Hollywood.

description: Now in its fifth filthy year, The Adult Panto Tour is cumming to Brighton, and this time, it’s totally [UNSCRIPTED]! - “Oh yes it f!?ing is!” This balls-to-the-wall panto smashes in the backdoors of your favourite pantomimes! With every character, scene, and even the title decided live by you, the audience. You’re in control, Daddy. From outrageous characters to downright filthy humour, no two shows are ever the same. title: Adventures: Journey Through Dungeons with Dragons
description: Imagine a stage where dragons, wizards, and brave adventurers come alive; not from a script, but through the laughter and creativity of children. Adventures! Is kid-friendly DnD-type improv show where young audiences guide the story, shape the heroes, and even roll the dice to determine what happens next. Every performance is unique: children shout out suggestions, help solve puzzles, and sometimes even hop up to improvise as goblins, fairies, or courageous wizards. With the help of a friendly story master Donal (the comedian behind the Science Magic shows) each quest is as much about giggles and imagination as it is about dragons and dungeons, a perfect introduction to storytelling, teamwork, and the power of adventure. In a world of screens, lets get back to using our imaginations.

title: Age Against The Machine
description: Dust off those shoulder pads and pastel eyeliners as the 80s are back baby. Bigger than the stetsons in Dallas and with more more energy than if you chugged a year's supply of Tizer, we are going large on the eighties! Expect 80s pop, disco, indie, dance, alt, power ballads plus 80s visuals, dance-offs, confetti and all the usual chaos that those funsters at Age Against The Machine and Club de Fromage bring to all their shows. Bring your leg warmers, bring your crew (don't bring your boom box), it will get messy as we sing, dance, laugh (and maybe cry) through the best tunes that the 80s had to offer. So get ready to have the time of your life and remember: nobody puts Baby in a corner (especially here, as the venue is entirely round). Please note, while this is Age Against The Machine event. we are not applying our usual 30 plus age limit, in keeping with the policy of the Fringe

title: AI Mozart: A 23rd Century Concert
description: Broadcast on Al Jazeera News and commissioned by Dame Wendy Hall’s Web Science Institute, SELLOUT EdFringe25 pianist Shiel performs music composed by AI. In 2025, Matthew Shiel and Fringe Classics made history by performing the world’s first piano recital of music composed by artificial intelligence at the Edinburgh Festival and this historic event was broadcast on Al Jazeera. AI Mozart is a ground-breaking live concert of original piano pieces composed by artificial intelligences in an eclectic variety of styles from Beethoven and Brahms to boogie-woogie and Latin salsa to ridiculous hybrid genres including Viking progressive death polka! Step into the future of music and be the first to listen to an AI composer LIVE at the Brighton Fringe! Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: AITEACH
description: Wine Dog presents their debut work, ‘AITEACH’, a performance packed with energy, physicality and a heavy pinch of homosexuality. This dynamic contemporary choreography tackles deeply personal themes including limitations, control versus creative outlet, and gender-dysphoria. Powered by our lived experiences of queerness and neurodivergency, Wine Dog have developed a physically engaging performance that explores the joy of moving in a body you don't necessarily feel comfortable in. The choreography stems from the influence of grounded contemporary dance movement which pushes the body to its limits as well as influence from queer clubbing and 70s disco. Striking a balance between intense physicality and humour, “AITEACH” is a powerful, high-energy piece that leaves the audience smiling, showing how powerful feeling “other” can be.

title: Alex Franklin: Kiss Me x
description: What if we kissed right now? Like, obviously I’m kidding. But genuinely? I’m just asking questions. About kissing. Like what do we think of that? I’m kissi- I mean kidding! But we could! Right here! Do you have someone to practice kissing with? Do you want it to be me? X As seen / heard on Channel 4 / HBO Max / BBC Radio 4. 'Deeply affecting and incredibly funny' ★★★★★ (The Wee Review). 'A one-woman multiverse of gleeful possibility' ★★★★½ (The Reviews Hub). title: Alex Mitchell: Tough
description: Alex Mitchell is on a mission to find out what it really takes to be a tough guy in modern Britain. He's had a strange couple of years – being thrust into the limelight on mainstream telly, getting into a less than healthy relationship, being abused daily online, becoming disabled, and his best friend gave birth to a now three-foot bully. But, has Alex toughened up? The easy answer is no... but he's trying. Britain’s Got Talent Finalist. 'Accomplished and intelligent' (NeurodiverseReview.co.uk). 'A natural' (Chortle.co.uk). 'Absolutely sensational' (Alesha Dixon).

title: Alexis Sakellaris: CHILD STAR
description: What if you got the opportunity of a lifetime... in your tweens?? At age 15, Alexis dreams of escaping rural Germany and becoming a famous singer. In 2013, it happens: he's cast in "Wir rocken Barcelona", a bona fide German “Glee”. With 5 other talented teens, Alexis is whisked off to Spain to film 8 episodes and countless music videos. Child stardom is in sight! Then it all goes downhill: turns out, it's a reality show, and Alexis notices he’s different from the rest of the group... or rather, not different enough. Between in-cast beef and problematic producers, Alexis grapples with being a minor on national TV, all while holding onto his biggest secret... With new original songs and unearthed footage, join acclaimed musical comedian Alexis Sakellaris (A STAN IS BORN!) as he dives into his past to share the cringiest moments of his LIFE. Previous work: ★★★★ THE STAGE Musical Comedy Award Nominee 2026 Brighton Fringe Best New Show Award Nominee 2025 Tour support Matt Rogers, Emma Holland

title: Alice Ella: Chronically Sick, Hormonal Slag.
description: Turning pain into punchlines and symptoms into chaotic musical comedy, expect original songs and parodies sprinkled with stand up, about the highs and lows of having ME, PMDD, ADHD, IBS, POTS... and great tits. This show will have you laughing, crying, and probably thinking 'what DID I just watch?!' As seen on Channel 4's The Last Leg, Brighton Girl Awards (runner up) & with over 100 million instagram views, Alice Ella is set to take her surreal, self-deprecating humour to the stage...armed with a bog roll, nipple tassels, and mostly: utter silliness.

title: Alice Keys: Rocket Fan
description: Alice Keys has fallen in love. And it’s going well! And now it isn’t. And now it is again. Join Alice as she guides you through the highs and lows of love, told in the most universal language of all. A language that runs through our blood from the moment we are born. This language is, of course, the songs of Elton John. Expect humour, silliness, tears, glittering glasses and plenty of (optional) singalongs drawn from Elton’s iconic back catalogue. Where will Alice’s yellow brick road lead her? Will her story end happily ever after? Will her costumes fit behind the piano? Find the answers to all this and more at Rocket Fan, a love story.

title: all my ex-lovers are dead
description: "all my ex-lovers are dead" is what you get when you drink a million mugs of tea and collage the diaries from your teen years across your bedroom wall like that meme from “It's Always Sunny In Philadelphia”; uncomfortably familiar but somehow reassuring. And a promise: even if it doesn't get better, at least it gets funnier. An all-too-honest, mostly autobiographical reflection on desire, the pitfalls of pity sex, poor life choices, why love is like potatoes, and how many cups of tea it takes to wake up and smell the coffee. A play for the Tumblr girlies, our younger selves, and the gals you meet in the bathrooms along the way. Previously selected for Artscape’s New Voices Programme (2021), STAND Foundation’s Pen To Paper Programme (2022), Toyota SU Woordfees (2023), the National Arts Festival’s Curated Programme (2024), Cape Town’s inaugural Heat Festival (2024), and Johannesburg's POPArt’s Next Draft programme (2025).

World class circus talent performing extraordinary feats before your very eyes! title: Amazing Stories of Blues and Soul
description: Ken Wood and the Mixers return to the fabulous Brunswick for one night only, with their fantastic four star Edinburgh Fringe show ‘Amazing Stories of Blues and Soul’. This lively, humorous show tells the story of Soul Music while a cracking eight piece band plays the songs of soul greats like James Brown, Otis Redding and Aretha Franklin. The show features the wild vocals and harmonica of the great Kenneth B Woode, ably supported by the great soul sounds of the might mighty Mixers. So put on your Hi Heel Sneakers, your sharpest suit or your poshest frock and get ready to dance the night away to some of the rockingest Blues, Soul and Ska sounds around. Amazing Stories of Blues and Soul, the most fun you can have with your clothes on!

title: An Evening Without Kate Bush
description: Enter Strange Phenomena, Howl with the Hounds Of Love and dance on the moors with Wuthering Heights. Kate's not there, but you are. This award-winning cult cabaret pays glorious tribute to the music, fans and mythology of one of the most influential voices in British music. Featuring classic hits like Running Up That Hill, Cloudbusting and The Man With The Child In His Eyes, ‘An Evening Without Kate Bush’ returns to Brighton after selling out the Corn Exchange last year. Whether you’ve been a fan for decades or recently joined the shoal via ‘Stranger Things’, there’s never been a better time to release your inner Bush and celebrate at this joyful, unique and mind-blowing show. 'You don’t need to be a Kate Bush fan to be transported into something very special' Scotsman ★★★★ Winner Best Cabaret Adelaide Fringe 2025

title: Andrew Frost - Pretending
description: Following a sold-out Edinburgh Fringe run, Andrew Frost returns to the Brighton Fringe with a brand new show. Ever tried to figure out a magic trick? Don't. Andrew wants you to experience magic. He'll do whatever it takes to suspend your disbelief and stop you from analysing how the tricks work. He will use humour, surprises, and general deceitfulness to keep you distracted. If that doesn't work, he's not above embarrassing himself; he is a magician after all.

title: Andrew White - What a Life
description: An only-child with two siblings, Andrew White enjoyed a precocious childhood befriending retirees and penning self-indulgent poetry, until a literal circus clown warned him of imminent death on his 18th birthday. All the Jester foretold came to pass, and now Andrew must reflect on life without three close loved ones and do his best to honour their memories. Don’t miss this hilarious, honest and heart-warming new stand-up comedy show. Tour Support for Catherine Bohart, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Jason Manford

title: Anna Dumitriu: BioArt Transformations
description: Art and science combine in “BioArt Transformations”, a cutting-edge new exhibition by internationally acclaimed contemporary artist Anna Dumitriu. A pioneer of the BioArt movement, Dumitriu works with living bacteria, DNA, and technology to create intricate sculptures, installations and textile works that draw threads across time from the history of science and medicine to cutting-edge research in synthetic biology, genomics and AI. Her strange and affecting objects take the form of relics of Dumitriu’s sublime laboratory-based processes created in contemporary biotechnological research settings, interwoven with historic stories of contagion and alchemy. A visually stunning and deeply thought-provoking experience, this exhibition is a must-see for the curious, challenging our view on the future of medicine, technology, and humanity. There will be a series of events throughout the exhibition. Book for these via the artist’s website https://annadumitriu.co.uk/news/

title: Anna Leong Brophy: Born Sexy Yesterday (Work in Progress)
description: Sure, Anna was born the old-fashioned way - an all-powerful Supreme Being spontaneously deposited in a perfect female human body who immediately fell in love with the first man she saw. But what happens to your average Supreme Being once you’ve saved the world, done the laundry and the years have rolled by? Anna Leong Brophy (How to Train Your Dragon, Shadow & Bone, one half of sketch duo EGG) takes a joyful frolic through the male gaze (yum), aging (gross) and sexy, sexy sci-fi, all while covered in blue goo.

title: Are You Even Indian?
description: Are You Even Indian? follows a British-born Indian woman and an Indian-born man who fall in love. It feels like fate. A meet-cute, wedding dances, Bollywood jokes, and plans for a future they believe they are building together. But as the relationship deepens, so do the cracks. Which country is “home”? What language will their child speak? Who gets to decide what makes someone truly Indian? As personal choices clash with family expectations and social labels, the couple must confront love, compromise, and the reality of living in between. Neither fully British, nor fully Indian, the show reflects the experience of a generation navigating relationships and heritage. When both your homes ask you to apologise for who you are, how do you decide where you belong? This is a love story about proving your identity, not just to each other, but within the community. Best Show, Bitesize Festival 2025 Riverside Studios, London
description_html:
Are You Even Indian? follows a British-born Indian woman and an Indian-born man who fall in love. It feels like fate. A meet-cute, wedding dances, Bollywood jokes, and plans for a future they believe they are building together.

As personal choices clash with family expectations and social labels, the couple must confront love, compromise, and the reality of living in between. Neither fully British, nor fully Indian, the show reflects the experience of a generation navigating relationships and heritage.

title: Babel Beast
description: Babel Beast is an experimental, multi-lingual cabaret of monstresses, made by and for mythical creatures of Ancient Greece. Whether repurposing pop culture, childhood nostalgia or mythic storytelling, this explosive one-woman spectacle is familiar yet strange, energising yet intimate, and fundamentally “a love letter to speaking many languages” (audience response). Natoli explores her multiculturalism and lived experiences of (un)belonging in between cultural spaces - herself divided between Italy, France and the UK - hoping to leave you with more questions than when you arrive! Cabaret lovers and Greek myth fanatics alike are invited for a night of frightening and seductive, strange and reflective encounters. From the Siren's song, to Medusa’s sharp tongue, to Lamia's ritual - the Sphynx has a wild line-up waiting for you...

title: Ballgowns & Bib Shorts
description: “Opera humour - so different. Fabulous!” Welcome to Ballgowns & Bib Shorts - a musical memoir of an opera-singing triathlete.! Following on from the success of Disgraceful, which debuted at Dorchester Arts in 2023, Grace Lovelass is back with a new show reliving the peaks and plateaus along her climb to becoming a professional opera singer and IRONMAN triathlete. Billed as a musical memoir, Ballgowns & Bib Shorts is the soundtrack to a decade of operatic and athletic adventures. Alongside comedy and candid insights, Grace presents an effervescent mix of classical, show tunes and popular music, as well as irreverent, custom-written parody, to take audiences on a journey that proves dreams really do come true... “Incredible, hilarious, uplifting and amazing' - get ready for a moving and inspirational evening with our wonderful singing triathlete this year, and see what can happen with a little motivation!

title: Bassie Gracie Presents: Screenshots
description: Bassie Gracie is a punk performance poet known for her musings as a musician, feel good feminism and general silliness. Frequently found shoeless on the Monday of a music festival telling a 70 year old geezer ‘punk was better in my day’. Her show combines jokes, anecdotes and rhyming couplets, intended to inspire, amuse and excite. 'Screenshots' maps her work from it's beginnings, tying her poetry to key moments in her life, in history and in Brighton. “Bassie Gracie captivates hearts and minds with her powerful words of wisdom and good times” - Beans On Toast Following in the footsteps of John Cooper Clarke and Kae Tempest, Gracie makes poetry accessible, engaging and, somehow, a financially viable career path.

title: Bear With Me
description: Get down, dirty and howl like the filthy animal you know you really are. Bear With Me is a comedic exploration of anger, womanhood, expressing ourselves fully and letting out the bear inside. Using clown, improvisation and audience interaction we’ll all shed something deep, primal and above all funny. Hop aboard this hilariously hairy and healing journey with cabaret clown extraordinaire Shea Wojtus as the stage becomes the woods and she becomes a collection of her favourite feral beasts.

Bear With Me is a comedic exploration of anger, womanhood, expressing ourselves fully and letting out the bear inside. Using clown, improvisation and audience interaction we’ll all shed something deep, primal and above all funny. Hop aboard this hilariously hairy and healing journey with cabaret clown extraordinaire Shea Wojtus as the stage becomes the woods and she becomes a collection of her favourite feral beasts.
meta_description: A wild and hilarious howl from the heart of womanhood. Come and connect with the filthy beast you know you really are. - Presuming Ed - Sat 30th 2026, Sun 31st 2026

title: BEAVERHAUSEN
description: BEAVERHAUSEN is back baby! For its second run of Brighton Fringe and he is SO excited. It was a success when debuted at KOMEDIA last year with multiple sell out shows (YAY) Beaverhausen is a silly sausage and queer gender-bending drag king who serves "an open hearted celebration of queer culture" with an extra dollop of big prat energy. He will leave you feeling camp, GAY and gorgeous. Join Beaverhausen as he takes you on a mission to save his hometown from something terrible.... Its drag, its burlesque and its bonkers!!!... So strap in, that's right ;) and enjoy a peek into Beaverhausens' world. Directed by "burlesque and musical comedy champion" - Selena Mersey. Veronica Von Beaverhausen is also a proud member of Clapback Club, award winning queer & feminist theatre makers and collective. AND co-producer of "BeaverTits", their sell-out cabaret show and fundraiser.

title: BeHeMoth: StarStruck (Work In Progress)
description: In the ruthless pursuit of fame, self-proclaimed “world’s hottest sketch group” Behemoth must stage their biggest show yet while appeasing the ever-growing demands of a shadowy corporate sponsor. Behemoth: StarStuck is a joyously subversive sketch comedy spectacle exploring the relationship between art, fame and commerce through clowning, multimedia chaos, grand theatrics, boybands and an ancient parasitic CEO made entirely of Velcro. Behemoth’s dreamlike, immersive style spills beyond the stage, with faux brand deals and influencer collaborations. Blending narrative seamlessly amongst scenes, Behemoth’s unique take on sketch comedy is refreshing for all. 2025 Sketch Off Finalists

title: Ben Hur
description: They said it was unachievable! They said it couldn't be done! And yet... The greatest book ever penned is brought to the stage by the towering team who produced One Man Two Guvnors, The Ladykillers, Brief Encounter and last year's smash hit The 39 Steps... and with just four actors! Complete with a bona fide chariot race, an authentic sea battle and a decadent Roman orgy (suitable for all ages), Ben Hur is The Greatest Story Ever Told.

title: Ben Macpherson Does Poems at Adults
description: Adults often have ears. Ben Macpherson has poems. It's time to put those together with you in the driving seat. You're in complete control of the show picking from 30 vibes, be that silly, sordid, sad or sweet. With poems covering everything from the world's best cheese to the intimate lives of cartoon characters, Ben Macpherson has the poem for you. Blending friendly chat with style and genre, Ben is a verbal tour de force, bound to leave you laughing, crying and so much more. “Absolutely Delightful” - Hollie McNish “Dazzlingly, Dizzyingly Brilliant” - Brian Bilston “Full of Humour and Drive” - Michael Rosen “Ben Macpherson is a joy” - Caroline Bird "Ben’s brimful of wit and panache” - Michael Pedersen "I had a blast watching Macpherson's show, it's a marvel to see an artist pour so much love into their craft. I loved the concept of choosing poems off the wall, and I loved their variety even more. Lovers of spoken word, please go and witness it yourself" Panos Audience member

title: Ben Pope: Work in Progress
description: Ben Pope (''bags of charisma'' **** - Fest) is back to square one. He is tired of his old jokes and he is scared of his new ones. This show will be roughly 60mins of the latter. Come and join him as he stress-tests new stuff: a routine about teeth, some thoughts about Catholicism, plums and escape rooms. Maybe he'll even read some bits of a book he's writing. It's definitely going to be a show for fans of collage, stylistically speaking. A real ransom note of a show. Come see! The UK''s print media can''t be wrong: ''Pope is superb company'' **** - Evening Standard ''Beautifully constructed'' **** - The Scotsman ''Could an audience with the actual Pope offer as much?'' **** - The Telegraph'

title: Bertie Hodd: Dad Jokes
description: Chuck the sudoku and the gender binary aside and join award-winning drag-king Dad for a queer reparenting extravaganza, fusing clown, drag and cabaret. Through the lens of toast, cycling jokes and Radio 4, Dad Jokes takes you on a coming-of-middle-age story about one regular dad's attempt to understand his queer child. Expect lectures in lesbianism, hymns on housework and a much needed visit from Mummy. Bertie Hodd is an award-winning multi-skilled hyper-hyphenated performer and live artist working across comedy, drag, theatre, clowning, directing and producing. Bertie's work is based in political comedy, (extra)ordinary cabaret and audience connectivity. They like to make work that is inherently stupid, surprisingly resonant and always, full of heart. Winner The Best of Brighton Fringe Award 2025 + Brighton's Big Drag Pageant 2023, Finalist Man Up! 2022 + Top of the Slops 2023, Winner Audience Choice Award 2019 (with Clap Back Club) + Pebble Trust Award 2019 (with Girl Code Theatre)

title: Best in Fringe Comedy - The Early Show
description: It's the very best in Fringe Comedy, in a early evening weekend show! This is a finely selected comedy line-up with an exciting mix of top comedy headliners, the pick of the UK and International comedy circuits and the best performers selected from shows all around the Brighton Fringe. See a bundle of hilarious comics all packed together into this laugh-out-loud and side-splitting hour of comedy featuring five fantastically funny comedians. It's different at every show. You'll see something you weren't expecting, something new and maybe something extraordinary! It's the perfect start to your weekend Fringe night out. See the acts here, and go and see the ones you love again later at thier solo shows aound the Festival. A sell out show internationally! "Enjoy every minute of it... If you haven't been to this show you should make it your priority ★★★★★" (FunnyTonne, Melbourne Comedy Festival) "Rapid-fire comedy. ★★★★" (RIP IT UP, Adelaide Fringe Festival)

title: Beyond the Book Festival
description: Beyond the Book Festival returns to Brighton Fringe on Saturday 16 May 2026, celebrating the power of stories in all their forms. Set in the historic Friends Meeting House on Ship Street, this vibrant literary celebration brings together readers, writers, and creatives for an unforgettable day of inspiration and connection. Our stellar line-up features global bestselling author Adele Parks in conversation with her movie-producer husband Jim Parks, offering unique insights into storytelling across page and screen. Discover new voices through our diverse programme of panels led by leading authors, industry talks behind the scenes of publishing, and exclusive agent pitching sessions for unagented writers. Beyond the Book Festival creates an inclusive space that celebrates creativity, diversity, and the joy of storytelling. Join us for unforgettable moments that go beyond the page.

title: Big Backstory
description: Big Backstory is a debut WIP show by Belgian artist Julian Gypens, combining stand-up comedy and contemporary dance to explore the amusing and fragile reality of living in a body that on a societal and scientific level has no ass... The show smashes two contradictory art-forms together in a daring balancing act. Where the tale of a man and his tragic separation from his rear-end is woven with choreography and gags. Can this comedian reunite with his long-lost buns, or is he cursed to dance with nothing to shake?

title: Big Quiffy Bingo
description: From the very towering tips of his perfectly-coiffed quiff down to the tips of his bejewelled toes, Boogaloo Stu is altogether completely ridiculous, simply fabulous and utterly idiotic. Big Quiffy Bingo is his gloriously playful and anarchic bingo experience, where utter filth, joyous fun and flash mob dances collide. Following his fifth annual sell-out season of shows at the Brighton Spiegelgardens, Stu is back at the Spiegeltent once again, this time joined by his very special guests The Two Wrongies. Feel the tension rise as Stu’s balls begin to churn; when they finally drop, you’ll feast on a smorgasbord of smut, outrageous antics, buttock-clenching workouts, hilarious prizes and some very ballsy bingo to boot. Stu’s gift hamper will be stuffed to the brim with all manner of exotic prizes, so come on down and win it big! Please note - this event features sexual swearwords from the outset..

title: Binding Agent
description: “Look. You two need to loosen up a bit, because you might be here for an unbelievably long time, okay? Just a piece of advice. - Do you want a smoothie?” Simon Huxley (corporate hack/ amateur historian), and Annette (Languages Teacher/ yogi) struggle to co-operate whilst trying to escape their unpredictable, health-obsessed kidnapper. Political, blender-adjacent dark comedy written by Harry Smithson. Directed by award-winning director Chris Yarnell. Following a sold out run at the White Bear Theatre, this politically driven piece of new writing explores the difficulty of nuanced discussion, disagreement in the modern age, and how pre-conceptions can blind us even during hostile, bizarre, and life-threatening situations.

title: Biscuit Barrel's Family Pack
description: 60 sketches in 60 minutes, without the rude bits! This is the family-friendly edition of Biscuit Barrel's lightning-paced sketch show. Featuring slapstick, songs, absurd characters, and even beatboxing. Not to mention all your favourite biscuits disrupting the show throughout, and giving you their side of the story. Are Rich Teas really that boring? Perhaps you just need to get to know them. Nominated for Brighton Fringe's Spirit of the Fringe Award 2025. As seen at Edinburgh Fringe, Hollywood Fringe, Brighton Spiegeltent, Leicester Comedy Festival, and ten other international festivals.

title: Black Brighton Market 'Festival Day'
description: The third Brighton Fringe edition of the popular Black Brighton Pop Up Market bringing you an exciting family-friendly day festival featuring a vibrant array of arts & craft stalls, performances, storytelling, workshops, and a cultural café - something for everyone! Addressing some of the limited opportunities for Black People/People of Colour, the pioneering Black Brighton Market venture creates a unique space for more communities have the chance to sell their art, goods and services, and to also showcase taster sessions, workshops and performances to the wider general public in the state-of-the-art Ironworks Studios. More details available on the Black Brighton Market website. All are welcome. THIS EVENT IS SPONSORED BY THE IRENE MENSAH BURSARY & FLO CREATIVE IN COLLABORATION WITH IRONWORKS STUDIOS AND THE ENTANGLED COORDINATES CO-CREATED THEATRE PRODUCTION ‘THIS IS NEW BRIGHTON’

title: Blip Barp: An Alien Love Story
description: Meet Blip Blarp — an ambitious alien with a mission: conquer Earth and produce 1,000 offspring. There’s just one problem. Dating humans is... complicated. As Blip Blarp navigates Earth’s baffling mating rituals — from awkward flirting to modern dating — she discovers that seduction is harder than planetary takeover. Blending comedy, burlesque, and extra-terrestrial absurdity, the show skewers swipe culture, romantic expectations, and the universal longing to be desired. Audiences can expect an hour of playful provocation and cosmic camp wrapped in intergalactic glamour. Equal parts sexy, smart, and silly, this is a romantic comedy from another planet — one that proves love, lust, and loneliness are truly universal languages.

title: Bluff Creek
description: In 1967, Bob Gimlin and Roger Patterson went for a ride in the woods with a movie camera and a mission: to catch a Bigfoot on film. And that’s just what they did. To some, it’s the real McCoy; to others, it’s just a man in a monkey suit. To Bob Gimlin, it’s free drinks at the Idle Hour Inn and a flock of fans lining up to hear him tell the story one more time. Now, decades later, an investigator from out of town is starting to ask all the wrong questions, and a figure from the past is threatening to let slip what really happened at Bluff Creek all that time ago. Based on real events, Bluff Creek examines the lives of the men behind the Patterson- Gimlin film, the iconic piece of footage that brought us the image of Bigfoot we know today. It’s a story about the cost of lies, the nature of belief, and the enduring fascination with America’s resident Abominable Snowman. Praise for Loose Thread's debut play The Graveyard: ★★★★ -“Dead Good” The Derek Awards, ★★★★- Binge Fringe

title: BOGGIN
description: “Generational loss... The older we get the more we lose, the stories, the people and the fragmented memories all get distorted, but what if we could clear this image? What if the VHS spat itself back out, the boogyman strangled us with the tape and sent us down the river? BOGGIN aims to help us navigate this low road.” “BOGGIN” is an experimental, auto-biographical performance that explores the concept of 'Generational Loss' and applying it to the human experience. “BOGGIN” aims to combine elements of family memories with creatures and tales from Scottish Folklore. All while communicating a story recently passed down to me by my Grandmother about our family's history and connections to Ethiopia and my family's part in the Great Abbai Expedition. The show aims to challenge viewers to care more about what their elders have to say, to care about their culture, to care about the documentation of one's life. A love letter to my Scottish heritage, to my Family and to my Uncle.

title: Borderline
description: Struggling musician Izzy and serial dater Marina both get diagnosed with one of the most stigmatised and misunderstood mental health disorders. Izzy strives to find peace in her relationship with her girlfriend, while Marina tries to find meaning beyond partying and one-night-stands. Will these two very different women find a way to navigate life in this neurotypical world? Our recommended ticket price is £15.00, although we have a sliding scale from £10. If you would like to see the show but the cost is prohibitive, please contact us to arrange a ticket. We also welcome any payment above the recommended price, which will go towards supporting production costs (we are just two theatremakers doing it all ourselves!)

title: Born Traitor
description: "This wasn’t the guy I’d written music with, performed on stage with... cried with at our friend’s funeral. He was something else. It was that look in his eyes when he saw the blood from his lip. His pupils were so tiny, he looked like an animal" Witch House Dreamers are a fictional British-American band who managed to find success in the late 1990s and early 2000s. With their blend of hard rock and dark psychedelia, nihilistic horror-inspired lyrics and being led by frontman Gary Machen, they seemed unstoppable. Even after two tragedies struck the band and nearly caused a break-up, they still managed to come together to save the band and themselves. However, when the sole female member, guitarist Harley Carter, comes forward to the police, she has a disturbing and potentially explosive story to tell about the band’s lead singer. With this single action, the band’s very existence is suddenly under threat and Harley’s reputation, reliability and even her safety are called into question

title: Bourgeois & Maurice
description: Cult-cabaret stars Bourgeois & Maurice return to Brighton Fringe for a new night of glamour-soaked nihilism and anarchic musical comedy. Known for original, razor-sharp satirical songs with a gleefully dark edge, the critically-acclaimed duo will mix old favourites with brand spanking new material for a riotous musical journey through the state of the world - frivolous, unhinged, and impeccably doomed. Their unique brand of pop-satire and far-fetched fashion, has won them legions of fans around the world. From New York to Kettering, from an ex-public toilet to the Royal Opera House, it doesn’t matter to them as long as there’s a piano to bash, a lick of clear mascara to apply and a (semi) willing audience watching. Come for the laughs. Stay for the darkness. Leave humming something you probably shouldn’t.

title: Brad Jon Kane's Untelevised and Unfilmed Birthday Celebration Special!
description: Upon entering the room, you play the role of "Live Studio Audience Member" of an untelevised, unfilmed birthday celebration show of comedian and entertainer, Brad Jon Kane! The birthday celebration happens every year, but this is the first time in history that it will take place in a public setting! - which is why your role as "Live Studio Audience Member" is so very important! As typical in untelevised, unfilmed birthday celebration shows, there will be games, special guests and quick-fire anecdotes from our big birthday boy host! Your ever so important duties will be to: - laugh wholeheartedly (belly out, proud posture!) - warmly welcome Brad into your family as a new family member, allowing him to join you to Christmas, Birthdays and your upcoming wedding. - improvise your role as "live studio audience member" when asked questions about your profession and hobbies. Don't worry, it'll make sense in the end! - Sketch Off Quarter Finalist 2026

title: Brighton Beach Boys play Bowie - Golden Years
description: The Brighton Beach Boys play Bowie: Golden Years- Greatest hits The band recreates David Bowie’s hits and favourites, drawing on material drawn from throughout his illustrious career. David Bowie emerged from London's vibrant 1960s music scene to become one of the most influential artists of the 20th century. His breakthrough came in 1969, leading to an extraordinary run of groundbreaking albums in the 1970s that redefined rock music. Known for his chameleon-like ability to reinvent himself, Bowie's artistic evolution carried him through multiple personas and musical styles across five decades, earning him a place among Britain's most celebrated cultural icons. Featuring the vocal talents of Glen Richardson and Stephen Wrigley, lead guitar by Adrian Marshall, with Dave Barnard on bass, Charlotte Glasson on Sax, Flute, and anything else, Jono Harrison on keys and Vinnie Lammi on drums. Whether it's Major Tom or The Jeanie Genie, Kooks or Ziggy Stardust, this is a show full of stories

title: Brighton Food Tours
description: Brighton Food Tours brings you the classic VIB (Very Independent Brighton) food tour. We take you off the beaten track to visit some of our favourite independent food and drink vendors in the city. We give you a taste of everything; food, drink and some wonderful local history and info about the Brighton food scene. You will meet some of the vendors, hear their stories and passions behind their creations and eat, shop and stroll around our fair city. Suitable for 12 yrs+ (babes in arms are welcome too). We can cater for all dietary requirements as long as we know about them in advance. Once you book your ticket, email [email protected] to let us know your requirements.

title: Brighton Fringe Ceilidh
description: Cut A Shine Ceilidh Klub Kaput at Caravanserai Get ready for 3 hours of ceilidh, hoedown and barn dances with the Cut A Shine Barn Dance Band. The band host monthly dances in London, and are taking to the road to tour our dance show across the country. About The Dancing Although called many things; Ceilidh, Country Dance, Barn Dance, Ceili Dance, Square Dance, Set Dancing - one thing is certain, it's all "traditional" and how people would entertain themselves before TV and raves. We have a live band on stage to provide the music, and a "Dance Caller", who is expert at teaching you all the simple and fun moves. First we learn the dance by walking through it with the caller, then the band strike up, and we dance to the music. You don't need any experience, just willingness to have a go and have fun.

title: Brighton Fringe Comedian of the Year
description: ★ Brighton Fringe Comedian of the Year 2026 ★ The Brighton Fringe Comedian of the Year competition is a key platform for identifying outstanding emerging stand-up talent. Bringing together comedians from across the UK circuit, the competition showcases strong original material, assured stagecraft and distinctive comic voices. Through a series of semi finals where the act with the largest audience vote with go through and one act that is chosen by an industry professional guest judge. one act is ultimately selected as the standout comedian of Brighton Fringe 2026 with two other placing gaining valuable industry recognition and profile at one of the UK’s leading comedy festivals. There are 6 Semi Finals in which 2 acts will go through from each with the Final being a 12 act double bill on Sunday 31st

title: Brighton Gay Men's Chorus Presents: Heroes
description: Brighton Gay Men’s Chorus is back for the Fringe with a feel-good show about the queer icons who helped shape us as well as the heroes right here in our own city. We’ll be celebrating the heroes many of us grew up with, from David Bowie, Freddie Mercury and George Michael, through to newer heroes like JADE who are carrying that legacy forward today. Alongside choral favourites and a handful of solos, the show also features interviews with LGBTQ+ heroes from Brighton & Hove, putting the spotlight on those doing brilliant things for our community right now. All the music is arranged by our in-house music team, giving familiar songs a fresh choral twist. Expect to laugh, shed a few tears and to experience plenty of queer joy, as we celebrate the artists who helped us feel seen growing up, and the local heroes still making a difference today.

title: Brighton Rocks Film Festival - Programme Launch!
description: At this launch event, Brighton Rocks Film Festival Director and team will introduce the festival’s ethos, give highlights of the upcoming event (15-21 June) and invite discussion around what audiences want and need from a film festival today. Filmmakers featured in this year’s programme will also present sneak-preview clips and talk about their work. Brighton Rocks is proud to showcase some of the most diverse, original and boundary-pushing films being made today. Now in its 9th edition, the festival has become one of Brighton’s key platforms for independent cinema. This year’s programme includes amazing films made in Brighton alongside LGBTQIA+, student and experimental work, documentaries and dramas. The festival will also feature special strands focused on challenging transphobia and responding to troubling political trends, highlighting film as a space for resistance and debate. Afterwards everyone is invited to continue the conversation informally at The Actors Pub.

title: BrightOn the Conchords
description: Ladies (and to a lesser extent Gentlemen) of the World, prepare for Business Time! Get ready for a hour of deadpan lyrics, awkward banter and musical mayhem as BrightOn the Conchords, Brighton’s premier (and probably only) Flight of the Conchords tribute band, take to the Fringe again. All the harmonies, all the laughs, with none of the accents. We’ll be performing at 2 venues, with 2 different sets packed with classic tunes, fan favourites and deep cuts - so if you are a big fan why not come to both gigs? Get your tickets soon as our last Fringe festival gig sold out!

title: Buddhism For Today and Tomorrow
description: In the mid-1970s, The Triratna Buddhist Order was emerging as a bold new Buddhist movement. Sangharakshita set out the movement's offering in four lectures given in the Royal Pavilion in 1976- later published as 'Buddhism for Today and Tomorrow'. The lectures set out the breadth and depth of the Buddha’s teaching, while directly applying it to a modern European life and society. 50 years later, the Triratna Order and community– now an international Buddhist movement– stand as legacy to Sangharakshita, who passed away in 2018 aged 93. Throughout the festival we are joined by four senior members of the Triratna Buddhist Order to revisit the theme of the relevance of Buddhism and its role in the world. The Four Gifts: Wednesday 6th May– ‘A Method of Personal Development’, with Mahamati Wednesday 13th May– ‘A Vision of Human Existence’, with Vishvapani Wednesday 20th May– ‘The Nucleus of a New Society’, with Parami Wednesday 27th May- 'A Blueprint for a New World', with Subhuti

title: Burn Baby Burn: LA Inferno
description: LA is burning. When a pair of old Angelenos lose everything, they must navigate overwhelming grief, toxic ash and paperwork purgatory, surrounded by clueless celebs, soulless politicians and insurance vampires. 6000 miles away, their (very) anxious daughter is trying hard not to unravel. Fringe Theatre Awards 2x nominee Burn Baby Burn is a stripped-back, sharp-witted mix of storytelling, satire and songs, exploring the human cost of climate change and the humans fanning the flames. Asking questions nobody wants to answer like: why was LA’s biggest reservoir empty? What the hell is a ‘firenado’? And how did 80s icon Steve Guttenberg become a low-key hero? With a childhood shaped by wildfires and her parents home destroyed in January 2025's blaze, Hunter's done what any good performer would do...turned her heartbreak and existential dread into a hilarious and heartbreaking show. Following her acclaimed debut solo show, in partnership with Slackline Productions, champion of women 35+.

title: Buster
description: Through cinematic mime and physical theatre, this high-octane performance follows silent film star Buster Keaton's journey from childhood through to his late career. Focusing on Buster's own perspective on what it means to be a performer, the show explores his highs, his lows, the friends he made along the way, his struggle with alcoholism, and his journey to eventually reaching what he described as "the happiest time of his life". Set to one continuous soundtrack, the ensemble brings the story to life through mime, clowning, lip-sync, puppetry, dance and live onstage lighting control, creating a film-like atmosphere that reimagines mime for a new generation. A celebration of genius, resilience and the timeless craft of physical storytelling. For older audiences, a chance to revisit a screen legend. For younger ones, an introduction to Buster Keaton's brilliance.

title: C'est Magnifique
description: C’est Magnifique, winners of the 2025 Fringe Outgoing Touring Award and brought to you by Chocolate Box Theatre, is escapist heaven for any musical theatre fan. Step back to an imagined cabaret haunt in 1930s Paris or Berlin and join Cyril the emcee with Gaylord, Babette, Goldie, Bert and the indomitable Zelda as they perform ballads, torch songs, comedy numbers, musical theatre favourites and a pop of pop. C’est Magnifique appears in the Wunderbarn for four fabulous nights, plus a very special C’est Magnifique in the Sky aboard the iconic Brighton i360. Performed high above the seafront, it promises sparkle, sophistication and sky-high entertainment. Selling out to packed audiences with rave reviews, they don’t just can-can , they will, will; accompanied by live music and (very) high kicks! Set to take cabaret to new heights with a special performance on Brighton's i360!

title: Cabarave
description: IT’S FRINGE!! IT’S CABARAVE!! IT’S TIME TO PARTY!! Why? ’Cause you’re filthy — and we’re gorgeous. Kick off your Fringe Festival the only acceptable way at Cabarave’s Filthy Gorgeous takeover of the iconic Spiegeltent on Opening Saturday. After a sell-out smash last Fringe, Cabarave were invited back to host the opening night in the festival’s most renowned venue — YES PLEASE. Expect an electrifying night of banging big beats, headlined by legendary party starter Krafty Kuts, plus show-stopping cabaret from the cream of the Brighton circuit. Get stuck into immersive fun with walkabout acts, Tarot readings, and a glitter artist ready to filthy you up. Filthy Gorgeous ticket holders you are also the stars of this party. This theme is about expression, not perfection — colourful, experimental, sassy hot messes head to our dancefloor. You can’t be too flamboyant for this party, and the best dressed wins tickets to our next event. Come pristine. Leave undone. Fringe starts here.

title: Cabaret Boheme
description: Cabaret Boheme is back at Brighton Fringe 2026! Join us on 24th May for a fabulous night dripping in feathers, tassels, and unapologetic sass. Studio 4 All Dance brings its students to the Cabaret Boheme stage in a dazzling celebration of self-expression because everyone deserves to feel fierce and unleash their inner show diva. Expect men in heels, high glamour, and deliciously high camp, with appearances from Brighton legend Dave the Bear and the irresistible burlesque babe Allegra Rose. The night is hosted by powerhouse performers Veronica Blacklace and Chris Hide, serving vocals, charisma, and pure cabaret magic. Add Purple Haze’s gravity-defying spectacle, and you’ve got a show that’s bold, brilliant, and bursting with attitude. Cabaret, Boheme Confidence. Chaos (the fabulous kind). Brighton, this one’s unmissable.

title: Cabaret Impedimenta
description: An impossible variety show hosted by John-Luke Roberts. Amazing acts try to entertain you under increasingly difficult circumstances. Spin the wheel, and find out if a singer can still sing with a bubble machine pointed point-blank in their face, or how a professional hula hooper gets on while haunted by a Victorian ghost, or if there is any point to ventriloquism when the lights are off, or if a comedian’s jokes work when the microphone is held in the mouth of the oldest audience member.

title: Can we talk about this? (Work In Progress)
description: Stand up comedian Fitzgerald Honger has a few jokes to run by you... Now whether you're approaching, currently in your thirties or would like to reminisce about they way things were, this show is for you. And if you're really young and want a glimpse of what your future could entail if you don't eat all your veggies, this is also for you. Of course all manner of other topics will be discussed and they'll be so relatable. Like super relatable - almost like I have gained access to your private emails through an elaborate phishing scam. You'll be surprised by how many people's password is still ABC123! In today's world, it feels like there are so many things that we don't really understand. I just want to ask the question 'Can we talk about this?'

title: Can't Kill the Spirit
description: Can't Kill the Spirit follows Chloe, a former Greenham Common activist who reclaims her voice of protest, forty years after her experiences at the Peace Camp, where during the 1980s thousands of women lived and campaigned against the installation of cruise missiles. When a climate demonstration leads to life-changing consequences for her and her family today, Chloe must decide where her priorities lie. Inspired by women of protest then and now, Can’t Kill the Spirit is a moving story of reconciling personal and global choices and a timely reminder of the need for upstanders in a world of bystanders. This "very impressive, engaging and emotive production" (theatreandtonic.co.uk), garnering **** reviews when it premiered in London in July 2025, links a vital part of British radical history with today’s climate concerns and protests.

title: CaraDANCErai!
description: Step into the rhythm at CaraDANCErai!, Caravanserai's interactive dance workshops, where the crowd becomes the show. Whether it’s dancehall, afrobeats, house, DnB, throwback pop, or RnB, our workshops are full of big, feel-good tunes, guided routines, simple line dances, playful battles, and collective movement that’s exciting without being intimidating. Follow along with the leader's moves or do your own wild, joyful thing; the sweet spot or between a dance class & jumping about with your mates at a club or festival. No pressure, no judgement, all ages & abilities very welcome. Sessions delivered by BamBam Dance Fitness on the 2nd, 16th and 30th Sessions delivered by Line Dance with Bea on the 9th TBC 23rd Free to join – reserve a ticket or just swing by!

title: Carnival Collective Presents
description: Brighton's genre-bending legends return to the Fringe for a rhythmically raucous evening. CARNIVAL COLLECTIVE will be delivering their combo of Latin infused drum & bass, funk, ska, dub and jungle played on a vast bateria of Brazilian drums and percussion, with a phat horn section, synths, dirty bass lines and lush vocal harmonies. Famed as the fastest selling show at Brighton Fringe 2023. Supported by: MAD APPLE CIRCUS A high-energy ska punk nine-piece from Bristol, known for their explosive live shows and genre-defying sound. Fusing infectious horn riffs, rapid-fire rap flows, and powerful female vocals. THE MAGNIFICANT KEVENS Veterans of the Theatre and Circus Fields of Glastonbury, and masters of musical comedy mayhem, playing with their audience not to them. Choose a song off the menu but expect a twist. Stay until close to explore the deeper realms of Jungle with DJ and producer ANDREW WHITWELL.

title: Carousel's Pop-Up Poets
description: Joyful, funny and touching, you've never heard the spoken word quite like this! Returning to The Fringe after ‘popping up’ to unsuspecting audiences in 2024, Carousel’s learning disabled comedians and poets have once again come to laugh in the face of societal preconceptions, and in turn make us all laugh at life in general. Performing are poets Charlie Hatton, Susannah Hall-Hirst and Electric Mutant X plus special guests. Expect surreal whimsey, psychedelic sci-fi word wizardry and poems of love, life and loss. Ending the night on a literal high-note is local Pop-Rock legends Beat Express. "I was awe-inspired by the levels of creativity, imagination, talent and charisma exhibited by all of your poets." (Audience member) The Pop-Up Poets are supported by local learning disability-led arts charity Carousel, a multi award-winning organisation championing learning disabled people's inclusion in the arts.

title: Caspar Thomas: The Art of Close-Up Magic (Volume 2)
description: Returning to perform at his 10th Brighton Fringe. With stunning magic, unbelievable skill and an elegant performing style Caspar demonstrates pure close-up magic and sleight of hand, with no stooges, no electronics and no camera tricks. All presented with refinement by an understated showman. “Master of sleight of hand... [Caspar] drew the audience in to the point where he had them eating, almost literally, out of his hand.” – FringeReview.co.uk “You won’t see this level of skill anywhere else in the world. He should be charging £100 per ticket.” – Jerry Sadowitz

title: CatGPT: Scratch & Rebuild!
description: CatGPT is the hilarious true tail of how a Welshman resurrected his cat Lentil 10 years after he died with robotics, custom AI, a synthesiser and lots more. L3NT1L is now reborn as a travelling life coach, offering profound (and sometimes useless) advice to crowd questions via his unique feline AI. Having been nominated for Best New Show at Brighton last year L3NT1L is aiming for a full-body makeover in time for his return. In this all-new version he promises a wild new kitty synthesiser (in conjunction with Orbital’s Phil Hartnoll) and neon whiskers. CatGPT is unlike anything else on earth and has wowed audiences from Edinburgh Fringe to Cheltenham Science Festival as well as chaotic tours to Japan, New Zealand and Australia. It’s an all-improvised feel-good show peppered with some poignant moments, neurodiverse logic and a hint of science. This year there’s also a special all-ages “kitten version” for younger cat fans. Bring your life questions, leave questioning everything!
