title: Cava Charley’s Backstage Pass
description: Dashing drag-dandy Cava Charley hosts an all singing, all dancing theatrical extravaganza. You’re invited into the backstage chaos as Cava deals with cast mate crises, management malpractice, and a building that’s crumbling underfoot. You’re an audience looking for a good time, you’re a tired artiste sharing gossip in the dressing room... you might be called upon to save the show! Packed with song and silliness, Backstage Pass is a funny and fearless look at the legacy of mental illness in the arts, our reticence towards self care, and what draws us to the magic and the mayhem of live theatre.

title: CHEKHOVIAN : Letters & Scenes
description: CHEKHOVIAN weaves together scenes from Anton Chekhov's four major plays, with intimate letters between Chekhov and the newly formed Moscow Arts Theatre and thus, reveals the artistic and personal struggles and joys that shaped their ground-breaking work. An intimate evening exploring how Chekhov so perfectly captured love, longing and the quiet complexities of the human heart. ​With scenes from The Seagull, Uncle Vanya, Three Sisters and The Cherry Orchard. Created and Directed by Sian Webber

title: Chemistry
description: Steph’s battling chronic depression. Jamie’s crashed after pushing himself way too far, way too fast. They meet in a psychiatrist’s waiting room - hardly the stuff that rom-coms are made of - but the spark is instant and undeniable. What follows is messy, electric and unexpectedly beautiful. When your own brain feels like the enemy, is falling in love the bravest thing you can do - or the most reckless? Studies show that 1 in 4 adults in England will experience a mental health problem each year. Darkly funny and deeply moving, Jacob Marx Rice's Chemistry is a frank exploration of how we manage love - and life - when we rely on chemicals to keep us balanced.

title: Chief Petty Officer
description: Chief Petty Officer is a sharp, visually driven comedy lecture about history’s smallest grudges and the moment they go too far. From imperial overreach to pop culture beefs, stand-up comedian and consumer champion Vix Leyton dissects petty acts that accidentally changed the world, plotting them on an ambitious scientific framework to determine who stopped at the peak – and who disastrously doubled down. Smart, structured nonsense with bite, insight and a whole lot of laughs. Vix is serious about pettiness, and she has a lot of views to share. Perfect for a BBC Radio 4-leaning brain, history lovers, anyone who has ever briefly considered placing Lego in someone’s shoe, and 50 Cent - he'll love this.

title: Chris Ford: Jehovah's Witness for the Prosecution
description: ​Chris Ford, known for his sharp wit and infectious charm, brings his Work in Progress show Jehovah’s Witness for the Prosecution to Brighton Fringe 2026. In this forty-minute set, with an opener from the London queer comedy circuit, Chris blends stories of growing up as a strict Jehovah’s Witness with life as an A&E nurse. The result is an honest, heartfelt, and hilarious exploration of identity, belief, and resilience. Through sharp storytelling and self-deprecating humour, Chris reflects on his teenage years navigating faith, shame, and desire within a world where conformity was demanded and questioning was forbidden. From secret moments of queer awakening to the absurdities of Kingdom Hall life, he delivers comedy that is as emotionally raw as it is laugh-out-loud funny. Jehovah’s Witness for the Prosecution marks a bold and deeply personal step from a promising queer comedian with a story that deserves to be heard, and laughed with.

title: Church of Italo Presents: Eat the Rich!
description: Legendary Brighton club night collective The Church of Italo know fashion. They know music, clubbing, drag and cabaret. And they know that there is only one serious solution to the problems facing society today – to Eat the Rich! Come down to Klub Kaput at Caravanserai and party like its 1789. Expect guillotine glamour, revolting revolutionaries and horrifying haute couture mixed up in a blender with punk, electro, disco and house and served to you on a silver platter. Featuring a cannibal cabaret starring some of Brighton’s best alternative drag talent, interactive set design from the iconique OddFabrications, music from DJ Dr Rat and friends, and presided over by the spectre of the revolution themselves, our very own Madame Guillotine, Mx Mother Misha! And of course it wouldn’t be the Church of Italo without a chance to involve you! The audience! Prizes will be awarded for the most revolutionary, most cannibalistic, and most aristocratic costumes of the evening. Let them eat c***!!

title: CIRCADIAN: CIRCUITS
description: Brighton drum & bass heavyweight Circadian announces his first-ever UK headline show, debuting in his hometown inside the iconic Spiegeltent this May — setting the scene for a truly unique setting for a one-of-a-kind drum & bass experience in the city centre. His tracks have been causing havoc on dancefloors globally and featured heavily in DJ sets over the past few years, experiencing a meteoric rise in the Drum & Bass Scene. Circadian now brings everything together for a special 90-minute journey set, showcasing his sound like never before. Joined by two of the freshest and most exciting names in the scene, T-Lex and Ora, this is a one-off show not to be missed by D&B fans.

title: Cirque du Piano = 6 hands x 30 fingers / 1 piano
description: Experimental circus troupe performs finger-gymnastic music for three pianists playing one piano together! "rousingly enjoyable" ★★★★ Three Weeks Playfully straddling categories of circus and a ‘serious’ classical music concert, our innovative ensemble take you on world tour of rarely heard music for three pianists playing one piano TOGETHER!!! Our musical programme visits Russia, Turkey, Switzerland and Hollywood USA before returning to our ‘bonnie wee’ Scotland for the GRANDE finale. Assembled from award-winning alumni of the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland, a captivating spectacle pushing the limits of what a piano can do! Programme includes a toy piano and 6-hand arrangements of Bach’s Toccata and Fugue in D minor, Mozart’s Rondo alla Turka, Scottish folk songs and a comic performance of Sousa’s all-American Stars and Stripes Forever. Fringe Classics are rated: "an enchanting concert" ★★★★★ Edinburgh Guide ★★★★★ Binge Fringe ★★★★★ Corr Blimey

title: Clandestino
description: Clandestino returns with a fiery, genre-defying performance fusing flamenco, jazz and rhythms from across the globe. At its heart is the incisive, impassioned footwork of flamenco dancer, Wafa Paton driving the music with electrifying force. Featuring master cantor Martin Paredes Mansilla and jazz vocalist Rachel Myer, the show unfolds as a powerful celebration of cultural exchange and resilience, revealing the shared human pulse that unites diverse musical worlds. Bold, soulful and deeply moving, Clandestino offers an unforgettable, joy-filled experience where global traditions meet and ignite on stage - rich with passion, virtuosity, surprise and rhythm filled connection.

title: Classy?
description: Eva-Jade Andrews (So you Think You’re Funny Semi-finalist 2025, Komedia New Comedian of the Year Semi-finalist 2026) and Chiff Powell (winner of mild mannered tax advisor of the year – this may not be an actual award) come from different backgrounds. Eva-Jade is fiercely proud of being working class. Chiff is painfully embarrassed of being middle class. Join them and some special guests for an hour of comedy where they may consider if it matters if you fill the kettle to the top before boiling it or whether your boiling water tap needs upgrading. Or, they may just tell some great jokes and you’ll have an amazing time and not really care what class people are just as long as they remain classy.

title: Clear - One Size Fits One
description: Get making, get curious, and explore the science behind asthma, eczema and allergies! Join us for a hands-on workshop where families can explore children’s health research through playful, imaginative activities. Designed for families with children aged 0–14, the space invites young learners and their grown-ups to step into the role of the expert, discovering allergic conditions in fun and accessible ways. You’ll get to make your own puppet patient as you move through a series of learning stations, picking up ideas about allergy symptoms, triggers and all the different things that shape how someone experiences their condition. No two puppets are the same. Neither are children. Together, we’ll explore why health journeys differ and why care needs to adapt to these differences too. Brought to you by a team of researchers, doctors and artists, this is a welcoming space to learn, imagine and connect. Whether you live with these conditions or are simply curious, come get stuck in.

title: Closure Cabaret
description: Distinguished guests, lovers and exes, dreamers and ghosters – hold on tight as we try to let go. Tonight, Razmatastique, your hopelessly romantic French-accented emcee, brings past lovers back to the Closure Cabaret for one final performance. From bad-boy playground pirates to emotionally unavailable illusionists, these heartbreakers return to the stage – jazz-handed into public inspection – to flaunt their most dazzling skills and questionable charm. Created and performed by Maria Ansdell, this absurd clown show blends character comedy, heartfelt storytelling and riotous audience participation to turn longing into laughs. "The room thrums with laughter... and encourages uplifting musings on the importance of self worth" - ★★★★ A Youngish Perspective

Distinguished guests, lovers and exes, dreamers and ghosters – hold on tight as we try to let go. Tonight, Razmatastique, your hopelessly romantic French-accented emcee, brings past lovers back to the Closure Cabaret for one final performance.

From bad-boy playground pirates to emotionally unavailable illusionists, these heartbreakers return to the stage – jazz-handed into public inspection – to flaunt their most dazzling skills and questionable charm.

Created and performed by Maria Ansdell, this absurd clown show blends character comedy, heartfelt storytelling and riotous audience participation to turn longing into laughs.

"The room thrums with laughter... and encourages uplifting musings on the importance of self worth" - ★★★★ A Youngish Perspective

title: Coco The Time-Travelling Tart
description: Darling, history is about to get naughty! Join London’s infamous socialite Coco The Time-Travelling Tart for a gloriously unhinged comedy tour through time. She’s part tour guide, part Champagne enthusiast... and absolutely the wrong woman to trust with the timeline. Along the way she tackles life’s big questions: How should a girl dress for battle? Will the future get its act together? And is Napoleon really worth the hype? Expect scandalous encounters across the centuries, glamour, and very questionable history. Smart, silly and seductive: perfect for culture vultures, comedy lovers and history nerds. Fresh from sold-out gallery tours and 30M+ views online, Coco is finally bringing her chaos to Brighton. Come help her make history - misbehave.

title: Cohabitors
description: Cohabitors explores the emotional terrain of shared living. Connection, closeness, conflict, and the quiet isolation that can exist even when we live side by side. Through a performance blending acrobatics, body percussion, and live music, we reflect on the tension between intimacy and disconnection in cramped, high-pressure living situations. The piece was inspired by moving to London for university. Stepping into a fast-paced city where high rent forces people to live in tight spaces. In these conditions, minor issues can grow quickly, and even small interactions carry weight. It asks: What does it really mean to live together? This is Clamber’s first work combining circus, live music, and community-led storytelling. With backgrounds in classical music, circus arts, and digital sound, our artists bring fresh perspectives that challenge convention and invite audiences to engage with circus in a new way.

title: COM-EDM
description: TOTAL COSMISCH: COM-EDM Crazy comedy mixed with wild dance music. Come and laugh your head off while getting your groove on. Fresh from a successful run at Leicester Comedy Festival, musical comedy duo Total Cosmisch (Dom Virgo & Matt Dearlove) return with a brand-new barrage of sketches, stories, characters and dance floor beats. Enter a world where struggling German DJs Fred & Dieter are on a mission to resurrect the original spirit of house and techno... while deep below, the Devil and his son Abezethibou attempt to overhaul the music policy in Hell. Blending surreal storytelling with club culture, gospel, rock, rave and soul, Total Cosmisch turn modern life into high-energy chaos — with manic theatricality, ridiculous characters, and harmonies that hit far harder than they have any right to. Part gig, part sketch show, part dance floor sermon. For fans of Flight of the Conchords, The Mighty Boosh & Meatloaf.

title: Comedy Club 4 Kids
description: Since 2005, the Comedy Club 4 Kids has been getting the best stand-ups, sketch acts and cabaret stars from the international circuit to do their thing for an audience of children (aged 6+) and their families... but without the rude bits! Over twenty years later, the company has expanded from one London residency to being countrywide, running shows and workshops all over the UK from Peebles to Portsmouth, even producing a book on how to write and perform stand-up, and hosting our own Radio Nonsense podcast to bring the sillies to families all over the world. In August 2025, we won The Victoria Wood Award, the Edinburgh Comedy Awards’ panel prize, in recognition of our two decades in this glorious endeavour. The show is family friendly, but without any patronising idiocy. In fact, it's just like a normal comedy club, but it's on in the day, kids are allowed in, and thus there is a higher than usual chance of the entire audience claiming to be called Bob.

title: Crazy Rabbit Ladies
description: Rachel Quinn (Finalist Komedia New Comedy Award and Finalist Hastings New Comedy of the Year) and Charlotte Madden (Chichester New Act Finalist and Semi-Finalist Komedia New Comedy Award) split an hour of work-in-progress stand-up comedy. Following sold-out runs at Brighton Fringe 2025, comedy buddies Rachel and Charlotte are back with new material and fresh takes on bumbling through life with a shared interest in rabbits. Charlotte serves wonky takes on living childfree as a former rebel. Comedic riffs on life’s everyday strifes and some wild family dramas. Rachel knows the version of herself the world sees doesn’t quite match who she really is. Expect jokes, warmth, and a bit of self-discovery as she figures it out with you. May or may not contain rabbit related material.

title: Custard Club
description: Five women. Four decades. Twenty tins of custard. One delicious secret. Custard Club tells the story of Nancy, a young woman whose fraught search for queer community pushes her to the most sensible solution she can muster: summoning four women from across the twentieth century to her kitchen table for an unforgettable evening of passive aggression, genuine aggression, impromptu drag numbers and custard fights. As euphemisms and costumes slip to reveal hidden identities, Nancy realises that the cost of community is not the ignorance of difference, but the celebration and nurture of genuine authenticity. At times, Custard Club is a poignant look at queer costuming, coding and community-building, at others a campy, raucous spectacle. There are many fingers in many pies. Dubbed fondly as the lovechild of Top Girls and 5 Lesbians Eating a Quiche, Custard Club is a heart-wrenching, trans-historical, egg-based romp. Why not pull up a chair?

title: Darling
description: Society is abuzz: the ball of the season is about to begin. Step into a Regency world of romance, rivalry and delightful mischief. A colourful cast of characters gathers beneath the watchful eye of the Queen. Some seek love, others favour and fortune, but beneath the polished etiquette, order begins to unravel. As tensions rise, dazzling acrobatics, aerial feats and playful physical comedy reveal the ambitions and secrets simmering beneath high society. Meet royals, ladies-in-waiting and a few unexpected guests. Laugh, gasp, and cheer as spectacular circus unfolds all around you. Secure your tickets posthaste! Darling, you simply must attend.

title: Darryl J Carrington: Tennish
description: Darryl J Carrington is a physical comedian and mostly non-verbal clown who loves messing around with objects, timing and visual gags. He has been making playful, inventive shows for over twenty years, rooted in physical theatre and contemporary circus. His previous show, Out of the Box, won Best Family Friendly Show at Brighton Fringe 2025 and went on to a highly acclaimed run at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe 2025, with critics calling it “utterly absorbing, charming and great fun.” Audiences loved his ability to connect across ages using inventive physical comedy. TENNISH is Carrington’s first fully adult comedy show, premiering at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe 2026. It starts as a tennis show before quickly going off-court, as rackets, balls and towels become instruments, obstacles and punchlines. Playful, chaotic and visual, TENNISH explores ambition, performance and big dreams — this time firmly for adults.

title: David Hoyle Live in Brighton
description: David Hoyle the original performance avalanche, returns to Brighton – an all singing, all raging bona-fide performance legend. He has appeared in his own Channel 4 TV series, in films and on-stages worldwide. A prolific visual artist, he recently exhibited an exhibition of his paintings at Goswell Road gallery in Paris. In 2024 The Aviva Studios in Manchester held a three-week long retrospective of David’s career (so far) called Please Feel Free to Ignore My Work. ‘The greatest living performer’ – Justin Vivian Bond ‘He is raw, sometimes frightening, but also thrilling in his look-no-hands recklessness’ – The Guardian ‘There is nothing quite like it: bold and unique, electrifying and disarmingly humane’ – Time Out

title: Debussy’s Romantic Piano by Candlelight – ‘Clair de Lune’
description: SELLOUT EdFringe24. Romantic piano pieces meet candlelit Venetian masked balls as award-winning Scottish pianist Matthew Shiel exposes the scandalous love affairs of the great composers. Features a new romance composed specially for tonight's artist by Dr Alfredo Caponnetto – Notturno VI, alongside digital animations by acclaimed Shanghai artist and Disney illustrator Emma Yitong Shen. An immersive musical experience designed to wind you down, find romantic inspiration and open your heart to live and to love. Fringe Classics productions are rated ★★★★★: 'Enchanting... amusing, enlightening stories' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com). 'An atmosphere which is all too often missed in our digital world' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Ripping good yarn' ★★★★★ (BingeFringe.com). Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: Deluge
description: A comic exploration of grief, inspired by a break-up and over 40 interviews gathered via a social media call-out. In the wake of a relationship, a woman tries to contain the overflow of her thoughts... She ends up stuck in a ladder, fighting against komodo dragons, house leaks, holes and jam jars that only seem to multiply. Deluge puts a magic realist spin on dealing with and overcoming grief. Blurring imagination with real stories by invoking a surreal, tender and humorous dreamscape.

title: DENTATA
description: DENTATA is a joyful, riotous, and feral exploration of feminine ferocity and pleasure. Rooted in the tension of a god-fearing upbringing in the American South, the piece weaponizes the grotesque mockery of the Bouffon to interrogate how women have been misunderstood, belittled, and underestimated throughout history. The performance is a high-stakes ritual of presence, conjured through the poetry of the Three Weird Sisters, the reclamation of a misunderstood teenage Medusa hoping for a date, and direct, playful confrontation with the audience. Centered around a giant, operatic vagina puppet, the work is as theatrically beautiful as it is provocative—prioritizing stunning visual imagery and extreme physicality to transport the viewer for sixty minutes. Directed by Jess Lazar (Funny or Die, Hulu) and co-birthed by SITI Company's Lucy Smith. Written and starring award-winning Julia VanderVeen, The Broad Street Review called it, “A rich expression of female ferocity: joyful and brave."

title: Destination: 'Old Hag'
description: Welcome to backstage! Here, you will find the Ladies of the Chorus getting ready for opening night of a small-scale production of Verdi's Macbetto. You may have seen them before as Cigarette Girls, Nuns, and Geishas... tonight, they are the Witches! Despite their numerous appearances, would you recognise them in The Lanes? Or know their names? Who really are the people you only seem to see 'upstage'? Set in a theatre broom cupboard, next to the building’s only toilet, Destination: ‘Old Hag’ is a Buxton Fringe- nominated production that draws on the Ladies’ many years in the world of opera, so join them as they chart the ups and downs of life - on stage and off - with humour, heart, and some live opera singing! Destination: ‘Old Hag’ was created and written by Samantha Houston, Bridget Hardy and Penny Rossano. It stars Bridget Hardy, Penny Rossano and Samantha Houston – three opera singers with more years’ stage experience than they care to mention...

title: Dinki Disco
description: Get ready to wiggle, giggle and boogie! Our Children’s Disco is a joyful burst of family fun for 0–6s and their grown-ups but older children are very welcome to join the fun! Expect baby disco beats, action songs, giant parachute games, bubbles galore and plenty of old-fashioned silliness. We’ll kick off with high-five meet & greets, warm-up dances, and toe-tapping tunes then dive into inflatable instruments fun for some noisy, giggly musical play. Little ones can bounce, wiggle or throw their best shapes — whether that’s gentle bouncing in arms or full-on freestyle moves on the dancefloor. It’s all about exploring music through movement, however little (or big!) that looks. Come for the dancing, stay for the smiles, laughs and family fun. Big fun for tiny dancers!

title: Dinner with the Sl**S
description: A Dinner with the SL**S brings together two of the UK comedy circuit's tastiest talents, Laura Moss and Amelia Fritz-Millet, to deliver a show that is delightfully mischievous, ridiculously silly, and effortlessly authentic. Join the irresistible duo for a delectable hour of stand up comedy. A Dinner with the SL**S is a whimsical blend of religious upbringings, eccentric families, and bizarre dating dynamics, with a focus on celebrating ‘sluts’ and everything they encompass. The show also has an interactive element where our delicious audience submit their problems, and we offer our best, sluttiest advice. As featured on: Comedy Central, Sky TV, Channel 4.0, Backyard Comedy Club, Top Secret Comedy Club, Big Belly Comedy Club, Up The Creek Comedy Club

title: Disco of the Damned: The Gathering
description: Join award-winning creatives Lost Hippo for a joyful, cult-themed party night of disco beats, cabaret, comedy and immersive theatre. This high-energy party is led by our charismatic host and self-proclaimed Prophet, and soundtracked by funk and disco all night long. Expect live cabaret acts, playful rituals, and euphoric dancing at one of the Fringe’s most iconic venues. On the surface, Disco of the Damned is a glittering celebration of disco, funk and joy. But peek behind the curtains and you'll see that this “Disco Cult” has more than meets the eye. Step into the Temple of Confession, be initiated in the Baptismal Ball Pit, or simply dance, watch and soak up the madness. Immersive theatre runs throughout the night, with no pressure to participate — the more you lean in, the more you’ll discover. Brighton's funkiest cult is back, reimagined with more cabaret, more ritualistic madness and even more funk. Come for the disco. Stay for the joy. Leave wondering what you just joined.

title: Do the Thing: Choose Your Own Musical
description: Where is it set? Who's it about? What is the plot? You tell us. Brighton Fringe stunners Do the Thing are here to give you exactly the musical you want to see. Not only will the audience decide the setting and characters but they can also direct the plot. Should the hero run off with the love of their life or become a monk and fight crime? Will the villain achieve world domination even though they couldn’t really be bothered? Will the hero and villain reject such reductive labelling, realise they’re just people, and hug it out already? Be careful what you wish for as things can and will get very silly indeed. Expect Do the Thing’s full throttle commitment to the daftest ideas, the most farcical characters and tunes you’ll be whistling for weeks after. All made up for you live by a cast of two (Yes Anderson & Stellar Firma’s Tim Meredith and Simon Plotkin) and an orchestra of one (Ian Scott).

title: Doctor Coppelius
description: "I am not a bad person. You say we're the bad people, I can't do anything right by you ... But tell me: if all the things I do are bad, what difference does one more bad thing make?" Everyone knows the love story of Swanhilda, Franz, and Coppelia but what is the story of the doll's creator, the sinister and reclusive and genius Doctor Coppelius? Now, he grants you an audience, with his tale of the sinister Wandering People, a woman named Clara. With dehumanisation and humanity, scaremongering and understanding, revenge and forgiveness, despair and hope, it is time for the tragic villain from the Delibes's ballet to give his side of the story.
description_html:
"I am not a bad person. You say we're the bad people, I can't do anything right by you ... But tell me: if all the things I do are bad, what difference does one more bad thing make?"

Everyone knows the love story of Swanhilda, Franz, and Coppelia but what is the story of the doll's creator, the sinister and reclusive and genius Doctor Coppelius? Now, he grants you an audience, with his tale of the sinister Wandering People, a woman named Clara. With dehumanisation and humanity, scaremongering and understanding, revenge and forgiveness, despair and hope, it is time for the tragic villain from the Delibes's ballet to give his side of the story.

title: Don't Panic
description: The year is 2108. Two stagehands, Mani and Kid, get stuck in a rundown New York City theatre as part of a standard nuclear warfare drill. As the threat of the world's end closes in on them, they debate the purpose of living in a world that’s poisoned the human experience, seeking answers to an impossible question: what is the point of trying if there’s nothing left to live for? Don’t Panic is a bold, two-hander dark comedy about two stage hands who, stuck together during a nuclear lockdown drill, debate the purpose of living in a world that’s poisoned the human experience, seeking answers to an impossible question: what is the point of trying if there’s nothing left to live for? Merging comedy, drama, and magical realism, the play delves into the roles of purpose, ambitions, and individuality in a society that is doomed to fail.

title: Don't touch my knob
description: The "Rock n’ Roll St. Brigid," Áine Duffy is a "horsebox hero" (Today FM) redesigning the stage. Her movement, "Don’t Touch My Knob" (DTMK), storms Brighton Fringe with an all-female night of comedy, music, and fun. Join three powerhouse Irish women for a defiant, high-energy show. Championed by Ed power in the UK Independent alongside Kneecap and CMAT, Áine’s presence is "like Dave Grohl gate-crashing an Irish protest rally. "Come for the craic, stay for the noise. Shinanne Higgins is a rising star of Irish comedy. Her solo show "10 Things They Hate About Me" sold out at Edinburgh Fringe in '24 and '25, Ireland's Best New Act. Meljoann is a multi-disciplinary. Their self-formulated hyperjack sound has soulful vocals against spiky lyrics; lush synths against industrial noise. She’s a member of the Spirit of Gravity experimental music collective in Brighton, she’s been supported by Deb Grant and Gemma Bradley at BBC6Music and BBC Introducing.

title: Dr Mew's queer sci-fi podcasts
description: Doctor Mew is an audience-interactive sci fi comedy about redemption, species regeneration and becoming feline. Clive a toxic sci-fi podcaster moaning about wokeness threatening his favourite sci fi program Dr Who is accidently is regenerated into a cat. His wife thinks it’s an improvement! His wife and her lover Betty give him a new name, Doctor Mew. Renamed, neutered and Doctor Mew has still not lost his love for podcast as he claws his ways to be the top cat in podcast world. Doctor Mew tells of his adventures as well as he shares his wisdom giving bad advice to famous characters the universe and talks about feline view of the world. Expect audience interaction, fabulous props and a few jokes about sci -fi. Audiences are welcome to cos play.

title: Dr. Jimmy Lyons: Existential Physics (or Why the Universe is Gay) Work In Progress
description: Dr. Jimmy has one aim: to discover the ultimate Theory of Everything (or become the first gay man to win a Nobel Prize in Physics). Why are waves gay? Particles straight? And quantum mechanics the ultimate textbook guide to being bisexual? Why are quarks queer? Tops difficult to observe? And bottoms ARE LITERALLY EVERYWHERE. Dr. Jimmy collides HARDCORE physics with queerness and existential dread as he scales the peak of all knowledge in this world-famous lecture. Along the way, he'll demonstrate his ground-breaking inventions, such as the ‘Pansychinator,’ which lets him communicate with all forms of matter, and his pièce de résistance: the Parallel-o-scope. This device scans the multiverse and connects him with alternate versions of himself - like Dr. Jimmie (spelt ‘ie’), or inevitably, a more successful and fulfilled Dr. Jimmy. So, join Dr. Jimmy at the frontier of human knowledge and stand naked (literally) before the universe. 16+ welcome at 3pm on Sat. 9th May

title: Dru Cripps: Juicy Bits
description: Master clown Dru Cripps presents his phenomenally successful loopstation-based shenanigans. This quadruple award-winner (British Comedy Guide, Malcolm Hardee, Comedy Store King Gong, Solo FX Sponsorship) and BBC New Comedian of the Year nominee uses questionable musical abilities and dangerous levels of confidence to transform audience suggestions into improvised songs, spontaneous musical sketches, and utterly winning chaos in real-time. A show so good it earned five 5-star reviews, sold out Edinburgh completely, and added extra dates. As seen on BBC, ITV and Dave, Dru was nominated for Edinburgh Fringe Comedian of the Year for this performance that leaves audiences rolling in the aisles laughing at something that really has to be seen to be misunderstood. Every performance is completely different. Every audience becomes part of the beautiful musical carnage.

title: Drum and Blaze
description: Drum and Blaze is a rising force in the music scene, bringing an electrifying mix of rhythm, energy, and inclusivity wherever they play. Recently making waves at major events like Brighton Pride and Lewes Bonfire, their performances are nothing short of spectacular. With a powerful drumming line-up, they know how to captivate an audience and ignite a crowd. Their contagious energy and vibrant spirit create an unforgettable atmosphere, leaving everyone craving more. Focusing on diversity and uniting people from all walks of life, Drum and Blaze proves that music has the power to bring us all together in one unbreakable rhythm. Get ready for a show that’ll get your heart racing!

title: DURO..........wait to be pampered
description: DURO! is a spellbinding stage play that weaves mystery, love, and destiny into a rich tapestry of African spirituality. When an Abiku, a spirit-child who has journeyed between worlds thirteen times, decides to stay on earth with her mother, her otherworldly spouse returns to lure her back for a once-in-a-century festival. Torn between eternal love and earthly devotion, Duro faces a heart-wrenching choice that blurs the line between life and the afterlife. Set in a world where music, myth, and politics intertwine, *DURO!* captivates with its poetry, symbolism, and emotional power — a timeless reflection on love, sacrifice, and the spirit’s longing to belong.

title: Ed Mulvey: For Sale - Dead Dog Never Worn
description: The shortest story ever told (1hr). His dog is for sale. And that's not all. Ed Mulvey delivers a double measure of grim hilarity with a chaser of unworn dog. After his imaginary dog died, Ed chose to exploit this for marketing purposes. Expect an exploration of ethical edge cases and sophisticated filth from a man who when in the gutter revolves to get a better view. A cry against help from the Elon Musk of emotions. Shortlisted for Best Newcomer Edinburgh Ish Awards 2025 Shortlisted for Best Newcomer British Comedy Guide 2025 Roast Battle UK Finalist 2023

title: Einaudi's Piano by Candlelight - 'Spirited Away'
description: SELLOUT EdFringe24! Award-winning British pianist Matthew Shiel relaxes you towards musical enlightenment. Japanese anime Spirited Away, Ludovico Einaudi’s contemplative piano pieces and Philip Glass’s soundtrack to the Academy award-winning film The Hours meet Japanese Zen stories, alongside digital animations designed by acclaimed Shanghai artist and Disney illustrator Emma Yitong Shen. Beautiful and life-affirming music is interwoven with Japanese storytelling, inspired by the wisdom and philosophy of Zen Buddhism to create a hypnotic and profound musical experience. Fringe Classics are ★★★★★ rated: 'meditative' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com). 'profound' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Ripping good yarn' ★★★★★ (BingeFringe.com). Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: Electric Dreams
description: Join us for an evening of original electro pop, bringing together three great electronic acts from in and around the Brighton area. Terahurtz - Brighton based duo Terahurtz, formed early 2019, delivers catchy, soulful and charismatic electro-pop. Terahurtz is a collaboration between synth fan Matt McEwan - (Telesumo and Astroscapes) - and offbeat, wholehearted pop writer & vocalist Emmy Hush - whose solo song catalogue spans 30 years. Both Emmy & Matt share a love of electro, 80s synth-pop & punk and have just released their latest album 'SPEED DIAL'. Pixelstorm - An energetic twist of modern electronica, 80s synth sounds, live keytar and epic vocals. Radio Wigwam best Electro Act 2026. 'Known for a larger than life sound and a distinctive stage presence, Pixelstorm continues to stand out within the electronic scene' Deluxe - Out of the comfort zone, riff heavy synthpop, a dusting of punk attitude & new wave vibes.

Join us for an evening of original electro pop, bringing together three great electronic acts from in and around the Brighton area.

Terahurtz - Brighton based duo Terahurtz, formed early 2019, delivers catchy, soulful and charismatic electro-pop. Terahurtz is a collaboration between synth fan Matt McEwan - (Telesumo and Astroscapes) - and offbeat, wholehearted pop writer & vocalist Emmy Hush - whose solo song catalogue spans 30 years. Both Emmy & Matt share a love of electro, 80s synth-pop & punk and have just released their latest album 'SPEED DIAL'.

Pixelstorm - An energetic twist of modern electronica, 80s synth sounds, live keytar and epic vocals.
Radio Wigwam best Electro Act 2026. 'Known for a larger than life sound and a distinctive stage presence, Pixelstorm continues to stand out within the electronic scene'

Deluxe - Out of the comfort zone, riff heavy synthpop, a dusting of punk attitude & new wave vibes.

title: Ellie Ross: Mrs Gary Breath
description: Annie Hughes is happily single, gainfully employed and financially... fine. But all that changes on the night of her 34th birthday when her parents make her an offer she can't refuse. Even if she really, definitely should. Part romcom, part social autopsy, this brand new solo play from Ellie Ross (BBC Radio 5 Live, i Paper's 50 Best Jokes of the Fringe, Leicester Square Theatre's SketchOff!) satirises our obsession with marriage and ticking life's boxes. Big-hearted, big-character storytelling about the cost of fitting in - and the freedom of opting out.

title: Embassy Court Tours
description: Walking tours of Embassy Court lasting around 90 minutes. Includes visits to a flat, the roof terrace, a reinstated modernist kitchen, a mural, a collection of artefacts in an 11th floor meeting room, as well as the front and rear of the building. The entrance of Embassy Court includes a mural, the blue plaque of former resident, Edward Zeff MBE, and Croix de Guerre awarded for his bravery after WW2.

title: Emma or Emily
description: ‘Tell me. Tell me exactly what you think about me. Which part of me do you like? Do you like my neck? Or elbow?’ One opera singer, one actor, an evening of drinking, debauchery, and deception. A failed opera singer, Emily lives a lonely but lush life as a housewife. A visit from her old friend Emma offers an escape from her isolation. But as the two drink and dance into the night Emma gradually brings the worst out of Emily, highlighting her flaws, amplifying her insecurities, and making her smaller and more helpless. Moon Kim, creator of the surrealist dreamscapes and ★★★★★ hits The Waiting Room and The Arms, returns with Emma or Emily — a new experimental opera-play exploring the fractured mind of an artist. Featuring an original score by Hungarian-American composer Ábel MGE, marking their second collaboration.

title: Enduring Violence: Bhopal's Ongoing Water Disaster
description: On the night of 2nd December 1984, deadly gas leaked from a Union Carbide pesticide factory in Bhopal, India. Now known as the world’s worst industrial disaster, it has claimed the lives of more than 25,000 people to date. But few people know of the ongoing ‘second disaster’ in Bhopal: four decades on, toxic waste from the abandoned factory continues to sink into the earth, poising the water of tens of thousands more people. This exhibition draws attention to the ongoing water crisis in Bhopal and the activism of water-affected communities. Produced by the University of Leeds’ LivingBodiesObjects project in partnership with the Bhopal Medical Appeal, it is the culmination of a collaborative research project in which we explored new ways of telling the story of groundwater contamination in Bhopal. Our interviews with members of water-affected communities, original artworks, striking photography, and experiments with digital storytelling form the heart of the exhibition.

title: Enrico Touché: Hot Knees - It's Just Business
description: From sold-out cabarets across Europe, Enrico Touché brings their unique brand of comedy to the stage in an hour of unbelievable business. Enrico has a dream: to be the best at business. But what kind of business? Hot Knees is a high-energy comedy about ambition, passion, and following your dreams to their illogical conclusion. Expect briefcases, suitcases, and cases of uncontrollable laughter. For lovers of slapstick and the absurd — meet Enrico Touché. You will be touched. A cabaret performer combining clowning, trapeze and singing with a magic touch. Enrico has entertained audiences internationally and across the UK’s leading cabaret and theatre venues, including Crazy Coqs (West End), Phoenix Arts, Wonderville, Alberts Schloss, and theatres nationwide with The 100 Watt Club. Recent highlights include sold-out performances at Wales Millennium Centre with Big Band Burlesque and featured artist in Belfast Lyric’s Vaudeville Christmas Show (2024 and 2025).

title: Eric Lampaert: Zero Minus One
description: After a brush with total amnesia, a mind wiped clean, comedian Eric Lampaert returns from the void with questions only madness can answer, fractured memories, multiple personalities, and a darkly funny perspective on what it means to be “human”. “A real tour de force.” (National Operatic and Dramatic Association) “A galaxy of brilliant silliness. Lampaert is able to contort his body and voice in the most sinister of ways” (The Fest) “Provides a constant frisson of energy with his loose, animated style” (Chortle) “He makes it look so easy but this is a man with very funny bones. Definitely one to catch” (Daily Express)

title: Essense : Heels Show
description: Bringing together the elegance of Paris and the strength of femininity, Essense Heels showcases the power and beauty of dance. Standing tall on their stilettos, the Essense cast promises a night of bold movement, grooves, and striking shapes. The cast brings together Brighton heels dancers, celebrating the diverse styles, stories, and energies they each bring to the stage. Directed and choreographed by Essense founder Pearl Makayi, who also takes to the stage throughout the evening, this showcase marks her first full-length production. Pearl’s artistry is a vital part of the Brighton dance scene, known for its confidence, sensuality, and emotional presence. Join us for a good dance and a good time as we celebrate femininity, power, and unapologetic self-expression. Expect fierce performances, high energy, and a night that invites you to feel, move, and leave inspired.

title: Eurydice
description: Eurydice. Presented by Pivotal Performance Company & Red Hot Theatre! Following last year’s 4-star hit of The Seagull, Pivotal Performance Company returns to Brighton Fringe, partnering with our partner emerging theatre company: Red Hot Theatre. This production marks Red Hot’s highly anticipated company debut, a milestone reached after a crowdfunding campaign fuelled by the desire to forge a career within the arts that is on their own terms. Forget the lyres and the robes. This is Eurydice through the lens of gig-theatre. Expect a high-octane blend of live music, visceral movement, and spoken word. We’re descending into the underworld not with a whimper, but with a bassline that thumps in your chest. In a world of fading memories and static, Eurydice must choose between the comfort of the shadows and the painful noise of the living.

This new musical traces her rocky life and career, from her discovery in a cardboard box to her West End rise and fall, via high-kicks, low-lifes, bruises and booze. In this latest creation from award-winning company Three Chairs and a Hat, Guy Brigg plays Ms Teeth 'n' Tits herself, along with all the other eccentric characters in Evangeline's rollercoaster story. Written and accompanied by Nia Williams.

title: Everybody's Dating Ollie
description: Hello there! So... do you fancy Ollie? It’s not a requirement to enjoy the show—but it certainly doesn’t hurt. Everybody's Dating Ollie is Ollie’s debut clown/comedy solo show: their ongoing experiment in dating an entire room of people at once. That’s right—sit back, relax, and enjoy as you all collectively go on a date with Ollie. Along the way, you’ll hear about their sexual orientation, relationships, and the all-too-familiar dating patterns we fall into and the relationship escalator that follows. Expect alternative comedy and clown-fuelled chaos, sprinkled with genuinely wholesome moments of connection. Note: There is a topless scene, but no nudity. Join this clown as they dive headfirst into the messy, joyful world of relationships—with you. Bursary Winner 2025: Katie Reddin-Clancy Solo Treasure Hunt.

title: ExtraOrdinary
description: What happens when you've been stuck behind a mask for so long that you don't even know what your real voice sounds like? Elaine has spent too long being seen and not heard - and sometimes barely that. We meet Elaine trapped in the hold of a toxic relationship, and losing herself in the noise of the world around her. Straight-laced and a sucker for rules, she yearns for something more. Find out what happens when she meets the effervescent Clementine. Can she break familial cycles and ensure she finds her own voice? A new play written by Gemma Webber and co-directed by Gemma Webber and Robin Hodges. Content Warnings: Mentions of bereavement, and themes of coercive control and violence.

title: Face it till you make it - The Panic Years
description: Face it till you make it - The Panic Years, created by Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the relentless expectations of social media. From the awkward intimacy of smear tests to the clash between generations, and the chaos of adulthood no one warned us about, this show celebrates imperfection and speaks the truths we all think but rarely dare to say out loud. A love letter and a reality check for anyone learning to survive adulthood one questionable decision at a time. description_html:
Face it till you make it - The Panic Years, created by Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the relentless expectations of social media. From the awkward intimacy of smear tests to the clash between generations, and the chaos of adulthood no one warned us about, this show celebrates imperfection and speaks the truths we all think but rarely dare to say out loud. A love letter and a reality check for anyone learning to survive adulthood one questionable decision at a time.

You can support Shouldn't Have Said that Theatre Company and keep up to date on future shows and all back stage shenanigans via their socials - Instagram: @shouldnthavesaidthattheatreco

description: The whole family is invited to celebrate in style as we kick off Brighton Fringe 2026. Join us for a fun-filled afternoon of performances and free activities outside in the beautiful SpiegelGardens glade. title: Fate Train
description: FOLKS. Theatre x BN1 Arts Centre Graduate Development Scheme 2026 Presents Fate Train Written and Directed by Max & Holly Hopkins Dawn, reeling from the death of their partner, spirals into isolation and makes a deal with fate. Hidden in the sounds of the city, Dawn refuses to fulfill their deal resulting in three strangers arriving on their door step. Split between, longing for the past, avoiding the present, and intimidated by the future, Dawn must unravel the mess to restore order. Fate Train, weaves Norse Gods and the modern day into a surreal tale where time, memory and self become blurred. Powerfully poetic, and unflinchingly human, it explores the myths we cling to when everything feels like it’s falling apart. Both intimate and epic, it invites audiences into a world where the divine and the human collide and asks who are we without the ones we have lost?

title: Fear Of Failure
description: Fear of Failure is a race through comedian Rodney Beckford’s life to this point. It’s packed full of personal and professional stories and tales about friends family and his particular take on the world. There are pearls of wisdom weaved into the show, all be it accidentally it must be said, within the funny stories, jokes and general mischief that this show is . He’s a very serious none serious person, and is uniquely qualified to be your host and guide for this most personal of stand up comedy shows. You're going to be amused, surprised and laugh out loud as you're guided through the comedians trails and tribulations. You'll be sorry it's over, delighted you came and perplexed as to why he's not on a bigger and better stage.

title: Fearless and Fabulous Women of Brighton
description: Early women doctors to suffragettes, entrepreneurs to entertainers, artists, music-hall stars, and campaigners, Brighton & Hove has always attracted women who dare do things differently. Join Louise Peskett, author of 'The Fearless and the Fabulous, a Journey through Brighton and Hove’s Women’s History', on these easy walks to discover the stories of some of the intriguing, adventurous, fantastic, outrageous, and scandalously little-known female characters of the city's past. All tours last approx 1 hour, 45 minutes.

title: Felix & Friends
description: Gird your loins for an evening of song, surrealism and smut! Felix & Friends is Brighton's long running cabaret carousel, hosted by a potty-mouthed drag slag and attended by her very peculiar friends. Expect to hear tragic tales of love and lust as Felix delves elbow deep into the annals of her dating history. Prepare to laugh, cry and squirm as her special guests take the stage to do... unspeakable things. Each show promises new performances, unique experiences and an avalanche of rainbow-flavoured nonsense. So take the plunge and come find us, every Friday night at Caravanserai: Junk Poets. Felix Le Freak is a musical comedian, writer and cabaret performer based in the UK. They are the winner of Drag Idol UK, author of Serving Face and the creator of sell-out productions including Fagwartz; Men and Other Problems; and their long-running variety show, Felix & Friends.

title: FilmPride: 3000 Lesbians... Launch Party
description: To celebrate FilmPride's short film screenings in the Light Room across 22 - 24 May, we take over the theatre space with a bang! Please join us for a very exciting launch night party featuring some very special guests, musical entertainment, and free popcorn. With a screening of the documentary celebrating YLAF - the York Lesbian Arts Festival - featuring many famous faces: '3000 Lesbians Go To York'. '3000 Lesbians Go To York' tells the extraordinary true story of how a lesbian bookseller (who also happened to be trans) created the largest gathering of LGBTQ+ women in the UK; and how, from 1998 to 2008, the quietly conservative city of York became the unlikely centre of all things lesbian. For one thrilling decade, thousands of women flocked to the York Lesbian Arts Festival each autumn, to meet their favourite authors, buy books, hear top female artists live on stage and dance the night away. Please note, all our films are captioned. More details released soon!

description: An exciting and ground-breaking one-woman thriller show! "The Final Episode" is a one-act thriller based around Carly and her podcast on conspiracy theories. It is all fun and games until we watch Carly unravel a conspiracy, revealing a deep hidden truth. Written and Performed by Kathryn Mincer ("Britain's Most Evil Killers") whose work as a writer was acknowledged at Toronto International Women's Film Festival, winning "Best Unproduced Script" in 2022.

title: Fishy Tales with the Seabream Sisters
description: Join the Seabream Sisters on the high seas for a rip-roaring storytelling adventure! Coral and Shelly might not catch many fish but they are experts at trawling the seas for stories, shanties and discarded treasure. Meet a salty sea worm with an astonishing secret, a grumpy lighthouse keeper who learns to love the sea, and find out how to calm a storm that’s shivering yer timbers. Don’t miss this voyage of discovery for the whole family, packed with original stories, clowning, puppetry and songs. You might even catch a fish! Nominated for the Family Friendly Award 2025 The Seabream Sisters completely captivated my children, who thoroughly adored the performance, and inspired them to play in a new way, again and again.

title: Flesh and Bone Play
description: Flesh and Bone is THE multi-media, dark comedy for anyone who relishes 2015 Tumblr, trauma dumping, and drone music. **Missing people from the 1960s/70s inter-communal Cypriot conflict are returning as zombies, disrupting borders and creating havoc, but not for Muppet-Joker. He's more concerned about how moving to Cyprus has landed him with emo siblings, Sage and Milton, divided by religious trauma, and their leader, Athena, who thinks she is a vampire spiritually connected to Gerard Way.** **Combining a real Tumblr drama from @Muppet-Joker with the fiery question of how we can move forward from silent tragedies, Flesh and Bone investigates how the roots of neo-colonialism sprout in Tumblr.com. **

title: FLIGHT: One Man’s Journey
description: FLIGHT is a powerful 55-minute one-man show that brings 21 vivid characters to life through storytelling, physicality and voice. Written by Martin Lytton and directed by Jacquie Crago, the piece unfolds as a rapid-fire journey through memory, movement and transformation. Performed by Naz Sheikh, the show shifts between intimate moments and bold, fully realised characters, using minimal staging and direct audience address to create a strong connection with the audience. Each transformation is precise and immediate, revealing how identity is shaped, challenged and reclaimed. At its heart, FLIGHT explores themes of belonging, displacement and the search for self. The narrative is rich with imagery and momentum, inviting audiences into a world that is both personal and universal. Like all living theatre, the production continues to evolve through performance and response. FLIGHT is ideal for audiences who enjoy solo performance, contemporary storytelling and ask urgent questions.

title: Flightless Birds
description: Flightless Birds is a split stand-up bill featuring weird little freak Sammer Elzubaidi and big little freak Ramsey Smith. Sammer is the tallest guy at the small-guy convention. His subversive style stands out in the conventional crowd. His favorite movie is Surf’s Up. Ramsey does comedy the same way he makes love — into a microphone. His blend of storytelling and observational bits makes him surprisingly smart for a dumb guy — though don’t let that fool you, he’s dumb. He’s a RAW Comedy NSW State Finalist (2024), has performed at Top Secret Comedy, and has opened for Rory Lowe. His favorite movie is The Godfather Part III. Now they’re teaming up for Flightless Birds, a split bill that’s about to rattle the UK comedy scene. Separate, they’re two of the most exciting new faces in UK comedy. Together, they will rule the world.

title: Flufflin & The Shooting Star
description: Have you ever wondered what your true gifts might be - or how to find them? Flufflin and the Shooting Star is a tender, magical immersive tale that invites children and adults to drift into the world of dreams and self-discovery. Meet Flufflin, a quiet little creature who lives tucked away in a loft and holds a secret: he can see people’s dreams shimmering in the dark. Yet even with such a gift, Flufflin feels unsure of his own purpose. What is he meant to do? What hidden talents belong to him? One night, everything changes. A Shooting Star appears, offering three gentle steps to help him understand his path. But the journey that follows takes an unexpected turn - opening a space for audiences to reflect on their own special gifts and the meaning they bring to the world. A dreamy, poetic story that nurtures children’s confidence, self-belief, and the courage to follow their dreams.

title: Flyaway Katie Brighton Fringe 2026
description: By the makers of Shoe Baby, Penguin and Arthur’s Dream Boat, Long Nose Puppets Flyaway Katie! Katie is all alone and feeling grey. The birds in the picture on her wall look so colourful-if only Katie could be as bright as them, who knows what might happen? The show is bursting with puppets, an array of songs, colour and surprises! Come and meet a blue, blue lion, a band of pink, pink mice and a multi-coloured caterpillar. Aimed at 2-7 year olds and is 35 mins of pure joy followed by 15 mins ‘meet the puppets’. Based on the book by Polly Dunbar and music by Tom Gray.

title: Forgive Me
description: This project brings together award winning collaborators. Written and performed by Tamsin Shasha and directed by Helen Tennison with sound design by Matt Eaton, Forgive Me follows on from the critical success of double award winning, Everything I See I Swallow (Shasha & Taylor Productions) and similarly responds to the challenges and turbulence of the world today. Forgive Me is an intergenerational dialogue between a mother and her autistic son, an avid gamer, and explores our individual and collective need to fight monsters. We follow the journey of a mother as she tries to understand the specialist needs of her child, while all the while navigating her own late ADHD diagnosis. Her son meanwhile navigates a digital world of online gaming, hyperfixating on YouTube and gaming. As the two worlds converge fantasy and reality become intertwine and mother and son discover that they need every weapon in their arsenal to fight an epic battle.

title: Found Out There - Public Typist
description: Follow the sound of clacking typewriter keys and step into a world of storytelling magic with public typist Adam Holton! Give him three random words and a character name, and watch as he types a unique story just for you—right before your eyes in just five minutes. Each story is roughly a 400 words and typed live. Donations can be made on a Pay What You Feel basis. Suitable for children, teenagers & adults. You can also find Adam in alternative spots: Outside the Theatre Royal or on the corner of Sydney Street and Gloucester Road.

title: Fringe Academy x Komedia Workshop: How To Tech Your Show
description: Are you a Fringe Performer, experienced or otherwise, who feels they could have a greater knowledge of tech? Join us at Komedia, in partnership with Brighton Fringe where two technicians with more than 30 years' combined experience in venues from 10 to 5000 capacity will walk you through every technical aspect of putting on a Fringe show. Discussion will include: • Basic stage sound: PAs, mixing desks, mics • Show lighting: different types of lighting, lighting desks, terminology, techniques available at different scales • Cues, scripts, playback devices, hiring equipment, assessing what's worth having, and operating technicians • Tech advancing: Knowing what to ask for, knowing how to answer questions, make sense of what's available, and fill out an advance form This workshop will be geared toward Fringe performers and crew. Attendees will receive a link upon reserving their place to register questions ahead of time. Subsidised by Brighton Fringe and Komedia Brighton
description_html:
Are you a Fringe Performer, experienced or otherwise, who feels they could have a greater knowledge of tech?

Join us at Komedia, in partnership with Brighton Fringe where two technicians with more than 30 years' combined experience in venues from 10 to 5000 capacity will walk you through every technical aspect of putting on a Fringe show.

Discussion will include:
• Basic stage sound: PAs, mixing desks, mics
• Show lighting: different types of lighting, lighting desks, terminology, techniques available at different scales
• Cues, scripts, playback devices, hiring equipment, assessing what's worth having, and operating technicians
• Tech advancing: Knowing what to ask for, knowing how to answer questions, make sense of what's available, and fill out an advance form

This workshop will be geared toward Fringe performers and crew. Attendees will receive a link upon reserving their place to register questions ahead of time.

Subsidised by Brighton Fringe and Komedia Brighton

description: Join Caz Slota from Spark Arts AM&PM and Tom Hooker from Lost Hippo to chat about how to best market your event for Brighton Fringe 2026. Caz Slota founded Spark AM&PM in 2018, providing marketing consultancy services for arts companies across the UK – from hyper-local community projects to major organisations with an international audience. Caz began her career in arts marketing and management at The King’s Head Theatre in London before moving on to Norwich Playhouse, spending the next 10 years promoting performances of every possible size and shape, and running the venue as part of the senior management team. title: Fringe Academy: Insurance for Fringe Artists: What You Actually Need (And Why)
description: Have you been asked for Public Liability certificate and thought “Do I really need this?” This session is for performers, producers, and touring artists and those new to Fringe. Fringe artists are often required to have insurance, but the reasons why (and what’s actually necessary) aren’t always clear. This session is designed to explain it simply. We’ll walk through: - Why venues ask for Public Liability and what it actually covers - Why venue insurance doesn’t automatically include your show - When Employers’ Liability becomes relevant (even for small teams) - Other covers you may hear about equipment, cancellation, cyber and when they matter - Using a real-world Fringe-style case study, we’ll show how these situations play out in practice and what difference the right cover makes This is a practical, no-jargon session to help you how to get set up and understand what you do and don't need. There will be time for live Q&A, so you can ask questions specific to your show or setup.
description_html:
Have you been asked for Public Liability certificate and thought “Do I really need this?” This session is for performers, producers, and touring artists and those new to Fringe.

Fringe artists are often required to have insurance, but the reasons why (and what’s actually necessary) aren’t always clear. This session is designed to explain it simply.

We’ll walk through:

- Why venues ask for Public Liability and what it actually covers
- Why venue insurance doesn’t automatically include your show
- When Employers’ Liability becomes relevant (even for small teams)
- Other covers you may hear about equipment, cancellation, cyber and when they matter
- Using a real-world Fringe-style case study, we’ll show how these situations play out in practice and what difference the right cover makes

This is a practical, no-jargon session to help you how to get set up and understand what you do and don't need. There will be time for live Q&A, so you can ask questions specific to your show or setup.

title: From Nightingales to Butterflies: English Cello and Piano Music
description: Cellist, Esther Ward-Caddle and pianist, Evgenia Startseva present a recital of English chamber music inspired by nature, and the transformative healing power of family and legacy. Central to the programme are the cello sonatas of Frederick Delius and Frank Bridge (written, respectively, between 1916-1917 for the celebrated cellist, Beatrice Harrison, who was famed for her live recordings of nightingale and cello duets in the English countryside). The programme also features Stephanie Cant’s 'Suite in Ancient Style', composed in 1995 (for Esther) and based on a piece by her daughter, Kate Cooper (who loved butterflies!), and the premiere of Peter Copley’s ‘Nocturnal’, composed in 2021 for his daughter, Maria’s Copley 18th birthday. Esther and Evgenia are honoured to present this event in support of Off the Fence, and in dedication of Kate’s passionate wish to support the homeless. There will be a retiring collection for donations at the end of the concert.

title: Funny Women The Glitter Project
description: Returning to Brighton for a special festival edition of The Glitter Project, featuring an INDUSTRY PANEL with experts covering comedy production, performance, media, mental health and wellbeing. The panel will be hosted by Funny Women founder, Lynne Parker, as part of The Glitter Project – A Safe Place supported by funds raised from The Glitter Challenge. This takes place in partnership with The Komedia, home of Funny Women's longest running pro-gig in the UK. Funny Women has been supporting and developing new comedy talent at grassroots with ground-breaking Awards, workshops, courses and open mic events for over 20 years. The priority is to build confidence in female and non-binary performers, writers and creators to give them the opportunity to pursue comedy careers. The inspiration for The Glitter Project is Jo Fletcher-Cross, who was diagnosed with a grade 4 glioblastoma brain tumour in summer 2022 on the eve of her her Edinburgh debut.

title: Gal Pals: Brighton Fringe
description: Gal Pals is coming to Brighton Spiegeltent! Gal Pals returns to Brighton for Brighton Fringe for our yearly day party - this time at the iconic Spiegeltent! Gal Pals is a queer pop party for lesbians, dykes, queer and bi women, transmasc, transfemme and non-binary people - playing the best in female-fronted pop, hip-hop, r&b and queer bangers! Expect to hear: Ariana Grande, Ashnikko, Beyonce, Bree Runway, Britney, COBRAH, Cardi B, Carly Rae Jepson, Charli XCX, Chappell Roan, Destiny’s Child, Doechii, Doja Cat, Dua Lipa, Janelle Monae, Jennifer Lopez, Kim Petras, Kylie, Lady Gaga, Madonna, Megan Thee Stallion, Rihanna, Robyn, SOPHIE, Shygirl, Sugababes and many more! Who Can Come to Gal Pals? Gal Pals is an intentional space prioritising queer women, trans and/or non-binary people and those of marginalised gender expressions. We ask allies to only attend with friends who are from those communities and to consider the space they take up as the venue has limited capacity.

title: Gareth Gwynn: Cyril (Work in Progress)
description: Cyril Gwynn (1897-1988), also known as The Bard Of Gower, was “a tall, sunburnt, unsmiling farmer”. Gareth Gwynn (1983-Present) is none of these things. Gareth did not spend the First World War in the Merchant Navy, nor has he ever been shipwrecked, run a farm or tried to emigrate to Australia - but he's found a book of poems by his great-grandfather, and he's determined to find something they have in common. This is also a work-in-progress comedy show about living in a city when your ancestors thought tarmac was all a bit much. Gwynn (1983 ed.) has written for The News Quiz, The Now Show, Have I Got News For You and many more. He is the co-writer of Ankle Tag (BBC Radio 4), Tourist Trap (BBC One Wales) and The Goodies: The Big Ben Theory (An Audible Special). On BBC Radio 4 he presented Gareth Gwynn Hasn’t Fin- ("Hilarious" - The Guardian) and is the co-presenter of the multi-award nominated comedy/history podcast I'm So Not Over It.

title: Geoffrey Mead's Clifton and Montpelier Tour
description: Clifton Hill & Montpelier is a quiet Brighton neighbourhood with distinctive architecture, a rich social history and a certain Brighton "quirkiness"! This tour looks at the geography, geology, building styles and historic characters that make the area one of the city's most attractive areas. Churches, chutney and radical feminism combine in this colourful district.

title: Geoffrey Mead's London Road Tour
description: London Road is an area that has undergone much change in the past few years; a dynamic urban area with new ethnic shops, a thriving market and examples of housing types over the past 200 years. It also has a large open park with aged trees, Georgian terraces, the parish church and evidence of past land uses and industries.

title: George Harvey - The opener
description: George Harvey is your opening act for an hour of afternoon comedy. His job is to get you ready for the illusive but incredible act that is to follow. They should be here any minute now.... George Harvey is a local comedy favourite who sold out his debut 2025 Brighton Fringe show, "Here You Go." Considered to be the best comedian from the knees down, his patented whimsical and boyish energy paired with his high-energy stage presence has led him to become a fan-favourite act and compere on the Brighton circuit.

title: Gerry Carroll - YOUNG
description: An Ordinary Man, A Hero of the Land of the Young. A solo clown odyssey written and performed by Gerry Carroll (age 73 and not slowing down). Long ago, the warrior-poet Oisín rode off with the golden-haired Niamh to Tír na nÓg – the Land of the Young, where time stands still. Three hundred years later he came home... and instantly turned into a wrinkly old man. Gerry has a soul-crushing office job, discovers clown and comedy, and explores what it’s like when you slowly turn into a wrinkly old man. What happens when we leave the Land of the Young? In this joyous and unexpectedly moving hour, Gerry weaves the ancient Irish legend of Oisín with his own life story – with song, dance, clowning, stand-up, a few too many shot glasses, and buckets of laughter. It’s funny. It’s touching. Because growing old is inevitable, but growing up is optional. Be 21 again! Bring your granny, bring your inner child, bring a shot glass if you dare. Book now – seats are going faster than Oisín’s youth!

title: GET BETTER
description: GET BETTER is an hour long, four-hander scripted theatre piece centring themes of disability, gender, and truth. The play follows Hazel, a woman in her 20’s working in a café, grappling with chronic fatigue and trying to maintain her dreams of opening a café of her own with her best friend. The world experiences a shift, and female identifying people inexplicably loose the ability to lie. In the wake of this change, Hazel decides to channel this new-found ability into pursing a diagnosis for the chronic illness she is experiencing but doesn’t yet understand. If she’s telling the truth, she has to be believed — right? If you’re curious to learn more about the play, head over to our Instagram, @getbetterplay.
description_html:
GET BETTER is an hour long, four-hander scripted theatre piece centring themes of disability, gender, and truth.

The world experiences a shift, and female identifying people inexplicably loose the ability to lie.

If she’s telling the truth, she has to be believed — right?

If you’re curious to learn more about the play, head over to our Instagram, @getbetterplay.

title: Get Real: The Return!
description: The last remaining members of international pop sensation Get Real suffer a final humiliation at the dilapidated Funworld Holiday Park, and it’s the final straw for founding members Chaz and KK. It’s time for the mother of all comebacks...a musical! Follow the group as they reform, overcome their past differences, and reclaim the glory of stardom! All references to 90s pop bands are purely circumstantial! This new comedy follows hot on the heels of three previous sell-out productions by Out Cast. Their spring 2025 show was a Bespoke Black Books pick of the Fringe: “You’ll be in for a treat... a riot of original songs, comedic chaos and exaggerated characters.” Additional preview show on Thursday 30th April - tickets at buytickets.at/outcastbrighton

title: Get Your Geek On
description: Join the nerdiest comedians celebrating all nerd culture has to offer. From video games to table top games, LPs to larping, film franchises to fantasy novels, comic books to cosplay – we’ve got your fandom covered! Geek out with the coolest comedians (who were all probably bullied at school). Embrace your geekside, unleash your inner nerd and quite possibly be crowned the biggest geek in the room - the highest accolade we could possibly bestow and your chance to win prizes! Compete with the comedians alongside your fellow audience as our two teams battle it out to determine who knows the most useless information - the audience or the comedians. Find your tribe as you delight in the dorkiest of dweebs and the funniest fanatics. Join your host and uber-nerd Juliette Burton, with her geekiest friends for a fun-filled fanatical comedy hour!

title: Ghost Light
description: “I think if you’re going to write a ghost story... you must experience ghosts for yourself.” In 1865 Henry Webster joins the notorious London Ghost Club, hoping to meet and be inspired by Charles Dickens. There he meets Edward Price who draws Henry into investigating a haunting at his own lodging house, belonging to the widowed Mia Sands. At night, the ghosts of two young children wander the house in search of light and safety - while fearing what waits for them in the darkness. But why would a ghost be afraid of the dark?

“I think if you’re going to write a ghost story... you must experience ghosts for yourself.”

At night, the ghosts of two young children wander the house in search of light and safety - while fearing what waits for them in the darkness.

But why would a ghost be afraid of the dark?

title: Ghost Walk of the Lanes
description: The Ghost Walk of the Lanes is an undemanding 80-minute tour of Brighton's oldest and most haunted quarter, visiting eight locations. Actors and storytellers are all suitably attired in Victorian costumes and carry a few surprises in their Gladstone bags! All the stories are long-established accounts of hauntings in and around The Lanes, alongside historical facts and peppered with comic respite. This is a family-friendly event that has received the TripAdvisor Certificate of Excellence. Concessions for under 16s and for students.

title: Give Your Body Joy: The Connor Felix Story (Work In Progress))
description: In 1993, Spanish pop duo Los del Rio released their most famous song to moderate success. In 1996, a remix of this song released by the the Bayside Boys took the whole world by storm - all thanks to a set of simple, easy to follow, dance moves. In 2014, one man decided to do that dance longer than anyone ever had before. Today, he still has not stopped doing that dance. The man is Connor Felix, the dance is the macarena and this is their story. Starring comedian and writer, Alex Richmond. This show is a work in progress.

title: Glenda & Rita Sing The Movie Greats
description: The Silver Starlets return to the Rotunda after last year's sell-out run, as they take you on a musical journey of their favourite movies throughout the decades! Glenda & Rita are two actresses from the Golden Era of cinema, immortalised in black & white and struggling to find work in this brutal, unforgiving technicolour world. They will take you on a journey, as they sing the songs from more of their favourite movies throughout the years, from 'A Star Is Born' to 'Spice World', and 'James Bond' to ''Priscilla Queen of the Desert'. From the award-winning musical comedy duo that brought you 'A History of Horror', 'Glenda & Rita: Live' and 'I'm Dreaming of a Black & White Christmas', this musical soirée is not to be missed!

title: Go Kid Music Club - ANIMAL JAM
description: Following last year’s sell-out show, family favourite Go Kid Music Club is back – and this time, the animals are taking over! Animal Jam is a big, joyful live gig for families who love music, nature and fun! Join Brighton’s favourite children’s singer-songwriter Al Start and her excellent Beastie Band for a heart-warming, catchy, animal-inspired set. Sing along to family favourites, discover brand-new songs, and watch your little cubs take over the stage at Brighton’s only Kids’ Open Mic, showing the grown-ups how it’s done! Dress up in animal onesies, costumes, or bring a (stuffed) furry friend – and let’s blow the roof off the Spiegeltent together! Relaxed, inclusive, unforgettable – the ultimate afternoon for primary-age kids and their whole family. The funnest, furriest family gig you’ll find!

title: God save the Queen!
description: Step into the royal inner circle with Queen Elizabeth II in God save the Queen! — the riotous one-woman comedy show where Queen Liz 2 finally tells her side of the story. From awkward palace dinners to scandalous secrets and royal rivalries, nothing is off-limits as Lizzy spills the tea with wit, charm, and a gin in hand. Whether you’re a die-hard royal watcher or just here for the gossip, this hilarious and cheeky take on life behind the palace walls is a must-see. One crown, one woman, zero filters. Long live the Queen... of comedy!

Follow your favourite gods like Cupid and Hercules as they paint the scene of Ancient Greece and bring you to the biggest party to hit Mount Olympus since 500 BCE. title: Gone to Look for America
description: Gone To Look For America is a vivid, funny and immersive journey of stories, songs and striking projected music videos exploring the wild soul of the American backroads. Join Steve Bonham and The Long Road as they delve into outlaw country, desert ghost towns, neon-soaked nights and the extraordinary lives of ordinary people. Drawn from months of trekking and travelling through the southern states, these tales reveal the grit, grace and contradictions of a country forever caught between dream and dust. Expect powerful acoustic music, big melodies and riffs, warm humour and storytelling that is both intimate and expansive. The projected music videos add atmosphere and colour, pulling the audience into the landscapes and characters that shaped the songs. At the heart of it all are Steve Bonham – the Vagabond Philosopher – and Chris “The Bishop” Lydon, pianist, composer and fellow wanderer. Two adventurers, many miles and one unforgettable trip into the mythic heart of America.

title: GOSS
description: Award-winning dancer and comedian Sam Burkett is here for the freshest gossip—and they want it LIVE. Watch them spin your stories into a hilarious blend of improv comedy and experimental dance. Expect the unexpected, and come ready to spill. GOSS is an improvised dance-comedy solo show where Sam gossips with the audience live. Each performance is entirely unique, shaped by spontaneous conversations, physicality, and the atmosphere in the room. Think crowd work special meets performance art; it’s about creating a space of genuine listening and connection that is also incredibly stupid. Presented by Just Something Different.

title: Gotta Have Faith (Play)
description: Growing up in 1980’s rural Wiltshire requires more than a little patience, especially when you're gay, trying to be a good Christian, with a love for George Michael. This testament is a nakedly honest, funny and poignant story of one man’s journey through drugs, sex, abuse and self-acceptance. It’s a heartbreakingly familiar story to anyone who knows that rock bottom is only a Careless Whisper away. The show has already had a successful run at the Etcetera Theatre end of April 2024 where it had an almost sell out 3 day run. It was also performed at the Watford Fringe 2024.

description: New work in progress show from BBC Galton & Simpson comedy winner Grace Mulvey, an Irish stand-up taking the UK comedy scene by storm. Co-host of the Fad Camp podcast (Irish Podcast Awards - Best Entertainment Podcast 2023), her debut show "Tall Baby" was named one of Rolling Stone magazine's "12 stand-out shows of the Edinburgh Fringe 2024".
