title: Graeme Rayner: Dadology
description: "Dadology" is Graeme Rayner's debut 1 hour show, and includes everything they're not telling you about being a dad. Graeme was a semi-finalist in the 2023 British Comedian of the Year competition and is, more importantly, a dad who has spent almost two decades navigating fatherhood with varying degrees of success. "Dadology" offers insight on everything from conception through to adulthood. "A Stand-Up Masterclass - This is stand-up with soul, and it deserves a national stage " - Sussex News "The laughs come thick and fast" - Lancashire Post

title: Graeme Rayner: KrowdWerk
description: “Krowdwerk” is exactly what you might think – an unscripted crowdwork show. Having several years of MCing experience I want to put those skills to the test with an interactive, audience-driven show. It will not be an hour of “what’s your name and what do you do?” but will instead be a conversational show with topics suggested by the audience etc. If you’re coming along, be prepared to get involved! Graeme was a semi-finalist in the 2023 British Comedian of the Year competition. “The laughs come thick and fast.” – Lancashire Post “Made me laugh out loud” – David Baddiel “In total command of the audience.” – Joe Pasquale “Heckle him if you dare. You’ll be smashed to pieces if you try” – On Yorkshire Magazine

title: GREEK MYTHOLOGY IN 69 MINUTES
description: GREEK MYTHOLOGY IN 69 MINUTES Interactive Musical Comedy by Tenorman WHY WATCH IT: “Rock, Pop, Jazz, Opera collide in this offbeat act.” Time Out London, UK. A show with outrageous humor. The audience isn't just watching – they're participating, taking on roles! Featuring multilingual songs, surreal storytelling, and the irrepressible stage presence of Tenorman, Greek Mythology in 69 Minutes is an original production that turns the classic myths into an accessible and incredibly fun adventure for everyone. THERE IS NOTHING MORE SURREAL, TRAGIC AND FUNNY THAN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. THE STORY: The world is in peril from the stupidity of some mortals and a gang of legendary heroes forms to save it, guided by the god Dionysus – who’s disguised as a mortal named Mr. Greek! A thrilling, chaotic, and unforgettable experience where the audience becomes the hero. Meet Medusa, Odysseus, Zeus, Hercules, Medea, and Hades! Written and performed by the awarded international performer Tenorman.

title: Guess Boo: The Mystery of my Nan's Ashes
description: After winning the "Best Show — Audience Choice Award" at Brighton Fringe 2025, Guess Boo returns! 24 dead celebrities, 1 pot of ashes, but who will be first to "Guess Boo?" is in the urn. As a team of amateur Mediums, you will assist the infamous Mystic Smeg as he implements his irreverent and questionable methods to contact those in the spirit realm. You will be tasked to pay close attention to the whisperings of the spirits as they talk, draw and even play musical instruments to give you clues to help solve this nonsensical mystery. You'll laugh... You'll cry... You may even be aroused.

title: Guru Dudu's Silent Disco Walking Tours
description: The Guru Dudu team returns with our legendary, hilarious dance-walking tours of spontaneous flash mob dancing through the streets, interpretative dancing around local landmarks and lots of cheeky fun. Everyone wears headphones (supplied) and can hear the fun commentary from the Tour Guide and the dance mix of uplifting favourites from all eras. Please wear comfortable footwear and preferably come empty handed (no bags, etc). We go ahead in all weather so come prepared if it looks like raining. TWO STARTING LOCATIONS 1. New Road – city tour 2. i360 - seaside tour Check location when booking. THEME TOURS Most of our tours are Classic City tours with an upbeat playlist from all eras. We also run special theme tours: 70's Disco – Fri May 22, 6.30pm 80’s – Sun May 3, 3pm & Sun May 31, 3pm 90’s – Fri May 15, 6.30pm & Fri May 29, 6.30pm Abba – Sun May 10, 3pm & & Sun May 24, 3pm Queen – May 17, 4.30pm Check Guru Dudu website for any updates.

title: Halfway Over the Hill
description: Clive Copeman (BBC New Act competition runner-up) and Arna Spek (99 Comedy Club Female bursary Winner) are this year again teaming up with Michalle Paker (Go New York Finalist) for a show about mid life. These three stand-up comedians have all reached the delicate age of 40 (and above). They are in the middle of life, seeking propose and wisdom but mostly can’t remember what it is they wanted to find. Come and hear their unhinged stories from halfway over the hill, with no clear view of the top!

title: Hammer & Tongue Poetry Slam
description: For the fifth year running, legendary poetry slam, Hammer & Tongue Brighton, bring their epic grand final to Brighton Open Air Theatre! Finalists will come equipped with their unique styles and perspectives to battle it out for the crown. Lucky audience members will hold up scores out of ten to decide the fate our our brave poets. It’s sure to be an epic clash of words, syntax and rhyme like no other! Featuring a headline performance from one of the UK's biggest spoken word artists and hosted by the hilarious Leanne Shorley, join us for the best in performance poetry from Brighton’s longest-running poetry night. Doors open at 6pm.

title: Hans Zimmer's Hollywood x Charlie Chaplin: the BEST Film Music EVER!
description: Inspired by the film Interstellar (2014), our show time-warps cinema history, intersplicing modern film music with silent cinematography as we travel through cinema history. With live music including Hans Zimmer’s film scores for Batman and The Da Vinci Code; John Williams’ Jurassic Park, Jaws, Star Wars, Harry Potter and Schindler’s List; Ennio Morricone’s Cinema Paradiso and Gabriel’s Oboe and Joe Hisashi’s Spirited Away and Howl’s Moving Castle. Includes digital cameos from Hollywood icon Charlie Chaplin. 'Enlightening' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com), 'An atmosphere which is all too often missed in our digital world' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Masters of their crafts' ★★★★★(BingeFringe.com). Director: Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: Happily Never After
description: The Maydays return to the Fringe with their 5-star improvised show – Happily Never After – a "darkly comedic" twisted musical tale inspired by gothic themes, fairy-tales, and the warped imaginations that brought us Lemony Snicket, Beetlejuice, Shockheaded Peter and Grimm's Tales. Plucked from the seed of your suggestion, The Maydays will take you on a unique journey – weaving a gothic tale that’s completely different every time. There’s no telling what strange characters, innocent beasts or talking objects you might meet along the way! The Maydays have been wow-ing audiences across the UK and Europe, with packed houses and standing ovations, for their spontaneous show that Reviews Hub called "touching, incredibly funny", and "rich in narrative twists and charm". With a bespoke, improvised soundtrack for every show, this will be a truly creepy, mischievous and fantastical experience.

title: Hate Mail
description: Journalism was a witness to history, a byword for truth, diligence and justice. Now it’s gawping a post-truth, clickbait car crash. Impish knows, because that's her background. But how did we get there? A place where facts are relative, hate is currency, and consent is for cookies. Investigative journalism's been reduced to people trawling the net to help themselves to whatever’s trending, a stolen showcase to help boost outrage and traffic. The race to the bottom is a sprint. We’re all masters of misinformation – left reeling from the personal spin on personal branding. A true twenty first century tale of good vs evil. Find out how it ends.

title: Headmix & Tragic Roundabout: Live Music Extravaganza
description: Come ready to bounce at this unforgettable night of unbridled brilliance! Two live bands that started in 90’s Brighton come together for the first time in so many years we can’t remember the last time. So let’s call it a one-off! Between them there's some banging new tracks plus some old favourites. Tragic Roundabout bring an eclectic melange of hardcore punked-up ska/klezmer blended with an unsubtle hint of the mystic eastern bloc. Radical, irrepressible and humorous vibes are expected. Headmix are bolder and bouncier than ever, intoxicating audiences at many popular festivals over the last few years in the UK and Europe. Skilfully combining bagpipes, fiddle, accordion, whistle, melodica and heartfelt vocals with a driving rhythm section. Expect a totally unique, irresistibly dancey live experience. “Their eclectic mix of instruments and raw stage energy are amazing!“ (Last.fm) Get there early for special guest DJ Don Dapper spinning Afro, Balkan, Cumbia, Dub and Swing!

Headmix are bolder and bouncier than ever, intoxicating audiences at many popular festivals over the last few years in the UK and Europe. Skilfully combining bagpipes, fiddle, accordion, whistle, melodica and heartfelt vocals with a driving rhythm section. Expect a totally unique, irresistibly dancey live experience. “Their eclectic mix of instruments and raw stage energy are amazing!“ (Last.fm)
Get there early for special guest DJ Don Dapper spinning Afro, Balkan, Cumbia, Dub and Swing!

title: Hereditary
description: Hereditary is a riotous deep dive into the contradictions of Irish identity, told through the lens of family, booze, sex, and a misguided stint in the army. From the generational curse of alcoholism to the uniquely Irish knack for being both sexually repressed and wildly promiscuous, no taboo is safe. Just when it seems it can’t get more absurd, along comes the paradox of a peace-loving hippy in the Irish Army. With razor-sharp wit and heartfelt chaos, Hereditary explores the things we inherit, the battles we fight—literal and metaphorical—and the joy of embracing the contradictions that shape who we are.

title: History Has No Safe Word
description: Professor Nathan Zangari is fading away. Once voted the sexiest professor on BBC2’s Open University, his highly celebrated archeo-linguistics paper on “Graffiti, Women and the Walls of Pompeii” is now but a distant memory. Now, he can’t sleep, he can’t get published, and... he can’t get laid. At the sleep-study clinic that he’s enrolled at, they observe him talking in his sleep, seemingly an intermediary for a long-forgotten Pompeian girl. When overambitious student, Callie, offers to capture his mumblings, they both find themselves sucked into the very real lives of those on the edge of destruction, some 2000 years ago. Best Newcomer (Nom) The Offies 2024 Winner, International Fringe Encore Series 2022 Best New Play, New Writing South 2012 Best New Play(Shortlist) NWS 2013 Bruntwood Prize (Longlisting) 2019 Best Male Performer, Btn Fringe 2013 Best Female Performer, Btn Fringe 2014 Best Female Performer (Nom), Btn Fringe 2013 Best Male Performer (Nom), Btn Fringe 2007

title: Holy Shift!
description: Holy Shift is a comedic, time-travelling dive back into the gloriously chaotic 90s, following a mum who suddenly finds herself raising a teenager whose idea of rebellion is... becoming a saint. Sin, she gets. Skipping school, sneaking out, questionable fashion choices-she's been there, done that, still owns the mixtapes. But a holy uprising? This, she did not see coming. Desperate to decode her daughter's devotion, she cracks open her old teenage diary stuffed with heartbreak, hope, and the kind of melodrama only the 90s could produce, while blasting out the Madonna anthems that once saved her teenage soul - preaching empowerment, reinvention, and the sacred art of surviving adolescence with her eyeliner intact. Through the music, memories, and a few emotional meltdowns, she begins to see her own youth more clearly than ever. And maybe starts to understand her daughter, too.

title: Home Away From Home: Bridges In Between by Amrita Chandradas
description: Amrita Chandradas is a Singaporean documentary photographer and her work explores the less discussed issues of displacement and struggles through on-the-ground personal insights. She graduated with an MA in Photojournalism and Documentary photography from London College of Communications and was recently bestowed as the UK Alumni winner for the "Culture and Creativity Award" by The British Council and The British High Commission. She was also a finalist for the Asian Women Photographers Showcase, Excellence in Digital News by SOPA News. Her work is featured at The National Geographic, The New York Times, The Washington Post, The LA Times, BBC World, British Journal of Photography, SONY, National Heritage Board, Singapore Tourism Board, Time Out Singapore, and Bloomberg Business amongst many others. THIS EVENT IS SPONSORED BY THE IRENE MENSAH BURSARY & FLO CREATIVE IN COLLABORATION WITH PLUS X INNOVATION

title: How To Survive and Thrive in an Impossible World - with a piano!
description: Mischievous, subversive, fun and fizzing with wisdom, “How to Survive and Thrive in an Impossible World – with a piano!” invites you to step back, laugh at a world that is quite clearly bonkers, and discover a better way to navigate it all. Award-winning psychologist and songwriter Steve Bonham teams up with master composer-musician Chris “The Bishop” Lydon for a highly original mix of humour, stories, songs and gentle audience participation. Part uplifting wellbeing session, part musical adventure, the show explores the “Five Rules of the Road” for living with more resilience, joy and freedom. Expect laughter, toe-tapping tunes, and ideas that sneak up on you - in the best possible way! It’s warm, witty, and just a little bit wild: a chance to liberate your happiness and wellbeing, one chuckle at a time. If you’re ready to rethink, reset and rediscover what makes life worth the ride, don’t miss this unforgettable experience.

title: Howls Moving Castle Ghibli Taste & Tales
description: Join us for an enchanting evening of Ghibli-inspired food and storytelling at Ghibli Taste & Tales presents Howl's Moving Castle! Welcome to Ghibli Taste & Tales presents Howl's Moving Castle! Join us for a magical evening filled with enchanting stories and delectable dishes inspired by Studio Ghibli's masterpiece, Howl's Moving Castle. This in-person event will be held at Blossoms Cocktail Bar and Restaurant, where you'll be transported into the whimsical world of Howl and Sophie. Immerse yourself in the fantastical ambiance, indulge in themed cocktails and gourmet bites, and relive the charm of this beloved film with fellow fans. Don't miss this unique opportunity to experience the magic of Ghibli in real life! There are three magical courses to dive yourself in to, fettered with two cocktails to quench your thirst throughout this experience. (Non-alcoholic cocktails available). Vegan/vegetarian options available.

This week, he heads to a quaint village to fulfill Mega-fan Jane Plemley’s last wish to have her funeral televised. As usual, he’ll lead the service and answer your burning questions. title: Humdinger Words for Lollygaggers and Fuddy-Duddies
description: This interactive comedy-cabaret-poetry extravaganza blends sharp wordplay, visual gags and mind-boggling trivia into an unmissable hour of laughs, learning and surprises. Performed by neurospicy Renaissance man, Ben Dali, the show draws on a life spanning many careers, from hypnotist to primary teacher. Expect pieces to shift rapidly between styles and moods without warning, from infectious sincerity to unapologetic filth. Highlights include a journey through English’s finest ‘Humdinger Words’; innocent childhood poems juxtaposed with depraved adult sequels; a visual comedy treat comparing country outlines to their outlandish doppelgängers. Along the way, Dali relives his diverse skillset, including genuine hypnotic effects and mentalism delivered in improvised rhyme. Designed for attentive, intellectually curious audiences who celebrate playful language, HW&OJB is ideal for anyone who enjoys comedy that subverts genre and expectations, delivering knockout punchlines in every bout.

title: Humdinger Words for Whippersnappers and Guttersnipes
description: Step into the poetic classroom of the UK's most entertaining supply teacher, as he welcomes you into a world of witty wordplay, tantalising trivia, megaLOLs and even rhyming magic! Humdinger Words for Whippersnappers and Guttersnipes is a PG hour of comedy-cabaret poetry led by stage hypnotist turned teacher, Ben Dali. Presented as a school day, this is a fast-paced journey through elements of the KS2 and KS3 curriculum. 'Mr Dali' combines poetry, comedy and insights into his eclectic background to deliver epic poems he wrote aged 7, a balloon-modelling tutorial poem, a joyful exploration of English's finest 'Humdinger Words', and genuine improvised rhyming mind reading. Designed for intellectually curious and creative 7–14 year-olds, the whole family will leave entertained, educated and inspired. The show includes a supportive Show-and-Tell segment, where children are invited to perform their own poems. So, budding young wordsmiths desirous of a platform: come prepared to share.

title: I Want To Speak To Your Manager (How I Was Radicalised And Became...Karen)
description: You’ve heard this woman in a café, complaining her extra-hot-skinny latté isn’t hot enough. You’ve seen her in a restaurant, demanding dishes that aren’t on the menu. You’ve met her in the ALDI car park, where her SUV takes up multiple spaces: the dreaded Karen. But could you – down-to-earth, humble, people-pleasing – become her? Holly did. And she’s ready to speak her truth. This debut one-woman-show from internationally-acclaimed slam poet and accidental keyboard warrior Holly Hughes is a bold, hilarious and brutally honest interrogation of who gets to be angry in today’s society. After all, what is so wrong with holding people accountable? When broligarch losers are destroying the world, is being a Karen REALLY that bad? Join the most hated woman on Google reviews to celebrate society's unsung heroes (serial complainers). After a sell-out run at Dublin Fringe Festival, Karen is ready for world domination. This is more than comedy. This is a movement. One complaint at a time.

title: I Was Dancing in the Lesbian Bar
description: Welcome to Holly Redford-Jones’ award-winning Lesbian Bar! Part stand-up, part gig, part queer fever dream. This bold new show dances through tales of unsung, unsanitised lesbian debauchery. A sonic love letter to sapphic institutions of a bygone era, it celebrates queer women of the past, present and future with humour, heart, and a dash of disco. Inspired by the cult Jonathan Richman track, Chesterfield local Redford-Jones asks the big questions: Where have all the lesbian bars gone? How do you even know when you’re in one? And how did Chesterfield’s Crooked Spire get so... bent?

title: Iain Anderson: TRAUMEDY, A Guide To Being A Fabulous Homeless Addict
description: Traumedy: A Guide To Being A Fabulous Homeless Addict is a true story about life through addiction to sobriety. From being young and dumb on the Glasgow gay scene, through homelessness on the streets of Barcelona, to being committed to a psych ward and then landing in recovery. It is a tragic yet hilarious true story about overcoming demons and finding the real meaning of what it takes to be happy. Following a smash-hit debut at the Edinburgh Fringe in 2024, Barcelona-based Scottish comedian Iain Anderson has sold out venues across Europe with his unforgettable show. From London and Glasgow to Berlin and Dublin and beyond, audiences have been captivated by a performance that's more than just stand-up—it's a powerful and hilarious rollercoaster of emotions unlike anything else in comedy today. ★★★★★ European Comedy ★★★★ EdFringe Review ★★★★ One4Review Winner of Best Solo Show 2025 at The Clubbie Awards in Barcelona

title: Improbotics Presents: Artificial Reality
description: Improvised show where audiences become producers and join the cast on the stage. With questionable methods, AI, wild characters, and deep fake celebrities, we sabotage reality TV. A fast-paced high-tech satire of AI slop and voyeurism from Improbotics, the award-winning pioneers of robot theatre. Led by an Executive Producer host, the crowd chooses setting, contestants, prize, and celebrities who turn up. 6 contestants compete for audience love. Because of budget cuts, "celebrities" must be recreated using our own questionable "artificial reality" methods (state-of-the-art deep fake, voice cloning, image and video generation, and special AR glasses to send lines from a chatbot to control the "celebrity" actor). As improvisers transform into contestants and get eliminated, replaced, or rebooted by AI, the show becomes a hilarious exploration of improv and reality TV mashed together, and a subtle critique of capitalism and of the rise of AI in entertainment.

title: In conversation with Duncan Lustig-Prean
description: An informal conversation with much humour and the chance for the audience to ask questions. Join renowned former BBC Radio 4 presenter, Nigel Wrench in conversation with Duncan Lustig-Prean. He led the campaign for LGBT people to serve in the Armed Forces and subsequently changed the law on two further occasions. He investigated racism in the Armed Forces and conceived the idea of women having full career opportunities in the forces and helped the fight to restore their medals. He ensured that the global majority was better represented by English Heritage and runs a charity supporting LGBT veterans. Somehow he finds the time to chair Brighton Fringe. From opera singer to becoming "one of the most influential naval officers of the last 50 years; he did not just change the law, he changed the culture of the United Kingdom's armed forces." (First Sea Lord) The proceeds of this event are donated to the charity, Brighton Fringe.

title: in the air
description: Chloe's mom has passed. Her mother has left behind a box of old pictures for her to sort through. As she sees the younger version of her mother and wonders what she was like... she meets Violet. Violet is put together and organized and ambitious... the very opposite of Chloe. But the two of them slowly realize that they have more in common than they think. Told from their adjacent balconies, "In the Air" is a story about friendship, fate, and how looking back can help with moving on. Both actors are prepared to play either role. There will be a coin toss before each performance to determine the casting of the show. Seen at Stratford East's Freelance Royalty Scratch and Sophie's Barn (Patron: Dame Judy Dench). Supported by Theatre Deli & New Diorama Theatre. Fundraising for The Maya Centre, a women-only charity in the heart of Islington, who believe that no one should face trauma or hardship alone.

title: International Poetry Festival
description: International Poetry Festival is a 3-day electrifying celebration of contemporary poetry at Brighton Fringe, organized by the Institute for Experimental Arts (Greece, UK, Tunisia, India, USA, Lebanon, Germany). This immersive event brings together cutting-edge poetic forms from across the world, blending language, visual arts and sound into a powerful live experience. The festival features video poetry screenings by internationally acclaimed artists, presenting striking visual narratives that create cinematic poems challenging how poetry is seen and felt. Vibrant spoken word performances showcase a dynamic mix of international voices alongside local Brighton poets, offering raw, intimate and politically charged work that reflects diverse cultures and perspectives. Poetry is fused with electronic music, stunning visuals, live soundscapes and digital experimentation. Venue: Presuming Ed-114-115 London Road- Brighton UK Dates: 27-28 & 31 May - starts at 22.15

title: INVASION (JoMarZeb)
description: A bold, multi-media spectacle from Philippine Theatre UK on its 23rd anniversary, INVASION (JoMarZeb) leaps across time—from the anxious streets of 1940s Manila to the quiet corners of modern-day Oxford. Three mischievous child-like ghosts, a teacher, a lawyer and a nun, crash into the present, pulling audiences into a haunting, humorous, and deeply moving human journey that blends horror, drama, comedy, music, film and history. At its core it lies a horrifying look at the atrocities of the Imperial Japanese Army during World War II, including the silenced suffering of the Comfort Women. Through a mix of historical truth and imaginative storytelling, the play reveals unlikely friendships, rock-solid resilience and the way unresolved justice echoes across generations. Helmed by award-winning writer and director Ramon C. Teñoso, JoMarZeb confronts memory, faith, loyalty, and the shadows of colonial trauma—reminding us that the past never really go... and sometimes, it follows us home.

title: Iterum Felix - Happy Again
description: Iterum Felix – Happy Again: Curiosities for the Future is an exhibition by artist Ryan Callanan, presented at Whistleblower Gallery. The show brings together works made from salvaged, reclaimed, and donated objects, reworked through painting and physical modification. Alongside materials sourced more broadly, the gallery has invited famous acquaintances and members of the wider community to contribute personal items, each carrying its own history before being transformed. Drawing on Callanan’s ongoing use of the smiley motif and references to rave culture, these pieces give overlooked objects a new form and presence. Visitors are also invited to contribute an object for possible reinterpretation as part of the exhibition. Through reuse, alteration, and recontextualisation, Iterum Felix – Happy Again reflects on renewal, nostalgia, and how value and meaning can shift when familiar objects are seen differently.

title: Jacob Hatton: BC
description: BC. Before everything. Let old Jacob take you back to the very start, and to the end. Archeology for perverts and the Bronze Age Collapse. Town planning by the deeply unwell. Do cats have our interests at heart? Jacob Hatton has been toiling in the joke mines, unearthing a brand new show that takes us back through time and all the way to the end of it all, via Lewisham because whatever journey you make, even through time, the roundabout there is going to be a nightmare. "Proper gags.... Hatton's a strong comedy technician" - Chortle. ★★★★ "Brilliantly British humour"
description_html:
BC. Before everything. Let old Jacob take you back to the very start, and to the end.

Archeology for perverts and the Bronze Age Collapse. Town planning by the deeply unwell. Do cats have our interests at heart?

Jacob Hatton has been toiling in the joke mines, unearthing a brand new show that takes us back through time and all the way to the end of it all, via Lewisham because whatever journey you make, even through time, the roundabout there is going to be a nightmare.

"Proper gags.... Hatton's a strong comedy technician" - Chortle.

★★★★ "Brilliantly British humour"

title: Jacob Lovick: The Famous Ashtray
description: In 1965, The Beach Boys, halfway through their legendary nonsense hit about sheep 'Barbara Ann', broke off to rattle what they christened the 'Famous Ashtray'. The ashtray in question was the most open secret in the 1960s music industry, and yet this was its first, and only, appearance in an actual recorded song. But its role in the development, unstoppable heights and despicable lows of 20th century pop cannot be understated. 'The Famous Ashtray' is a psychedelic, character-comedy explosion right in the middle of the dark heart of the 1960s and everything terrible that's happened ever since. Jacob Lovick is a clown, writer and comedian. This is his first 100% factual show. Praise for his previous work: ""Why wouldn't you go?" , ★★★★ - A Youngish Perspective, 2023 ★★★★ - Broadway Baby, 2023

description: Jade Gebbie (British Comedy Guide Performance Award Finalist 2025, writer for The News Quiz, Nature Table) has always been a people pleaser, but now she’s hit her mid-thirties she’s delving into how it’s held her back, annoyed her loved ones and why overthinking fictional scenarios isn’t a reasonable excuse for missing a work meeting. Come along to this debut from Jade for fun, laughter and a bit of anxiety... after all, she is a millennial.

title: Jane Eyre
description: The attic burns with secrets untold! In Polly Teale’s bold reimagining, Bertha Mason - the infamous “madwoman in the attic”- steps from the shadows and emerges as the living embodiment of Jane’s suppressed longings and rage. A daring interpretation that reveals the storm beneath Charlotte Brontë’s classic. Dive into Jane’s inner world and unearth the psychological battles between passion and restraint, duty and desire. Arresting and emotionally charged, this adaptation breathes raw, urgent life into one of literature’s most enduring heroines — a Jane Eyre like no other.

title: Jane Postlethwaite is Bone Idle
description: In her new work-in-progress show Bone Idle, Northern Queer Comedian Jane Postlethwaite takes you on a hilariously insightful journey through the absurdities of her current life armed with her trademark dark, dry wit & unflinching honesty. Jane tackles living back in a rural village where she grew up in the North, coming out later in life as a lesbian but being single for over 10 years & her own personal quest to try be a better person. She's a multi-comedy award finalist. Nominated for Biggest Gay Award at Brighton Fringe 2024. Heard on BBC Radio 4. She's also an online lesbian sensation as @janedoescomedy with 88k+ Tiktok Followers & over 20 million views on Instagram. "Unmissable 5 star show" ★★★★★ (Comedy Coroner). "A darkly funny show" ★★★★ (Funny Women). "Absolutely hilarious" ★★★★ (Broadway Baby). Heard on BBC Radio 4, the BBC New Comedy Awards and BBC Radio Cumbria.

title: Jaz Mattu Fights His Audience
description: In my comedy career, work life, and actual life, people have tried to fight me. Jaz Mattu Fights His Audience is a show about all those moments and how I navigate conflict, presented as an absurd, multimedia stand-up experience blending clown, music, and theatre. Expect inventive staging, projections, and sound that pull you into the chaos of the fight. Please don’t try to fight me during the show. “Truly unique.” (ENTERTAINMENT NOW ★★★★) “Ambitious and innovative.” (FEST) “Mattu has a great deal of creativity and ingenuity.” (BROADWAY BABY)

title: Jenny Zed's LOST THE PLOT
description: All sensibilities have been torn down, and your sanity is next. Jenny Zed's Lost the Plot is the multi-media nightmare that could've only been concocted within the overcooked, whimsical brain of Brighton's milfy masked drag menace. Expect singing, lip-syncs and overall stupidity. But above all else, expect the unexpected. It's time to take a break from every other self-exploartive life story and cut-and-paste narrative through-line. Enter the Jenny-verse, and enjoy the ride (it's gonna be a bumpy one).

title: Jo Jarvie: Crazy, Stupid, Funny
description: Jo Jarvie is an award-winning comedian based in Belfast, Northern Ireland. After a 12-year hiatus, Jo returned to the comedy scene with a vengeance, crafting hilarious anecdotes that resonate with audiences. She was the first-ever English person to place in Ireland’s history at “Show Me The Funny”, (proving that she’s just too funny to ignore!). A runner-up in the competition, Jo is known for her high-spirited performances & unapologetically dirty jokes. A graduate of the Guildhall School of Music & Drama and a devoted West Ham United fan, Jo brings a unique blend of theatrical flair and football fanaticism to her comedy. Her fearless approach to stand-up has earned her accolades and a growing fan base. Come on the journey of what you learn when it all goes wrong! “Watch out for Jo Jarvie, she’s funny, fearless and funny as f**k”- Dapper Laughs “Jo is a great act with a great stage presence and delivers hilarious material”- Phil Chapman “Cockney charm with Belfast bite”- Paddy Raff

title: Jonny Brook: This Isn't The Life I Expected
description: Jonny Brook, a child in his 40's, trying and failing to make sense of world he never expected to be in. Born with functioning human limbs, the last 20 years has seen him fail and fail again, to deal with how increasingly disabled he's becoming, and how he fails to deal with Doctors, Nurses, Surgeons, His Family, Kind Prostitutes, Empathetic Drug Addicts, Naked Pensioners, and so many more sources of interaction, he's hilariously incapable of dealing with...

title: Jonny Woo: Suburbia
description: Jonny Woo's latest personal show, Suburbia, about his life from the suburbs of Kent to the queer scene in London and the sex clubs of New York received rave reviews and the Brighton Fringe Award for Excellence at The Edinburgh Fringe, 2025. Blending story-telling, drag, song, spoken word and burlesque, this is easily his most revealing show yet, in more ways than one. Now in his fifties, Jonny reflects back at the people and moments that made him the man and the performer he is today. He grew up in The Medway Towns and found drugs and the club scene in the early 1990's before exploring his drag alter ego in NYC at the turn of the Millennium. Memories of his first sexual experience and his first "drag" inspiration echo like ghosts throughout and amongst the wild confessions he confronts his fears and triumphs in this unique coming of age story. The presence of a mysterious suburban "cross-dresser" permeates his narrative in a show which is as much a feast for the eyes as for the soul.

title: Jordan Jones: A Street Mattress Named Desire
description: Following a sellout work in progress run in Brighton Fringe 2025 - Komedia New Comedy Award 2025 semi finalist Jordan Jones brings his debut standup and musical comedy hour back to the Brighton Fringe for 2026. 2014 was the second worst year of Jordan's life. He was working a dead-end job, his girlfriend left him, his band was breaking up, and he was still living with his mum (and not in a smart, saving money way). Apparently, living on a council estate and working at Sainsburys part-time wasn't a suitable backup plan for a failed music career. Come and see Jordan Jones as he explores how he got into that mess in the first place, his attempts to better himself by moving to Brighton, and when 2024 knocked 2014 off the top spot for the worst year of Jordan's life, how a life threatening illness put everything he learned to the test. Expect music, tales from working class suburbia, awful jobs, and overcoming the urge to forage for street furniture.

title: Joz Norris Is Hugh Jackman Is The Phantom Of The Opera
description: 20 years ago Hollywood icon Hugh Jackman was offered the role of the Phantom of the Opera, a role that award-winning comedy legend and idiot Joz Norris has dreamed about his entire life. He turned it down, and has become a laughing stock. Now, an artist known for taking himself too seriously despite his creative output consisting of utter nonsense will play another one in this musical extravaganza that will reset the course of history. Creator and star of BBC Radio 4's The Dream Factory and A Small Talk On Small Talk Winner of the Comedians' Choice Award for Best Show 2019 and the WoW Award for Best Comedy 2025 Two-time nominee for the Malcolm Hardee Award for Comic Originality, Runner-Up for the NextUp Biggest Award in Comedy, and nominee for the Chortle Award for Best Variety Act.

title: Julia Knight: Songs of Joy & Justice
description: Award-winning performer Julia Knight returns to Brighton Fringe with an hour of quirky, original comedy songs highlighting life's joys, absurdities and injustices in equal measure. Find out why Julia loves avocados but avoids extreme sports. Expect songs about crows, billionaires and George Monbiot! Also a brand-new song improvised from audience suggestions on the day. The show also features songs from Julia's solo shows 'I Am Not Victoria Wood" which had sell-out runs at Brighton and Edinburgh Fringe Festivals and the award-winning "Zeit-Heist: More than a feeling".

title: Kane & Abel Magicians: Twin Feats
description: 30 years after being pulled out of class to partake in twin studies, Kane & Abel take you inside the mystical, magical and hilarious world of life as a twin. Those conducting these studies were hoping for more than coincidences or feats but miracles or magic. Kane & Abel recreate the study in impossible circumstances for the ultimate Magicians Twin Feats. 'Real magic' (BBC Radio Bristol). Top Weekend Comedy Pick (Sunday Times).

title: Kate Robb: Doghouse
description: Doghouse is traditional standup with a few twists which bring the show to life. “Marriage, motherhood & a dog with sticks where they shouldn’t be — this is family life as it really is: messy, honest & brilliantly funny" Ian Whitcombe Comedy. What previous audience members have said: “My face hurt from laughing by the end” “Proper belly laughing from start to finish!” "Such a clever performer and so, so funny" "Absolutely fantastic set from start to finish. Still chuckling..." “Awesome - one to watch in my opinion, clever writing with a naughty edge at times - great performer too” Kate was a Funny Women Awards semi-finalist just months after starting standup & is a regular on the UK comedy circuit, performing at venues up & down the country. She has been described as: “Talented & going places” Notts Comedy Review; “Sharp, sarcastic, brilliantly bold” Ian Whitcombe Comedy; “A star in the making” Den of Laughs; "Definitely one to watch" Gag n Bone Man Comedy.

title: Kate-Lois Elliott: Work In Progress
description: After a sell-out Edinburgh debut, Kate-Lois Elliott (British Writers' Guild Award Nominee, British Comedy Guide Awards, Funny Women Content Creator Nominee, The Times Best Jokes Of The Fringe) is back with a new show about modern monogamy, fertility, Gingham fetishes and how 'the good old olden days’ weren’t even that good, actually. Picking apart stories from history and her own life - and occasionally bursting into song - from Queen Victoria’s alleged sex mania, to the real origin of “the average Joe,” to Elliott’s own deranged obsession with making it into the tabloids, this is a show for anyone who’s ever wondered whether to lean in, burn it all down, or take up cross-stitch and see what happens. Music by Katie Pritchard & Kate-Lois Elliott
description_html:

title: Kathy Maniura: The Cycling Man
description: One of the best reviewed shows of the Edinburgh Fringe 2025. Middle-aged and newly single, The Cycling Man has spent the salary of an NHS nurse in Decathlon and he’s on the verge of a breakdown. Get to know this absurd and deeply flawed man as he gets to know himself. Goofy drag king show from award-winning British character comedian Kathy Maniura, BBC New Comedian shortlisted, 2023 and Sketch Off winner, 2020. “Simply genius” ★★★★★ EdFringe Review “A masterclass in character comedy” ★★★★ The Skinny “A brilliant, side-splitting parody” ★★★★ Broadway Baby "A cracking ride” ★★★★ Fest Mag "A complete gem of a show” ★★★★ One4Review Directed by Cecily Nash.

title: Katie Hand: Don't Let Him Go to Portugal (Work In Progress)
description: A surreal and philosophical musical comedy show combining stand-up, puppetry, and animation to tell the autobiographical story of an outrageous breakup and how through it I discovered the meaning of life and also a bunch of stuff about worms. By artist/comedian whose credits include ‘One to Watch’ Funny Women 2025 Stage Awards, Semi-finalist Musical Comedy Awards 2025, Soho Theatre Labs Comedy Plus Alumni 2025, and with over 4 million views on Instagram. A true alternative comic, the piece bends between genres in a celebration of unbridled creativity and absurdism.

title: Katie-Rose Petley: Dirty B*tch
description: After a sold-out run last year, Canadian comedian Katie-Rose Petley is back with Dirty Bitch: a filthy, fearless dive into being raised in the church, progressive parenting, and sex pre-9/11. Still unhinged. Still hilarious. Katie-Rose Petley is a Canadian stand-up comedian known for her fresh, filthy, and unfiltered storytelling. With material that’s dark, shameless, and disturbingly relatable, she’s been described as “delightfully vulgar.” Whether she’s unpacking religion, motherhood, or the ups and downs of dog ownership, Katie-Rose delivers big laughs with bold honesty and zero apologies.

title: KEPT
description: KEPT: What Remains When the Noise Stops A visceral theatre piece tracing a life rebuilt through rhythm, memory, and art. From a young man “kept” in the charged intimacy of a Chicago summer to the grief that follows years later in Amsterdam, KEPT journeys through desire, loss, and restoration. Drawing on years in percussive theatre and storytelling, it transforms lived experience into sound — using rhythm and story to explore the ways we break, stray, and begin again, and the things that refuse to disappear.
description_html:
KEPT: What Remains When the Noise Stops

A visceral theatre piece tracing a life rebuilt through rhythm, memory, and art. From a young man “kept” in the charged intimacy of a Chicago summer to the grief that follows years later in Amsterdam, KEPT journeys through desire, loss, and restoration. Drawing on years in percussive theatre and storytelling, it transforms lived experience into sound — using rhythm and story to explore the ways we break, stray, and begin again, and the things that refuse to disappear.

title: KIDZ SILENT DISCO JAM
description: Kidz Silent Disco Jam! Perfect for kids who love to dance, have fun, and be themselves! This is not your average silent disco - it's a full-on interactive and entertaining experience that invites kids to step into the spotlight and let loose as they join a fun-filled silent disco party. Rocking silent disco headphones, the kids have a blast and feel like the star of the show! Watch as they come together to show off their moves, unleashing their inner stars and expressing themselves through dance. This is all about confidence, creativity, and pure fun! Led by Charli Ohlala, the fun happens inside the headphones as she entertains, plays games, sparks dance-offs, and gets everyone involved - yes even the Mums and Dads, if they are up to it! We'll have glitter and face paints waiting, and parents can jump in to help their kids get party-ready - it's their time to shine! At least 1 adult ticket must be purchased per booking. All attendees must have tickets.

title: KINDER
description: The Brighton Fringe Excellence award-nominated and List's Best LGBTQIA+ Edinburgh Fringe Show award-winner makes its Brighton Fringe premiere! After an unexpected call derails a performance planned by newly arrived drag artist Goody Prostate, booked to headline the local "reading hour", they are forced to pull their act together in real time and get ready to face a crowd of unruly children and their parents. But as they race against the clock to pull off a new number in time, they spiral into a chaotic interrogation of childhood, memory, and a long overdue examination of what it means to "grow up". The library is open - sit down, be quiet, and listen. Because sometimes, reading is what? Fundamentalist.
description_html:
The Brighton Fringe Excellence award-nominated and List's Best LGBTQIA+ Edinburgh Fringe Show award-winner makes its Brighton Fringe premiere!

After an unexpected call derails a performance planned by newly arrived drag artist Goody Prostate, booked to headline the local "reading hour", they are forced to pull their act together in real time and get ready to face a crowd of unruly children and their parents.

But as they race against the clock to pull off a new number in time, they spiral into a chaotic interrogation of childhood, memory, and a long overdue examination of what it means to "grow up".

The library is open - sit down, be quiet, and listen. Because sometimes, reading is what? Fundamentalist.

title: Krapp's Last Tape
description: Every year, on his birthday, Krapp records a tape. This year it is his 69th birthday and he is recording his last tape. As he does he finds himself listening back to one he recorded when he was a much younger man. This one act play is one of Samuel Beckett's greatest works. A witty and compelling reflection of the life of a man and the hopes that he once had. From the team behind Edfringe Sell-Out "The Unknown Soldier" Grist to the Mill. Performed by Award-winning actor and writer Ross Ericson.

title: Lady Bolognese: CLASS WAR
description: "Ridiculously funny" and "Extraordinarily talented" [The Reviews Hub] clown duo LADY BOLOGNESE are encouraging YOU, dumb-dumb, to GO BACK TO SCHOOL. But beware: your dinnerladies, Barbara and Veronica are in charge now. And they’re serving up a scrumptious blend of greasy friendship and anarchic delight, with hints of impending peril. If you behave, you’ll be home in time for cocoa and Fray Bentos. It’s Thelma & Louise meets George’s Marvellous Medicine meets a big pile of offal. So feed, learn, and come of age. Lady Bolognese have prepared their specialty. Come to class. It's gonna be a riot. (Adult audiences only, Rated 14+)

title: Late Night With Terry Wogan
description: Terry Wogan is back from the dead as part of his ongoing attempts to return to light entertainment, no matter the cost. Featuring interviews with "real" celebrities and accompanied by a live band. Previous guests include: George Lucas, Ray Parker Jr, Anthony Hopkins, Liza Minelli, Tim Henman, Scarlett Johansson and several different Michael Caines. Every night one lucky member of the audience will give Terry £5000. Terry Wogan is a recipient of a KBE / dead. Featuring Benjamin Alborough as Terry Wogan. Lineups announced on Instagram.com/terrywoganvevo Mervyn Stutter's Pick of the Fringe 2025

title: Laughing Horse CLEAN Pick of the Fringe
description: The Pick of the Fringe has been a hit show at Brighton Fringe for 19 years, an annual selection of the very best in stand-up comedy at Brighton Fringe. And this is the clean version! It's the same succulent smorgasbord of fantastically funny stand-up comedy headliners and the best in fresh new talent, just with the swear words taken out, and comedy suitable for everyone! (And there's a swear jar on stage, just in case the comedians mess up!) There are four different comics at each show, all selected from shows all around Brighton Fringe with something for everyone - so for the biggest laughs and best comedy acts (without a lot of the rude bits), this is the show for you! title: Lawrence Dodd: This Can't Be It
description: Loz Dodd believes he’s made something monumental in this playful, meta show exploring the strangeness of life and Perpetually derailed, deluded, and paralysed by overwhelm, Big Loz Dodd arrives to do something very special indeed - something that might just deliver the kind of profound insight that shakes our ‘contemporary situation’ to its core. Blending performance art and contemporary clowning, This Can’t Be It is a semi-improvised, feverish & open hearted exploration of how we stay open to the mystery when everything is closing us down. Directed by Lara Ricote and with Rachael Dobbie on tech.

title: Legends of Bubbleland
description: From the creator of The Bubble Show® comes a dazzling new adventure full of bubble artistry and visual magic! Journey through the awe-inspiring legends of Bubbleland, as they are brought to life in a breathtaking theatrical experience made of soap and water. Watch as the master of bubbles breathes life into: light, water, plants, animals, stars, and even the essence of human nature itself — in a celebration of creation! This magical production is a feast for the senses, featuring fire effects, poetic puppetry, light-displays, majestic music, and brand-new bubble masterpieces never seen before. Whether you’re discovering bubble theatre for the first time or returning for more, this show promises a fresh and unforgettable delight for all ages. Don't miss this mesmerizing family spectacle that will leave you spellbound. Book your tickets today and enter an enchanted realm where dreams do come true — through the wonder and joy of bubbles!

title: Lenny Sherman: One Of The Good Guys
description: Charming Cockney geezer Lenny Sherman THINKS he’s one of the good guys. But the reality is the 6’4”, 18 stone, scary, intimidating, inappropriate, doolally, ex con, is blind to his flaws and thinks his crazy behaviour is normal... And that’s where the comedy lies. The big lovable rogue has a good heart and means well. He’s doing his best to grow and become more progressive, but cor blimey! He ain’t half got a long way to go! “Power-packed performance jammed full of gags – a real blast.” - Chortle. “Deft Joke Writer” -Loaded Magazine. “Laughed my t*ts off!” - Irvine Welsh. As seen on BBC1, ITV, C4, Dave & Apple TV. As heard on Radio 4.

title: Leslie Bloom Solves a Murder
description: Welcome all members of the Toddlingham neighbourhood watch to our AGM and gala fundraiser. Top of the agenda this year is.. MURDER. "The Watch" treasurer and all round good egg, Tony Rose lies dead, but whodunit and why? Let Leslie Bloom of “Toddle Watch Murder Division” guide you through the clues, suspects and red herrings as she attempts to solve this unexplained death in her own unique way. Expect incriminations, interrogations and possibly even something silly. Everyone is under suspicion, even you. Combining crafted comedy, pure silliness and whimsical improv, Leslie Bloom Solves a Murder is an hour of joyful laughter that celebrates the detective in us all. Leslie's alter-ego, performer Simon Topping, is an improviser and character comedian; founder of Extra Topping Comedy in 2022 and part of sketch duo Topping and Hards. “With his very funny antics he combines physical comedy, clowning and authenticity”. ★★★★★ (Reviews Hub)

title: Lessons from Teacher x
description: Cam is a hopeless romantic, a secondary school teacher falling for a handsome stranger. Crystal is an online model, punishing men for money. One has pupils, the other only fans. But they share a shameful secret... Barbara Smith (EastEnders) brings 15 unique characters to life in a gripping new play from Erin De Frias and Dorsetborn. Lessons from Teacher is a gripping new play about love, suspicion, and the warring roles we play in adult life. Who do you trust to see the real you? “A fun and contemporary take on women’s sexuality, with questions on desire, male attention and sex’s part in the everyday” - Theatre 503 Age recommendation: 14+

title: Liberace and Liza - A Tribute
description: Liberace & Liza Minnelli never performed together, but that didn’t stop David Saffert & Jillian Snow from creating this hysterical and wildly successful mashup of two over-the-top icons. Saffert’s dizzying piano skills and Snow’s powerhouse belt team up in Cabaret, New York, New York, Over the Rainbow to surprise covers by Lady Gaga and Britney Spears. There are as many laughs as there are sequins when their Liberace & Liza take hilarious (and sometimes mischievous) turns vying for the spotlight. No matter what, they both come out winning and audiences are left in awe, humming and quoting a night they’ll not soon forget.

title: Lidl Class
description: Courtney thought moving to England would be a breeze, considering she is from Canada and speaks the same language? Or do we? Mad isn't mad, and it isn't oregano, it's oreGAWno. Courtney takes us on a journey through her isolation, rejection sensitivity and homesickness while she tries to navigate a class system coming from a country that has no class, trying to de-code passive aggressiveness all while raising a an ND family, ageing and finding new love-the budget version of course.

title: Life Would be Pretty Dull Without Sex, Raves and MDMA
description: Winners of the 2025 Keeping it Fringe Edinburgh Award. Bex Wall is bringing her 'Powerhouse' show to Brighton Sex? Yes please. Raves? Absolutely MDMA? Just a dabble. Relive the power of 90s raves Featuring iconic tracks by dance legends Orbital, this play takes the audience on a hilarious deep-dive into Bex’s world.... Bex is a woman in her 40's struggling with life's expectations in a twist of fate she rejects safe and abandons herself to life. Consequences be damned she embraces a reality that’s messing exhilarating, cruel and wild. Be prepared to be moved by the beat, embrace pumping bodies and welcome that baseline in with this highly personal yet universal play. This story comes from somewhere real, says Bex Wall. It’s about the weird, unspoken places grief takes us—and how music, madness and human connection can carry us through. Bex’s stories are unhinged, unfiltered and bloody hilarious. - says Phil Hartnoll, Orbital

title: LIGHTHOUSE
description: It’s not just the past that haunts you. From the producers of recent Edinburgh Fringe 5 ★ smash-hits "Death, the Devil and the Fablemaker" and "Blue Morpho", comes another stormy night to remember. Set in Caillte Lighthouse off the coast of Angus in the North Sea just after WW1, the story follows two keepers and their training of a third before things occur that question their loyalty, friendship and ultimately, their sanity. LIGHTHOUSE is a gripping twist of a piece that delves into the human psyche of trust, paranoia and greed. With stylish, well-placed humour, jeopardy, suspense and a stellar cast, be prepared to see this sensational piece take the UK by storm. Listed by The Herald as one of the "Top 20 Must-See Shows of EdFringe 2022", LIGHTHOUSE will take you on a 55 minute journey to hell and back like no other. "Rock solid performances and a great sense of atmosphere go a long way to illuminate this new murder mystery thriller!" - The Scotsman

title: Like Lovers Do
description: Like Lovers Do – An Acoustic Celebration of Annie Lennox and Eurythmics is a bold, stripped-back take on one of pop’s most iconic catalogues. Award-winning performer Paul Diello and his band rework classic Eurythmics and Annie Lennox songs into powerful acoustic arrangements. Driven by rich three-part harmonies, the show brings a fresh edge to familiar favourites, revealing new textures, emotion, and intensity beneath the originals. Focused, dynamic, and musically sharp, Like Lovers Do honours the spirit of Eurythmics while giving the songs space to breathe, surprise, and hit hard in a live setting.

Driven by rich three-part harmonies, the show brings a fresh edge to familiar favourites, revealing new textures, emotion, and intensity beneath the originals.

Focused, dynamic, and musically sharp, Like Lovers Do honours the spirit of Eurythmics while giving the songs space to breathe, surprise, and hit hard in a live setting.

title: Lil Wenker: BOYKING
description: ALL HAIL BOYKING! Eight-year-old Prince Wilfred (“Willy” for short) seeks power at all costs including but not limited to: death, destruction & dastardly deeds. Nowhere near in line for the throne, Prince Wilfred ruminates on a plan for total world domination while guzzling Mummy’s Milk. Bow down—this coronation’s a killer. Created by the award-winning performer-director duo Lil Wenker and Cecily Nash. Through the classic combination of clown, bouffon, and musical theatre, this brand-new comedy by Lil Wenker and Cecily Nash explores humankind's insatiable thirst for power through the eyes of a supposedly wronged prince.

title: LINE DANCE EXTRAVAGANZA
description: Line Dance Extravaganza: The Ultimate Social Dance Experience! Simple steps. Big energy. One unforgettable party! Get ready to kick up your heels and hit the dance floor! This social Line Dancing party is taking festivals by storm! The best part? No partner, no experience and no pressure! The moves are simple, the music is infectious, its fun, social and a great night out! Laughter, and great times on the dance floor! Our easy-to-follow dances are perfect for everyone, whether it's your first time or you've danced before. It’s the ideal way to enjoy a social, lively night out with friends, meet new people, and experience the joy of country line dancing. So grab your cowboy boots, we can’t wait to get the party started and make this an unforgettable experience! Bring a hat or buy one at the event - we even have a cowboy hat station where you can get glitzed and glammed – because what’s a country party without a little sparkle?

title: Locomotive For Murder: The improvised Whodunnit
description: Welcome aboard Locomotive for Murder: The Improvised Whodunnit, the totally improvised, totally hilarious murder mystery, where killing the cast and cracking the case is in your hands! Offie-nominated Edinburgh Fringe favourites, Pinch Punch, are bringing their five-star, sell-out show back to Brighton Fringe for a fifth year running! So, don your deerstalkers and oil your moustaches for a night of shaky alibis, silver-tongued sleuths, thrills, spills, and good old-fashioned kills. Four characters board a train, but not everyone will survive... Luckily, a world-famous detective is on board, ready to root out the killer. But who is the murderer? Only one person knows—the murderer themselves! Can you put your wits to the test and solve the case? Using real-life audience stories and suggestions, watch Pinch Punch create a unique, one-of-a-kind, never-to-be-repeated whodunnit. If you love comedy or murder mysteries, then this is the show for you!

title: Lollipops
description: Lollipops is a short, impactive comedy play telling the story of a night out that we all know too well. Two young women. Best friends, flatmates, frequent schemers. Together, they are taking on all the trials and tribulations of a big night out. Fresh off the back of a break-up, Pops is looking for a good time, meanwhile Rubes has some demons she must conquer along the way. Join them as they embark on a clubbing adventure, helped by an intricate game plan, strategic tequila shots, and a sweet treat for the road. Can these besties secure the night out of their hopes and dreams, or are they destined for a big fat cry in the toilets? Tricera Collective is a new Birmingham based theatre company, composed of young artists making contemporary, relatable theatre. The company writes, produces and performs original plays offering a comedic insight into the lives of young, creative people. Tricera makes their Brighton debut with Lollipops, a play to make you laugh, cry, think, laugh again!

title: Lolyamorous: Speed-Dating Comedy • Saturday
description: First Dates meets chaotic comedy chat show in this hilarious, unscripted hour of on-stage matchmaking. Put your name in a bucket to have a 2-minute date on stage with another random audience member. When time’s up, we throw to our panel including two comedians and professional Love Coach, Cate Mackenzie (The Undatables - Channel 4) to chat about your love lives, how the date went and if we all hear wedding bells. Participation is voluntary and all relationship statuses, orientations, genders and ages (18+) are welcome. Not single? No problem. Watch from the crowd and watch the sparks fly. This show is presented in association with WhiteGlo.

title: Lost Chance Saloon
description: Yehaaa Pardners! We is opening those Saloon doors wide for a fine time of dancing and cavorting! So come on in all you cowgirls and cowpokes and wranglers. Forget about your saddle sores & lasso yourself a whisky. We’ll have kick ass live music from: Transformer with naughty dancefloor infused party filth Hot Face hitting hard with fun garage punk rock energy Polite Bureaux grabbing your feet with their rawkus electro punk poetry We’ll have dancing girls and fine cabaret acts like Would You Rather?, Teeny Turner and Team Mud that’ll make you spit out yer firewater. We’ll have a charity casino run by the Wonky Trolley with Blackjack and Roulette earning much needed gold for the food banks of Brighton. And we got those gunslingers; The Lost Chance DJs, on the run from Glastonbury’s naughty Corner. We got Mr DJ Bollocks, Bushy Wred, DJ Rocksome & Wreksome & old man DJ Byrne. So party away & dress mighty fine. This a respectable place for a knees up and we gonna have a humdinger!

title: Lost Voice Guy - Talk in Progress
description: Britain’s Got Talent winner Lost Voice Guy is back with a brand new show in the making. Join him as he tries out fresh material, messes about with half-baked ideas, and probably says things his iPad will regret later. Expect sharp observations, silly stories, and a healthy dose of mischief as he tests new jokes about life with a Geordie voice, technology that talks back, and the joy of being disabled in a world that still can’t quite cope. It’s a rare chance to see him building his next tour show from the ground up. If it goes wrong, he’ll just blame autocorrect.

title: Lovett
description: Newly-widowed Mrs Lovett needs to find a way to survive. Using skills learned from a whaler father, a courtesan mother and a butcher husband, Eleanor “recalibrates” her life - and her relationship with God. An origin story set before her infamous partnership with Sweeney Todd, we discover what would later lead her to turn pie-maker. You may think you already know Mrs. Lovett, but this new play explores what made her: the brutal choices and disappointments of a life in poverty. When the difference is life or death, morality becomes a moveable feast - and Eleanor is getting hungry... BoonDog Theatre is a multi award-winning collaborative theatre company based in the Midlands, creating exciting new character driven stories that start conversations that last long beyond the audience leaving the theatre. Their work is darkly comic, aims for the heart, and what they describe as “covertly political”. BoonDog Theatre are proud to be Associate Artists of the Royal and Derngate, Northampton.

description: Join multi award-winning sketch duo/astronauts Low Effort Sketches as they prepare for a dangerous space mission. On the launchpad, Andy starts feeling the pressure, leaving Alice to contend with a string of unlikely characters hell-bent on scuppering the mission. Will they ever make it to space? T-minus 50 minutes until launch... ‘Hilarious’ (Chortle), fourth-wall breaking sketch comedy with ‘a fantastic sense of surreal mischief’ (Spectator). title: Maddie HW: Gaylord
description: GAYLORD is the electric UK debut from award-winning Australian comedian Maddie HW, fresh off sold-out tours across Australia and rapidly making her mark on the London comedy circuit. Known for her high-octane energy and inventive style, Maddie blends stand-up and physical comedy into a fast-paced hour about moving countries, navigating relationships, and making fun of those silly British accents (sorry). Described as “charming and vivacious” (Festmag) , Maddie takes the everyday and flips it into something surreal, bold, and hilarious.

title: MaestroClass
description: Every performance begins in silence. And it falls to one person, one pair of hands, to call the music into being, to shape it, and finally let it return to the desolation of silence. Meet Maestro, a conducting one-of-a-kind, recently returned from the world's greatest concert halls. Part clown show, part musical experiment! Now he faces his most unpredictable ensemble yet - you! MaestroClass is an hour filled with play, fun and (a little) audience interaction where harmony and chaos are equally possible. Maestro has a vision. Whether his students share it is another matter entirely. Created and performed by Norwegian actor and clown Karl Magnus Moshuus, the show has already delighted audiences at Glasgow International Comedy Festival, Brighton Fringe, and Edinburgh Fringe last year. Now returning for it's second year at Brighton Fringe! Come unleash your inner virtuoso!

title: Maja Bloom and Sudeepta Sanyal (Work In Progress)
description: Two female comics, Sudeepta and Maja, combine forces to perform a powerful WIP at Brighton Fringe: They are 40, Foreign and Ferocious. Maja Bloom is a multilingual actress, singer and stand-up comedian best known for playing Carrow in the Fantastic Beasts franchise. She has a loyal and growing fanbase (including at least one Oscar winner who showed up at her show unannounced). Calling herself "the Foot Putter" (title of her debut solo show) because of her knack for saying the unexpected, she charms audiences with her frank takes on society as a Brit who never feels quite British enough. Maja's fresh comedic style is the one to watch. Sudeepta Sanyal is a stand-up comedian based in London. Runners up at the West end New Old Act 2025, she is also a part of the SOHO Theatre Comedy Labs plus 2026. She is a cohort of Brown Sauce X Tara Theatre 2025. Her stand-up is rooted in cultural confusion and emotional truths, delivered with honesty, with zero interest in being someone she is not.

title: MAN!FEST: A Boy Band Fantasy
description: A drag musical comedy about your new favourite boyband trying to survive their second world tour. Join Ash Trey, Cody Pendant, Ruben Cox, and Ewan May as they attempt to tackle the threat of female empowerment and the maturation of their tweenage fanbase, while meeting the demands of the all-powerful ‘Management’. MANIFEST tackles toxic masculinity and the corporate capitalisation of young women through over-produced pop songs and strictly regimented choreography. Following their acclaimed debut at Brighton and Edinburgh Fringe with 60 Minutes of Mood Swings in 2023 and 2024 and their role on Chichester Festival Theatre’s Artist Development Programme in 2025, OnTheNose returns...

title: Mandy Sweats Presents: The Hole Truth
description: After a two sold-shows back in October, the papier-mâché menace Mandy Sweats brings their debut theatrical show back for Brighton Fringe. In ‘The Hole Truth’, hysteria mounts as a horrifying figure haunts the toilets and plug holes across a ruthless city. The figure in question is Mandy – and trapped in their little hotel room, you have no choice but to watch them re-enact the shocking, perverse, and bizarre things they have seen. Expect murderous babies, singing dogs, bloody crime scenes and one very erotic piece of steak. A blend of surreal horror and sketch comedy in the vein of David Firth and The League of Gentlemen, Mandy Sweats Presents: The Hole Truth is a brand new breed of theatrical terror.

title: Mark Daniels: That's Terrifying. Can We Brunch Now?
description: 81% of millennials say they can't afford a mid-life crisis, but So You Think You’re Funny finalist, Mark Daniels, is asking... what if they’re just pretending they aren't having one? And what if they’re just not trying hard enough to have one on a budget? He’s found brilliant ways of coping with a whole range of existential anxieties from environmental apocalypse to the replacement of humankind by AI, with simple things, like bottomless brunch and ridiculous jackets. And he doesn’t blame himself – why didn’t our school days prepare us for this sh*t instead of teaching us non-stop about Henry the f*cking Eighth? Following his sell-out Brighton Fringe show Acute Britishness, this late-30-something queer comedian is back delivering his satirical, self-deprecating wit about all sorts of important topics from AI to Shakespeare to the Sugababes. It’s a show for anyone who’s ever spent £12 on smashed avocado on toast and thought... wow... what in the God’s f*ck am I doing with my life?

title: Mark Row: A Class Act
description: Teacher turned comedian Mark Row has had enough of the endless carousel of uninspiring lessons, inane questions and kids with attitudes that stink worse than their PE kits. But will a new life in the limelight prove to be any better? Bringing together the best bits from his two previous shows 'A* in the Making' and 'Don't You Know Who I Am?' this show demonstrates why Mark Row doesn't need to stay in the classroom to prove that he's a class act! You really need to give up teaching and focus on the comedy - Joe Pasquale You really need to give up comedy and focus on the teaching - OFSTED

title: Mark Row: Every Day's A School Day
description: They say that every day's a school day, which is never more true than when you're a teacher stuck in the classroom with no obvious means of escape. Fortunately, Mark Row has discovered that it's not just the kids that can learn something valuable, as he reflects on the lessons he's learned along the way that have kept him out of trouble! Whether it's the good advice he was given by the wise people in his life, or the mistakes that he has realised never should be repeated, there is one thing that always rings true - if you're not learning, you're laughing! Razor sharp, totally original. Had the whole room howling from start to finish. Comedy that speaks to everyone! - Chuckl

title: Mat Smith and Kieran Carr Spilt Bill
description: Mat Smith (as heard on BBC, Komedia New Comedian semi-finalist) and Kieran Carr (2023 NZ Comedy Guild Awards, Leicester Square Theatre New Comedian 2025 semi-finalist) are back at Brighton Fringe with an hour of brand new standup. Kieran has performed in the UK and in New Zealand. He was a staple on the Auckland scene there, winning an award at the 2023 NZ Comedy Guild Awards. Since returning, he performs around the country at Glee Club Birmingham, West End Comedy Club, Backyard Comedy Club and last year's Edinburgh Fringe. Mat is an award-winning comedy writer who has been performing comedy since 2022. He has had material featured on BBC Radio 4 and BBC Scotland. Described as “precise, direct and unwavering”, he's performed at Hot Water, Komedia, The Bill Murray, Backyard and many more. He's also a bad bisexual with a black belt in judo but no driver's license.

title: MEAT
description: After a sell out tour in 2025, MEAT comes to Brighton fringe; a brilliantly bold, tour-de-force performance, hilariously funny, profoundly emotional, a powerhouse production. In an age of patriarchy, where women’s bodies are commodified objects, MEAT is an electrifying roar of fury, a rallying cry of protest, a unifying celebration of strength packed with heroism & heart. Shame breeds in silence, this show gives a safe space to roar, for anyone who’s got a body. Follow ‘A girl’ from age 9 into her 30s, detailing her journey through puberty, misogyny, and identity. A solo performer, but a story that is all of ours. Based on verbatim accounts. MEAT is a playful and poignant exploration of identity. A dirty dive into the foundations of power, an injection of a new narrative. A theatrical hybrid of poetry, clowning, cabaret and dance featuring original music written by multi-award winner Elle Dillon-Reams.

title: Melody Mermaid in the Jungle
description: Come on a jungle adventure with 'Melody Mermaid' from 'Ukeboxbaby' and her 'Ukeboxbaby' friends. There will be chances to play the ukulele on stage, giant balloons, ribbon sticks, water guns, bubbles and more. In addition, at this show, Melody will invite you to play along to her songs as an audience, for the first time, so if you have any shakers, ukuleles or any instrument at home, please bring them along. Pls note this is optional. This show is designed to inspire children to enjoy the magic of music by learning an instrument or singing. Music is proven to be so good for children's cognitive development - even more so in today's screen-time heavy world. 'Melody Mermaid' has starred in 'Ukeboxbaby' shows on main stages at Camp Bestival, Latitude Festival, Elderflower Fields Festival, in Soho Beach House, at Komedia Brighton and many more. 'Melody Mermaid' from 'Ukeboxbaby' is a believer that music is the strongest form of magic. She has also launched a ukulele story kid's book.

title: Michael Brunström: William Tell versus the Algorithm
description: Medieval Swiss folklore meets Artificial Intelligence in this surreal version of the William Tell legend from incomparable absurdist Michael Brunström. Can the hunter and marksman William Tell take on the might of Austrian Imperialism (AI)? Down with tyranny! Death to the algorithm! Freedom for Switzerland! Michael Brunström is a surrealist comedian based in London. He has created numerous full-length solo shows, including “Parsley”, “The Great Fire of London” and “World of Sports”. His show “Copernicus Now” was nominated for the 2024 Best Show Award at the Leicester Comedy Festival. He’s a winner of the Malcolm Hardee Award for Comic Originality and a member of the Weirdos Comedy Collective.

description: New York-raised and now London-based, Mike Capozzola has performed in most of the USA and in 25 countries across the UK, Europe and Middle East at comedy clubs, theatres, comedy festivals, JCC's, synagogues, Hillels, Chabads, science museums, Jewish Community Centers and two Kosher breweries. He's the son of two educators: a Jewish mother and an Italian-American father. title: Mile Die Club
description: Your evening begins with a flight. As the Brighton i360 lifts you 450ft above the city, fizz flows, the view is breath-taking... and something is very wrong. You’ll descend into the i360 dining room for an indulgent three-course dinner, where the suspects mingle between the tables. Between courses, you’ll interrogate them, challenge their alibis, uncover clues and compare notes with your fellow guests. And before the final course is cleared, you’ll need to decide: who deserves to be exposed as a murderer? This is not theatre you watch. This is a crime you solve. What’s included: A premium three-course dinner An immersive, story-led murder mystery experience Exclusive ascent on the Brighton i360 Live interaction with a professional cast Fizz on arrival and wine with the meal A final reveal — and bragging rights if you get it right Perfect for thrill-seekers, special occasions, corporate entertaining, or anyone who believes dinner should come with a body count.

title: Mindful Ki & Aikido Taster Free Drop In Taster Sessions
description: Join the Brighton Ki Society for an afternoon of Free mindful Ki and Aikido taster sessions with UK Chief Instructor Bob MacFarland Rokyudan, Full Ki Lecturer(Awarded Ki Society HQ, Japan). Please arrive by 2:15 pm, wearing loose, comfortable clothing such as a tracksuit or gi, for the start of the Shin Shin Toitsu Do (Japanese yoga) session at 2:30 pm, where you will learn the unification of mind and body, natural movement, meditation, and breathing. At 3:30 pm, join the SST Aikido workshop to apply Ki principles for harmonious self-defence, health, and self-development. From 4:30 pm to 5pm, there will be a Ki and Aikido demonstration and a Q&A session. For further information telephone Bob on 07769588938.

title: Miss Havisham's Clothes Swap
description: Miss Havisham requests your presence at an evening devoted to the exchange of garments and the offering of sartorial wisdom. Confined to her gently rotting bridal attire for a mere 200 years, she now contemplates (oh the horror) getting dressed. Having heard speak of a modern affliction named “fast fashion,” she seeks enlightenment. You are therefore required to bring one item of clothing that you are willing to exchange at that curious altar of cast off selves we call 'fashion'. Inspired by Dickens iconic character, this is an interactive show about getting dressed. The joy of self-expression is set against the impact of global supply chains. And yes, there will be an actual clothes swap. Created and performed by Edinburgh Fringe First Award winner Helen Tennison, supported by Stanley Arts, London Metropolitan University and Actors of Dionysus.
description_html:
Miss Havisham requests your presence at an evening devoted to the exchange of garments and the offering of sartorial wisdom. Confined to her gently rotting bridal attire for a mere 200 years, she now contemplates (oh the horror) getting dressed.

Having heard speak of a modern affliction named “fast fashion,” she seeks enlightenment. You are therefore required to bring one item of clothing that you are willing to exchange at that curious altar of cast off selves we call 'fashion'.

Inspired by Dickens iconic character, this is an interactive show about getting dressed. The joy of self-expression is set against the impact of global supply chains. And yes, there will be an actual clothes swap.

Created and performed by Edinburgh Fringe First Award winner Helen Tennison, supported by Stanley Arts, London Metropolitan University and Actors of Dionysus.

title: Momma's Got a Brand New Toy Kitchen
description: She's peopled her last please, now she's figuring out hey ‘why can’t she be desired’? Why can’t she be naughty? Why can’t she be made out of HDPE plastic? Her exterior’s hard plastic but inside she's stagnant air from 1991. Bex can’t hide behind a toy kitchen mask anymore... she's 5ft8. A fragile woman made entity! Whose masquerading as someone whose normal. But not really. Instead of sharp edges Bex has soft curves, a sturdy base and a small rack. Who is she but a toy kitchen? Join whimsical and (sometimes scary) comedian Bex Turner for a (mostly) stand-up show of weird tales, Autism and lots of crude Crudités! Bex Turner is a high energy, eccentric, surreal, stand up/character comic. Her likeability combined with her comedic sense of adventure makes audiences love her. She was a 2018 Quarter Finalist at Leicester Square New Comedian of the Year and a 2015 Semi Finalist for Laughing Horse New Act of the Year. "certainly very funny was Bex Turner" - Chortle

title: Monarchs Anonymous
description: What happens when you force Henry VIII, Marie Antoinette, and history's most chaotic royals into group therapy? A hysterical historical mash-up where Kings clash, therapy endures, and laughter reigns supreme. The Monarchs are not amused. Marie is moping, Mansa Musa’s in a mood, and Tutankhamun has tottered off back to his mummy. Worse still, someone has leaked their secrets to the press! Can our long-suffering therapist, Dr. Thompson, stop the session from descending into chaos? Pull up a throne and come face-to-face with the biggest egos of the last 600 years. They are unfit to rule, unwilling to change, and quite frankly, a right royal pain in the past. Audiences are raving: “A riotous hoot! Like a history class with your favourite comedians!” “Hilarious, emotive, thought-provoking – a history lover's dream!” “Funny, original and plain bonkers!” Join us for a royal appointment you won’t forget. No time machine required! Note: the show will run for 90 mins without an interval

title: Monologue!
description: The story is a façade. The energy is real. Fresh from the vibrant contemporary scene of Tehran, "MONOLOGUE!" makes its UK debut after a highly successful run at the iconic Molavi Hall. On the surface, it begins as a crime narrative told across six episodic fragments. But don’t be fooled. This is not traditional storytelling; it is a post-modern rebellion against structure. Starring Arshia Etemadi as "The Actor," the performance blurs the terrifyingly thin line between a scripted character and a living human being on stage. Clidook Group invites you to witness a raw experiment where no two nights are ever the same. Performed in Persian with English Surtitles—until the improvisation breaks the rules, shifting into English and universal physical language.
