title: Monster
description: 'Monster' is a 'hilarious' and joyful spoken word show by Naomi Wood (Gobbess) for anyone who has been afraid to dip their toe in or felt all at sea. A tentacled tale of myth and monstrosity about feeling exposed and learning to love both people and pond life. A ‘highly relatable’ show that will leave you feeling ‘seen’. This is a show about the transformative power of chucking yourself in the deep end.

title: Mr Dodger's Drama Class
description: Be part of the classroom in this interactive parody drama lesson, leaving you at the mercy of Mr Dodger. Solo debut from Biscuit Barrel founder James Horscroft, David Nobbs Comedy Award finalist and Brighton Fringe Spirit of the Fringe Award 2025 nominee. As seen at Edinburgh Fringe, Hollywood Fringe, and Leicester Comedy Festival, with this show marking his sixth year at Brighton Fringe. Featuring the most unhinged drama games you've ever witnessed or, if you're brave enough, participated in. All led by an intensely disciplined performing arts teacher, desperate to make a rundown community college feel like a top-end drama school. Based on a character who's made numerous appearances in "Biscuit Barrel: The 69-Sketch Show", the Dodger is now rolling out of the Barrel and into the classroom.

title: Mr Sleepybum's Dream Machine
description: Winner Best Kids Show – Leicester Comedy Festival/UK Kids Comedy Festival 2025 Mr Sleepybum is sad. He can’t dream. To solve this problem, he invents a machine that magically brings dreams to life, featuring dinosaurs, robots, car chases, the world's largest blanket, and much more! This award-winning interactive show is filled with sketches, slapstick, and silliness, ensuring maximum fun for the whole family. Mr Sleepybum is a critically acclaimed kids comedy show created by character comedian Jody Kamali. Mr Sleepybum burst onto the scene in 2023 with a sell-out run at the Edinburgh Fringe. He has since toured the UK, appearing at theatres and festivals, which included a 12-show off-West End run. The Dream Machine is the much-awaited sequel show.

title: Murder at the Manor
description: There’s been a murder at the manor! Whodunnit? Was it the suspicious butler? The flirtatious French maid? Or perhaps... Truffles the guinea pig?! After four sell-out performances at the 2025 Brighton Fringe, Murderers Anonymous are back in the middle of their 2026 tour with this original slapstick farcical comedy play. Follow Detective Fred Herring and the eccentric Day family as their perfectly pleasant evening spirals into chaos when someone is (GASP!) MURDERED! Described as “Fawlty Towers meets Agatha Christie meets the game Cluedo” (Brighton on the Inside) and “a hilarious, fast-paced comedy that will have you howling with laughter!” ★★★★★ (Reviewing Brighton) - this is a murder mystery seriously off the rails! It would be a crime to miss it!

title: Murder! A Live Traitors Experience
description: Welcome to Murder!, Brighton’s live Traitors-style experience. Take part in a game of deception, deduction, and betrayal, where nothing is as it seems and trust is fragile. The goal is simple: survive. But it won’t be easy. Some players are Traitors, secretly murdering the Innocents, while the Innocents must uncover the hidden Traitors and stop them before it’s too late. Strangers can quickly become close allies, and your friends might turn out to be your worst enemies. Along the way you’ll complete devious challenges, uncover clues to the Traitors’ identities, and fight to stay alive as the murders unfold - all while enjoying free popcorn! You’ll take part in tense debates at the round table to vote off players you suspect are Traitors. Those who remain at the end will be up for winning our grand prize! Could you get away with murdering your friends? Think you’re the next great detective? Come play Murder! WITH FREE POPCORN FOR ALL PLAYERS!

title: Music Box the Improvised (Comedy) Musical
description: Step into a world where nothing is planned and everything is possible. Music Box takes your suggestions and turns them into a brand new, completely improvised comedy musical - songs, plot twists, dodgy choreography and all! No two shows are ever the same but whatever happens, it’s made up on the spot, powered by your ideas and our questionable decision-making. title: Musica Moderna: Monteverdi. Moving Fast and Breaking Things
description: Founded in 1971, Brighton Consort is a local a cappella choir of around 20 singers. We specialise in 'Early Music' (1400s–1600s), with a large focus on the Renaissance period. With charitable status, we aim to make the music of the past accessible to all, performing several concert programmes each year and hosting public workshops. The choir is led by Greg Skidmore, a renowned expert in Renaissance music. This is our first time at Brighton Fringe! We'll be bringing to you the works of Italian composer Claudio Monteverdi. At the turn of the seventeenth century, Monteverdi challenged the strict conventions of Renaissance choral music, and embracing dramatic, text-driven expression. In this programme, Brighton Consort traces the creative tension between tradition and innovation, exploring how Monteverdi’s revolutionary approach reshaped music and paved the way for the Baroque era.

title: My Date with Pierce Brosnan
description: Love life leaving you bored and unfulfilled? Want to be swept off your feet by the sexiest clown in Paris? For one lucky puppet this wish will come true! Mademoiselle Cafetière is lonely and looking for love, until she meets the man of her dreams: Hollywood actor Pierce Brosnan. There's just one catch: Pierce Brosnan is a talking dummy controlled by the audience. Grab your phone and scan the QR code to join in - every message you type in will be spoken aloud by this lucky dummy. Will he win the heart of our gorgeous heroine before midnight chimes and the spell is broken? Anything can happen in this outrageous love story where you pull all the strings!

title: My Neighbor Totoro Ghibli Taste & Tales
description: Step into a world of enchantment at Blossoms Cocktail Bar in the heart of the Lanes for an unforgettable evening of cinema and cuisine. Join us for an immersive experience as we bring the magic of Studio Ghibli’s beloved 'My Neighbour Totoro' to life like never before. As you settle in to watch this heart-warming classic, each scene will be paired with dishes inspired by the film's charming rural landscapes and whimsical characters. From fresh, vibrant meals to sweet treats, every bite will transport your taste buds straight into the world of Totoro and Mei, as we serve up food that’s as delightful as the story itself. Whether you're a die-hard Studio Ghibli fan or simply seeking a one-of-a-kind night out, this event promises to be a sensory journey that celebrates both the magic of cinema and the joy of food. Grab your spot for a truly magical evening where film and flavour meet. Seats are limited, so don’t miss your chance to be part of this exclusive experience at Blossoms.

title: Nadav Tabak - Live Organic Trance
description: Experience the electrifying world of Nadav Tabak, the guitarist and composer who first made waves with Opal Ocean and now unleashes a solo show bursting with energy, emotion and pure musical wizardry. Armed with an array of instruments, a looping pedal and fearless creativity, Nadav blends acoustic organic trance, tribal rhythms, exotic melodies and fiery Spanish-rumba techniques into a genre-bending sonic journey. Every performance is improvised, immersive and intensely personal, a hypnotic soundscape that evolves in real time. He has captivated crowds at festivals worldwide and shared stages with icons like Manu Chao, Hilight Tribe and many more Get ready for a performance that’s explosive, intimate and unlike anything you’ve heard.

title: Narrative Songwriting Workshop - Friends, Families and Communities
description: We are all exposed to a myriad of anecdotes and stories – overheard at bus stops, told at the dinner table, past on through families and communities. These can be a rich mine of material to help us explore feelings, motivations, relationships. Stories of specific people can be a way in for others to reflect on their own experiences – love stories, overcoming adversity and tales of adventure. In this workshop we will look at turning these stories into songs. How do we keep an eye out for anecdotes and make sure we capture then? What are some of the conventions of folk, rock and pop songs? How do we distil some of the ideas that come out, making them into ear catching hooks and choruses? Phil Jones is an experienced workshop facilitator and the writer behind Fringe production, “Snow Along the Line”, a multimedia show about the life of a woman born in 1928 and the life and times she lived through. Bring pads, instruments and any photographs.

title: Natal Attraction
description: The debut solo show from the creator of THE MOTHERHOOD PROJECT (Battersea Arts Centre) ★★★★ The I Paper ★★★★ The Stage ★★★★★ West End Best Friend Winner of the Applause Create Tour Connect Bursary 2026 “I want to spend my forties eating poppadoms in my knickers, not pretending to care that someone went down a slide.” Natal Attraction is a wickedly funny solo show about motherhood, “barrenhood,” and the expectation that women should want to nurture at all costs. Through five vividly drawn women from across the UK, the show rips into what happens when desire, doubt, and duty collide with the ticking clock. Mischievous, sharp, and quietly resonant, Natal Attraction shines a light on the messy, contradictory realities of the female experience - and asks who we’re really trying to please, and why.

title: Natter
description: Natter is an award-winning comedy set in 1980s Salford - following the trials and tribulations of Helen and Linda, two 'old women' who spend their time grappling with useless husbands, a homosexual son and, most importantly, what's on telly! (Helen & Linda may, or may not, be played by two gay men in drag!) Winning Best Comedy at Manchester Fringe 2024. Natter is written by, and stars, award-winning artist, Joe Henry-Evans (Best Newcomer, Manchester Fringe 2021) alongside John Thacker, and directed by Qweerdog's artistic director, Stewart Campbell (Jump, Brighton Fringe 2024). Natter was also nominated for the Write for the Stage Award, Canal St LGBTQ+ Award, as well as the Audience Choice Award at GM Fringe. Join us to find out if Helen is having it off with the vicar; just what links the Didsbury mafia with Linda's gay son; and what exactly does Helen have against poor old Cilla Black!

title: NEDDY GOES TO GLASTO
description: Neddy Goes to Glasto is a high-voltage, one-woman rollercoaster through grief, sisterhood, and the magic of losing — and maybe finding — yourself in a field of strangers. Thirty years after her first Glastonbury, Neddy returns to celebrate her sister’s 50th birthday. The only problem? Her sister's dead. Determined to outrun her grief for one wild weekend, Neddy dives headfirst into a mud-soaked world of chaos, connection, and unlikely companions. And in the beating heart of Shangri-La, she stumbles across what might just be her salvation... Fierce, funny and gloriously unfiltered - Neddy Goes to Glasto is a riotous sprint through the festival of dreams.

title: Nerine Skinner: Decisions, Decisions (Work In Progress)
description: BGT Semi-Finalist, Nerine Skinner, has always been indecisive and needs your help. Ever since she was little making the smallest decision has been tricky and when you have so many ideas, how do you pick what to use? Especially when your mind questions everything! In 2023 Nerine came runner up in the Funny Women Awards and in 2024 took her debut show to Edinburgh, "Nerine Skinner: The Exorcism of Liz Truss" receiving 5-star reviews and a sold out run. Join Nerine in her second work in progress as she asks for your help to decide what's in her show. Stand up, songs and character comedy. This could change! Nerine's been featured on SKY News, BBC Radio 2,4,5, Richard Herring's RHLSTP, Laura Kuenssberg and Britain's Got Talent (The Dots) and was recently shortlisted as a writer for SNL UK. "A tumbling ball of invention and spontaneity" Jon Culshaw

title: NGJ FRESH SOUNDS SHOWCASE 2026
description: New Generation Jazz present our Fresh Sounds emergence showcase featuring JEMIMA WHYTE and OLI HOWE - two of the most exciting up and coming artists currently working in the space where jazz meets urban beats - both performing with their full live bands - with DJs warming up beforehand.. It’ll be a party like no other! Jemima Whyte is a saxophonist, vocalist and composer bringing an electrifying blend of northern dance culture, jazz, afrobeat, soul and retro warmth with her horn-heavy band laying down some irresistible grooves. She's been making a big impression with appearances at Love Supreme Festival and Jazz Re:freshed. Oli Howe is a pianist, composer, and producer. His unique take on slick and funky fusion has gained him millions of streams across three sensational albums, he's performed all around the world with sell-out shows at Ronnie Scott's and Jazz Cafe, sharing the stage with legends such as Billy Cobham, and his signature reharm videos are an insta sensation.

title: Nick Elleray: Don't Bring a Smile to a Frown Fight
description: 'Life-affirming', 'wildly physical' and 'apt to induce wonder in the viewer': these are all phrases Nick Elleray has used to describe Cirque de Soleil. Since exploding onto the London comedy scene in the first part of the second decade of the first century of the current millennium, Nick has been seen as a comedian's comedian, a niece's uncle and a landlord's tenant. Many attempt to follow the path he has trod. Most of them succeed as it's actually quite a well-trodden path with clear signs and good facilities. Yet only Nick has been regularly acknowledged as a standup comedian that consistently uses the name 'Nick Elleray'. Long may he continue to do so. In this show Nick will probably talk about biscuits, laziness and testosterone, amongst other things.

title: Nina's Show
description: Nina's Show is here for the first time (aka it is making its Work in Progress debut). Welcome to her world. A comedy variety show with some variety - though you can expect, more than anything else, some smiling and (likely) glitter. Happy-go-lucky meets constant confusion with Nina, a Midlands gal who started comedy in Brighton a few years ago. She's the winner of 'Boast in Show' and 'Boast in Show: Ultimate Champion', gaining the crown through her "planning and plotting [which] went beyond the nice and dangerously towards the obsessive". Today, she is here to entertain you with something we think is comedy and, ideally, will charm you along the way. Perhaps you'll need to stand up and join in. Perhaps not, as there might not be time. But BE PREPARED regardless! Come along if you can - I just thought you'd like it. ----- (Under 18s can attend Saturday's show at 4pm)

title: NIUSIA
description: Following a sell-out, Scotsman Fringe First award-winning Edinburgh premiere, the debut work from Filipa Bragança Award​​-nominated artist Beth Paterson makes its Brighton Fringe debut! Niusia was a Holocaust survivor. She saved people’s lives in the camps, set up a new life for her family as a refugee, and was an iridescent entertainer. But her granddaughter, Beth, only remembers an angry, dying woman. She's ready to learn her stories, but what she discovers is all the questions she didn't know existed (and wasn't allowed to ask). Through NIUSIA, Beth weaves together memories, handed-down stories and interviews to examine the precarity of identity and the haphazard cultural legacy second, third and fourth generation immigrants are handed. She asks: what does remembrance look like when all I remember is the space where questions should go?

title: No Show
description: The Crucible if it had dry humping meets Skins if it f***ed glee. (it isn’t a musical but someone might sing at some point). Press night, a living room. As the cast await the appraisal they start to unravel their own piece, with some realising it could be more controversial than first thought. Relationships are on the line, dynamics shift and drinks flow. Descending into chaos, these players start to question their morality and reality. Will the show ever be performed again? We’ll have to see what the reviews say. From the creators of sold out show RANK, this dark comedy with an ensemble cast will have you on your knees praying for an answer.

title: Nocturne Musical
description: East of the sun and West of the moon comes a tale of finding your courage and staying true to yourself. Nocturne is a new original musical created by composer and lyricist Hedda Rustad Carlsen, writer and art director Hannah Debansi and writer Leah Sperring. The musical is rooted in Norwegian folklore and folk music, filled with puppetry and beautiful set design, ready to transport you into the fairy-tale forest. Join our protagonist Solveig as she delves into the forest of ancient fairy tales, encountering familiar characters and dark mysteries. We invite you to get lost in the magical worlds and fables we often forget as we grow older. ★★★★★ "Every song had a lyric that sent shivers down the spine and unexpected comedy moments that entertained and delighted all who saw it. " - Theatre in Brighton (2025) ★★★★★ "If I had a magic flute of my own, I’d whistle up a ninety-minute version and a transfer to the West End." - The Wee Review (Edinburgh Fringe 2025)

title: Norman's Big Day Out
description: Norman is an unassuming, exhausted, and hapless man who charms the audience with his inability to perform mundane tasks and then wows them with extraordinary skill.​ Out to make a delivery the house is nowhere to be found, so with no other option he delivers to himself. Come see Norman and watch the ordinary become extraordinary as he explores his new home through a delightful mix of acrobatics and antics. Every day is different for Norman so you never know what he'll get up to, he might juggle his lunch, stand on his hands, flip through the air or even lasso a wild horse. There's more than enough adventure for everyone so come join Norman both in the audience and maybe even on stage for a show you'll never forget.

title: Not A Band Presents Theme-A-Geddon!
description: Not A Band return to the Brighton Fringe with their latest show " Theme-A-Geddon!" A joyous celebration of the golden age of 80's and 90's TV and Film Theme tunes, played live by a four piece band. Expect Knightrider, The A Team, Baywatch, Only Fools and Horses, Dangerzone and many many more. With costume changes, live visuals and lots of silliness, this isn't just a band, it's Not A Band! With special Fringe guests and surprises, this is a show not to be missed for any fan of 80's and 90's pop culture. "Possibly the greatest band ever" - Geoff Barrow (Portishead) "Brilliant, book em!" - Sean Williamson (Barry, Eastenders)

title: Not Another Quiz Night
description: After a hit run at the Edinburgh Fringe and moving to London's Clapham Grand Theatre, Not Another Quiz Night makes it's debut at the Brighton Fringe! Join Jake Bhardwaj and his team of ‘celebrity’ guests for the most chaotic, high-energy quiz show in town. This isn’t just a quiz – it’s a full-blown spectacular. Expect music, comedy, mayhem, interactive bonus rounds, ridiculous prizes, surprise appearances. Whether you're a quiz whiz or just here for the party, there's something for everyone. 'Consistently one of the most entertaining and mad things I’ve seen.’ Joe Lycett 'One of those rare shows that makes controlled chaos look effortless.' Jordan Gray

title: Now That's What I Call Bach!
description: Tasmin Sarkany (BBC New Comedy Awards Nominee 2025) plays Amelia Crotchet, a classical musician in a double act with her violin. She's about to play the most important concert of her career: a complete performance of Bach's Chaconne for solo violin - but unfortunately, Amelia is easily distracted. Expect silliness, musical puns, historically questionable facts about Bach and (if she gets round to it) some beautiful classical music.

title: Offgridland
description: Flight. Mode. The two most wonderful words...but even with his mobile blocking the outside world, can our desperate, overworked hero ever switch off? It's time to go to his favourite place: Offgridland. Just ignore the knocks on the door. And the constant ringing of his landline - a phone he can't even find because he hasn't used it since 2007. And that slightly worrying internal narrative that he's actually just living in a sitcom. And his fear that his car, which no longer has a handbrake, could come crashing through the walls at any moment. No, it's fine. It's all totally fine. Just give the guy a break, yeah? Just for an hour? A frantic, claustrophobic comedy play that blends theatre and stand-up, told in real time by multi-award winning writer/performer, Paul Richards, Offgridland is the true story of a man trying to switch off from the perils of technology and, you know, other people.

title: Old School Indie
description: Fancy dancing to some classic indie like you did back in the day? Then join Old School Indie, the U.K's biggest indie party (currently in 18 cities) for a night of floppy-fringed floor-fillers! Dig out your Gazelles & hit the floor to the best in indie from across the decades. You know the words, all you need to do is bring the moves! Whether you are a child of the New Wave, Britpopper, rock n roller or 00s sleazer, we have some tunes for you. Taking in the whole glorious gambit with one or two ska & northern soul classics thrown in for good measure. Brought to you by the people behind Feeling Gloomy & Afternoon of Indie, we've been putting on indie events for nearly 21 years in the U.K, America & Germany, played the likes of Glasto, Isle of Wight, Latitude, Camp Bestival, Green Man, even main stage at Knebworth (not that year...) & worked with the likes Jarvis, Cocker, Suede and Brett Anderson, are often on BBC Radio 6 & do loads of fun indie-related stuff. You are in safe hands.

title: On Barren Hill (Work In Progress)
description: A dreamlike, visually striking & ultimately hopeful Dance-Theatre show interrogating the relationship to the natural world-the tension between grief & growth-after infertility & loss. Bathe in a heady combination of movement, lyrical text, opera & projection. Devised in & celebrating diverse environments across Europe, join a cyclical journey across a lush Czech climax forest, barren Mediterranean cliff edge & harsh shingle dessert before plunging into an acrid silver lake. The work will feature co-created elements from creative workshops with women with lived experience of the themes. There will be a post-show discussion at each performance & a small display of photography & artwork relating to the show's development. Fertility Network UK will also be present. *Pine needle syrup cocktail/mocktail with each ticket* Intimate & unique 40 capacity venue- book ahead to avoid disappointment! Work in progress show with intention to tour. R&D supported by South East Dance

title: On Voids
description: Delilah Sykes is a multidisciplinary artist who primarily works in sound, text and installation. Sykes’ exhibition On Voids uses silence as a way to examine deep human truths. She builds intimate worlds that explore presence and non-presence as a way to understand love, loss, and the often amusing nature of our world. With On Voids, Sykes builds an archive of silence. Unlike most archives however, she fills it not with documents and artefacts, but with a collection of ‘nothings’. How else might we find evidence that we have lived? Sykes has a magpie's eye for stories, objects and histories that can tell us an often forgotten version of ourselves. With projected text, an assemblage of shells, sound, and artist film, she asks: What is silenced? What was never said? What might we have left behind?

title: One Foot in The Dark
description: The answers we are looking for are waiting on the other side of discomfort, challenging norms and stories from the past. One Foot In The Dark is an exploration of our known and unknown experiences of identity, culture and change through text and movement. It is an honest exploration of two artists understanding navigating through life, questions and unknowns. In a world where we are many things at once, how do we say what we need to, when words and bodies speak different languages? Drawing from their distinct practices, poetry and dance, Saili and Divija step into each other’s forms to navigate themes of migration, miscommunication, and belonging. The work reflects on what is lost in translation, and what can still be found when we reach across distance, culture, and discipline. Inspired by the idea of six degrees of separation, this is a search for home: not as a single place, but a shared journey, towards understanding, towards one another, towards a sense of return.

title: Opera Gala - Sussex Symphony Orchestra
description: A fantastic afternoon of operatic favourites with two fantastic soloists. A real treat for music-lovers. Nadine Benjamin is a charismatic and versatile soprano who is in increasing demand on both the operatic stage and the concert platform. Hailed by "Opera Now" magazine as one of their favourite top ten sopranos in “a new generation of sopranos who are destined to have impressive careers”, Nadine won the inaugural Fulham Opera Robert Presley Memorial Verdi Prize in 2015. Gilles Van der Linden is a talented tenor who celebrated his debut at La Monnaie in the role of Rodriguez in Massenet's Don Quixote. The same year he was selected for the "Académies du Festival d'Aix-en-Provence" where he obtained the title of "Laureate" and was invited in that capacity by the festival of Verbier (Switzerland).

title: Organised: Crime
description: Organised: Crime is a bold new dark comedy making its way to Brighton Fringe 2026, after a sell-out debut run at Camden Fringe 2025. This sharp, female-led production from emerging theatre-group Gut Rot Girls (Eve Callow-Salt, Jane Herbert, and Martha Powell-Dooley) blends biting humour with bloodstained storage hacks - think Marie Kondo meets The Sopranos. Meet Agatha’s Angels, a premium home organisation service with a zero-judgement policy - and zero witnesses. Behind the label maker lies a thriving criminal operation, and when a routine clean-up spirals out of control, three women must mop their way out of a deadly mess. With fast-paced dialogue, twisted plot turns, and themes tackling workplace danger and loyalty, Organised: Crime promises dark laughs and darker secrets, all hidden behind immaculate cupboard doors. Expect mess. Expect mayhem. Expect women who know exactly where to dispose the bodies.

title: ORWELL: The Road to 1984
description: A timely portrait of a writer whose words remain a warning - and a hope. George Orwell is perhaps the most influential English writer of the twentieth century. His classic tales Animal Farm and Nineteen Eighty-Four which warn of the dangers of totalitarianism - erosion of free speech, abuse of power, increasing control of all aspects of our everyday lives - resonate now more than ever. Orwell’s dystopian concepts such as 'Big Brother is watching you', 'Room 101' and 'thoughtcrime' have become everyday phrases. But where did his vision of the future come from? In this powerful and moving play, MARK STRATFORD takes you on a fascinating journey through the extraordinary experiences that shaped the work of this perceptive and compelling writer. In this age of misinformation, political extremism and digital surveillance, ORWELL: The Road to 1984 is a play for our time.

title: Oscar Leonard: Help, I'm Psychic!
description: Oscar's life was perfectly fine until a drunken encounter with a psychic turned everything upside down. Now he's stuck with mind-reading powers he didn't ask for and definitely can't control. Tonight, you'll witness something very unpredictable: Oscar learning to harness his abilities in real-time. Oscar will attempt to read your thoughts (with varying degrees of success), predict futures that haven't happened yet, and subtly influence decisions you haven't even made. Some moments will astonish you. Others might spectacularly misfire — but that's half the fun. This isn't your typical mind-reading show. It's funnier, messier, and far more honest. Oscar combines comedy with mentalism, weaving everything into a narrative that pulls you from sceptical observer to active participant. You're not just watching someone perform tricks—you're joining him on a journey of coming to terms with his newfound abilities. Will he succeed? Who knows. Will there be chaos along the way? Absolutely.
description_html:
Oscar's life was perfectly fine until a drunken encounter with a psychic turned everything upside down. Now he's stuck with mind-reading powers he didn't ask for and definitely can't control. Tonight, you'll witness something very unpredictable: Oscar learning to harness his abilities in real-time.

Oscar will attempt to read your thoughts (with varying degrees of success), predict futures that haven't happened yet, and subtly influence decisions you haven't even made. Some moments will astonish you. Others might spectacularly misfire — but that's half the fun.

This isn't your typical mind-reading show. It's funnier, messier, and far more honest. Oscar combines comedy with mentalism, weaving everything into a narrative that pulls you from sceptical observer to active participant. You're not just watching someone perform tricks—you're joining him on a journey of coming to terms with his newfound abilities.

Will he succeed? Who knows. Will there be chaos along the way? Absolutely.

title: Other
description: A stand-up show about the experiences of being different, trying/pretending to fit in, and learning how to embrace your otherness. Have you ever felt like you don’t belong? Do you know just enough football lingo to get by at work? Do you find yourself quietly nodding along during group conversations about Love Island instead of joining in? Do you not understand, nor care, about social media trends? We get you. If you feel like an ‘other’ in your own way(s), this show is for you! Rohan and Mani explore the challenges and triumphs of not fitting in - from finding out what happens when you throw a chair at school, to watching in horror as innocent poppadoms are smashed to pieces at a ‘curry night’, to still being the gamer kid in your 30s, and more... Individual stand-up sets from each comedian are followed by a joint final section where Rohan and Mani are onstage together for an interactive audience quiz, asking the important questions: How ‘other’ are you, and have you embraced it?

title: Palmeira Mansions Tours: Booking Required
description: Take a glimpse back at Victorian life in a house tour which reveals the opulence, taste and lifestyle of a nouveau riche gentleman. Palmeira Mansions are two south facing terraces built 1883-4, these large Victorian properties are on the north side of Palmeira Square in Hove. Number 33 Palmeira Mansions was purchased in 1889 by Arthur William Mason at the age of twenty-nine, a widower after the tragic death of his first wife, he moved into the house with a young daughter Christine. In his early years living in the house Mr Mason used the family fortune to refurbish the interior in a lavish eclectic mix of fixtures, including elaborate fireplaces some with stunning overmantels, carved mahogany doors, a variety of lincrusta wall papers and exquisite stained-glass windows were added to various rooms. 33 Palmeira Mansions is now part of the English Language Centre in Hove. 33 Palmeira Mansions is on the corner of Salisbury Road & Church Road, Hove BN3 2GB. Note there are many stairs.

title: Parody of the Rings
description: At the local cinema, all hell breaks loose when the ushers misplace the DVDs of The Lord of the Rings trilogy – every single copy! With only the soundtracks left in hand, the team of hapless ushers must somehow improvise an entire three-film saga in front of a live audience. Get ready for an uproarious evening of comedy, chaos, and utter nonsense, as they attempt to recreate the epic tale of hobbits, elves, and orcs – without any visuals, any planning, or any clue what they're doing. Will Frodo ever make it to Mordor? Will Aragorn get crowned king? Will Gollum stop stealing the show? Will anyone even remember who’s supposed to be who?! It’s a show that’s more fun than a barrel of hobbits, and definitely more chaotic than a Gandalf speech. If you’ve ever wanted to see Gollum fight a battle with sound effects, or Gandalf desperately trying to remember what happens next, then you’re in for a hilarious treat!

title: PAUL MERRYCK - LAGER JUS
description: Lager Jus – 25 Glorious Years of the 21st Century For all of you who think the old 2000s so far has been a bit shit – think again! From Richard Madeley’s Millenium Cupboard to the new series of Pro-Celebrity Dogging, the 21st Century has been an absolute delight. It’s been an amazing time too for veteran Essex Comedian Paul Merryck, so join him as he takes you on a journey through time, joyously and tenuously intertwining seismic global changes with the continuous unravelling of his ridiculous life. WARNING: Unlike many fringe shows, there’s no profound message or meaning that will gradually reveal itself as the show progresses, teaching you something about the performer, the world and, perhaps, yourself. On the contrary, you’ll learn absolutely nothing and may leave the show a little bit thicker than when you came in. There are lots and lots of jokes though. “gag-focussed delivery... . high laugh rate... . you’d think that the spirit of Mike Reid is alive and well” Chortle

title: Paul Silverthorne (LSO) plays recent works for viola by Magnus Lindberg, Brett Dean and Poul Ruders
description: Join us for a thrilling evening with internationally acclaimed violist Paul Silverthorne and pianist Roderick Chadwick. Described by The Times as “a virtuoso in sensitivity and technique,” Paul Silverthorne has built an exceptional career that balances international solo performances with principal viola roles in two of London’s most distinguished ensembles: the London Symphony Orchestra and the London Sinfonietta. A passionate advocate for contemporary music, Silverthorne has inspired leading composers to write specifically for him, drawn to his expressive range and fearless artistry. Partnering with him is the remarkable pianist Roderick Chadwick, hailed in Fanfare as “an incredibly musical pianist", in a programme featuring works by Magnus Lindberg, Brett Dean, Poul Ruders, and other significant voices of our time. Together, they create a musical experience of intensity and striking modernity. Expect a night of bold colours, daring rhythms, and unforgettable performances.

title: Pavilion Gardens Cafe History Tours 2026
description: The Pavilion Gardens Cafe History Tour, led by local historian Geoffrey Mead, takes visitors on a rich journey through the heart of Brighton's cultural and social past. We begin at the cafe in the Royal Pavilion Gardens. The tour highlights the cafe origins in the 1920's, its role as a community hub, and its evolution across three generations of family ownership. Blending architectural insight, local anecdotes and stories of everyday Brighton life. The tour offers a warm engaging look at how the Pavilion Gardens Cafe became a cherished landmark in the City. At the end of the tour you will be offered tea or coffee with cake and a chance to ask questions and have a chat with fellow tour guests.

title: Pearl and Dean Ride Again
description: After a sell out run at Brighton Fringe 2025 and being shortlisted for 3 awards - including best new show - Pearl and Dean are back with a brand new offering! Join the most "heterosexual" couple in show biz, for more high jinks on the high seas. Climb aboard our cruise ship, the Watery Queen, to hear tales of our brief spell in the pop charts with our critically acclaimed album "Separate Beds". Find out which Nashville Ingénue reworked our greatest hit and made herself a fortune. Delve deep into the private lives of Peter and Marsha and find out just why "Love Will Keep Us Together" even if fame and fortune continues to evade us... Expect new songs, new costumes, new hair and tall tales from the shallow end of showbiz. Join us for an unforgettable evening of singing and swinging aboard the Watery Queen! Pearl and Dean Ride Again - it's a "hull" new world!

title: Percy Nyx: Prince of Dreams
description: Enter a surreal world of dreams, where glittering drag prince Percy Nyx guides us through the subconscious of Alex, a dreamer caught between childhood stories and adult truths. Blending twisted fairy tales,poetic storytelling, live vocals and drag finery, this genre-bending theatrical cabaret explores identity, gender, and trauma with moving moments of laughter and reflection. Percy Nyx: Prince of Dreams dives headfirst into the magic and meaning of what lies beneath sleep. From Lex Pickett—multi-award-winning performer comes a dazzling journey through dreams, memory, and self-discovery. So sit back, relax, and dream a while with me... (Outstanding Theatre Fringe Award 2025), SohoPlayhouse NY Encore Series finalist, and founding member of multi-award winning cabaret collective ClapBackClub

title: Perfect Little Flirt
description: "16th March 2003, 11:40pm Note to self: A NEW START! Here I am born again, to live again to try again to start again. The old me is dead and on the stroke of midnight, I will be reborn." Perfect Little Flirt is a chaotic and hilarious ride into Sal’s actual teenage diary — unfiltered, gender confused and deeply earnest. Come for the mega highs and desperate lows, stay for the absurd social commentary that comes with being socialised as an early-2000s “good girl.” Expect excrutiatingly dull lists, so much “getting off” and a relatable dose of cringe. With joy and sorrow, the show cracks open the diary as a tool of gendered self-surveillance and asks what it really costs to be the Perfect Little Flirt.

title: Physical Media
description: Meet Tony; he's struggling - but he has amazing music taste. Meet Amelia; she's over here from America for a meeting but really just wants to be in a Richard Curtis movie...welcome to the last DVD and CD shop in town, a warm nod back to the pre-digital age! A brand new, quirky comedy play from the pen of multi award-winning writer, Paul Richards, about loneliness, missed opportunities, and unexpected friendships...but let's be honest, it's mostly about Britpop music and why you should actually still own physical things. Performed by Alex Machell and Nicole Horst, with Paul Richards.

title: Piano Concertos by Candlelight: 21st Century Baroque (feat. Candlelight Orchestra)
description: SELLOUT EdFringe25 pianist Matthew Shiel performs J.S. Bach’s Piano Concerto No. 1 in D minor alongside English premiere of renowned Italian film composer Dr Alfredo Caponnetto’s brand new piano concerto with his Candlelight Orchestra. Bach’s D minor Concerto unleashes relentless drive and shadowed intensity whilst the thunderous rhythms and haunting lyricism of Caponetto’s new piano concerto pull you into worlds of fierce urgency and electrifying brilliance. Matthew Shiel is award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera. Matthew will conduct his acclaimed Candlelight Orchestra from the piano as Mozart and Bach did for an unforgettable evening of musical elegance.

title: Piran Armes is a Bit Weird (Work in Progress)
description: Piran is the 2025 Chichester Fringe New Act of the Year Winner and 2025 Komedia New Act Semi Finalist. A born and bred Brightonian, he's never quite seemed to fit in. He has an odd name, a job nobody understands and now he's reflecting on how his past has led him to the quirky character he seems to have landed himself as. He shares silly thoughts, unique impressions, bizarre pastimes and origins of his unusual name. Piran delivers laughs with charm, wit and just the right amount of madness. He's a bit weird.

title: Planted
description: A chaotic, brutally honest one-woman show about turning 30 before you’re ready, navigating modern dating, and trying to tick your way to happiness with a doomed to-do list. From smear tests and vibrators to disastrous run-ins with exes and painfully cringe first dates, she throws herself into “self-improvement” with the unearned confidence of a woman spiralling beautifully. But running through it all is one stubborn basil plant: half-dead, slightly judgemental, and impossible to throw away. As the list starts to unravel, the comedy gives way to something deeper—a story about grief, friendship, and the unexpected things we cling to when life won’t go to plan. Funny, filthy, and quietly devastating, if all goes to plan...

title: Pleasure Incorporated
description: What if sex workers could get drunk at the office Christmas party? Had colleagues they could gossip with? Went to performance reviews with their managers? What would it be like if the oldest profession in the world was just a regular office job? Drawn from Jemima’s lived experience of escorting in Berlin and as a survivor of childhood sexual abuse (CSA), this lyrical one-woman play is an insightful, witty and sensitive exploration of one woman’s route to selling sex; what it means for her to do the work and how that spools out into her friendships, relationships and society. Set in the cubicles and corridors of an imagined corporation, bringing together live performance and video art, Pleasure Incorporated is a funny, tender, surreal and compelling examination of Jemima’s search for answers; how to heal from childhood trauma, and how to gain bodily and financial autonomy in a hostile world.

title: Plowman and Cummins are Plowman and Cummins
description: Clown duo Plowman and Cummins present a surreal trip to the hairdressers. Expect bouncy blow-dries, falling over, mess, beauty, violence and classical singing. Using absurd visual comedy, physical theatre and splintered narrative, Plowman and Cummins herald a slapstick revival. The salon dissolves into moments of chaos, danger and silliness as objects, costumes and bodies morph in and out of reality. Part-clown part-performance art, ‘Plowman and Cummins are Plowman and Cummins’ is their debut hour-long comedy show, appearing at Brighton Fringe for the very first time. For fans of foolishness, laughing and the feeling you get when you watch someone run for the bus as it drives away.

title: Polly & Grace: Shy Bairns Get Nowt
description: Wildly weird character comedy on what it means to be a woman in 2026. It's a ride. May contain the ultimate threesome - work, dating and, er...screws? A split-bill work-in-progress. Grace: Meet Kitty, millennial hustle-martyr. She's landed her dream job and promptly weaponises therapy-speak and dodgy office perks, including a yoga class led by her twin Piper — which may or may not be a cult, disguised as lunchtime flow. A chaotic takedown of hustle culture, heartache, and the corporate-to-cult pipeline. Polly: Is a little more wildcard energy than lovely Grace. Characters could include a thirty-something egg, a ghost hunter with telephonic tits and a horny hardware store worker. Or not. Perhaps come along and find out?

title: Poppies
description: Johnjoe and Jim clash over the wearing of a poppy. The best friends address what the symbol represents to their respective heritage and attempt to navigate their way through this historic subject matter in relation to their friendship and the British Empire’s devastating, lasting impact in Ireland. This naturalistic conversation develops into surreal hypotheticals that explore autonomy, societal expectations and childhood memories. Amidst this, their relationship falters as they both realise they are fundamentally different people. Poppies depicts a modern day tragedy of stubbornness, repressed anger and identity in a time where this lack of footing soft launches so many young men into dangerous ideals.'

title: Porgy and Bess at BOAT
description: A new jazz suite from Chris Coull, featuring award winning vocalist Imogen Ryall, playing the music of George Gershwin in an engaging and enthralling story that is both accessible and permeates the traditions of the post bop jazz movement. Embark on a story through time as we take you to 1930’s Charleston and a love story between down on his luck ‘Porgy’ and the sassy ‘Bess’. Featuring songs instantly recognisable (Ain’t Necessarily So, I Love You Porgy and of course Summertime) unhackneyed classics (I’ve Got Plenty Of Nothing, There’s A Boat Leaving Soon For New York) and rarely heard gems (Buzzard Song, Prayer, Strawberry Woman). In this project, Chris has brought together three of the finest rhythm section player London has to offer, one of the finest vocalists on the UK scene and his own skills to create a dynamic, swinging and above all thoughtfully hip jazz suite, that swings from hard bop grooves, sumptuous ballads, toe tapping Latin grooves and orchestrally inspired harmonies

title: Portals (An immersive dance odyssey)
description: Portals is an immersive dance adventure for all ages that drops you into strange and spectacular worlds where the rules don’t quite work. Gravity gets funky, time hiccups, and bodies move in ways usually reserved for dreams. Guided by headsets and a glitchy digital assistant, you’re led through the chaos by a troupe of seasoned world-hoppers. Follow their lead, embrace the weird, and discover a space where curiosity, movement, and playful exploration are not just allowed, but encouraged.

title: Posey Mehta: Asking For It
description: Posey Mehta is a submissive and a masochist. And they are asking for things they have no legal right to ask for. You must decide whether to give them what they want, even if that means breaking the law. In this powerful protest against the Spanner Ruling and the Domestic Abuse Act 2021, Mehta submits their body to the audience and asks them to hurt it. What follows is a surprisingly tender, joyful, and wholesome dismantling of so many of the tired stereotypes that exist around kink. Following in the tradition of--but ultimately subverting--Marina Abramovic's Rhythm 0 and Yoko Ono's Cut Piece, this bold new piece of participatory performance art offers a window into a world where violence isn't cruelty, but care. Mehta is an "unhinged" (Ed Fringe Review) award-winning live artist, blending drag, clown, and performance art. As their drag alter-ego Mitzi Fitz (K Factor season 2 winner), they run the sell-out sensation Big Fat Cabaret, London's best (and only) fat-centric cabaret.

title: Prateek Kohli: Denial
description: A stand-up comedy show about the everyday absurdities of India, the US, and the UK where Prateek has lived and performed. The absurdities get political, historical and sometimes to outright denial. "Commentary on racism within his native India is territory much less explored in comedy, which he cleverly manipulated in smart political analogies" - Chortle “He took us on an informed, interesting journey around the world, using absurd logic with his endearing self-effacing humor laced with sharp insights into language and traditions from a seldom heard perspective.” - Express and Ham "His act is a unique breath of fresh comedy air. Prateek has a delightfully novel way about him, as he delivers his hilarious material, probing cultural mores and very original sparky political analogies." - Jill Edwards, Comic Boom Comedy Club Awards: Komedia New Comedy Award - Finalist West End New Act of the Year - Runner up BBC New Comedy Award - Shortlisted

title: Princess Sparkles
description: Step into the not-so-glittery world of Princess Sparkles: a professional princess-for-hire who’s very available, a teeny bit unstable, and just that little bit too much. Appearing at five-year-old birthday parties somewhere near you, she’s flustered, running late, and it looks like more than just her costume is coming apart at the seams. Want to feel like a child again? Ever worked with children? Ever wondered why people warn against it? Prepare to be hurled into a chaotically fun-filled birthday extravaganza complete with games, “magic,” and stories gone wildly off-script. But beware: she is definitely not Elsa, and this party might not be suitable for children... or adults... or anyone, really. As her crown slips, so does her grip on reality. Lily Maryon’s debut show is an unhinged hour of character comedy with a theatrical twist, casting the audience in the role of the children as Sparkles fights to keep the party and herself together. Sketch Off Quarter Finalist 2026

title: Pseudonym
description: Part theatre, part spoken word. "Bridget" looks back on her life and contemplates the messy tapestry of experiences that have led her here. She confronts her struggles with body dysmorphia, creative complacency, parasocial relationships, and the brazen decision to move to the UK following a mental breakdown. A raw and intimate look at self-discovery, identity, and the art of becoming. Praise for Deanna Strasse and her work: "Ms. Strasse is a wordsmith. She grabs the language and does not let it go." -- Krane Reviews, on "Summers in Prague" "Deanna Strasse...has created a brilliant piece that is not only interesting but educational in a fun way." -- GJD Review on "Dead Doves and Lemons..." "Hilarious, great writing, and keeps you on the edge of your seat!" -- Landon Hudson on "The Cafe mocha Murders"

title: PureJim: Jim Hooker (Work in Progress)
description: Jim Hooker is from Romford where the stereotypes are true, the family secrets are questionable, and everyone's on the rob. A storyteller from the cockney-Essex borderlands, Jim tells us the he chaos of his upbringing, eccentric relatives with colourful pasts, parents dispensing wildly unqualified advice, and the silliness of everyday life. His storytelling blends observation with unexpected twists. Expect vivid character voices, escalating absurdity, and jokes your nan would pretend to be offended by before sharing them in the group chat. description_html:
Jim Hooker is from Romford where the stereotypes are true, the family secrets are questionable, and everyone's on the rob.
A storyteller from the cockney-Essex borderlands, Jim tells us the he chaos of his upbringing, eccentric relatives with colourful pasts, parents dispensing wildly unqualified advice, and the silliness of everyday life.
His storytelling blends observation with unexpected twists. Expect vivid character voices, escalating absurdity, and jokes your nan would pretend to be offended by before sharing them in the group chat.

title: Racists, Recessions And Revolutions
description: "Democracy isn't power to the people - it's power to the story. And why let the truth get in the way of a good story?" Nadica has it all; a Balkan Mum from a country everyone seems to think shouldn't exist, a British dad who's never around and a post-uni life timed perfectly to be cancelled by Covid. What more could a girl ask for? Well a bit of cash wouldn't hurt. With the rest of her life in ruins, one Lockdown hustle beckons like a siren's call; lying online, for fun and for profit. Join Nadica as she masterminds some of the Internet's most viral headlines: Could Covid Be A Lie? Was The Election Stolen? And What Really Went Down In That Prison Cell... As her work takes her from the ideological fringes and into the halls of power, truth and lies blend and bash and the combined powers of Racists, Recessions and Revolutions roll together to destabilise the very fabric of society as we know it - no spoilers, but we all knew it would happen eventually right?

title: RATMAGEDDON
description: Dive headfirst into RATMAGEDDON, a surreal, high-energy comedy adventure from RatBag. Fresh from Soho Theatre’s Comedy Lab, this work-in-progress blends sketch, clown, and character comedy to create a feral, apocalyptic world where the audience becomes gloriously complicit in the chaos. Expect big characters, bold choices, rapid transformations, and joyful theatrical mayhem as two performers push each other right to the edge. With musical madness, playful rule-breaking, and unpredictable twists, RATMAGEDDON invites you not just to watch but to join the disorder. Unashamedly silly, wildly imaginative, and dangerously fun, this is cult comedy in the making.

title: Ray Fordyce: Quincunx
description: Quincunx is Ray Fordyce's favourite word. It means a pattern of five and there are five areas of his life he want's to talk about and how they all connect. From growing up in a rough neighbourhood of Dundee, to student life to being a massive gay-mer, Ray shares it all on stage.

title: Red Biscuit: Little Town Blue
description: Set in the U.S, the show follows three high-school seniors on their final hurrah before college. The boys (Brad, Chad, and Sven) are forced to forego their frivolities when a fallen tree blocks their route, forcing them to spend the night in a mysterious haven: Little Town Blue. At midnight, this seemingly idyllic town rears its ugly head, and the three boys must battle their way through til morning. Little Town Blue is a characterful, hilarious, and relentless hour of physical comedy that takes the audience on a madcap journey through rural America at breakneck speed.

title: Redlights (Work In Progress)
description: Last year after a series of hospitalisations Nayonica (BBC Upload festival, Sky Comedy Rep shortlist, Chortle Student comedy semi finalist) read Matthew McConaughey's memoir Greenlights, which was marketed as a manual to turn life's "red lights" into "Greenlights". Only it turned out the red lights in Matthew's' life were that he was too handsome, too successful and had too many millions of dollars. So Nayonica, has created this hour of stand up in response for her fellow uglies and undesirables, looking back at all the redlights in her life. During this comedy show Nayonica revisits all the times in her life when planning and doing the sensible thing ended up going wrong (like when she took a gap year to go to Glastonbury and then Glastonbury took a gap year), then eventually turned out really well. Now she’s wondering if she should continue being logical and planning things or if she should just surrender to the chaos. This question is to complex for a stand up comedian to answer.

title: Reflections
description: Reflections is an immersive interdisciplinary performance combining digital animation with live sound and music performed on an analogue synthesiser. The narrative follows Martha through ordinary yet layered moments where reflections become the main carriers of story — a computer screen, car mirrors, sunglasses, a lake’s surface, and finally a mirror at home. These reflective surfaces reveal and distort reality, exploring themes of perception, memory, and subjectivity. Accompanied by an atmospheric soundscape of composed music and environmental recordings — including cars, footsteps, rustling leaves, and birdsong — Reflections creates a multi-sensory environment that invites audiences to slow down, listen, and reconsider how images shape their understanding of the world. Margareta is a visual artist and art theorist. Her interdisciplinary practice spans painting, installation, animation, video and sound. Her works are exhibited internationally (New York, Toronto, Paris, Belgrade).

title: Refuge
description: "Pretty, powerful, prideful Prutland. This majestic country will make you want to stay forever. The picturesque neighbourhoods will trap you in their opportunities. Xavi had to flee from their home, and was brought to safety in a house in Prutland. But every rose has its thorns." Xavi escapes her toxic household and is welcomed into a house in Prutland. While some of her new housemates are welcoming, a few others don't appreciate Xavi's presence. Is Xavi truly safe inside this new house? Waxen Wings' first show in Brighton will leave audiences to ponder on how thin the line between fiction and reality is. A must-see show that will entertain and inform 16+ audiences.

title: Reminiscing With Adunni Alaalo
description: Award-winning performer Bola Stephen-Atitebi, recipient of the 2023 Brighton Fringe International Encore Series Award for her performance in Esther’s Revenge, brings her captivating storytelling to Reminiscing with Adunni Alaalo. This 50-minute interactive performance celebrates Nigerian oral traditions, weaving folktales, riddles (alo apamo), folk songs, and live African percussion into a joyful, immersive experience. Rooted in Yoruba culture yet drawing connections across Nigeria’s diverse traditions, the show begins with an invocation and audience call-and-response, continues with theatrical storytelling punctuated by African drums, and invites the audience to join in participatory songs and chants. A highlight of the performance is the Reminiscing Circle, where audience members share their own childhood memories, creating a bridge across cultures and generations. After the storytelling, a curated pop-up displays textiles, crafts, and cultural items referenced in the tales.

title: Resonant Void
description: Directly from Tokyo (Japan): Resonant Void is an experimental solo Butoh performance that moves through shifting states of presence, rupture, and quiet emergence. Using a mosquito net, plastic sheeting, two black skirts, and a portable lighting and projection system, the work creates a delicate terrain where the body meets materials that conceal, resist, and transform. The mosquito net forms a fragile chamber that holds breath and memory; the plastic sheets become boundaries that distort, shimmer, and eventually break; and the black skirts shift between creature, mask, shadow, and weight. Light and projection carve unstable edges, allowing the performer to drift between visibility and disappearance. Sound, gesture, and texture unfold in slow waves, inviting the audience into a contemplative space where identity loosens and meaning forms in the intervals between movements.

title: Rhapsody in Blue - Jazz Sessions by Candlelight
description: Award-winning Scottish pianist Matthew Shiel leads his jazz band on a swinging, toe-tapping odyssey through blues, Berlin cabaret, ragtime and New York jazz piano improv with poignant storytelling – by candlelight. Programme includes Joplin’s The Entertainer, Brubeck’s Take Five, Gershwin’s showstopper Rhapsody in Blue and cabaret music by the Jewish composer Erwin Schulhoff (1894-1942) – who was murdered by the Third Reich for the "crime" of composing jazz. Fringe Classics are ★★★★★ rated: 'Enchanting... amusing, enlightening stories' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com). 'An atmosphere which is all too often missed in our digital world' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Ripping good yarn' ★★★★★ (BingeFringe.com). Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: Rice Guys Finish Last (Work In Progress)
description: Two comedians: one Chinese (Marco), one Arab-Kenyan (EJ). It might sound like an unlikely pairing, but one thing’s certain – they’ve both had hilarious experiences trying to make sense of the world. Expect charming storytelling, sharp one-liners and self-deprecating humour as they unpack growing up in the 90s, navigating culture, religion, sexuality and their parents. These fast-rising London comics have performed across the country – now they’re teaming up to share their stories side by side in this work in progress show. Is it perfect harmony... or a culture clash waiting to happen? You won’t see this mix anywhere else. How could you? These two bring experience, energy and fresh perspectives to the stage. Whether you’re a die-hard comedy fan who loves well-crafted jokes or you’ve just wandered in looking for a laugh, you’re in safe hands.

title: Rob Preston is Bobby Prezinsky: Unity Concert for Peace
description: The show is a character comedy hour following Bobby Prezinsky: a hungry, wannabe presenter desperate to host something - anything - of global significance. Convinced the world is irreparably broken, Bobby decides the only solution is to stage a vast, world-changing Unity Concert for Peace, despite having absolutely no qualifications or experience. We follow Bobby’s attempts to pull the concert together. Along the way, he encounters a series of characters keen to exploit his naivety and take advantage of his perceived momentum: Jacques De Klerk, the slick CEO of influencer marketing agency VibesTalent; Gregory Onions, a former Royal Marine turned motivational speaker; and Pieter Verkels, a Belgian dating coach, among others. The show blends extended character monologues with quickfire sketches, all anchored by Bobby’s delusional but sincere optimism and belief that his concert can change the world.
description_html:
The show is a character comedy hour following Bobby Prezinsky: a hungry, wannabe presenter desperate to host something - anything - of global significance. Convinced the world is irreparably broken, Bobby decides the only solution is to stage a vast, world-changing Unity Concert for Peace, despite having absolutely no qualifications or experience.

We follow Bobby’s attempts to pull the concert together. Along the way, he encounters a series of characters keen to exploit his naivety and take advantage of his perceived momentum: Jacques De Klerk, the slick CEO of influencer marketing agency VibesTalent; Gregory Onions, a former Royal Marine turned motivational speaker; and Pieter Verkels, a Belgian dating coach, among others.

The show blends extended character monologues with quickfire sketches, all anchored by Bobby’s delusional but sincere optimism and belief that his concert can change the world.

The winning team receives honour, glory, and maybe a round of drinks if we've sold enough tickets. title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Twelve at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

title: RPJ: Role Playing James
description: Step into a world of wild imagination, chaos and comedy. Under the guidance of ex-comedian and self-proclaimed “James-Master” James Bennison, audience members are plucked at random and thrust into the spotlight as the heroes of a totally improvised fantasy adventure. Armed only with enthusiasm (and maybe a grenade), they must battle bizarre creatures, solve ridiculous riddles, and roll gigantic dice, all while James desperately tries to keep the story on the rails. Whether you’re a seasoned role-player or have never touched a character sheet in your life, this show invites you to join a roaring quest where everything is made up and no two nights are ever the same. Expect epic triumphs, catastrophic failures, and the kind of heroic nonsense that can only come from comedy colliding with high fantasy. If you love laughter, adventure and glorious unpredictability join the party. Your destiny awaits, whether you want it or not.

title: Ruby's Glowup- Drag Makeup Tutorial Workshop
description: Ever fancied achieving a fierce and fabulous drag look? Join Rubella Vaxine for a 4-hour fun packed tutorial to discover your inner Drag Diva as she paints her face step by step for you to follow along, talking you through her favourite products and techniques. The workshop is designed for anyone interested in the art of drag for beginners (no previous knowledge needed), enjoy an afternoon of transformation including a free glass of bubbly on arrival. Make up, brushes and drag style false eye lashes are provided (some items will be for shared use), you are more than welcome to bring your own makeup if you would prefer and if you would like to bring a ring light, please feel free. Basic memory aid resource to takeaway to practice with. Photo opportunity at the end of the session with fun wigs provided, and if you would like to bring any sparkly numbers to change into that would be fabulous! Once finished, why not slay the night away out on the town - Book now for an unforgettable time!

title: Rules Schmules: How to be Jew-ISH
description: Suzie Depreli – not a typically Jewish name, is it? – brings you one woman's passive aggressive mission to educate the world about what it means to have an orthodox family that ate sausages, an Asian Catholic husband that uses more Yiddish words than her Nana, and celebrate Passover without believing in God. And that this is all ok! With original songs performed live and anecdotes that will have Jewish and gentile audiences alike feeling like they were present for it all, this is a heartfelt, humorous musical journey through the pressures and pitfalls of understanding your identity in a world full of prejudice. This is how to be Jew-ish in Britain today.

title: Running With Scissors
description: Running with scissors is a one-woman show that slices into the high stakes world of women surgeons: where precision is everything, authority is questioned, and one wrong move can change a life. With dark humour, raw honesty and scalpel-sharp storytelling, the show explores what it costs to cut your way into a profession that wasn’t built for you. From history to present day, the theatre of war to the NHS, from ambition to burnout and confidence to doubt, Running with scissors exposes the blood, bone and bravery behind the mask. Told by an NHS surgeon turned storyteller, this is a show about hands that heal, systems that wound, and women who refuse to be careful.

title: Sadomusical
description: Art is pain. Pain is art. Two “definitely not masochistic” artists take on the challenge of a lifetime: to create musical comedy with feeling—while feeling the real pain of electrocution. In what may be their worst idea yet, veteran musical improvisers Lee Apsey ('CSI: Crime Scene Improvisation,' 'Your Flaws: The Improvised Musical') and Jayda Fogel ('Bird & Tax Collector,' '¡Horario Estelar!') weave hilarious and sincere songs, sketches, and stories with the inspiration of the audience and the threat of being shocked, shackled, dunked, and beaten with a stick. Special guests from the Fringe may also appear to hammer down on their poor life choices. You’ll be shocked, and so will the players—literally. From the award-winning co-creator of multiple Edinburgh Fringe sell-out hit 'CSI: Crime Scene Improvisation.' Artist Accolades: WINNER - The Comedy Award - Colchester Fringe 2024 WINNER - Best Improv/Musical - Leicester Comedy Festival 2023

title: Salma Habib Lyons Work in Progress comedy show
description: Salma is a Brit Pakistani stand-up comedian from a working-class background, known for blending sharp political commentary with personal stories drawn from her formidable Pakistani mother and Irish family. Laugh & explore with Salma how the core of our funny lives are the great and awful humans who make us—especially the one who tried to raise her. With sass, heart, and well-aimed political punches, her material is both fearless and refreshingly original.

title: Sam See: Sam See It, Sam Say It, Sam Sorted (Work In Progress)
description: Award nominated Sam See has a brand new hour of stand-up comedy! This show promises to be rib-tickling, side-splitting, and [INSERT THIRD THING BEFORE PRINT]! Whether you want gay jokes, Asian jokes, immigrant jokes, or whichever minority group you support, he’s probably one of them! He’s totally written this copy, not his agent, who definitely hasn't banned him from doing what he loves: slideshow comedy, educational humour, and heartfelt messages. NOPE! None of THAT will happen, that’s not what famous comedians do! Join Sam as he tries to tick the boxes, be a 'successful comedian', and understand what it means to be yourself in this modern world.

title: Sameera Bowers: What Is Going On?
description: Sameera’s mum was proud when she graduated with a degree in Economics from Cambridge. Less so when she went to clown school. Now Sameera needs to find a job. On LinkedIn, she is a “changemaker, global innovator and value-driven impact leader.” But in life, she has no idea what she’s doing. In this multimedia comedy hour, Sameera searches for clarity through psychic guidance, grapples with the science of perfectly spherical cows, plots her rise as the leader of an Indian tutoring empire and wants to make her grandma proud. If all else fails, she plans to become the first 22-year-old living in an old-age nursing home. Sameera was a member of The Cambridge Footlights (2025) and this year trained at the École Phillipe Gaulier clown school in France. In 2025 What Is Going On? completed a sellout run at the Camden Fringe and Sameera performed in the 5-star, award-winning comedy play Penelope Quadrangle at the Edinburgh Fringe, in which critics called her performance “genius” (Varsity).

title: Sammy Stitches x M.A.S-Boutique: Hooked & Worn
description: SammyStitchesUK & M.A.S-Boutique present a vibrant springtime collaboration at Brighton Fringe, blending handmade crochet artistry with boutique fashion flair. Samantha of SammyStitchesUK showcases her hooked crochet animals — from cheeky frogs and curious cats to bright, blooming flowers - alongside colourful spring wear crafted in wool and magical gifts designed to delight. Each piece is lovingly made, celebrating the joy of yarn, hooks, and imagination. Adding a stylish twist, M.A.S-Boutique unveils their brand-new fashion line, bringing elegance and contemporary design to the showcase. Together, this relaxed, family-friendly event invites audiences to explore one-of-a-kind gifts, playful companions, and fresh seasonal style under one roof. It is a joyful celebration of creativity, community, and craft, perfect for Brighton Fringe visitors seeking something original, heartfelt, and truly unique. Discover handmade treasures and boutique fashion in a welcoming space

title: Sarah Bradley: How to Write a Romcom
description: Ever wished your life was more like a movie? Join romantic comedy scholar Sarah Bradley as she takes you through a journey of comforting cliches, timeless tropes, falling-in-love montages and predictable endings. We’ll attempt to answer the big questions (can enemies really become lovers? Is working in print media the real secret to finding love? Can you choose yourself and still get a happily ever after?), while coming up with a love story all our own. Reality is overrated anyway.

title: Saul Henry: Church of Saulentology
description: Saul Henry returns with his latest solo comedy show where he asks: 'Why join a cult when you can start your own?' Raised by atheists (with a Mormon Nana), Saul has spent his life chasing happiness, exploring oddball beliefs and cults, and dabbling in spiritual practices—until he accidentally almost joined one himself. Now, when companies, billionaires and tech bros command followings that would make cult leaders weep with envy, what isn't a cult these days? In 'The Church of Saulentology', Saul reaches his enlightened conclusion: true happiness means inventing your own ridiculous doctrine. And he's here to show you how- featuring a deeply unsettling Childcatcher-Colonel Sanders-Noseybonk-like figure who serenades unsuspecting chickens into the fold. Irreverent, thought-provoking, and absurdly funny. Tithing not required, but blind devotion is encouraged.

title: Saving Myself
description: Set over one night, SAVING MYSELF centres on Lilly, a young woman alone in her flat, surrounded by books, half-remembered rituals, and an unlit candle. Since childhood, Lilly has been blamed for things going wrong: pets that disappeared, toys that broke, the uneasy silences that followed her through her family home. Over time, these stories became fact, shaping how others treated her and how she learned to understand herself. After a violent assault, Lilly reaches breaking point. With no one left to turn to, she fixates on the idea that something inside her - a demon, an inheritance, or the damage left by trauma - has to be destroyed. Moving between realism, poetry, folklore, and ritual, the play unfolds in real time as Lilly fights to take control of her own body and story. Raw, darkly comic, and unsettling, SAVING MYSELF asks what happens when care is replaced by superstition, and what it costs a woman to save herself.

title: Saxophone & Piano Classics Duo “Contrasts”
description: Duo “Contrasts” returns to Brighton Fridge with new program covering fiery classics, lyrical gems, and original compositions. Formed by saxophonist and pianist with extensive chamber and solo experience, the duo focuses on expanding the classical repertoire for saxophone while preserving the clarity and balance of traditional chamber music. The program features carefully curated works from the 19th and 20th centuries alongside newly written pieces by the performers, created to highlight rhythmic precision and expressive depth. Each composition has been selected to demonstrate the versatility of the saxophone within a classical setting, from cantabile lines reminiscent of vocal arias to agile, technically demanding passages. The piano part is treated as an equal partner, shaping texture and structure rather than simply accompanying. Duo “Contrasts” is aiming to make classical music accessible without compromising artistic standards, welcoming questions after the concert.

title: Scary Mary
description: Daughter of Feminism, Mother of Sci-Fi, the original Goth girlfriend: Mary Shelley is spooky AF. Scary Mary is a darkly funny, gothic deep-dive about the bold, talented, and spooky AF Mary Shelley. The Monster Mash-Up of Siouxsie Sioux and a Tim Burton leading lady, Scary Mary is here to drag her secrets out of the crypt and onto the stage. Find out if she really lost her virginity on her mother’s grave, how her smash-hit Frankenstein galvanised from winning Lord Byron’s Lake Geneva Summer of 1816 ghost-story contest, and exactly what is inside that heart-shaped box in her writing desk... Part Post-Punk fever-dream, part confessional séance and the debut show from Friday’s Child Productions, Scary Mary crashes sizzling solo gig-theatre into historical profile to resurrect the literary icon whose life was every bit as strange, electric, and genre-defying as the monster she created.

title: Science Magic: Messy Mayhem
description: Ignite young imaginations and nurture a lifelong love of science with Science Magic —a high-energy, interactive comedy experience designed for children aged 5 to 12. Created and performed by a comedian and zoologist who has delighted audiences in over 20 countries, this show combines clever science tricks with laugh-out-loud moments, all using everyday objects kids can find at home. Each performance demystifies scientific concepts, encouraging children to see themselves as budding scientists who can experiment, explore, and discover on their own. Science Magic is more than entertainment; it’s an accessible gateway to curiosity and learning, priced affordably so that every child, regardless of background, can experience the wonder of science. Let’s inspire the next generation of scientists by showing them that magic is real—when you understand the science behind it!

title: Scott Manley Hadley: Bald Personality Disorder
description: Scott Manley Hadley aka SOLID BALD has no hair. And no worries. Well, some worries... This alternative comedy WIP performance boasts jokes, sure, but also sequins, thighs, an interactive bald-themed quiz, poetry, costumes, live shaving (risk assessment permitting), original music, and plenty of surprises. To paraphrase Cher, do you believe in life after hair loss? Scott Manley Hadley does, so why not watch as Scott explores body image, mental health, gender, parenting, millennial life, financial hardship, dog ownership and finding hope even when you don't have hair. It can be done! Scott Manley Hadley is a poet turned comedian who has been Highly Commended in the Forward Prizes for Poetry and whose blog, TriumphOfTheNow.com, has been quoted in print in the New Yorker. Scott has appeared as a talking head discussing hair loss on BBC3, Metro online and on the Huffington Post. West End New Act of the Year Semi-Finalist 2025. Bald but not Forgotten.

title: Second Thoughts - The Play
description: When Clement Achebe, a successful insurance manager from Lagos, leaves behind a comfortable life in search of greener pastures in England, he soon discovers that dreams abroad aren’t always as golden as they seem. Through laughter, regret, and raw honesty, Clement shares his journey from boardroom to broomstick, exploring migration, ambition, and the fragile balance between pride and survival. This powerful one-man play blends humour and heartbreak in equal measure, bringing to life the universal struggle of finding meaning far from home. Second Thoughts is a touching exploration of identity, belonging, and the choices that define us, a story for anyone who’s ever wondered if the grass is truly greener on the other side.

title: Sex & The Sewer
description: Lydia L'Scabies, putting the art in tart, the pro in prolapse, and the ‘fun gal' in fungal infection. Prepare to be taken on a darkly humorous trip down memory lane. From sexcapades, scandals, suspected arson, and sexually transmitted anything, let Lydia be your guide in this feature-length solo show of your worst nightmares. Sex and the Sewer premiered at Brighton Fringe in 2023 to thunderous applause and perilous screams - don't miss your chance to see this award-winning performer in the flesh. "Lydia L'Scabies... title: Sex, Drugs and DnB
description: In England, the drug related death rates have been on the rise since 2011. A rising problem that needs to be understood... and what better way to understand than to listen to a girlie telling you about addiction AND recovery? An autobiographical show. Showcasing poignant, real world stories from the bottle and beyond. Be among the first people to witness a recovering addict turn their experience into art (a solo performance). Trust me, its going to be one hell of a ride... some would even say an emotional rollercoaster. Get ready to laugh.. Maybe cry and vibe to some banging drum and bass tunes. We collectively have the power to positively affect the rising problem of drug and alcohol related death rates... because stigma is a killer... ‘Beatrice turns her journey of loss and hardship into a fearless celebration of resilience and hard won joy’ - Esme Davis (Freelance director) Be part of the movement destigmatising addiction and recovery, but most importantly, let's party!
