title: Shadow Light: Songs My Brother Taught Me / Exhibition
description: My first exhibition of new work, since I took part in the 2nd Art & Science Symposium & Exhibition at the Tsinghua Academy of Arts in Beijing in 2006. The seeds for this show were sown that same year when a treasured acoustic guitar of mine got broken and instead of ditching it (a luthier informed me it was beyond repair) I began photographing it. 20 years on, I've created 20 works – prints, collage, assemblage – that are inspired by and a tribute to my late brother Stephen who tragically took his own life at age 20. Fifty years on, I’m still shining a light into those shadows.

title: ShakeItUp: The Improvised Shakespeare Show
description: Shakespeare hasn’t written anything new for an age, so we thought we would give him a hand... Using his language, your audience suggestions, and our overactive imaginations, ShakeItUp create a brand-new Shakespeare play live on stage. Pick your authentic Shakespearean genre: Epic History, Romantic Comedy, or Gruesome Tragedy! Pen your wittiest Shakespearean line for our brave actors to pull from their suitably-Shakespearean breeches when they need a helping hand – the twist? They’ll have no idea what the line will be before they say it! Then sit back and watch the hilarity unfold in a totally bonkers play that Willy Shakes himself would be proud of. Expect big laughs, high drama, and plenty of Bard-based Bedlam!

title: Shakespeare’s Lost
description: The Brighton Shakespeare Company returns, this time with a new play, a comedy inspired by the works of the bard. Waiting for Godot meets William Shakespeare via the cartoons of Gary Larson. A man washes up on another castaway's island, a teacher who is in possession of an icon containing a copy of The Complete Works of William Shakespeare. Unbeknownst to the castaway teacher, the icon contains an imprisoned and mischievous genie who grants them three wishes, one of which is the conjuring up of Shakespeare's Juliet from Romeo & Juliet. Comical confusion ensues when only one of them can communicate with Juliet as all she knows is Elizabethan English. The wishes get more chaotic, interference comes from other dimensions and the plot twists and turns, leaving only one of them marooned on the deserted island.

title: Sherlock Holmes vs Arsène Lupin: A Drag Crime Caper
description: A new camp comedy pitting England’s most famous consulting detective against France’s most infamous gentleman burglar. Arsène Lupin voyages across the Channel to challenge Sherlock Holmes, with Dr John Watson caught in the middle of a tug-of-war between these literary legends. The Department of Ulterior Motives (DUM) specialises in high-concept, low-budget productions that blend physical performance and accessible storytelling with a mischievously political edge. Their debut production, an adaptation of The Man Who Was Thursday, was selected for SPRINT Festival 2026 at Camden People’s Theatre.

title: Shine
description: 'Energetic and vivacious', Freya Bardell performs her Brighton Fringe debut. Part satire, part existential meltdown, Shine is a high-energy comedy about clawing your way out of the rat race. It follows one woman’s wildly overconfident quest to “shine” in a system designed to grind her down. At the centre is a hilariously delusional, self-declared genius who is absolutely certain she can do everything better than everyone else. Packed with laughs and sharp social critique, this show takes the toxic girlboss off her pedestal and asks if ambition is even worth the burnout.

title: Sid Ozalid: Amazing Wonders of Scotland
description: If you like silly you will love Sid. High energy, interactive nonsense for the whole family. From the Edinburgh Fringe to international stages in New York, Paris, Melbourne and beyond, Sid has built a legendary reputation for shows that are unpredictable, magical and utterly unforgettable. A wonderful blend of mayhem, magic, poetry, dad dancing and mentalism. This is your chance to learn all about Scottish culture, why Scotland invented all the cool stuff in the world and finally learn what is hiding under the kilt. You will not believe your eyes or your ears, and may end up being more Scottish than you were on the way in! Expect the impossible. Sid doesn’t just perform magic — he plays with reality. Engaging nonsense for everyone from the age of eight to 80!

title: Sing, Sign and Sensory
description: Step into a world designed especially for your little one. Sing, Sign & Sensory invites babies (0–24 months) and curious young sensory seekers into magical, purpose-built inflatable pods where their very first creative adventures begin. Inside these gentle, glowing spaces, your baby can explore a soothing blend of music, simple storytelling and interactive animations—all crafted to spark early communication, bonding and joyful discovery. Each workshop is thoughtfully inclusive and easy to enjoy, offering families a relaxed way to introduce their baby to the wonder of theatre. With soft projections dancing overhead, tiny breezes from fans, textured props to grasp, and safe, comforting scents filling the air, every moment encourages your little one to engage, respond and connect. Whether it’s their first time reaching for a new texture, following a floating light or signing along to a song, Sing, Sign & Sensory creates memories you’ll treasure.

title: Sing-a-Long-a-Musical
description: Singalongamusical is an interactive singing experience like no other. All the song words are projected up on the big screen, for you to sing along with our dynamic hosts - all accompanied by a live band of West End theatre musicians. There’s something for everyone in the medleys of songs - whether you love the golden age of Rodgers & Hammerstein, or you prefer Disney family favourites. You’ll be encouraged to dress up with us and act out Summer Nights from Grease, and we will be Defying Gravity* just like Elphaba in Wicked. We guarantee you’ll be Les(s) Miserable when you come along! *no actual flying can be guaranteed

title: Skysteamer to Athens: Steampunk Storytelling Extravaganza
description: All aboard - the skysteamer is about to depart! Join C.J. Carter-Stephenson as he brings his steampunk novelette SKYSTEAMER TO ATHENS vividly to life. See a model airship soar over your head and meet a talking automaton puppet in this thrilling reader's theatre performance. A lavish sensory experience, which will transport you back to a fictionalised version of the 19th Century, where top hats and corsets are the height of fashion and the wonder of steam is transforming the world. Cast off your mooring ropes and prepare to soar into the clouds.

title: Smoove & Turrell (Live) + Support
description: Get ready for an unforgettable night of soul power and killer grooves as Smoove & Turrell bring their explosive live show to the Fringe. Fusing northern funk, vintage soul and funky beats, this band have built a reputation for electric performances with the incredible vocals of John Turrell always front and centre. From deep, heartfelt soul ballads to floor-filling funk anthems, Smoove & Turrell deliver a set packed with fan favourites and fresh cuts alike. Their chemistry is undeniable, their sound is timeless yet modern, and their live band brings an intensity that records simply can’t contain.

title: SNAILS OF THE UNEXPECTED
description: Chaos Theory is a sketch group consisting of four actor/writers – Mikylajane Durkan, Yasser Kayani, Maria Beadell and Bruce Williams, with director/writer Stephen Dinsdale. And there is music with Bunny Hopkins, legendary bluesman! Between them they have played a puddle in an advert, been the face of British Gas at the Olympics, worked with David Icke, worked with Dario Fo, stripped for Danish TV and written for BBC Films, BBC TV, ITV, BBC Radio 4 and the West End. Chaos Theory has been going for about six years and has played the Edinburgh Fringe, the Brighton Fringe several times, the Camden Fringe many times, Faversham Fringe and the Vault Festival. They write their own sketches and the show is always developing and they also support new writing from others. Sketches vary a lot, from light and frothy to dark and disturbing to running for your mum – but in a good way! From the icecream godfather to surgeons sexting to a wasp café. Pretty quickfire. Always funny!

title: Snow Along the Line
description: Snow Along the Line traces the life of an extraordinary yet ordinary woman whose journey spans nearly a century of change. Born in 1928, only months before the Equal Franchise Act granted women full voting rights, her story begins in the industrial heartland of North East England and traces the decline of the mining industry to South Wales. Through songs, poems, archival images, backing tracks and narration, theatre-maker and musician Phil Jones, and actor Hannah Barrie, captures the rhythm of a life shaped by both sweeping change and everyday triumph. The production explores themes relating to death of a loved one which some audience members may find upsetting.

title: Sock Party
description: Tom and Marta arrive at a sock-themed party. They didn’t expect to meet. Marta is bold, and Tom doesn’t know how to have a conversation with anyone, but they end up understanding each other more than they could’ve anticipated. In a colourful expression of drama, clowning and original music, Sock Party is a playful journey through love and connection, asking how as adults we manage to create new friends, celebrate and play. From the Creative Team that brought you Blip, 'a tender meditation on intergenerational tensions' (The Scotsman) that sold out Brighton Fringe in 2025.

title: Something! Sometime! Someone!
description: How baffling that people hear Charlie as she enunciates her name Rushali (ROO-SHA-LEE) - In this debut comedy hour a cacophony unveils as you uncover that yes it is rude to call an Indian Aunty - “a peanut eating pachyderm”. Just like New York City’s sewer covers, Rushali was made in India - right in the heart of Bollywood - Mumbai. She smuggled herself(legally) into the USA in her twenties only to find herself in Amsterdam’s comedy scene (no, not the red light district.. cmon.. what a cliche!) attempting to make the Dutch laugh - a feat not for the faint hearted. As she navigates this world wondering if her dead grandmother is controlling it - she pays homage to Bollywood as one rightfully must when they are born dancing to it's tunes.

title: Son of Dave @ The Electro Blues Shack
description: Blues — but not as you know it. The legendary Son of Dave returns to the UK for a rare live performance at the Electro Blues Shack, Caravanserai. From the wilder edges of foot-stomping Americana to gritty electro-blues we’re reviving our infamous club that was a resident at The Green Door Store for many years. Headlining is Son of Dave, a one-man blues phenomenon (Benjamin Darvill, formerly of Crash Test Dummies) whose hugely influential looping harmonica, beatbox grooves, stomping rhythms and growling vocals have earned cult status — from soundtracking Breaking Bad and Preacher to appearances on Later... with Jools Holland. Support comes from Brighton’s Country Ties, bringing their blend of country, bluegrass and Americana with rich harmonies and barn-dance energy. Between sets, DJs including Nick Hollywood (Electro Blues/White Mink) and The Chairman spin vinyl blues and vintage cuts. Live blues, country, Americana and bluestronica - the perfect way to close the Fringe weekend.

title: Sound of Silence
description: A woman sitting in prison recounting her story in word and song what happens when heaven turns to hell? When the components of dancing through life turn sour and the relationship breaks down Love turning to hate, despair turning to anger coping turning to struggle where hurt is nothing but pain and suffering. The struggle is taking back control of unsustainable mood, thoughts and behaviour. A never ending inner battle of fighting an internal war.

title: Sounds Familiar Music Quiz
description: Sounds Familiar is more than just a Music Quiz, it’s the perfect combination of your favourite playlist and the best party you’ve ever been to. We're the only Music Quiz to have sold out The Camden Roundhouse, supported beloved Boy Band '5ive' and played Glastonbury festival more times than David Bowie. Expect rounds stuffed full of Chart Classics, Old Skool Anthems, Indie Bangers, marvellous Mash-Ups, Power Ballads, Guilty Pleasures and over 100 killer tunes from the 60s to the current day, all wrapped up in one epic Quiz. 10 TO A TEAM MAX. title: Space Planet Mission: The Improvised Sci-Fi Epic
description: Stories that boldly go to strange new worlds. Characters created at the speed of light. This is Space Planet Mission, the most spontaneous sci-fi epic in the universe. It could be set in Earth's dystopian near-future, or a long time ago in a galaxy far, far away. But you won't need to know your Klingons from your Cylons. Join us for a supernova of cosmic comedy, accompanied live by an improvised soundtrack. Featuring a stellar cast from hit improv shows including 3, St Doctor's Hospital, and the London 50-Hour Improvathon. Between them, they have reached the final of the Leicester Square Sketch-Off 2025, been runners up in the British Comedy Guide Pro Talent Awards, and appeared on E4, BBC Radio 4 and BBC Comedy Feeds.

title: Spalien Acecraft: Speaningless Moonerisms
description: Fourteen years after their official final gig, Spalien Acecraft are back on planet Earth with their unique streak of interactive intergalactic latter-day cabaret for pop-sensible extra-terrestrials. Featuring original celestial minstrel Johnny Acecraft, three authenticated relations and one tolerated acquaintance: sisters Maliniverse and The Elbot on harmonies harvested from shared childhoods and questionable life choices, wife Jopiter on genuine musical talent, and key-stabber extraordinaire Nathanova. The band will emulate their past daftness with favourites including Orange for a Head, Man with the Magical Armpits, Speedy Bikini and more, and they may also get round to writing some new stuff. Audience participation will be gently encouraged and enthusiastically misinterpreted. Bring your offspring: the band will censor their extremest egregiousnesses. This is a pay-what-you-can, bequeath-what-you-believe, share-what-you-dare affair before they re-disappear into the shimmery nowhere.

title: Spiegeltent Soul Afternooner
description: Join us for a total celebration of the northern soul scene. Get ‘out on the floor’ with 3 hours of Northern Soul classics, Motown favourites and a host of monster soul tunes. Featuring DJ Colin Slater, a founder member of the 'Across the Tracks' soul club run out of an iconic Brighton venue, and old soulie Neil St John, a Northern Soul DJ in South Yorkshire clubs for 10 years in the 70s and a regular attendee at The Twisted Wheel, Wigan Casino and the Torch. Come along and help us celebrate the return of the historic Tivoli Spiegeltent and dance your socks off!

title: Spiralizers
description: Spiralizers is a chaotic, cathartic four-way split bill born straight out of a WhatsApp group. When comedians Oni, Caroline, Vyome and Selam realised their group chat was basically a live feed of collective spiralling, they did the only sensible thing — turned it into a show. Expect sharp stand-up, unfiltered oversharing, and three comedians processing life in real time. From existential dread to everyday absurdities, Spiralizers takes the kind of thoughts that usually stay in the group chat and throws them onto the stage. Come watch the spiral unfold live at Brighton Fringe.

title: Spirited Away Ghibli Taste & Tales
description: Join us for a magical evening filled with delicious Ghibli-inspired treats and enchanting tales from Spirited Away! Welcome to Ghibli Taste & Tales presents Spirited Away! Join us for an unforgettable evening of mystery, magic, and mouth-watering dishes inspired by Studio Ghibli’s iconic masterpiece. Hosted at Blossoms Cocktail Bar and Restaurant, this in-person event invites you to step into the spirit world and follow Chihiro’s journey through a land of strange creatures, ancient bathhouses, and unforgettable encounters. Lose yourself in the atmospheric setting, savour themed cocktails and gourmet bites, and relive the wonder of this beloved film with fellow fans. Don’t miss your chance to experience the magic of Spirited Away brought to life through food, drink, and storytelling. Enjoy three enchanting courses, paired with two cocktails to guide you through this otherworldly adventure. (Non-alcoholic options available). Vegan and vegetarian options available on request.

title: spit nights | poetry meets improv jazz
description: A night of never-to-be-repeated moments, combining the most exciting talent on the jazz and poetry circuit. Europe's biggest poetry event comes to Brighton's iconic The Old Market for a night of improvised jazz and spoken word. Combining the most exciting talent on the jazz and poetry circuit, where no two events are ever the same. The one rule of the show: no rehearsals. Expect explosive interludes, unpredictable collaborations, and poetry designed for anyone who thought they didn’t like it. Headliners will be announced online and on our socials @spitnights “the most exciting poetry night in the country” - Harry Baker (poet/author) “spit nights gives me confidence about the next generation” - Jordan Stephens (musician/actor/author)

title: SQUELCH!
description: Would you jump down the toilet to rescue something you loved? Complete opposites and nothing alike - two brothers are stuck in a bitter sibling rivalry. When they lose their goldfish down the toilet, they embark on a bombastic adventure down the drain, meeting strange monsters and menacing creatures... Split Hairs return to Brighton Fringe with their unique and tender story of love, family, and finding your way through the unknown. With riotous slapstick comedy, enchanting puppetry, and an exuberant live score - join us for a bizarre and exciting journey where even in the messiest moments we can find hope, joy and kindness. For ages 5-105.

title: Stepdads: The Stepdads Les Mis
description: Cult comedy pioneers Stepdads present the world’s first ever stage adaptation of Victor Hugo’s novel. They will be joined by alternative comedy’s finest to faithfully adapt this colossal epic, including the 58 page digression on the Battle of Waterloo. Previous acts include: Mark Silcox, Joz Norris, Josh Glanc, Elf Lyons, Lorna Rose Treen, Christian Brighty and more! Stepdads are BAFTA-winning Disney+ heartthrob Luke Rollason (★★★★ “gloriously daft” Telegraph) and The Flop’s Tom Penn (★★★★★ “Nothing happens” BeyondTheJoke).

title: Stephen Catling: Clownfish Out Of Water
description: When clowns can't breathe water and fish can't breathe air- what's a poor clownfish to do in this cruel world? In 2025, Catling (Malcolm Hardee Award Nominee 2023 & 2025) lost his slug mask. It was kinda essential part of his cult hit show "Moving on...really really slowly." This time, Stephen explores his life now that Autism has been “unmasked,” as well as his fin flapping rage against an increasingly soulless entertainment industry. "Fans of Harry Hill will appreciate the quickfire absurdity of the hour, cascading with daft jokes, preposterous props and manic sound effects. " Chortle

title: Stompa Phunk
description: Stompa Phunk has been knocking around Brighton long enough to know what works, the right music, the right moment. Our annual Brighton Fringe party at the Spiegeltent has become something of a ritual. One night a year where this iconic festival venue swaps cabaret for house music. Wood panels, low ceiling, dancefloor. It shouldn’t work for acid house... but it does. Residents Serge Santiago and David Parr plus a special guest TBA. Expect whatever feels right in the moment. It’s intimate, and it sells out.

title: Strange Face Adventures with a Lost Nick Drake Recording. The story continues...
description: An unforgettable storytelling experience that blends mystery, humour and genuine emotional punch. In his original acclaimed award-winning solo show, Michael Burdett recounted the true tale of how he stumbled upon a long-lost Nick Drake recording — a discovery that would have been remarkable enough on its own. The story now takes an unbelievable turn in this updated new version of the show when, against all odds, Burdett uncovers a second lost Drake recording. Armed with the first recording, Burdett travelled across the UK, inviting strangers in shops and living rooms, on mountains and street corners to listen. Their reactions, funny, moving, and profoundly human, became the heart of an adventure that reveals why Drake’s music still resonates so powerfully. Through vivid storytelling, arresting photographs, and warm, sharply observed humour, Burdett crafts a deeply engaging atmosphere. Featuring on-screen revelations from James Acaster and others. Coincidences abound.

title: Strippers Advice Bureau
description: Do you have a problem? Struggling to know where to turn? Bring your dilemmas to this comedy show, where real life strippers hold space, listen carefully and offer free, non-judgemental life advice to all. Stacey Clare (creator of hit Fringe show "Ask A Stripper", author of "The Ethical Stripper" and cast member of "The Illicit Thrill") and friends are on a new mission to bring empathy, compassion and understanding out of the strip club and onto the stage. These unshockable, hardened and world-weary women have been giving free therapy to men in strip clubs for years, so what better way to share their wealth of knowledge and experience by helping others overcome their guilt, shame and neuroses. All dilemmas are anonymous as these atypical agony aunts understand completely the importance of discretion. So if you're looking for help and need a safe space with a friendly face... come share your load with a couple of sex workers. Laughter, wisdom and sage advice guaranteed.

title: Stuart Thomas: Bad Fatty (Work In Progress)
description: Stuart Thomas returns with Bad Fatty, a bold, fast-paced stand-up show from a slightly chaotic Welsh man who’s done being polite about how the world sees fat people. Stuart tears into the clichés and lazy stereotypes—especially the way fat men get flattened into the wheezing sidekick, the punchline, or the cautionary tale. He’s had enough, and he’s taking no prisoners. Alongside the big stuff, Stuart dives into the simple joys that keep him going—most importantly, his undying love for Premier Inn. The purple haven. The land of perfect pillows, identical hallways, and a breakfast buffet that feels like a hug. It’s not glamorous, but God it’s good. Bisexual, unapologetic, and buzzing with energy, Stuart unpacks what it means to live joyfully in a body the world loves to misunderstand. Bad Fatty is funny, fiery, and full of bite—a celebration of refusing to shrink yourself just to make other people comfortable.

title: Tabula Rasa
description: Four people. One room. Four chairs. One table. No doors. No exit. No memory of how they arrived, and no certainty they’ll ever leave. An absurd, psychological pressure cooker where identities slip, alliances dissolve, and the familiar turns feral. Panic fizzles into laughter; play becomes ritual; every choice counts. As time ticks on, the four discover that the greatest threat might not be the room at all, but the versions of themselves it drags to the surface. People are hell. There will be blood. A comedy. Kind of. TABULA RASA invites you to witness a world wiped clean, where meaning is unstable, reality is negotiable, and starting again is never as simple as it sounds.

title: Tactile
description: “What will be left when I die?” As our lives become more virtual, tangible objects, the things that carry our personal histories, are increasingly disappearing. The play follows 3 siblings, Saf, Chris and Jem, as they reunite to sort through their late mother’s belongings. What begins as a practical task becomes a deeply emotional excavation of memory, love and loss. Through humour, tenderness and confrontation, Tactile examines how grief reshapes the bonds between us and how the things we hold both physical and emotional connect us across generations. At its core, Tactile explores society’s ‘return to the analogue’ as coined on social media, in a culture that is becoming ever more digitised. It examines what “real” connection means at a time when so much of our communication and memory storage happens online. By focusing on the intimate and the material, the piece offers audiences a space to reflect on the value of touch, presence and what we can inherit and learn from each other.

title: Tadiwa Mahlunge: Hakuna Ma Tad Tad
description: “I'm back with a new show about how being a refugee forged my ambition, self belief and why I believe you deserve the same Kanye-level confidence I have (without the life-altering trauma). I have in no way cynically leveraged my life story and timed this tour to profiteer off the tense mood on immigration in the nation, and any such accusation will be met with litigation from my mother." - Tadiwa Tadiwa Mahlunge is one of the fastest-rising stars in British comedy, named by The Times as one of their “Top 3 Stars of Tomorrow”. Tadiwa has appeared on Stand Up Sketch Show (ITV2), Am I The A**hole (Comedy Central), Comedians Giving Lectures (Dave), Comedy Central Live, The Football’s On (TNT Sports), and Live from Aberystwyth Pier (BBC Wales). On radio, he’s been heard on The News Quiz (BBC Radio 4), Best of the Fringe (BBC Radio 4), and What Just Happened (BBC Radio Wales). His stand-up has earned rave reviews across the board: ★★★★ “Expect great things from this charming comic.”

title: Take Shelter!
description: Step back in time to explore a unique part of Brighton's wartime history with one of our air raid shelter tours. During the war, every school would have had its own air raid shelter, now one of the only surviving examples in the UK is under the playground at Downs Junior School. There is lots to do and see during your visit to Take Shelter: Tour the shelter with one of our knowledgeable guides (ticketed), play one of our vintage fair games, browse our World War II museum, chat with our veterans about their wartime experiences, soak up the vintage sounds and learn wartime dances on our dancefloor, or enjoy a cup of tea and bite to eat from Mrs Button's Tea Room and take home a souvenir from the Take Shelter shop.

title: That's Just The Way It's (un)Done
description: “When I was a child I dreamt of happily ever after. When I was 27 my dreams came true. Somewhere along the way, I realised my dreams were not my own.” That’s Just The Way It’s (un)Done is an abstract autobiographical theatrical cabaret that dances through the dangers of heteronormative conditioning into a world of queer fluidity. Join Rowan For Now as they explore what it means to outgrow yourself – shedding the skins of strict narratives and peeling back everything they knew to be true. Through nostalgic imagery, storytelling, lip-sync, humour, and chaos, Rowan reveals the freedom and grief that emerge when normative paradigms begin to unravel. Developed as part of Rowan’s MA in Queer Performance, this piece continues to evolve, much like its creator. This iteration is both a performance and a process, part ritual, part revelation. It invites audiences into an experience of untangling and reimagining. A queer Happily Never After.
description_html:
“When I was a child I dreamt of happily ever after. When I was 27 my dreams came true. Somewhere along the way, I realised my dreams were not my own.”
That’s Just The Way It’s (un)Done is an abstract autobiographical theatrical cabaret that dances through the dangers of heteronormative conditioning into a world of queer fluidity.

Join Rowan For Now as they explore what it means to outgrow yourself – shedding the skins of strict narratives and peeling back everything they knew to be true. Through nostalgic imagery, storytelling, lip-sync, humour, and chaos, Rowan reveals the freedom and grief that emerge when normative paradigms begin to unravel.

Developed as part of Rowan’s MA in Queer Performance, this piece continues to evolve, much like its creator. This iteration is both a performance and a process, part ritual, part revelation. It invites audiences into an experience of untangling and reimagining. A queer Happily Never After.
meta_description: A queer Happily Never After. Shedding the skins of normativity. A theatrical cabaret exploring what it means to outgrow yourself. - The Actors Theatre - Tue 12th 2026, Wed 13th 2026

title: The Art of Touch: A Journey into Massage
description: Step away from the rush of everyday life and immerse yourself in The Art of Touch, a blissful Brighton Fringe workshop celebrating the beauty of connection and the craft of massage. Guided by the renowned Jing Institute, you’ll spend three restorative hours discovering the principles of professional massage in a warm, nurturing space. Give and receive deeply soothing treatments while learning effective, hands-on techniques you can use straight away. More than relaxation, this is an invitation to explore a new path, whether you’re curious or considering a career in massage therapy, it’s the perfect introduction to the skills, philosophy, and joy of holistic touch. Under the expert guidance of Jing Directors Rachel Fairweather and Meghan Mari, you’ll be supported every step of the way. Come alone or with a friend; everyone is welcome. Leave feeling inspired, grounded, and gently transformed, a few radiant hours that could spark a whole new journey.

title: The Blue Knot
description: Are you ready to set sail onto the open seas and save the people of the harbour? Time is running out and we need your help! The Blue Knot is a 45-minute interactive sea-shanty musical adventure for children aged 4 to 8 years old and their families. It features original songs performed live on musical instruments, alongside singing and moving to music together. Our story tells the tale of a Captain who is on a journey to help feed the harbour community. The Captain wants to find food for the people of the harbour but will greed get the better of them? WINNER - Festival Directors' Discretionary Award - Durham Fringe Festival 2025 Created and performed by Merryweather Theatre, an arts company which writes and develops musical adventures specifically for young children and their families. With 50+ performances in theatres and arts venues around the UK, we are very excited to be bringing The Blue Knot to Brighton Fringe!

title: The Brighton Beach Boys perform Pet Sounds vs Sgt Pepper
description: Yes! The Brighton Beach Boys return with the Psychedelic Orchestra to perform Pet Sounds vs Sgt Pepper's Lonely Heart Club Band. Bring the show back to it's original home, this will be the first time the band have performed these two classic pillars of popular music in a decade. Featuring all of the usual fun, with a forensic ear for recreating the original sounds of each album, accompanied by strings and brass, making the music come alive. Please come and enjoy the genius of Brian Wilson, the melodic genius of Paul McCartney, the wit of John Lennon, the experimentalism of George Harrison, recreated by Steve Wrigley, Glen Richardson, Theseus Gerrard, Adrian Marshall, Jono Harrison, Tom Arnold, Charlotte Glasson with Special Guests.

title: The Bubble Show®
description: Step right up, ladies & gentlemen, boys & girls! Mr Bubbles is back in town, and he’s making a splash with The Bubble Show®! Enter a world of wonder with the internationally acclaimed bubble show that has delighted thousands of families across festivals and theatres worldwide. Created and performed by Guinness World Record holder The Highland Joker, this theatrical experience blends storytelling, magic, science and bubble artistry into one unforgettable journey. From tiny bubbles to giant bubbles, smoke bubbles, fire bubbles, a sparkling bubble cube and an erupting bubble volcano - every moment bursts with colour, excitement and interactive fun. A joyful, family-friendly celebration of creativity that leaves audiences smiling, inspired and spellbound. A Brighton Fringe favourite since 2019 and proudly made in Sussex. Other similarly titled shows now exist, but if you want the original award-winning production, be sure to book The Bubble Show® at Wagner Hall.

title: The Changing Room
description: A vivid, fast-paced portrait of teenagers on the brink, set in and around a swimming pool, The Changing Room follows a group of young people as they navigate identity, friendship, and the restless energy of growing up. Moments collide and overlap – worries are shared, friendships formed as the ground begins to shift beneath their feet. This is a play about losing your footing and stepping into who you might become... The award-winning Kaleidoscope Theatre Company brings this vibrant, funny, and touching play to life with its trademark ensemble work and bold staging, offering an honest look at the exhilaration and uncertainty of youth – and one magnificently embarrassing dad.

title: The Coffee Machine is Broken
description: The Vitruvian Theatre Company’s Brighton Fringe debut! A hospital waiting room - everyone’s favourite place to spend an evening. A woman faces an impossible choice between life and death. A father and son reach for each other but miss every time. A daughter split open by grief, longing for it to end. A young nurse stretched past breaking point. A man, drunk, loud, and impossible to ignore. All different. All waiting. Over the course of a single night in, ‘The Coffee Machine is Broken’ explores the fragile connections people cling to when everything feels uncertain. Lives hang in the balance, strangers collide, and love is tested in all its messiest forms. Conversations overlap, inner thoughts spill out, and moments of connection appear where you least expect them. Tender and darkly funny, this play captures the raw, human moments that exist between life and death, when all you can do is wait.

Conversations overlap, inner thoughts spill out, and moments of connection appear where you least expect them. Tender and darkly funny, this play captures the raw, human moments that exist between life and death, when all you can do is wait.

title: The Complete History of Videogames
description: Louie Falls loves videogames. He's written a quiz book, he has gaming tattoos and even has a useless degree in videogame culture. So join him for his videogame stand up show, as he shares his knowledge of the greatest hobby in the world, and its unusual history. What was the first videogame? Are there actually millions of copies of E.T. buried in the desert? Can Doom really run on a pregnancy test? Come and find out! Maybe that videogame degree wasn't so useless after all.

title: The Cringe Factor: Rock and Roll Part 2
description: The Cringiest show at the Fringe is back... with a sequel nobody asked for! Join Ian Fox, a comedian who's aged like a carton of milk left in the sun, and his guests dissecting the cringe-worthy, the mind-boggling, and the downright bizarre. This hit new show from the 2025 Fringe returns with a brand-new selection clips, that should've stayed buried and with a ton of cultural debris from yesteryear for the comics to wade through. Belt yourself in for sharp wit, awkward laughter, and a whole lot of nostalgia or nausea digging up the cultural graveyard. The host and guests may have aged badly, but these cultural relics have aged even worse. Join us for a no-holds-barred dissection of the past. Expect sharp wit, awkward humour, and a whole lot of WTF moments. "Ian Fox is Several divisions higher in comedy league" - Chortle, Edinburgh Fringe "C*nty F*cks" - Kate Copstick, as seen on Childrens TV in the 80s & 90s, and erudite Scotsman Journalist

This hit new show from the 2025 Fringe returns with a brand-new selection clips, that should've stayed buried and with a ton of cultural debris from yesteryear for the comics to wade through.

Belt yourself in for sharp wit, awkward laughter, and a whole lot of nostalgia or nausea digging up the cultural graveyard.

The host and guests may have aged badly, but these cultural relics have aged even worse. Join us for a no-holds-barred dissection of the past. Expect sharp wit, awkward humour, and a whole lot of WTF moments.

"Ian Fox is Several divisions higher in comedy league" - Chortle, Edinburgh Fringe
"C*nty F*cks" - Kate Copstick, as seen on Childrens TV in the 80s & 90s, and erudite Scotsman Journalist

title: The Daddy long legs rides again
description: Starting where Banjo Groyne meets Madeira Drive next to The Volk's railway shed, everyone will experience The Pioneer train (The Daddy Long Legs) using their phones with an innovative smartphone app created by artist Luciana Haill. The historical facts and cultural connections are presented in a talk that becomes a guided walk for 1.3 miles as we follow part of the route this train made between 1896 and 1901. At the end, we will all have an interior tour inside the opulent carriage and upper deck of The Daddy Long Legs, stopping for souvenir photos. This is fully accessible using pavements and paths at beach level and ending close to the car wash near ASDA in Brighton Marina.

title: THE DANCE OF TIME (a black comedy about life and death)
description: In this black comedy there are shouts of joy, cries of despair and a roller coaster of emotions as Tom and Mary struggle to come to terms with an uncertain future, with their complicated relationship, and with the meaning of life. Whatever that is of course. In hilarious exchanges, they launch into a thought-provoking dialogue about some of the broader questions of life and death while at the same time managing to keep us all amused. Throw in a little music, some shenanigans, a love story, bravery in the face of impending tragedy, some wry comments on modern society, a little dancing, some rather surprising moments, and you have a play that not only has messages about life that you did not know you needed - but is also a play that is also guaranteed to make you laugh. Only a few performances at the Fringe before going on tour. With two top class actors, this play, written by Tim Coakley and staged by renowned director Petina Hapgood, is a play you simply have to see.

title: The Designated Fire Wardens
description: Join this up-and-coming comedy duo for an hour of riotous laughs, whacky characters, quick-fire sketches and some groovy tunes. Who do you trust in a moment of crisis? The relevant emergency services or a couple of newly-certified, haphazard and rather chaotic busybodies? The second one. Obviously. They have whistles. Armed with a high-vis vest, half a tinfoil blanket and an unwavering air of authority, these Designated Fire Wardens promise to take you on a slightly mad journey filled with absurd characters, surreal musical interludes and plenty of totally gleeful comedy. Seriously, this is not a drill. Straight from Soho Theatre’s Character Comedy Lab and off the back of sold-out performances in London, Durham, Leeds and Bristol, this is an unmissable debut hour of sketches from the ‘ridiculously talented’ (EdFringe Review) duo, Alannah and Phoenix, named by Chortle as ‘one to watch’. From the winners of Best Sketch Show at Edinburgh Fringe 2025 (Derek Awards).

title: The Doris Show
description: The Doris Show starring Doris Daydream celebrates the music and the life of Doris Day, the legendary famous movie star and singer. The one hour tribute show gives an insight into Doris’ life - how she started, her husbands and leading men (there were lots of those!) and backgrounds to the songs. The show features all of Doris’ big hits including Secret Love, Deadwood Stage, Black Hills of Dakota, Perhaps, Perhaps, Perhaps and of course the acclaimed Que Sera, Sera.

title: The Double G Comedy Rodeo
description: A mash-up of chaos, charisma, and killer comedy, the Rodeo flips everything you think you know about a comedy show. It’s in-your-face, up-close, and happily smashes the fourth wall, pulling the audience right into the action. Expect fast laughs, fearless performances, and a show as unpredictable as it is unforgettable. Spontaneity rules; comics bounce off the crowd, moments spiral into mayhem, and somehow it all lands as a perfectly timed, delightfully anarchic experience. Think mad meets bad, led by a ringmaster with a wicked grin and a talent for finding hilarity in the chaos. Bold, raw, and joyfully unfiltered, it’s a celebration of live comedy at its most explosive. At its heart, the Rodeo is about connection, between performer and audience, between laughter and surprise. Those in-the-moment sparks, sharp quips, playful interactions, and unexpected outbursts keep you on your toes and grinning all night. Wild, raucous, and unapologetically fun.

title: The Drunk and Heckle Show
description: Veteran Canadian comic David Tsonos hates one thing: the drunk heckler. Once the sun goes down in Brighton they come out, and since he can't beat them, he is inviting them. The audience are issued paddles and raising them when they wish to heckle which makes for a controlled-chaos atmosphere. Watch David compere with three different international guests each night as they take on the hecklers. Some comics say it is their favourite show to perform on.

title: The Elephant In The Room
description: Step into the extraordinary life of Joseph Merrick in a moving new musical that tells the true story of the Elephant Man. Through powerful songs and heartfelt storytelling, this production shines a light on his courage, humanity, and determination to live life on his own terms. More than a tale of adversity, it is a celebration of resilience and the universal desire to belong.

title: The Emperor's New Clothes
description: Using Czech-style marionettes, ‘The Emperor’s New Clothes’ is an energetic and fun rendition of the classic Hans Christian Andersen story. Following the success of last year's show, this traditional tale is back at the fringe for children and families to enjoy. Join the pompous, spoilt Emperor and his fawning court as he finally gets his embarrassing comeuppance. Brought to life by one performer and six marionettes, join Strings Attached Puppets for this tale of vanity, lies and underpants.

title: The Eric Tinker Experience
description: Aussie/pommie stand up and musical comedian Eric Tinker brings his “bloody weird and so much fun” ‘Ericsperience’ to Brighton, for one show only! Between eccentric gags he raps as MC Boomer, sings a love song to Margaret Thatcher, appears as a meditating cow and more. Also Eric will be ‘reading the mind’ of an audience member and improvise a song from their thoughts! How wrong can that go? Eric has performed successful shows at the Adelaide, Edinburgh, Perth (WA) and Brisbane Fringes over the last seven years. He has appeared in clubs, pubs and festivals around Australia and in his native England. His style is influenced by the likes of Spike Milligan and The Bonzo Dog Band. "His songs, like Philosophy, are brilliant, dripping with double entendres and fantasy.” The Barefoot Review. “Eric Tinker is blessed with a keen sense of the ridiculous.” The Clothesline. "Eric's show is really bloody weird and so much fun!" Kate Rudge, Bris Funny Fest director.

title: The Families Behind Our Healthcare Heroes
description: Healthcare workers face high stress and burnout, yet little attention is given to the families who support them. This project explores the experiences of family members through creative, participatory workshops using comic‑making and poetry writing. The sessions introduce these methods & offers a space to reflect on the emotional and practical demands placed on families, including those from marginalised or under‑represented backgrounds. This is a facilitated participatory workshop for family members of healthcare workers, who live with them and support them, where the participants will be drawing on their experiences to make poetry and/ or comics. All materials are provided. You may choose whether or not to consent to your artwork being used in future research publications (ethical approval pending at the time of writing, information sheet and consent sheet will be provided on the day); you are welcome to join the workshop even if you prefer not to share your creations for research.

title: The Fools Present: Nerd Ball
description: The Fools and their nerdy silliness are back at the Fringe! Have you watched a tv show more times than you can count? Cosplayed your favourite characters? Written bizarre fanfiction? Reader, do you remember the horrible golden years of tumblr? If any of this resonates with you, Nerd Ball welcomes you with open arms. Join the best of Brighton's alternative performance scene in a celebratory explosion of our special interests and fan culture. Expect outlandish drag, outrageous vocals, and performers who will dazzle you with the lengths they are willing to go to for their favourite things. We promise you weirdness, wonder and warmth, in our exploration of how the things we love make us who we are.

title: The Fox, The Slipper and The Severed Head
description: After a first attempt and development at Brighton Fringe 2025, The Fox, The Slipper and The Severed Head is back in 2026 for a soft launch! A cautionary tale to those who do not believe in the supernatural. When the mediocre Scholar Wang drunkenly takes on a dare to invite the Red Judge of the Underworld to dinner, he finds his fortune magically turning for the better. But be careful of what you wish for. A Chinese Grimm tale of greed, lust and vengeance, based on the colourful ghost and fox tales in Strange Tales from a Chinese Studios by Pu SongLing in C18th. From the teams behind "The Ballad of Mulan" and "Moby Dick".

title: The Goth-Father
description: “Revenge is a dish best served GOTH” Back-comb your hair, apply the purple lipstick and pull up your fishnet tights. Lads, brace yourselves for an evening of comic mayhem, as the most deluded man in rock returns to become the legend he knows he always should have been. In 1983, Athos Dark created Gothic Rock with his band The Leeds 6. Now, after 40 years in the wasteland, The Gothfather is back. And you are invited to the launch of The Leeds 6’s new greatest album: Exile. “If Syd Barrett did comedy, it would be The Gothfather” - Andy Thomas, Des Kapital With special guest bass player: Crow Maggie.

title: The Great 80s & 90s Movie Soundtrack Songbook
description: After three sell-out shows last year, The Great 80s & 90s Movie Soundtrack Songbook returns for its second year — bigger, louder, and packed with feel-good anthems. Created and performed by Paul Diello, this high-energy live show is a full-throttle celebration of the movie soundtracks that defined the era. Backed by an electrifying 8-piece band and powered by punchy three-part harmonies, the night is wall-to-wall hits from the big screen. From Top Gun and Dirty Dancing to The Bodyguard and Pretty Woman, every song is designed to get the room singing along. Big choruses, iconic moments, and pure nostalgia — this is a party from the first note to the last.

title: The Hunger Artist by Franz Kafka
description: Have you ever given so much of YOURSELF to something you LOVE, there’s nothing left of YOU? How far would you go to find MEANING, to fulfil your PASSION? What is Art? And what does it mean to be an Artist? Franz Kafka’s ‘Hunger Artist’ nourishes his soul the only way he knows how, by starving himself, in his small, barred cage, before crowds of spectators, some admiring, some sneering and contemptuous. Seeking acclaim for his craft and acceptance, validation, spiritual meaning and perhaps love from his audience, this is his ‘art’ and, though often torturous for him, he will stop at nothing in his pursuit of perfection. In this, Franz Kafka’s final work, he died on 3rd June 1924, we celebrate his life and work with this Solo retelling of this dramatic short story. Performed by Jonathan Sidgwick (Eastenders, Coronation Street, Hollyoaks, The Mousetrap West End Tour. Jon has worked several times for Steven Berkoff (Messiah at The Old Vic; Oedipus - Edinburgh Festival, Spoleto Festival).

title: The Illustrious Reign of King Louis XIX (In its entirety)
description: Paris, 1830. King Charles X unwillingly signs the final document of his reign—his abdication. This clears the path for his son and heir, Louis XIX, to ascend the throne and usher in a new era of prosperity, wisdom, and dignified leadership for the Kingdom of France. Twenty minutes later, it's over. House of The Onion presents the untold and (mostly) true story of history's shortest-reigning monarch. A young king inherits a nation in crisis. Does he have what it takes? Absolutely not. Does anyone tell him this? Also no. What follows is a glorious disaster. Advisors nod along. Courtiers applaud. Someone suggests a war. Someone else suggests two wars. Things escalate with remarkable incompetence - the kind reserved for real monarchs. It is a tale of spectacular confidence and enthusiastic inadequacy. Sometimes history is written by the victors. Sometimes it’s written frantically, while looking for an exit unoccupied by citizens with pitchforks.

title: The London Bridge Show
description: A Human Statue tells an anecdote about a couple on holiday taking a selfie on London Bridge. A world is built using stories, video and props. The Human Statue begins literally and metaphorically stuck. Through the show they will attempt to make sense of the world. The stories will be gory, tragic, beautiful and surreal and find meaning in what can be overlooked. This work-in-progress comedy show is for people who, when chopping the onions, it is the onions who are crying. It will blend comedy and performance art to achieve a mixed result.

title: The Music Of George Harrison - Live! The All Things Must Pass Orchestra's Farewell Tour
description: The ALL THINGS MUST PASS ORCHESTRA, a ten-piece band devoted to the music of George Harrison, has been filling theatres with sell-out performances since 2013. For over a decade, the Brighton-based ensemble – led by singer and multiinstrumentalist Alex Eberhard – has lovingly recreated Harrison’s kaleidoscopic sound world, from the ground-breaking All Things Must Pass album to treasured classics like Something, While My Guitar Gently Weeps, Here Comes The Sun, and My Sweet Lord. The next run of shows will be especially poignant: In 2026, the ALL THINGS MUST PASS ORCHESTRA will embark on their final tour. “Thanks for paying my dad so much respect” Dhani Harrison

title: The Mysteries of Eleusis
description: Can you keep a secret? The Theatre of the Mysteries presents: The Mysteries of Eleusis! Travel to ancient Greece and become an initiate of a 3000 year old secret! Expect immersive myths brought to life through metaphor, music and dance. Meet the gods, walk in their footsteps and revel with them as you are guided through the esoteric to find just how deep the mysteries go! Audience participation is required so you really ought to dress for the occasion. The dress code is ancient Greek myths although it is not mandatory. LGBTQIA+ friendly with music by the Olympic gods of jazz; local legends, Hill Collective. The event will be in 4 parts with regular intervals in-between. The last hour will be a celebration party. We hope you will stay with us for the full journey, but you are welcome to depart, as you wish!

title: The Night Ali Died
description: Ali is wanted dead by a London gangster and is placed under police surveillance for his protection, yet for some reason, on one night, Ali decides to sneak out into the night and leave his family behind, sparking a bloody domino effect that ricochets through the sleepy streets of his rural city. Through the brutal accounts of Ali, a henchman, a detective and a mob boss, find out what on earth happened on the night Ali died. The Night Ali Died premiered at Prague Fringe 2025 and won three awards in its first ever run including a vote as audience favourite. Described as "like watching a Tarantino film onstage" by Theatreandartsreviews.com, its cinematic style and bold and punchy soundtrack took over the Prague festival with an electric buzz. ★★★★★ "A GRIPPING CRIME THRILLER THAT UNFOLDS WITH CINEMATIC PRECISION" Colchester Gazette ★★★★★ "LIKE WATCHING A TV CRIME DRAMA CONDENSED INTO ONE HOUR" Broadway Baby

title: The Party Clothes Swap
description: The Party Clothes Swap Come and swap your pre loved party gear and get yourself some amazing new clothes while saying no thanks to fast fashion. The Party Clothes swap is a zero waste, circular economy sustainable fashion project for all - brought to you by Sew Fabulous Brighton and Brighton Fashion Collective The way it works: Bring your pre loved garments to exchange for tokens to spend at the swap. All items will be graded according to amazingness. You’ll get different ‘value’ tokens depending on the quality of what you bring. What is suitable? Good condition clean clothing Think... Sparkly, glittery, festival clothes, party garments, outrageous outfits, costume, fancy, over the top, rave, party, festival, fun. We want people to have access to good fun party gear. To look good and to feel good without costing the earth. This is a space packed with party clothing with on-site stylists to help you choose your new look. Expect fun and sequins.

title: The Queen, Shakespeare and Me
description: Affectionate musical comedy tribute to a thoroughly modern, fully empowered female role model – a woman who headed up a country & united a commonwealth for over 70 years, earning respect, admiration & affection from around the world. This powerful, colourful lady head of state reflects on her long life and reveals her "all" to her people – her undying love for her corgis, the men in her life, her singlehanded success at forging peace processes, her unexpected, delightful designs for leading the country, with a surprise "coming out" at 96. "Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale her infinite variety" You are regally commanded to join Her Majesty for tea, free cake & biscuits, and a glimpse into unseen aspects of her life. With audience participation, SingalongaLiz, Shakespeare & quiz. We may mourn her passing and the end of an era, but HM would not have wished us to be miserable. Don't miss the UK’s answer to Mrs Maisel! Handbag – the regal older lady’s Fleabag!

title: The Quest for Love, Murder and Revenge in Space!
description: The Quest for Love, Murder and Revenge in Space is a fantasy adventure showcasing the epic– oh, wait, no! It's actually a dramatic romance demonstrating the power of– oh, still wrong? That's right, it's a murder mystery in which– Wrong again? A rollicking western, perhaps? A sci-fi saga? The truth is it's none of them... and all of them! Each night our protégé arrives to find the seasoned cast in shambles. It appears that, despite perfectly reasonable instructions from the director, the show hasn't quite been settled on yet. One wants a romance while the other wants sci-fi and, in the end, it's easier to let the audience decide. So, we let them. They can actually decide the title, too. Oh, and provide some challenges for our heroes to overcome. The result? Usually, a very endearing tale about the power of friendship, predictable romances, magical items, gunslinging, and a wild host of one-night-only characters. You couldn't write the stuff! Main image courtesy of Pete Burnett.

title: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner
description: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner and other salt-stained shanties A blustering theatrical blunder through brine, blood and ballads. Framed around the themes of Coleridge’s famous epic, two troubadours fumble their way through a number of short stories, songs and poems on a quest to find the emotional truth of the sea. Including retellings of true shipwrecks, selkie folktales and of course The Rime itself, this is an entertaining evening not to be missed!

title: The Skins
description: When Jen discovers an underground lair of alien creatures with plans of world domination, she must do everything in her power to thwart their efforts before undercover extraterrestrial ‘Beryl Stump’ is elected the next UK Prime Minister. With the help of Princess Diana’s diary and the noble efforts of her wonderful audience, Jen might just make it. This anarchic comedy blends puppetry, pop-culture parody and a deep love for Princess Diana to create a fast paced and delightfully absurd performance. It's not one to be missed. ★★★★ "It rings with soul and sensation." - Binge Fringe for Halfway Down's previous production 'Saint'.

title: The Slide Show Must Go On
description: Ben and Chris have discovered the internet and now they can’t stop. Join them for a whimsical tour of pop culture’s lesser-known niches as they brain-dump their gratuitous "research" in a series of hilarious PowerPoint lectures, with different bizarro topics each night. Previous events have delved deep into detective fiction, conspiracy theories, power ballads, dystopian sci-fi, weird animals and the eating habits of politicians. This Brighton Fringe special features new talks and a fresh format allowing for even more amusing and intriguing minutiae. Each talk concludes with a Q&A and the opportunity to put your hosts on the spot. Doors at 7pm for a 7.30pm start. Some tickets will be available on the door.

title: The Sound of Wunderlich
description: The boys from Der Wunderlich Revue are back again in an Alpine adventure, ram-packed with naughty nuns and yodelling yokels. Expect the usual mix of smut and singing, nudity and stupidity. And of course men cheerleading. Plus mime, unicycling and magic. Possibly some circus skills. Maybe some silly games. With the incomparable compering talents of Mr Desmond O'Connor on the 21st and Mr Meredith on the 10th. Brighton's, and probably the UK's, longest running all-male cabaret. You'll laugh till you wet yourself. "A batshit bonkers baffling bonanza of men at their sublime silliest." Five stars, says To Do List. Who could disagree? The most Fringe show in the Fringe. Book your seat quickly as these shows will sell out.

title: The Summer Palace
description: When an internationally acclaimed theatre director working under a totalitarian regime is brought in for questioning, he thinks his political activism is under scrutiny. But the person facing him across a desk in an otherwise empty room turns out to be an actor he hasn’t seen for nearly twenty years, and they have unfinished business. The director begins to realise that the interrogation isn’t quite what it seems as the personal and the political become dangerously intertwined and the stakes are raised in a deadly game of cat-and-mouse. The twists and turns of darkly comedic stage play mark the return to the theatre of writer Paul Bassett Davies, who began his career in experimental multimedia performance before moving to radio and television, where he worked with some of the biggest names in British comedy and drama. He is author of four published novels and several stories, one of which was praised by Stephen King as “Mordantly funny... Poe would have liked it".

title: The Tale of the Original Jekyll and Hyde (told by a Mad Hatter from Stockport)
description: Deacon Brodie isn’t that well known out of Edinburgh. Yet he inspired Robert Louis Stevenson to write Jekyll and Hyde. Similarly, Stanley isn’t that well known outside of Stockport. Yet he’s campaigned with Napoleon AND been up Everest with Mallory. Maybe, anyway. By day, in the 18th Century, Deacon Brodie was a respectable cabinetmaker and councillor. Whereas by night, he was a masked burglar in Edinburgh's Old Town. Somehow, Stanley gets roped into his escapades. And he could flipping well swing for it. In public! "Andy D commands the stage with an undeniable presence" The Scotsman.

title: THE TIME WARP EXTRAVAGANZA : A Rocky Horror Drag & Dance Spectacle
description: THE TIME WARP EXTRAVAGANZA: A Rocky Horror Drag and Dance Spectacle ★★★★★ review. Step into the world of Rocky Horror at this unforgettable drag & dance show. Fierce, fabulous, and filthy in all the right ways! A full-throttle celebration of everything that makes Rocky Horror Picture Show a cult phenomenon. Audience participation highly encouraged. Get ready to sing along with your favourite songs, dress up in your wildest Rocky Horror outfits, and immerse yourself in a night of pure fun and flamboyance. Whether you're a die-hard fan or new to the madness, this is one extravaganza you won’t want to miss! This isn't just a show—it is a cultural reset. Equal parts wild, wicked, and wonderful, it proves that Rocky Horror is still the bold, beating heart of queer theatre—and that drag, at its best, is an unstoppable force of expression and joy. It doesn't just reimagine the iconic story—it reinvents it for a new generation of misfits. this spectacle demands to be seen!

description: Brighton’s very own award-winning topical sketch comedy troupe are back with their slick, fast-paced satirical sideswipes at the great and good. If you like your humour topical with a twist and your songs with a satirical bite, then you’ll love this much loved Brighton company. title: The Ultimate Bubble Show
description: Ray Bubbles, International Bubbleologist and 4 time Guinness World Record Holder, is on a mission to master the art of bubble-making and create the ultimate square bubble! This show promises a whirlwind of excitement and surprises, as Ray uses various different gases to craft stunning bubble sculptures, effects, and magical displays. Prepare to be amazed as Ray creates a volcano bubble, a soap bubble carousel, a bubble ghost, and even a tornado inside a bubble! The show was inspired by Ray’s work with SEND schools, adult neurodivergent groups, and science centres across Europe. The performance is suitable for children and adults with additional needs. Join Ray and see if he can create the ultimate square bubble! This is the UK & Ireland's favourite bubble show with over 130 dates in 2025 and combined audiences of over 30,000!!!

title: The Universe Speaks
description: The universe speaks, whether we choose to listen or not. Just Improv's 'spectacular' long-form improvised comedy finds the humour in our fascination with superstition, belief and fate. You are the Universe. You will guide our characters through sliding doors moments that could change their lives forever. How would your life be different if you listened to the signs around you? Just Improv have been showcasing their 'wild wit' since 2022, performing sell-out shows at Brighton, Camden and Edinburgh Fringe Festivals. Just Improv regularly host a Games Night to connect with other improv groups across London.

title: The Vampire Run
description: A dark and stormy night. A vampire’s castle outlined by lightning. An hour to find and kill the dreaded fiend... And one idiot trying to manage the escalating chaos. Join a fast-paced, hilariously deadly choose-your-own adventure where three players at a time must decide how to approach a variety of obstacles to reach the vampire’s chamber. BUT one of the players is evil, and the audience must decide who before it’s too late. This is the debut solo show from award winning writer and performer Jonathan Laury (Best Comedy, Unrestricted View Film Festival 2019). Think something like Knightmare but with a social deduction twist and spiralling madness as time ticks on and the castle starts to change... Shortlisted for a Glitch award, and described by audiences as "Brilliant, slick, funny, 10/10 would go again" and "My favourite theatrical experience of 2025." It’s a comedy, it’s a horror, it’s a game, it’s a show! And it’s definitely the most fun you can have dying horribly.

title: The Winner Rolls It All
description: The Winner Rolls It All is a fast-paced, high-stakes board game musical. Featuring a score parodying one of Eurovision's most iconic winners, and a book crammed with pop-culture references, think The Rocky Horror Show meets Starlight Express...but for huns. A riff on Mamma Mia! and featuring a score made up of parodies of ABBA songs, with a story about a board game that everybody is familiar with. Featuring bonds, stocks, investments, betrayal, capitalism & Gemma Collins quotes, there's something for everyone in this hilarious parody musical.

title: The Workplace
description: Delve into the dysfunctional lives of the employees of... We've not got that far yet! After sold-out shows in Cambridge, London and at the Brighton (2024 & 2025) and Edinburgh (2025) Fringes, The Workplace has come back to Brighton! Join us for your new favourite workplace sitcom. Based on suggestions from the audience, a new cast of eclectic characters live out the every day of working the 9 to 5, with ego clashes, office politics and absurd workplace situations. If you're a fan of The Office, Parks and Recreation, or comedy in general, this is a show you don't want to miss.
description_html:

title: Theatre of Wholiness
description: A queer cosmic cabaret of myth, mischief and metamorphosis... Part theatre, part ritual, part glitch in the matrix - Theatre of Wholiness invites you into an intimate space where identity is shapeshifting, joy is medicine, and the ordinary loosens its grip. Lumi the Host - glitch-witch storyteller and sassy steward of the in-between - guides you on a journey through archetype, embodiment, humour and presence. Co-created with the room as a one-off collective moment. This is a space where wholeness isn’t something you chase - it’s something you activate. Playful. Tender. Disarming. A celebration of queer becoming and the art of returning to yourself.

title: Theologist
description: Raised Catholic and at one time considering priesthood, Oli Riordan has studied Catholic theology for a decade for the same reason every other bisexual atheist studies Catholic theology. You have to know your enemy. Join Oli (Dublin's Next Top Comic Runner Up 2019, Leicester Square Theatre New Comedian of the Year Semi Finalist 2025) for his debut hour as he delves into the world of Catholicism and the Bible as only he can, pausing only to point out the silly, interesting and thoroughly relatable aspects of the world's best-selling, but least read, book.

title: Therapist Zero
description: “Therapist Zero” is a poignant and comedic one-person show by Brian Leonard that explores the tumultuous journey of parenting a spirited daughter, Emily, who challenges traditional norms and expectations. Set against the backdrop of countless therapists and mental health professionals, Leonard navigates the complexities of familial love, resilience, and the quest for understanding. With humor and heart, the show unpacks the trials of raising a child with behavioral challenges, reflecting on his own upbringing and the lessons learned along the way. Join Brian as he takes you on an emotional rollercoaster filled with laughter, insight, and the transformative power of connection.

title: tHERapy
description: tHERapy is a compelling one-woman play that intimately portrays the life of Deola, a Nigerian woman navigating the complexities of identity, family, and societal expectations. The play intricately weaves together vignettes exploring themes of familial relationships—particularly the subtle dynamics within her extended family—love and heartbreak, and the challenges of gender in Nigerian culture, including situations of sexual harassment and assault. Through Deola's candid storytelling, the play offers a poignant reflection on the strength and vulnerability of womanhood.

title: This Is How I Got Arrested...
description: "This Is How I Got Arrested... After Smuggling Drugs Across The Border But Never Actually Getting Caught With Any Drugs" is a hysterically funny, dark-humoured & courageous one woman show that breaks the fourth wall and leaves you questioning what is fact and what is fiction. Meet Sophie, a young girl from a working class world who takes the phrase "girls just wanna have fun" too far. This epic fast-paced story starts with tales from Sophie's upbringing, through to the moment of her arrest in a foreign country. Lies will be uncovered, stories will be told, and mischief will be coursed. All under the pressure of a ticking clock, only time will reveal the truth. Themes of Drug's Use, Toxic Femineity, Alcohol & Strong Language.

title: Toast: Stories from the End of the World
description: Would you savour the last beer on Earth... or save it? At the end of the world, two friends scavenging through the ruins stumble across the last beer (ever?) and maybe wish they hadn't. As they reminisce, we are taken back to look at people, places and events framed by bended elbows, pub sofas and people you know from every night out. Toast is a darkly funny, intimate end-of-the-world play about connection, memory and the last drink on Earth. Adapted from Chance Dean’s original short play Toast, first performed during Witwerks Theatre’s sell-out run of Dramatic Draughts: Four Short Plays About the Pub, this new Fringe-length version artfully expands the story with just two actors playing a cast of vividly drawn characters from before the apocalypse.

title: Tom Hutchinson - Maintaining The Dream (Work in Progress)
description: Is Tom Hutchinson a success? In his 20s he achieved the only dream he ever had: to make music full time, and create an album that's an unmitigated success. So...now what? Why does it feel like nobody prepared him for life after the eternal chase? Join red-haired rambler and ADHD-addled orator Tom Hutchinson for a hilarious work in progress stand-up show about the big questions; if all we ever learn is how to chase our dreams, how the hell do we live on afterwards? A Work In Progress hour about joy, obsession, failure, autism, granddads, Fatman Scoop and somehow The Sims. Red-haired rising star of the South West, Tom Hutchinson is “a joy to watch”, brimming with high energy absurdism and sparky tangents, bringing everything from wild improvisations and witty asides to introspective anecdotes and relatable ramblings. Bristol Comedy Competition Runner Up 2026. Bath Comedy Festival New Act 2022 Finalist. As Heard on BBC Upload.

title: Topping and Hards: Comedy from the Box
description: Josh Hards and Simon Topping are all at sea, they don't know what to do with their lives. This all changes when Simon, surfing on the beige web, lands on a mysterious website (which they can never find again) and decides to spend his life savings on "The Box". "The Box" makes the boastful claim to be the finest collection of comedy sketches ever written (Duo Edition); "Guaranteed" to be the funniest series of comedy shorts that anyone has ever witnessed, but something doesn't seem quite right. Who has compiled the material and what do they want you to believe? Come and witness the awesome comedy that lies within "The Box" as the boys unbox the scripts and perform it for the very first time, and maybe the last, before your very eyes and for your delight. Do fame and fortune beckon or is there more to "The Box" than first appears? Topping and Hards: Comedy From The Box is a part crafted, part improvised silly romp of a sketch show with a joy centred and surreal heart.

title: Tour of St Nicholas Church
description: Tour of St Nicholas Church Enjoy a warm welcome to Brighton's ancient mother church, to hear and see some of the 1000-year history, treasures and characters of this beautiful building. Highlights include the very rare Norman font, the magnificent windows by Kempe, and the Wellington memorial. Some comments from previous tours: “Fascinating, a place so rich in history” “A knowledgeable and entertaining speaker” “Really interesting, so much detail – wish I could remember it all” The 12.15pm tour on both 16 and 23 May may be combined with the 'Clifton and Montpelier' tour, which leaves from the church at 2pm Access for wheelchairs is through the main South door. There are toilets in the church including facilities for the disabled. There is a loop system.

title: Toussaint Douglass: Accessible Pigeon Material
description: Best Newcomer Nominee Edinburgh Comedy Awards 2025 ‘Douglass has cracked how to make the inside of his mind seem not just accessible, but a very fun place to spend time.’ ★★★★ Guardian Hello friend. Open your mind, take out what’s inside and wish it well on its future endeavours. You’re going to need the space for this one. I’m talking life, love, loss, finding your way and pigeons... quite a lot about pigeons actually... it’s pretty pigeon-heavy to be honest. So please join me, Toussaint Douglass, for an hour of joyfully absurd, charmingly awkward stand-up. title: Town of Cats / Binbag Wisdom / Brassic Parp
description: TOWN OF CATS Energetic nine piece festival favourites, Town of Cats bring their love of afrobeat, latin grooves, big dirty riffs, trashy 80’s pop, half remembered musicals and entirely hallucinated toilet poetry to the stage. Humorous, dark and introspective tales delivered with a blasting brass section and electrifying synth solos, Town of Cats deliver a whirlwind performance that’s infectiously fun, gets audiences to their feet and keeps them dancing. BINBAG WISDOM Bristol's premium rap jokesters Binbag Wisdom return to Brighton with their brand new theatre-come-hip-hop extravaganza 'The Ballad of Wee Willy Winky' or 'How to Find Purpose in a rap desert'. Is it a live hip hop gig? A musical? I'm not sure. But it's funny, unique and needs to be experienced BRASSIC PARP DJ style pop medley mashups from only the best bits from 90s & 00s bangers. Performed by a seven piece horn section dressed up as Jurassic Park characters.

title: Trans*Atlantic
description: Nominated last year for Best New Show, Trans*Atlantic is back at Brighton Fringe on tour! Join the acclaimed transgender American comedian Dian Cathal at the Komedia on Saturday, May 2nd, 2026 for the Brighton stop of his international tour. Cathal (As seen on BBC, Apple TV, HBO) presents his explosive, 5-star reviewed hour of stand-up exploring the messy crossroads of identity, culture, and politics. Which is it: Americans or trans people the British dislike more? Hailed as "Combative, clever, and very, very charming" (Reviews Hub), Trans*Atlantic uses razor-sharp wit to dissect the cultural tension between his New York roots and his life as a "gender immigrant" in the UK. Set against a backdrop of global chaos, this is vital, fearless, and necessary comedy. It's a powerhouse hour blending sharp political takes with hilarious personal anecdotes. What: Dian Cathal: Trans*Atlantic When: Saturday, May 2nd, 2026 Where: Komedia, Brighton

title: Transient and Trifling
description: This is a show about human connection. Transient and Trifling is taking BDSM out of the underground and onto the altar. Breaking away from the typical notion that kink performance must rely on fetish fashion and shock value, Transient and Trifling shines light on raw, vulnerable and visceral portrayals of BDSM. A cabaret-theatre show that is as much a love letter as a call for change, this edition features four award-winning performers reflecting on their personal experiences and the history and culture that shapes the community today.

title: Trauma: An Exhibition
description: Trauma: An Exhibition Trauma isn’t just a moment we experience, feel, carry — it’s a texture. A glitch. A bruise on the everyday. This art event explores what we carry, what we hide, and what is handed down through bodies, rooms, families, borders, and institutions. Through work that’s bold, raw, and sometimes sardonic, we’ll explore trauma as both intensely personal and quietly collective—the kind that lives in memory, language, movement, paperwork, and silence. Expect an experience that doesn’t “inspire” you out of pain or tidy it into a neat ending. This is art that remains complex: fractures, repairs, repetitions, the politics of who is believed, and the strange creativity of survival. Some pieces will pull you in softly. Others might catch you off guard. All of them ask the same question: what happens when we stop treating trauma as a private problem — and start seeing it as a social one? Come for the artistic vulnerability. Art by Blood N Guts Murphy and Broken Glass.
description_html:
Trauma: An Exhibition

Trauma isn’t just a moment we experience, feel, carry — it’s a texture. A glitch. A bruise on the everyday.

This art event explores what we carry, what we hide, and what is handed down through bodies, rooms, families, borders, and institutions. Through work that’s bold, raw, and sometimes sardonic, we’ll explore trauma as both intensely personal and quietly collective—the kind that lives in memory, language, movement, paperwork, and silence.

Expect an experience that doesn’t “inspire” you out of pain or tidy it into a neat ending. This is art that remains complex: fractures, repairs, repetitions, the politics of who is believed, and the strange creativity of survival. Some pieces will pull you in softly. Others might catch you off guard. All of them ask the same question: what happens when we stop treating trauma as a private problem — and start seeing it as a social one?

Come for the artistic vulnerability. Art by Blood N Guts Murphy and Broken Glass.

title: Two-Stroke, the Northern Soul Musical
description: Two-Stroke is “a lively and enjoyable trip through the world of Northern Soul music” (Isle of Wight County Press), a fast-paced new musical inspired by the electric buzz of the 1986 National Scooter Rally. Three teenagers pile onto the ferry in search of the best weekend of their lives — chasing music, freedom, and the thrill of escape. Over one unforgettable weekend, friendships are forged, tensions rise, and plans take unexpected turns, all set to the driving beat of Northern Soul. Featuring iconic tracks including Beggin', I’m On My Way, and Out On The Floor, Two-Stroke captures the joy, heartache, and rebellion of youth, celebrating the unifying power of music and the moments that shape who we become. Written by award-winning playwright Joe Plumb, this “musical delight” (Isle of Wight County Press) is fun, heartfelt, and bursting with soul.

title: Undercooked
description: Undercooked is a Work in Progress stand-up split bill featuring Sara Lessmann and Stephanie Bradshaw. Lessmann, a 2025 West End New Act of the Year Finalist, brings a whimsical, endearing style of comedy rooted in storytelling . Her set explores life as a queer late bloomer raised in the USA, in a buttoned-up religious home. Bradshaw, a finalist in the 2023 OnlyFans Creative Comedy fund, debuted her 45-minute show Big Fish at the 2024 Edinburgh Fringe, reflecting on growing up in a sleepy Cumbrian town with an outsized desire for stardom. Toby Hadoke (XS Malarkey) praises her “warm, likeable” presence and “lovely timing". Notts Comedy Review noted the show’s feel-good charm. In Undercooked, Lessmann and Bradshaw dig into the deeper meaning of being small-town people-pleasers. Come see this preview from two rising comics!
