title: Unforgettable: An Alzheimer's Cabaret!
description: Welcome to our Care Home Cabaret! Jane, the senior carer and MC, is here to care for you. Fret not, any issue, Jane has a tissue! Behind the lace curtains, one woman is sitting with her tale of Familial Alzheimer’s Disease and the 50/50 risk it might touch her soon. A cast of improbable companions come to the rescue, each carrying their bits of the story. Mischief sparkles, a diva wails operatically, props are misplaced, Lady Vivienne causes chaos and will Sarah seize the spotlight? The horse tries to make sense of it all... our shimmering chanteuse invites you to join the club - Dead Dads’ Jazz Club - for a night on the town with our dead loved ones. An unforgettable soirée, even if she might forget it. "So personal and universal at the same time. So rich and beautiful with the songs, the mask, the clowning and everything. It was fantastic!" (Audience member)

title: Unidentified Fluid Other: Gaylien de Mars
description: Exploring the feminine and masculine in a binary world to find the answer to the ultimate question: ‘Who am I?’ Unidentified Fluid Other is a ‘one-alien’ multi-media performance art piece with out-of-this-world live vocals by one of Brighton’s top vocalists and Titans Season 1 winner, Gaylien de Mars. An alien who can transform at will into anyone, Gaylien takes on many faces to better understand the human experience. In Unidentified Fluid Other, Gaylien reflects on their experiences moving through this often overwhelming world, trying to find belonging and identity. This show holds a mirror to the human spirit through music and stunning projections. Exploring themes of human behaviour, addiction, self-discovery, gender, misogyny and feminism. Gaylien de Mars is a highly versatile drag artist and performer, with incredible vocals, thought-provoking concepts, and clever stage design. Their pronouns are they/them. They’re a member of the LGBTQIA+ community and are neurodiverse AuDHD.

title: Up Your Vision!
description: This year, Eurovision carries on as usual - and while the UK stays in, Brighton knows better! Despite its ongoing ethnic cleansing of Palestinians, Israel remains in the competition whilst Russia has been excluded since 2022 for its invasion of Ukraine. The double standard is clear. And we’re not the only ones calling it out: Iceland, Ireland, Norway, Spain, Slovenia, and the Netherlands have withdrawn from Eurovision this year in protest. So we’re doing something different: Up Your Vision! is our joyful, defiant alternative. Expect an evening of live music, big voices, bold performances, comedy chaos and a raffle packed with prizes. We’re celebrating talent and showing that taking a stand doesn’t mean cancelling the fun - it means redefining it. Thank you for choosing to be a part of a night that’s loud, proud, and full of heart & hope. Solidarity never sounded so good. All proceeds will go to PalMusic UK, supporting music education and cultural exchange for young Palestinians.

title: Vaxinated 2 - A Musical Booster
description: Step into a world where glamour meets showtunes! Rubella Vaxine takes centre stage in a high-camp, high-octane drag extravaganza featuring iconic numbers from the great musicals. From belt-your-heart-out ballads to razzle-dazzle showstoppers, she spins Broadway classics into something delightfully queer, wickedly funny, and unmistakably Rubella. Expect bold looks, big vocals, and even bigger personality as she brings your favourite musical moments to life with a twist. Whether you’re a theatre kid at heart or just here for the glitter, this show promises laughs, gasps, and a standing ovation you’ll feel in your soul. Accompanied by the phenomenal talent of Izzy Owen on keys and maybe some surprise guests will also be popping in too. You will not want to miss this one-night only event at the Brighton Fringe! Lights up. Wig secured. Let the overture begin.

title: Verity Sharpe: Landlord's Wet Dream
description: “Charismatic and chaotic” comedian Verity Sharpe returns to Brighton Fringe with her debut show. Landlord’s Wet Dream is a bold and hilarious deep dive into the realities of renting—damp walls, exploitative landlords, and the looming threat of eviction— that offers a silver lining of laughter to the dark cloud that is temporary housing. Sharpe also squares up to the wider issue of profit-driven housing, wielding jaunty punk poetry, razor-sharp stand up, and anthemic original songs . Come for the comedy, stay for the biting capitalist critique. Or, come for the biting capitalist critique but be warned... you will laugh. Hard. “One of the most important show you’ll see all month”- Reviews Hub

title: Vibrate
description: This show takes the audience on a journey from early archeologists' discoveries, to Victorian toys and advertising, through to the modern toys of today - a show which explores how adult toys have been presented through history, and how attitudes have widely changed and shifted from a focus on female 'hysteria,' to an emphasis on wellbeing and self-care.' All presented through story and original songs.

title: Vibrations from Mars
description: Vibrations from Mars is a space-themed guided storytime meditation show that takes you on a journey through the cosmos. Led by certified yoga & sound meditation instructor and drag artist Gaylien de Mars, you'll be teleported around the universe, visiting distant planets before returning safely back to Earth. This is an immersive, sensory experience with live sound textures from crystal bowls, koshi chimes, tuning forks, steel drums and ethereal vocals, along with visual elements to help keep you in the stars. Explore how vibration is experienced as sound and sensation, helping to relax the nervous system and rest. Explore the deep connection humanity has with the cosmos. This experience is crafted with *accessibility and neurodivergent comfort in mind, and is a safe space for the LGBTQIA+ community. No yoga experience needed – just curiosity, and a willingness to float through an otherworldly, soothing adventure. *the venue is accessed via 1 step and is not wheelchair accessible.

description: We’re back! Following our sell out run at Brighton Fringe 2025 we’re back for more for 2026! Featuring an array of cabaret’s finest performers Violet’s Vaudeville Vault showcases old school variety from magic to burlesque, sideshow to circus, mind reading to hand balance, with all sorts of odditease inbetween. Each show provides a brand new line up. You never know what you’ll find in the vault. “Violet’s Vaudeville Vault is one of those rare gems-a rich, raucous showcase of talent that bursts from the seams with theatrical mischief, musical flair, and vintage cabaret charm” ★★★★1/2 Sussex News Best Show - Runner up The Brighton Seagull awards Brighton Fringe 2024. “Violet’s Vaudeville Vault was a showcase of charisma, uniqueness, nerve, and talent night after night” The Brighton Seagull

title: W*NKERS
description: W*NKERS: Spring Fling is a show dedicated to the Weird traditions of the Spring and the horniest time of the year; think pastoral scenes featuring fingers as udders for that critical STI check, the personification of Francophone Syphillis as a cabaret girl who’ll leave you tongue tied, and an ode to Bank Holidays. All against the background of looming fascism. It features co-creators Bel Parker and Jordana Belaiche as House White and House Red. They aren’t exactly human and they aren’t entirely demonic either. They are raucous, queer and absurd – grotesque demon jesters that pierce the veils to many worlds, shapeshifting through characters, to feed on the mayhem they create. It’s definitely comedy.

title: Waiting for Texto!
description: Part Stand-up comedy, part play, Waiting for Texto is a hilarious, painfully accurate depiction of the angst of unrequited love. Described as "Bridget Jones meets Baby Reindeer", This play is for anyone who's agonised over sending a text. jokes, poignant moments and a twist! Terrifically impressive, warm and funny - ★★★★ The Scotsman I loved this play and can’t wait to see what Aideen writes next. She’s a keeper! - One4review ★★★★★ An hour of beautiful solo writing, brashly, boldly and skilfully delivered, with deeper roots into some important questions about life - Funny Women

title: WENCH
description: Martha Tallow is always thinking sideways, smiling inside-out and waking up in other people’s business. She talks too much, loves too freely and refuses to behave. Now the village wants blood. WENCH! is a darkly comic musical about hysteria, harlots and the stories people tell when they feel threatened. Accused of apostasy, impropriety and being generally inconvenient, Martha finds herself on trial before a community desperate to tidy her away. Blending filthy humour with lyrical ballads, WENCH drags the witch trial into the present day without ever pinning it to a single moment. Who gets labelled unnatural? Who is shamed for desire.? Who is punished for thinking differently? Part musical farce, part courtroom drama, part personal manifesto, WENCH skins the turnip of society to reveal the maggots beneath. Felix Le Freak is the winner of Drag Idol UK, the author of Serving Face and the creator of shows including Fagwartz, Men and Other Problems, and Felix & Friends.

title: When Gary Left Annie (Almost)
description: It started with an offer of a gherkin on a bench during lunchtime, it ended with a relationship that dragged well beyond it's sell-by date. Gary adores Annie but he absolutely has to leave her. Annie "quite likes" Gary but really shouldn't have ever entered a relationship with him in the first place. It's obvious to everyone that they need to part ways, but sometimes real life just gets in the way; why would you dump somebody you're still so, well, 'comfortable' with? A comedic exploration of relationships, companionships and the little things that keep couples together...and the really annoying things that drive them apart! A brand new comedy play written and performed by Helen Pain and Paul Richards.

title: When I Wake Up Again
description: A recording of; When I Wake Up Again is an immersive theatre piece exploring a family's journey through love, loss, and resilience. Through intimate storytelling, movement, and live music, the performance takes audiences inside the lives of a couple and their children, confronting the joys and heartbreaks that shape a life. Director and performer Sammi combines evocative voice, powerful stage presence, and symbolic elements—like water and puppetry—to create a poignant, multi-sensory experience. ★★★★½ Audiences are invited to move within the space, following the performers and engaging closely with the narrative. This is a deeply human story, where grief, love, and memory intertwine, leaving us to reflect on our own choices and connections. Please note: this event can be booked from Friday 1st May 2026.

title: When The Tide Comes In
description: An original play commissioned by Hungry Wolf Visionary Youth Theatre Company and written by Naomi Denny in collaboration with the young company. As nine young people approach the end of their GCSEs, a celebratory gathering on the beach takes a devastating turn. A singular, defining event inexplicably binds the group, forcing them to grapple questions of collective responsibility and individual guilt. Hungry Wolf VYT represents a new generation of youth theatre for Sussex’s most promising acting talent aged 14-24 years. With work both thought-provoking and relevant, Hungry Wolf VYT has established a reputation for hard-hitting, truthful and uncompromising material.

title: Where Are We Going With This?
description: Where Are We Going With This? depicts two University students meeting on a bench at 2am after a bad night out. This touching two-hander follows the two students' struggles to open up to one another, exploring the awkward comedy and profound intimacy that can occur in those one-off conversations with a stranger. They flirt, fight, and fumble along a tightrope walk of social norms and personal boundaries, discussing their outlook on the future, their bouts with loneliness, and, eventually, why they ended up on a bench in the middle of the night. He is a quick-witted and jovial pessimist. She is a distrusting, cynical optimist. Together, they bring out the ugliest and most tender parts of one another as they learn that often, the hardest part of opening up to a stranger is enduring the silence that follows.

title: Whisky Tango Foxtrot!
description: It’s the late 1970’s, Jack Bryan, a young father, shop steward and steel worker experiences the pain of redundancy just before Christmas and the impending birth of his second child. Caught up in the decline of traditional British Industries he joins the police at a time when that organisation is also in a state of rapid change. For the next 30 years Jack witnesses, first-hand, some of the most important events in late 20th century, British social history: the race riots of the early 80s; the miners’ strike of 84-85; and the decimation of British Industries due to both globalisation and Thatcher. Coupled with all this, Jack finds himself entering a world which very few people get to know... a kind of psychotic Narnia that is governed by its own rules. In the telling of this story Jack attempts to explain to the general public the reality of this parallel world. The results are both grittily honest, totally enlightening and hilariously funny.

title: Who Killed the Plot
description: Who killed the plot? Well, it was definitely one of us... but can you figure out who? Join us for a hilariously unpredictable improvised murder mystery with a twist! Watch as a cast of quick-thinking improvisers blend the best of short-form chaos like lines from a hat and Party Quirks, with the intrigue of a long-form whodunit. Expect wild characters, ridiculous twists, and plenty of unscripted moments. Will we solve the crime, or just make things even more absurd? There's only one way to find out, come see the mystery (and the madness) unfold!

title: Who Wrote This?
description: After a whirlwind run across six Fringe Festivals and eleven different shows, ‘Who Wrote This?’ returns to Brighton Fringe 2026! Get ready for an hour of unpredictable improvised comedy where your suggestions drive every scene and anything can happen! Audiences last year raved that the shows were ‘hilarious, high-energy and brilliantly unpredictable.’ With every moment made up on the spot, no two performances are ever the same. Join the chaos, fuel the fun, and help create a show that will only happen once. Don’t miss it!

title: Will & Noah: Dinner Dinner Chicken Winner
description: Roll out the red carpet and dust off your medals. Will & Noah invite you to a sketch show set in the world's furthest-reaching awards ceremony. It’s a night to celebrate life’s winners, including but not limited to: best original song, product of the year, soup of the day, oldest man, funniest in the office, Sunday Best, Monday Worst and longest time survived in a lion cage (spoiler: it’s the lion). Under our roof, all winners are created equal. No achievement is too small. After a successful Edinburgh Fringe run with ‘Will & Noah: Too Much Time on Their Phones’ (although not successful enough to win any awards) Will and Noah have, by complete chance, designed a show format in which they win loads and loads of awards. Blending musical comedy, video and audience participation, this fast-paced dynamic sketch show is a wholesome slice-of-life look at what it means to win.

title: Will BF: MOON TEAM IIIV
description: In 1979, a sci-fi movie changed everything. It launched careers, changed Hollywood forever, and left a cultural footprint bigger than Neil Armstrong’s boot on the moon (metaphorically - Neil was a size 7). You all know its name... That’s right! It was called ‘Moon Team’! Huh? You were going to say something else? Star Wars? No, never heard of it - you’re probably misremembering... Where was I... ? Ah yes: Moon Team. A film beloved by all, but not the film we’ll be watching today. No, we’re voyaging to the galaxy’s grimy underside to watch Moon Team’s beloathed prequel sequel: Moon Team IIIV (2, if you think about it!). Hang onto your space hats (technical term) for a surreal sketch comedy odyssey through the best worst film never made, as BBC New Comedian 2025 semi-finalist Will BF presents his award-winning (Buxton Fringe Best Comedy Show 2025) hour of sketches, songs, live video games and sci-fi multimedia havoc.

title: Will Davies: Heroic Scale (Work In Progress)
description: It's a big, scary world out there and it's easy to feel small and helpless in the face of it all. But is that true? Can just one little thing make a big change? Does every little help? Can you really make a mountain out of a molehill? Who's to say? Join Will Davies as he attempts to find out in this new hour of fresh stand-up comedy. (Work in progress).

title: Will Owen: Looking Fab At Fifty
description: 26-year-old comedian Will Owen thinks the future looks bright (no further questions). Frest from the Edinburgh Fringe 2025, Will seeks to replace the empty gratifications of his forlorn life with the deep profundity of light entertainment. He’s gay! He’s lonely! But most importantly, he’s live! One of 2024’s best reviewed shows of the Fringe. Winner: Leicester Square New Comedian of the Year. Tour support for Ania Magliano and Rhys Nicholson. ★★★★★ (BroadwayBaby.com). ★★★★★ (ThreeWeeks). ★★★★★ (Entertainment-Now.com). ‘Dazzling’ ★★★★ (List). ‘Hilarious, authentic, in control... gives us what we didn’t know we needed’ ★★★★ (OnTheMic.co.uk).

title: Wit Happens - The Comedy Magic Of Katherine Rhodes
description: Katherine makes her Brighton Fringe debut bringing her sharp humour, wit and incredible magic to the stage of Comedy At The Caxton. She dazzles, sparkles, entertains and mystifies in equal measure. Watch as the laws of physics are cast aside, the impossible is made a reality and your sides split from laughing (not literally!). Her offbeat style of humour and magic will have you talking about this show for the rest of your life (no guarantee, your mileage may vary, terms and conditions probably apply). “A fresh and unique style. Class act. Funny too!” - Marc Almond “So good I think magic might actually exist” - Heather Peace

title: Witchcraft & Magic Tour
description: Returning after a sold-out run at last year's Fringe, come with us to find out about the mystical and esoteric history of Brighton. The tour will take you from a ley line nexus to the first blue plaque to ever honour a witch, covering stone circles, an alleged occult cult and the satanic panic along the way. Find out about the witchcraft trials in Sussex, Aleister Crowley's link to Brighton and life for witches in Regency England. If you have any questions or accessibility requirements please email me at [email protected] This tour is AI-free - thisisaifree.com

title: Witches, Britches & B*tches
description: From shameless show off to opera singer, step into a world of captivating musical storytelling as Georgia Mae Bishop takes you on a journey through music and theatre’s most iconic Witches, Britches, and B*tches. In this enchanting and intimate evening of stereotypes and surprises, expect a thrilling fusion of operatic arias, show tunes, and pop anthems, ranging from Mozart and Verdi to Beyoncé and Lady Gaga. The magic of theatre as you’ve never seen it before, with killer vocals that defy genre and touching anecdotes of the life of a wannabe theatre kid turned professional opera singer.

title: With All My Fondest Love
description: While hoping to reconnect with his ex-girlfriend, Noah discovers a dusty box of unread diaries and letters in his attic. Hidden inside is the forgotten story of his grandparent’s marriage. This tender true story is one grandson’s attempt to piece together their forgotten lives, from Frank Sinatra and amateur cricket to burnt letters and unspeakable loss. Noah’s family history entwines with his own messy romance, challenging him to find hope and forgiveness within the chaos of heartbreak and memory. Celebrated by the Oscar-winning writer Simon Beaufoy (The Full Monty, Slumdog Millionaire) as a 'quietly devastating, beautifully written piece of drama'. Bring your own family together for this moving and hopeful one-person play told across three generations. We'll remember those we have loved and lost and celebrate how joyful remembering them can be.

title: Wrap Party
description: Wrap Party lifts the hatch on life behind a film-set catering van. Harry and Ollie are lifelong friends and working-class caterers, trying to survive one last chaotic service before the wrap party begins. As A-list egos flare, inspections loom and impossible coffee orders stack up, their tiny van becomes a pressure cooker. Multi-rolled by two performers, the show blurs the line between performance and reality as Harry clings to the business he’s built, while Ollie dreams of escaping catering for an acting career that always feels just out of reach. When Ollie is suddenly offered a real audition in the most surreal and morally dubious way imaginable, hope collides with loyalty, and their friendship starts to fracture. Fast, funny and rooted in real film-set experience, Wrap Party is a lightning-quick comedy about invisible labour, ambition and class.

title: Yes Anderson: The Budapest Grand Piano
description: The films of Wes Anderson occupy a unique and vivid universe unto themselves: love and yearning; complicated family dynamics; existential angst. Binoculars. Maps. Suitcases. This year, to celebrate their fifth Brighton Fringe, Tiny Dynamite are bringing you Wes Anderson’s art deco aesthetic, with an iconic hotel setting and concierge experience not to be forgotten. Enter the whimsical, colourful, idiosyncratic, world of Wes. Based on audience suggestions, the performers will conjure a fully improvised, never to be seen again, medley of rooted characters and story fragments. Our show will also include a live improvised soundtrack. So pack your leather valise, leave a laconic note, and join us in the ballroom for The Budapest Grand Piano, a show that will make you laugh, make you contemplate missed opportunities, and provide a bar so you can actually have a cognac this time. Ticket holders are eligible for discount codes at the Mercure Brighton Seafront, including 15% off your room.

title: You Oughta Be in Pictures
description: “Keep your pretty head, son, or you’ll lose it.” Hollywood, the 1940s. A charismatic film director auditions a young actor for a horror film whose lead has died under mysterious circumstances. As he shares tales of exploitation, broken promises, sex, cults, and the fine line between artifice and truth, it transpires there may be a more malevolent eye than that of the camera in the room. But the horrors on screen were never real – were they? Award-winning theatre-maker Polis Loizou returns to Brighton Fringe with a tale to fill you with dread... and doubt. ★★★★ “A disquieting patchwork of realities.” – The Wee Review

title: Zoe Thorne: Gold Top
description: Thought you wouldn't be able to see any grownup shows at the Brighton Fringe this year because you have a baby? Think again! It's hard to get out to see live comedy when you're a parent, and it can be expensive and nerve-wracking organising a babysitter. Gold Top is a daytime comedy gig where you can bring the baby along too, so there's no need to miss out! Comedian and mum of two Zoe Thorne will ensure plenty of laughs as she talks about the trials and tribulations of parenting and childbirth, and you can feel like a grownup out on the town again. But you're also free to feed, change and rock your baby without worrying about disturbing the rest of the audience, plus you'll be home by witching hour. And best of all, there will be no winding of any bobbins in sight. Please note: all babies should be under the age of 1 as there may be some unsuitable language - this isn't Rhyme Time. All proceeds will go to Royal Papworth Hospital Charity.

title: The Covetousness
description: Adapted from a rarely performed scene in Tang Xianzu’s Ming Dynasty masterpiece. The Dream of Nanke, this production brings back a nearly lost gem of Chinese theatre. With only fragmentary scripts and oral accounts surviving, our team spent months researching ancient scores and historical materials, reconstructing the piece with scholarly care and artistic integrity. It is a dream on the edge of waking, where loyalty clashes with destiny. Performed by a huadan (female warrior) and hualian (painted-face male role), the piece breaks conventional casting, blending intense physicality with emotional depth — a rare portrayal in the world of Chinese opera (Kunqu). This is not just a revival, but a reawakening of a forgotten cultural dream. Performed in a traditional Chinese operatic vocal style with Chinese and English surtitles. Workshops are also offered — an opportunity to explore Kunqu up close; details via the Rotunda Theatre.

title: Almost Fine
description: I’m fine... I think. A smile appears. A body follows—or tries to. Almost Fine is a solo contemporary dance created and performed by renowned Taiwanese dancer A-Yao (Peishao Huang). Small actions—walking, sitting, scrolling, breathing—begin to slip. Almost Fine is a physical exploration of those slips: the cracks in how we try to “be normal.” Playful, precise, and a little messy, the work moves through instinct, fragments of chaos, and childlike impulse. It becomes a game, a glitch, a quiet ritual of being. The piece explores identity—gender, culture, and nation—and how these forces shape the way we show up in the world and inhabit our own skin. It asks how our bodies are trained, coached, and gently pushed into roles we never consciously chose. It is not about breaking. It is not about control. It is about living in the space of "almost"— the fragile, honest, alive moment before we find our footing again.

title: Cameron Sinclair Harris: Speak Now or Forever Hold Your Peace (Work In Progress)
description: Dearly beloved. Please be seated. This is a participatory event. You are invited to celebrate the union between The Bride & her darling groom Nathaniel on their Special Day. There’s The Bride now! Blushing, presumably. Giddily, she waits for her sweet prince Nathaniel to join her at the altar. Still waiting. There she is! Yup. Still waiting. She waits. & dreams. & waits. Cameron Sinclair Harris returns to Brighton Fringe with her second solo show, the follow-up to her cosmic hour 'PLANETS!!!'. Follow the Bride through an anarchic hour of love, longing, puppets, lemons & the Radio Times. This is not a Work In Progress. It is a Wedding In Progress.

title: INTO THE PIT
description: AK Golding’s debut solo show, INTO THE PIT, is an anarchic coal-mining cabaret that drags you headfirst into the grit, graft and glory of coal’s past. Part family history, part identity crisis, AK digs into what it means to come from a working class mining family while being a queer, neurodiverse theatre-making millennial. Expect high-octane skits, surreal character monologues, clowning, Billy Elliot spoofs, shadow puppets and original show tunes in a chaotic whistle-stop tour of the mining industry in the UK. Come into the pit and see if you can help bring a little light to the dark.

title: Mitch Benn: The Lehrer Effect
description: When, years ago, a teenage Mitch discovered American comic songwriting legend Tom Lehrer, the course of his life was changed forever. Mitch looks at the legacy of the elusive Lehrer, the creative debt he, and all those who mix humour and melody, owe to him and performs some of the more Lehreresque of his own songs. Since Mr. Lehrer placed his entire catalogue in public domain, expect a few Mitch Benn-ified covers of some of Tom’s most famous creations as well. Tom Lehrer died at the age of 97, immediately before the premiere of the show in Edinburgh, which means that the show is now a heart-felt eulogy rather than living tribute to the artist.

title: Roedean Festival Fringe Concert 2026
description: This festival concert is a highlight of the year with Roedean’s talented young musicians performing solos with orchestra. This year the programme features a stunning line up of music and will include movements from Elgar’s Cello Concerto, Sibelius’ Violin Concerto, Glière’s Harp Concerto, Rachmininov’s Piano Concerto No.2, Dvořák’s Song to the Moon, Massenet’s Va! laisse couler mes larmes and Waxman’s Carmen Fantasy. Roedean Choir will be joined by staff and musicians from our wider community to perform four beautiful and atmospheric choral works: Elgar The Snow, Ešenvalds Stars, Grieg Spring and Whitacre Cloudburst. This promises to be a truly uplifting event.

title: The Shape of Things Undone
description: It's four in the morning. Christine — a carer, forty-something — is heating a sandwich in a microwave she has an understanding with. Upstairs, they're opening the new wing. Speeches. Free biscuits. Down here, she's doing the checklist. An audio performance about what gets lost when medicine looks ahead — and what stubbornly persists. On the eve of a genomics research launch at a care facility being quietly transformed: a mug with no name, a father who looked at Christine like the girl who delivers the prescriptions, and what it means to hold a story when science is looking at people who haven't arrived yet. Voices overlap, pause, return. Time loosens. Sit inside a story, not behind it. Tender, dark, unexpectedly funny. Written by Lita Doolan (multi award-winning playwright). ★★★★ WhatsOnStage Performed by Julie Broadbent (OffWest End nom). 'Whatever I forget or remember — I'll still know where this mug is.'

title: Chunxiang’s Schoolroom Prank
description: Discover one of China’s most charming stage traditions in Chunxiang’s Schoolroom Prank, a lively Kunqu comedy full of wit, elegance and playful chaos. This production brings classical Chinese opera to the Fringe in a fresh, joyful and accessible way. Cheeky, clever Chunxiang turns a strict schoolroom upside down with sharp humour, quick timing and irresistible comic energy. Live singing, graceful movement and rhythmic dialogue create a vibrant world where tradition meets contemporary theatrical flair. The show also features the refined artistry of male dan performance, a classical cross-gender technique adding expressive beauty and a rare glimpse into the imaginative gender traditions of early Chinese theatre. This is an ideal introduction to Kunqu for newcomers and a treat for lovers of world theatre. Performed in traditional Chinese operatic vocal with Chinese and English surtitles. Workshops are also offered, an opportunity to explore Kunqu up close, details via Rotunda theatre.

title: Fasted
description: What do you see when you look in the mirror? A true likeness or a body fat with shame and self-loathing? Fasted takes us on a journey from a ballet school in Northern England to an austere fasting centre in Soviet Russia, overseen by the charismatic yet flawed Ivanov. Our protagonist, the ballerina Nina Glover, goes in search of a solution to her dysfunctional self-perception. Her search is hindered by the unwanted attention of an admirer, Conrad Salt, a fellow ballet student, who follows her, believing his romantic intensity will win her heart.

title: Darryl J Carrington: Out of the Box
description: Out of the Box is a multi-award-winning family show delighting audiences across the UK; an hour of joyful chaos, jaw-dropping skill, and irresistible fun. Fresh from five-star success at the Edinburgh Fringe and being named Best Family Show at Brighton Fringe, Darryl J Carrington transforms everyday objects into extraordinary adventures. A toothbrush stars in a balancing act, a string sparks a heist, and a tea party lands on someone’s head! With over 20 years of circus and clowning experience, Darryl’s silent comedy blends world-class juggling, inventive physical theatre, and heart-warming audience interaction. His humour transcends age and language, captivating children, parents, and grandparents alike.

title: An A to Z Guide to Dating
description: An A to Z Guide to Dating is a one-woman musical based on a dating book that writer-performer (and Best Newcomer at Musical Comedy Awards) Grace O’Keefe’s mother wrote in the late 80s. Publishers said it would set feminism back 20 years. 40 years later, let’s find out! This theatrical equivalent of a little girl struggling in her mother’s heels, the musical explores relationships, romance and living life to the fullest as a fully grown woman. Featuring original music by Grace O’Keefe (Summoning Sondheim, Sense & Sensibility), the show will leave audiences with a deeper appreciation of their mothers and a chaotic new approach to dating. This is the first preview of the show, which will be premiering with Underbelly at EdFringe 2026.

title: (E = F) Our Expressions Equal to Our Fulfilments
description: Talented Nigerian marginalized persons had always craved for the international spaces to display our amazing talents for poetry, music, song, dance and artistic expressions recognized with our diversity, however, the deprivation of our freedom of self-expression due to our diverse orientation has limited the fulfilment of our destinies. As a remedial endeavour, Our Expressions Equal to our Fulfilments (E=F), an initiative of ICATHED, a set of artistic expressions which includes but not limited to poetry, music, song, dance, digital & visual arts installations, story-telling & cultural exchanges, aimed at self-discovery and self-actualization of discriminated, marginalized and the underprivileged individuals, groups and communities in Nigeria has been a binge. The more the safe spaces we get to express ourselves, the more our fulfilment comes handy. At Brighton Fringe, we intend to entertain our audience with our aura-spiced and charismatically garnished show.

title: Globe Rotter (Work in Progress)
description: Roxane McLeod has spent over a decade as a yacht stewardess, travelling the world, and now she’s dropping anchor in Brighton for her first Work in Progress Show, GLOBE ROTTER. Expect sharp, slightly naughty laughs about family, failed self-improvement attempts, dating, and the chaos of life on the move. With absurd tales from life at sea, R-rated observations from her travels, and playful self-deprecation, Roxane delivers a fresh, fearless perspective. Opening the night is our Special guest, Shameel Kumar! Shameel is a New York native, Brighton-based comedian with a passion for the absurd. His material covers risqué topics such as the nutritional content of strawberries, the anatomy of mosquitoes, and the textural differences in facial hair. A brand-new show, catch it early!

title: Where I End and You Begin
description: Woyzeck loves Marie. Marie loves Woyzeck. Life is hard. Woyzeck kills Marie. Buchner’s original play Woyzeck, written in 1836, considers the impact of societal pressures on a poor soldier and his wife: Woyzeck feels powerless and humiliated. Marie must pay the price. It feels inevitable. Even now. Devised and developed by the company, Where I End and You Begin explores contemporary gender roles through the prism of Buchner’s classic play. Through extracts, verbatim material, physical theatre, songs and new writing we question what it is to be afraid of each other - and how we might be brave.

title: Selena Mersey: Bisexuelle
description: Johnny is a hard-boiled detective from New York City, California, and he’s got just one more case to crack before he retires from the CSI Miami. See, somebody did a very bad thing, and he'll be damned if they get away with it. But there’s one thing Johnny didn’t expect. The clues all start to point towards the mysterious dame he met this morning... Featuring original music, dance, questionable puppetry, and side-splitting laughs, Bisexuelle is the hotly anticipated second show from Brighton Fringe multi-award winner and Musical Comedy Awards finalist Selena Mersey. title: The Whole Time (Work In Progress)
description: 'A show for burnt-out millennials who now understand why they liked Mulan so much as a child to feel seen, as well as their Guardian-reading aunt who will come away with a catchy definition of what non-binary actually means.' The Whole Time is a standup comedy show, with a mix of autobiographical and observational humour. The show focuses on the recurring theme of realising things about my identity far later in life than I should have done, covering topics like queer dating / avoiding rock climbing, awkward therapy appointments, tedious gender clinic forms, being a nonbinary mess and just wishing to be a rat. “effortlessly put the crowd at ease with their quippy one-liners and slightly chaotic charm." - sheffield magazine “Sharp, clever and well observed, and with so much charm." - vix leyton "Oh your tattoo is cool!!" - kemah bob in the soho theatre bar

title: Makeup Masterclass Workshop
description: The Art of Facial Transformation: Feminising & Masculinising with Make-Up Make-up has the power to completely transform how we read a face. In this exciting and insightful masterclass at Brighton Fringe, we’ll explore the techniques behind feminising and masculinising facial features using contour, highlight, shading, brow design and structural illusion. Through live demonstration and clear, practical guidance, you’ll learn how subtle placement and tonal shifts can soften, sharpen, sculpt or redefine facial structure. We’ll break down how make-up influences perception, expression and gender presentation. Whether for performance, drag, theatre, photography or personal exploration. This session is open to performers, creatives, make-up artists and anyone curious about the artistry of transformation. Expect expert tips, real-time transformation and plenty of “how did that just happen?” moments, plus a Q&A at the end where you can ask your questions and dive deeper into the craft.

title: SLAY
description: Following a PTSD diagnosis, small-town, small-screen-obsessed Kit escapes the North East for London-until a text from her ex detonates her recovery, sending her spiralling through therapy, chaos and increasingly unhelpful coping fantasies. In a generation that processes trauma via TikTok, Kit is determined to stay the main character (because if she doesn’t laugh, she’ll cry and probably post it). As symptoms, triggers and disordered eating collide, she faces a choice: confront her past or continue doing what she does best - be delusional and avoid it. From Offie nominated actor-writer, Lois-Amber Toole's SLAY is a darkly funny, unapologetically North East reckoning with class, internet culture and the pressure on young women’s bodies.

title: Brighton Fringe Jazz Club
description: A stellar company of musicians perform tributes to two powerhouses of the jazz world. Contrasting events with sublime lineups and great music. Fri 8th May: Water From An Ancient Well: A Tribute to Abdullah Ibrahim The unique, laid back, funky style of this South African composer and pianist makes his music unique and joyous. The band guides you through Dollar Brand’s musical life from Capetown roots to the anthem of struggle against apartheid. Authentic reinterpretations of his most famous pieces and new tributes to his musical style. Fri 22nd May: Joss Peach’s Sidewinders BAFTA winning pianist leads his new 6 piece ensemble featuring a 3 horn front line of Josephine Davies, Robbie Robson and Matt Seddon- each highly acclaimed players in their own right. This carefully curated set of lovingly arranged latin, swing, calypso, boogaloo and groove classics will take you on a diverse musical journey and leave you with a big jazzy smile on your face! Photo credit: Wayne John Grundy

title: El Viaje
description: Based on interviews with my grandmother, Abuela, El Viaje uses storytelling and song as devices to share her exciting, and heart-breaking, journey from Cuba to Puerto Rico, to North America. A journey that immerses audiences in the captivating history of my Cuban family, set against the backdrop of Cuba's political and cultural history, accompanied by original live music. Using prose, comedy, movement, and my guitar, I recount my family’s extraordinary history. From my Bisabuelo (great-grandfather) fighting in the Cuban rebellion against Fulgencio Batista’s regime, to my Abuela’s incredible journey: escaping a dictatorship, winning a scholarship to study electrical engineering, and then becoming a prominent advocate for refugees throughout the Syrian crisis. This show vividly animates the challenges and triumphs of mine and my Abuela’s family. It is an honest and engaging account with themes of courage, empowerment, feminism, and justice echoing throughout.

title: Kah'Loca
description: KAH’LOCA is a 20 minutes family friendly outdoor work. Women dressed in bright colours with flamboyant funky headpieces use dance-theatre to challenge the narrative of what it means to be a woman and bring to public attention the scale of violence against women. KAH’LOCA's costumes symbolise a desire for everyone to have their own voice, to speak out, be bold and step up for themselves, empowering the harmed and shaping once mute spaces. A combination of voices, classical guitar song, gritty percussive sounds and protests creates the overall soundscape, overlapping with the breath of the dancers in motion to create a multi-sensory live performance which celebrates female strength and resilience.

title: Bitch On The Mic (Work In Progress)
description: A raw, playful and darkly funny theatre show that cracks open the world of a woman with late-diagnosed ADHD, haunted by the voices that told her she was never enough. Blending clown, character work and storytelling, Jess leads the audience through the chaos of a mind pushed to breaking point and the slow, stubborn climb back to herself. Packed full of sharp humour, surprising tenderness and a host of unruly characters who refuse to stay quiet. Highly relatable and absolutely ridiculous, this is a show about shame, transformation and the power of speaking the truth out loud. Bitch On The Mic invites you to witness what happens when a woman stops running from her past and steps into the light to dance with it instead. It’s a celebration of resilience, rebellion and the freedom that comes when you finally reclaim your power from your inner critic. Expect a rollercoaster of emotion, powerful messages and insights. Please note: This show is not for the easily offended or faint hearted.

title: These Roots Are Made for Walking
description: When women's stories are recorded, who gets the last word? Three original solo plays illuminate untold stories from history, exploring themes of queerness, feminism, bodily autonomy, gender roles, and activism throughout the 20th century. The triple bill opens with "She," a fictional tale set in 1941 filled with tap dancing, sapphic waltzes, and undercover nuns. Inspired by Sarah Waters’ Tipping the Velvet, "She" explores the beauty and risks of queer love during WWII. "Lesbians Eat Fire" follows Joanne, a fictional character created from the lived experiences of the Lesbian Avengers and lesbian activists from the early 1990s. The play demonstrates carrying the fire for those who came before us and staying alive when hope feels impossible. "The Eye of Dawn" is a take on Mata Hari, a 1920s Dutch exotic dancer accused of espionage. A woman caught between myth and truth, this play gives a voice to the multiplicity of her existence with a healthy serving of absurdism, irony, and show.

title: Around Asia in 7 Dishes
description: Around Asia in 7 Dishes Thursday 28th May | Blossoms Cocktail Bar, Brighton Blossoms Cocktail Bar invites you to an intimate evening of refined culinary exploration. Around Asia in 7 Dishes is a thoughtfully curated tasting experience celebrating the depth, elegance, and diversity of Asian cuisine. The evening will unfold through seven meticulously crafted small dishes, each inspired by a different Asian country. From subtle, delicate flavours to rich and expressive compositions, every course has been designed to showcase balance, technique, and authenticity. This immersive dining experience will take your palate on a seamless journey across Asia, offering a celebration of culture, craftsmanship, and flavour in an elegant and relaxed setting. Spaces are limited. Early booking is recommended. Please note Vegetarians option is available but we are we are unable to cater for Vegans.

title: Cocktails & Jazz Sessions
description: Join us for an unforgettable evening of live Blues, Soul, and Jazz at Blossoms Cocktail Bar, nestled in the heart of the vibrant Lanes. Let the smooth melodies and soulful tunes transport you to a world of rhythm and emotion, while our talented musicians deliver performances that will have you tapping your feet and smiling long after the night is over. To complement the music, we've crafted a special cocktail menu just for the season, featuring unique concoctions designed to enhance the mood and flavors of the night. Whether you're sipping a drink or swaying to the music, the atmosphere at Blossoms promises to be a celebration of good times, great company, and even greater music. Come for the cocktails, stay for the music, and leave with memories that will have you coming back for more. It’s an evening where the magic of live performance meets the art of mixology—only at Blossoms Cocktail Bar

description: Ren brings a varied set of piano-based melodies and guitar driven songs. Ren's performances are known for raw emotion and create a powerful connection with the audience exploring themes of identity, sexuality, heartbreak, loss and life's fragility. Expect your soul to be stirred in the beautiful and transforming surroundings of The Church of the Annunciation in Hanover. Plus very special guests - Jay Cons - extraordinary guitarist and musician with flare, complexity and strength. Julian Stevens - Brighton based trans poet, dancer and artist. Julian’s writing involves whimsical journaling, observational lists and poetic streams of consciousness. @julians.corner/ [email protected]

title: Mr Boy: The Beauty Contest for Boys
description: A one-man beauty contest in which I play all the parts. What is a boy meant to be? It’s a question on everyone’s lips, and one I intend to answer once and for all in the fairest way I can think of: a beauty pageant. Over one hour, the characters of the Mr Boy pageant will vie for your approval, as at the end of the show, the audience decide the winner! That’s right, you get to pick Mr Boy! Lucky you. It’s going to be high-energy, it’s going to be fun, it’s going to mad. Thanks!

title: Dr. Kara Kibara: The Love Guru
description: Dr. Kara Kibara is a self-proclaimed and successful online dating expert. She has helped thousands of people find love, but she herself is still single in her 40s. In this interactive cabaret show, Dr. Kara goes back to her past and reflects on what went wrong (or maybe right) that caused her to still be loveless. She shares her insights on dating, relationships, and love, and she offers her advice on how to find the one. This is a funny, interactive, heartwarming, and thought-provoking cabaret show featuring original songs, snippets of familiar 80's and 90's tunes as well as choreography, projections and monologues that will leave the audience laughing, crying, and inspired. It's A must-see for anyone who has ever been on a date or who has ever dreamed of finding love.

title: Caravanserai The Return: Opening Night Extravaganza
description: After a three-year wait... Caravanserai is back in Brighton! The doors are swinging open, and the walls are coming down for our opening night party. For one night only, our main venue Klub Kaput will drop its walls, and the party will be open to the whole site for FREE! We are bringing together our favourite bands and DJs from throughout the years at Caravanserai and inviting you back into our family to celebrate a jaw-dropping month ahead. Our launch night is proudly sponsored by Caffrey’s Black Stout. Entry is free, but we welcome donations on-site to support our artists! Please note that anyone under the age of 18 must leave the site by 9pm, and this will be enforced.

title: Every Single Sound In The World
description: An alien arrives on Earth with a simple task: collect every single sound on the planet. Unfortunately, humans and their animals, vegetables and minerals make a lot more sounds than she anticipated. Please help her! She can’t go home until she’s collected them all. In this live, reactive show, Natalie Patuzzo uses voice, objects, and audience interaction to construct (and dismantle) a world one sound at a time. Described as an ‘endearing yet wickedly funny physical performer’, Natalie Patuzzo’s debut solo clown show is chaotic, playful and full of sounds you may have heard before and may never want to hear again.

title: ClownUnity - Teeth and Tenderness
description: ClownUnity - Teeth and Tenderness is a heart‑opening clown performance that celebrates human connection in a post‑truth world. It’s a show that combines bite and softness, truth and absurdity, vulnerability and joy. This performance is made possible by the Brighton Fringe Community Award 2024, awarded for #LewesCurious events and continues the pay‑it‑forward chain of community creativity — one offering making space for the next - each step opening the door for more joy, play, and collective imagination. This is not a polished product. This is a communal ritual of presence, play, and connection, remembering we all belong.

title: Ghost Songs To Die For with Madame Isolde
description: Ghost Songs to Die For is a spooky, gloriously chaotic cabaret of true ghost stories, original songs, and one classic cover, with a distinctly Brighton twist. Your guide is Madame Isolde, a medium from 1936 who has drifted back into the future with insight from the astral plane and very strong opinions. She resurrects haunted theatres, restless spirits, and Brighton legends, including a ghost said to haunt the very Theatre Royal where the show is staged. Each tale becomes a song that is eerie, ridiculous, and unexpectedly touching. Alongside original music written for the show, Madame Isolde summons one iconic voice from beyond the veil, because even Brighton ghosts have impeccable taste. This is not a horror show. There are no jump scares and nobody climbs out of the audience. Expect dark humour, theatrical storytelling, and an atmosphere that feels perfectly at home by the sea. ***** Please also search for MADAME ISOLDE’S CRAZY ZODIAC CABARET *****

title: Cowpokes in a Bunkhouse
description: One story. Three endings. In a cluttered Wild West bunkhouse, three cowpokes try to keep a restless herd calm through the night. When singing fails and tempers fray, they turn to storytelling. Each cowpoke has their own version of the same tale about two brothers, a stolen elixir and a choice that changes everything. But as the stories grow wilder, darker and more revealing, it becomes clear these aren’t just bedtime tales for cattle. Rivalries surface, old wounds reopen and the line between fiction and truth begins to blur. As dawn approaches, the trio must face whether logic, aggression or kindness wins in the end. Blending physical comedy, audience interaction and sudden emotional turns, this offbeat western explores masculinity, memory and the stories we tell to live with ourselves.

title: Brighton School of Samba
description: Brighton School of Samba – Carnival Comes to the Streets A high-energy explosion of Brazilian rhythm and dance, bringing the spirit of Rio Carnival to Brighton. Brighton School of Samba (BSS) is the city’s original Rio-style samba school, delivering vibrant, high-energy performances inspired by the world-famous Brazilian carnival. Formed in 2007, BSS is a not-for-profit, community-led group built on the traditions of Rio’s “escolas de samba”, combining powerful percussion (bateria), dance, and carnival performance. The group regularly performs at major events across Brighton and beyond, including Brighton Pride, Burning of the Clocks, and Brighton Fringe. This performance features a full samba bateria of drums alongside dancers, creating an immersive street-carnival atmosphere. Expect driving rhythms, colourful costumes, and an infectious energy that gets audiences moving. BSS is open to all and reflects Brighton’s inclusivity.

title: Fringe Academy: Equity 101
description: The Brighton & Sussex branch of Equity is the local voice of your union. Chaired by local actor Carole Bremson, we are a vibrant community of professional performers and creative workers across theatre, film, television, radio, variety, and more. As part of the UK’s trade union for the performing arts and entertainment industry, we represent, support, and advocate for our members throughout Sussex. We host regular monthly meetings, events, workshops, and campaigns to strengthen our industry, promote fair working conditions, and build a supportive network for local talent. Whether you’re a seasoned professional or just starting out, we’re here to help you thrive. Come along and meet us to find out more about Equity and the work we do. Fringe Academies are free to attend and are designed to help Artists make the most out of their Brighton Fringe experience!

title: Feast & Fable | A story-cookalong
description: Join the Story Baker for a magical, immersive experience as we make and break bread together around the fire. The Story Baker has a wonderful recipe. It’s as old as time, yet it’s still made, every day, in people’s homes the world over. The ingredients? A traditional folk tale with many twists and turns; a willingness to join in (everyone in the audience can participate) and, of course, YOU! Our expert fire chefs from Woodfire Camping will help you cook your creation over the fire before we sit to share and break bread together. This enchanting and hands on show shares traditional tales from around the world and is about cooking, sharing, contributing and friendship. Please read before booking: Due to the nature of this event children must be accompanied by an adult. 1 adult to no more than 2 children. No child under 18 will be admitted unless accompanied by an adult. All ingredients supplied - flour and fire will be present; gluten-free options available.

title: Between Strands
description: Between Strands is a performance about hair, memory, and the bonds that endure beyond loss. Drawing on East Asian cultural symbolism, it follows the tender relationship between a granddaughter and her grandmother, weaving in Taiwanese traditions, stories, and the rituals that shape their farewells. In many Asian cultures, hair carries lineage, identity, and the thread that connects generations. This physical performance explores how a single strand of hair can hold memory, intimacy, and hope. Using shadow work, gesture, and breath-led movement, the performers trace the shifting space between holding on and letting go, revealing the invisible threads that keep us connected. Blending contemporary performance with cultural heritage, Between Strands opens a quiet yet powerful space to reflect on belonging, transformation, and the ties we continue to carry.

title: Out of the Bin
description: Out of the Bin is an interactive theatre piece that transforms overlooked rubbish scattered across our streets into sound, movement and art — revealing stories of homelessness that too often go unnoticed. Blending physical play, object manipulation and audience involvement, the performance dives into everyday waste we discard without thought. Rubbish is produced endlessly, yet for some, a bin becomes a place of survival — even an unexpected source of hope. Through these fragments, the piece uncovers moments of resilience, humour and humanity hidden in our cities. As discarded objects are reimagined, Out of the Bin questions what happens when something — or someone — society has thrown away suddenly becomes “valuable.” When trash turns into treasure, does the world truly see it differently? And in that shift, can a person begin to lose themselves? Playful, tender and socially urgent, Out of the Bin invites audiences into a world where waste becomes wonder.

description: On the 23rd of May, taking place from 15:30 to 18:30, will be an incredible afternoon of poetry performances. There will be a writing workshop for the first hour, then four featured artists for the second third (Olly Taylor, Amy Baker, LJ O’Hagan & Harry Coleman), as well as an open-mic which will take place in the final third. The sign-ups for the open-mic will be at the event. Come down if you are in the Brighton area, whether you want to perform or merely to observe what the Brighton poetry scene has to offer. This event will be at The Rossi Bar (8 Queens Road, Brighton, BN1 3WA)

title: Supernatural
description: Supernatural revolves around a single choice - one that changed everything. Over the course of an hour, a room full of strangers is drawn into something intimate and personal - the choices you’ve made, the reality you're shaping, and how one decision, made now, could send ripples far into the future. With an uncanny ability to peer beneath appearances, David Stewart invites you into an experience of mystery, meaning, and possibility. As the night unfolds, a magic is revealed that feels less like illusion, and more like something present - subtle, flowing through it all. By the end, there is only one question: What will you choose today, that shapes your tomorrow? Your fate is in your hands. Doors open 30 minutes before the show starts. Please make sure you arrive in good time.

title: FilmPride: LGBTQ+ Short Film Showcase
description: FilmPride, Brighton's LGBTQIA+ film festival dedicated to independent queer filmmaking, presents a selection of short films and from its past and present programmes. Carefully curated to reflect specific themes and spanning all genres, the films - from all corners of the world - are rooted in lived experience, creativity and community. All films are captioned. Each screening will be followed by a Q&A with filmmakers. Each screening is unique - please check out the FilmPride website for the full line up of the films presented at each screening event. SESSION THEMES: FRIDAY 22nd MAY 6pm: "The Observed Self" 8pm: "Spaces of Reclaiming" SATURDAY 23rd MAY 4pm: "Echoes & Awakenings" 6pm: "Under The Surface" SUNDAY 24th MAY 4pm: "Bodies & Belonging" 6pm: "Legacies of Resistance"

title: Jody Kamali: This Is My Dad
description: After his father’s sudden death, Jody digs through his belongings, uncovering the man he thought he knew. Suddenly, he’s back for one hour to tell us who he was. Playing both himself and his father, Jody unpicks his Dad’s bizarre life in a thoughtful, funny theatrical solo show about grief, secrets and the contradictions within us all. A preview for the Edinburgh Fringe from award-winning, Brighton Fringe veteran Jody Kamali.

title: He's six years older than me
description: She is doing an At-home STI kit. Her train of thoughts goes to where they met the first time, what music they were playing and what they were talking about. She is still doing the STI kit. She is thinking of all the sexual encounters she has been in. Their eye colours, their star signs, their ages. She is trying to find the pattern. She is trying to find where it all started. And she is still doing the kit, thinking the instructions are not so easy to follow. This play is a conversation about consent, power dynamics and privilege with arguments for and against the act of stealthing. It is a young woman’s self-reckoning or self-sabotage confronting the questions arising from the sexual encounters she has been in. Within the belief of fixing the problem or whatsoever, she digs into every detail and all things in common that these men had and/or did. ‘He’s six years older than me.’ So was he and so was him. How is that relevant in this situation, in this conversation, in this play?

title: This is Philosophy
description: Part Lecture. Part Stand Up. And Part Lecture. Join Professor Morgan Morganthaller, lecturer in contemporary semiotics at Burton-on-Beans University and recent divorcee, for a night of academic rigour and cultural intrigue. Past lectures include: Duality of self: Can it ever be Man AND Muppet? Forgettable Deniable: Masculinity in the philosophy of Katy Perry. The Bill Passed: Understanding the affects of Brexit on Geese. Professor Morganthaller’s new lectures will tear the mask from contemporary culture, and gaze into its meaty meaty face. Expect revelations that will shift the fabric of society as we know it. Stationary will be provided.

title: For The Record
description: Nancy and David are dead. And even worse, they are stuck in the same room together for all eternity, unless they can come to an agreement. Through creating a timeline of music from their lifetime of memories, they uncover whether their lives and loves could have ever worked out differently. They quickly learn that if they are not honest with their feelings, the way out of the room will lead somewhere... unusual, certainly not the afterlife they'd expected. For The Record is an emotional and comedic exploration of the afterlife, secrets, and regrets. With a mystical door, cryptic vinyl player, and mysterious puppets, could the-one-that-got-away have ever been The One, if only they had met at the right time? Salt Air Stories is a brand new production company, made up of passionate storytellers who want to explore myths and fables in all of their forms. Join us around the campfire. From the creative minds behind OffFest Nominated comedy, Rambunctious Scallywag:

title: As the Winds and Waters Are
description: If you brought together four unrelated characters from four separate novels by one author, what dynamics would unfold? What experiences and perspectives would spark warmth, empathy, or compassion between them, which miscomprehension or conflict? Would they be drawn to one another, or find themselves in hostile confrontation? Anthony, Jackie, George, and Ralph, each with strong connections to Hove and Brighton, gather at Patrick Hamilton’s final Norfolk seaside home on September 23rd, 1962, in a liminal space between the author's life and death. Each character can only speak from the historical and social perspective in which Patrick set them, but with his own historical insight, how does Patrick himself respond to their interactions? In taking leave of their creator and of each other, there are promises, reassurances, resolutions, and ends left dangling— some ominous...

title: Bound by the Wind: Echoes of the Past
description: What really happened on the day Mulan laid down her sword? Bound by the Wind: The Echoes of the Past returns to the legendary warrior at the moment history skips over — the night the war ends, when victory no longer gives answers. As Mulan confronts a captured enemy and the loyalty of those closest to her, the play narrows its focus to a single turning point: a decision that will cost lives, break faith, and redefine what heroism truly demands. This new version strips back the epic scale of previous productions to explore the human cost behind the myth — not the battles fought, but the choice to stop fighting. Blending stylised stage combat, physical storytelling, and an immersive soundscape, Bound by the Wind offers an intimate reimagining of a legendary figure caught between duty, identity, and freedom. Created by a multicultural team of emerging artists, the production invites audiences to witness the woman behind the war god — and the moment she chooses to leave.

title: I'm Sorry to Disappoint You All
description: I'm sorry to disappoint you all: Two friends tangled together. Cocooned in their high rise flat and swallowed by the vastness of the city, an unravelling begins. Dynamic contemporary dance unfolds against swirling projected landscapes, set to an eclectic, layered sound score by composer Phoebe Coco. Drawing on the duo’s shared mental health struggles and frustrations with the current climate for artists, I’m sorry to disappoint you all is a touching exploration of isolation, futility and the search for deeper connection. In This Room: An inquisitive, intergenerational group of local artists, makers and creators have teamed up with Charis and Mette of The 12th Floor to experiment, create and connect. What you see is the result of 3 workshops full of play, hard work and dedication. The 12th Floor thanks this wonderful group for taking a leap of faith with them. The 12th Floor are proud recipients of the South East Dance Fringe Roots Award.
description_html:
I'm sorry to disappoint you all:
Two friends tangled together. Cocooned in their high rise flat and swallowed by the vastness of the city, an unravelling begins.

Dynamic contemporary dance unfolds against swirling projected landscapes, set to an eclectic, layered sound score by composer Phoebe Coco.

Drawing on the duo’s shared mental health struggles and frustrations with the current climate for artists, I’m sorry to disappoint you all is a touching exploration of isolation, futility and the search for deeper connection.

In This Room:
An inquisitive, intergenerational group of local artists, makers and creators have teamed up with Charis and Mette of The 12th Floor to experiment, create and connect. What you see is the result of 3 workshops full of play, hard work and dedication. The 12th Floor thanks this wonderful group for taking a leap of faith with them.


The 12th Floor are proud recipients of the South East Dance Fringe Roots Award.

title: Family Fortunes (But for Bad People)
description: Remember Family Fortunes? This is like that, but for horrible people. For this filthy comedy quiz, we've asked 100 scumbags all the worst questions we could think of. Things like: "What would you not want to catch your dad doing?"; "Name a politician who deserves to be eaten by a shark" and "What animal would you most like to shag?" Now it’s up to you to guess what the top answers were... There’s points, there’s prizes, and there’s a 95% chance we’re going to hell, so come and play before ITV have us killed. We've had sell out shows at Brighton Fringe and Edinburgh Fringe two years running - book early so you don’t miss the filth.

title: How's Your Head? (Work In Progress)
description: Aarian is a triple threat. Brown, Blind and Bi. Arguably already too many things to talk about in a debut stand up hour. Then 2024 came, and he only had to go and get f**king brain cancer. The highly anticipated debut stand-up show from the multi award-winning writer of the smash hit play It’s a MotherF**king Pleasure. Join Aarian ahead of his run at the 2026 Edinburgh Fringe Festival to explore his relationship with his Persian identity, his time in hospital battling an aggressive brain tumour, and his obsession with 2019 Netflix spectacular The Crown. Directed by Dec Munro Written & Performed by Aarian Mehrabani Commissioned by Lowry as part of the 'Developed With' programme. With additional support from Arts Council England.

title: Ryan Mold: Resistant
description: A funny, honest look at what happens when life changes faster than you can keep up. From technology that's confusing, to government rules that make no sense, to the creeping reality of getting older — it all feels a bit harder than it used to be! But Resistant isn’t just a rant. It’s about trying to find your place in a world that won’t stop changing, and laughing about it along the way. My knees click, my patience for influencers has gone, and apparently my phone is “retro.” If you’ve ever felt left behind by technology, confused by modern life, or just tired of being told to “embrace the future,” this show is for you. Join me for a night of relatable, warm, and sharply funny stand-up about surviving the madness of modern times — one reluctant update at a time. "Absolutely brilliant" – Nick Helm "Full of very funny and original jokes, a laugh-a-minute" – London Theatre "Keeps a room onside being laugh-out-loud funny. He’s a classic British comedian" – theQR.co.uk

title: Caitlin Powell is Buffering (Work In Progress)
description: The world is loud, slow and often wrong. Join neurotic and petty (gay and correct) rising star Caitlin Powell (BBC New Comedy Award nominee, Funny Women, and LGBTQ+ New Comedian finalist), as she smashes every button on the metaphorical keyboard and tries to figure out what the hell is going on. This is a ‘brilliant’ (Jen Brister) and ‘delightfully self-effacing’ (Chortle) work-in-progress show that will resonate with the girls, gays and theys (or anyone with the pattern recognition of a bloodhound).

title: Nathan Cassidy: Ewwww!
description: Winner Best Show Brighton Fringe 2022/2023 with an extraordinary new standup show. WARNING: FULL-FRONTAL NUDITY - Ewwww! After a single throwaway comment from a 20-year-old, Nathan Cassidy begins to unravel — is he old now? Does he care? In this daring and hilarious new hour, Cassidy responds the only way he knows how: by taking the biggest risks of his professional life. With time running out, what will he do to chase the high? Maybe nudity. Maybe a mic drop you’ll never forget. Expect powerful storytelling, brutal honesty and an image that might live with you forever. Another not-to-be-missed show from the multi award-winning, critically acclaimed star. This one’s unforgettable. Star of 6 Specials (inc 3 Amazon Prime), BBC Radio 5 Live and million downloaded podcast 'Daily Notes'. European Tour 2025. 13 Comedy Award wins/nominations 2019-2025. And catch his other show From the Ashes at the Fringe too!

title: Nathan Cassidy: From The Ashes
description: Multi-award winning standup comic tells the story of how this year's Ashes series changed his life. A story of love, loss and Travis Head’s moustache. Last Christmas Day, Nathan Cassidy is alone in a basement, feverish and half-drunk, watching England getting destroyed in the Ashes. But as the Ashes slips further out of reach, something strange begins to happen... a surreal journey of survival, redemption and hope. From the award-winning star of 25 solo shows and one of comedy’s most original voices, this show is a hilarious and heart-opening journey through cricket, collapse, and the strange magic of Christmas. And, like all the best stories, like the ones his grandad used to tell him as a kid, this one might only be half true too. 13 Best Show wins/nominations in UK Comedy Festivals since 2019. Star of 3 Amazon Prime specials, BBC Radio 5 Live, and million downloaded podcast Daily Notes. Catch his other show Ewwww! at the Fringe too.

title: Dignity Off
description: Sunjai can't help being silly. Matt can't help being crude. The two friends try to change each other; to give their friend their dignity back. Will they succeed or will they change themselves?' Sunjai Arif (Bath Comedy Festival New Act winner 2019) and Matt Roseblade blow the dust off their debut show - Dignity Off. Ten years after the shambling, chaotic original, the boys return with the same format and a decade of comedic experience. Will it be polished? Nope. Will it still be shambling and chaotic? Yes. Will it be funny? Oh yes.

title: That's so Sapphic Podcast with Jane Postlethwaite & Alexi Melvin
description: Join social media sapphics Jane Postlethwaite (@JaneDoesComedy) and Alexi Melvin (@TheAlexiMelvin) for a special live recording of the popular hit lesbian podcast, "That’s So Sapphic". A show created exclusively for Brighton Fringe, Jane and Alexi will dive deep into the world of sapphic culture — past, present, and delightfully niche. Growing up with little sapphic representation, they look back at the clues that were always there, from film and pop culture to those questionable teenage fixations that now make perfect sense. If you're a fan you know that in each episode we explore a new theme as we get to know each other on and off the mic, all while asking the central question: what really makes something sapphic? We'll be selecting a sapphic theme, getting to know our audience and doing a Q&A.

title: 100% Badgers with Matt Hobs
description: 100% Badgers is the sell-out 2025 Edinburgh Fringe stand-up comedy show where Matt Hobs talks about how the worlds of humans and badgers collide. The show takes us on Hobs’ journey from a passing interest in badgers, to an obsession that leads to him standing in a field looking at a caged, frightened badger caught up in the misinformation of the bovine TB debate. We’ll learn about the European badger and its dachshund nemesis, the presidential American badger, the world-famous internet sensation that is the honey badger and many more.

title: Dan Kelly: How I Went to Every Supermarket in the World
description: Dan has been a data collector for 10 years. You know when they say how much it costs to live somewhere? He's the guy who has to find out. He goes round the world and walks around supermarkets recording the prices of apples/cheese/you name it into his Dictaphone. He's been to pretty much every supermarket, in every country, in the world. This is the story of how. From the man who brought you How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon. Praise for How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon: “A great life-affirming tale” - The Dark Chat “Very entertaining and funny” - John Fleming

title: Madame Isolde’s Crazy Zodiac Cabaret
description: Madame Isolde sees your future and it’s chaotic. Expect comedy, cabaret and complimentary astrology as this delightfully deranged hostess leads a night of variety, cosmic confusion and questionable revelations. From somewhere between 1936 and deep space, Isolde returns to deliver her Horrorscopes filled with glamour, drama and just enough doom to keep things interesting. Joined by a line up of eclectic performers, she presents a dazzling mix of cabaret delights. Equal parts absurd, camp and celestial, this is cabaret for those who prefer their future read with sequins, satire and a touch of chaos. **** Please also search for GHOST SONGS TO DIE FOR with MADAME ISOLDE ****

title: Alexander Bennett: All It Takes (Work in Progress)
description: Wondering when you’ll catch a break? Wondering when the hard work will pay off? This is a (Work in Progress) show for you. Expect songs and strong feelings. Alexander Bennett is a ​Chortle and Leicester Comedy Festival Award nominated comedian. He is the creator and co-host of the award nominated cult podcast Born Yesterday. His solo shows have received rave reviews, been recommended by the British Comedy Guide, featured in The Times Top Jokes of the Edinburgh Fringe and performed on the West End at London's prestigious Soho Theatre. He has been featured in the Guardian, Metro and IPaper and has been heard on BBC Radio 4 and 5 Live.

description: Not a lot has been written about the decade 'the nineties' and so Adam Larter will FINALLY try to get to the bottom of what happened in this decade. title: Musical Chairs: Musical Stand-Up Comedy with Friz Frizzle and Friends
description: A concentrated blast of musical stand-up comedy. Song ruiner and piano masher Friz Frizzle is joined by the daft, exuberant Ashley Frieze on guitar, and the droll Edi Johnston on ukulele. Joyful jokes and silly songs. “Frizzle is energetic, inventive and beautifully crass”, Independent. “Ashley Frieze, a true master of his craft”, Jongleurs. “Johnston Took The Roof Off”, Laffacake Comedy It’s simply an hour of wonderful musical stand-up comedy, proving you can have multiple musical comedians on the bill... and nobody gets hurt. Three comedians, four instruments, but only one microphone. This is a compilation with something for everyone. A hit at comedy festivals, around the UK, featuring acts who've earned their stripes in Comedy Clubs all over, this is a side-splitting line-up that'll leave you rolling in the aisles and humming the jokes next morning. They'll sing, you'll laugh.

title: CK Nath: A Bit of Foreign Stuff (Work In Progress)
description: High energy and full of bite! Join CK as she takes you through the juiciest bits of her life growing up in India, finding herself in the UK and the inevitable culture clash in this work-in-progress show. Blending sharp stand-up, vivid storytelling, and cheeky character work as she channels the conservative Indian mother as easily as a middle class British nanny. CK is a finalist of West End New old Act 2023; So You Think You’re Funny? 2022 and Komedia New Comedy Award 2024 semi-finalist.

title: The Buttercream Boys
description: The Buttercream Boys is a joyful, uplifting improvised comedy show where Marc Salmon and Nick Adamson spend an hour trying to make a real audience member’s dream come true. Every performance is unique—chaotic, heartfelt, and created with the whole room. Marc is a West End Old Act of the Year finalist (“naturally funny” – Mark Simmons; “a very funny dude” – Mike OT). Nick is a long-time performer with The Free Association, known for his warmth, quick wit, and razor-sharp improv instincts. Fresh from Lambeth Fringe and now running a popular monthly residency in Elephant and Castle, the show consistently sends audiences home smiling, energised, and a little bit in love with the world again.

title: Lilla Multipass: Woman(33)
description: Woman (33) is a one-woman clown/character comedy show starring Woman (33), who has a bag of tote bags, a uterus in retrograde and an inkling that her life is one size too small. From crying in front of Costa to looking for solace in IKEA, this is the story of finding joy. The show takes place in her first-ever therapy session, as she attempts to piece herself back together after that Costa incident. She realises recovery will take more than a bubble bath or a trip to IKEA, and begins confronting the parts of herself she’d rather ignore: the environmentally (self)conscious consumer, the concerningly mature inner child, and the hustle loving "IT GIRL" persona she hides behind. The woman behind the Woman is Lilla Multipass (★★★★★, Morning Star) – a London-based Hungarian clown with a knack for psychology – blending two of her passions in her debut solo show. Come, get on this joke-packed emotional rollercoaster and let your inner child out to play: it’s cheaper than therapy!

title: Juliette Burton: Sexy Nerdy Work In Progress
description: Every night a different nerdy work in progress show! Villain Era: As the sun sets, a new antihero awakens. Calling all big-hearted heroes! Award-winning comedian Juliette Burton is summoning YOU. Join the dark side in a comedy quest for grown-up geeks and justice-hungry joy seekers ready to battle the REAL dragons: inequality and bad sex lives. It’s time to iron out the kinks! Luckily, there’s a room full of you. Collaboration is key, chaos is guaranteed and laughter is your ultimate power-up. You don't just watch the show - you play it. Board To Distraction: Board games meet brainpower: Fast-paced fun where everyone leaves smarter, smugger and significantly more competitive. When comedy and board games collide, there’s no chance of being bored!
