title: Struan Logan: Chaotic Good
description: After several years out of performing, comedian and writer Struan Logan returns to the craft with new found sense of zest, bringing his unique blend of narrative driven stand-up with all the pieces falling into place. Last time Struan was in Brighton was on a whistle-stop tour as a globetrotting headliner and festival comic with his solo show being performed across three continents. As a writer Struan has sprung back in by working with Scottish paper The National, providing opinions that are political but focus on the personal over partizan. Chaotic Good does the same but you’ll have to see the whole hour to find out how. “A truly authentic comedian.” ★ ★ ★ ★ – Voice Mag “The time just flew.” ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ – Weekend Notes “Humble.” ★ ★ ★ ★ ½ – Adelaide Advertiser “Adorably goofy.” ★ ★ ★ ★ – The Music “Cringe.” ★ ★ – The List
description_html:
After several years out of performing, comedian and writer Struan Logan returns to the craft with new found sense of zest, bringing his unique blend of narrative driven stand-up with all the pieces falling into place.

Last time Struan was in Brighton was on a whistle-stop tour as a globetrotting headliner and festival comic with his solo show being performed across three continents.

As a writer Struan has sprung back in by working with Scottish paper The National, providing opinions that are political but focus on the personal over partizan. Chaotic Good does the same but you’ll have to see the whole hour to find out how.

title: Ask A Stripper: Pulling Back The G-String
description: Ask A Stripper is a comedy show like no other. Real life strippers reveal all in this fiercely feminist and riotously funny Q&A. Ever wanted to know what strippers eat for breakfast? How do they really spend their money? Ever dated a customer? Stacey Clare (author of "The Ethical Stripper") and Sabrina Jade (star of reality TV show "Slagwars") return to Brighton Fringe with their candid humour, razor sharp wit and iconic stage presence. No one does crowd-work like strippers can - from working long hours performing for boozed up punters, there is no heckle they can't shut down. Ask A Stripper lifts a lid on a deeply mysterious, stigmatised and misunderstood industry, and offers insights on working conditions, regulations and how sex work is represented in popular culture. From backstage banter, to brutal honesty, this show will have you laughing as much as questioning everything you've ever thought you knew about the sex industry. Ever had a burning question? There's a cream for that.

title: Alice India: In Bloom
description: Award winning comedian (ADHD Comedy Award Winner 2025 and North Bridge Media New Comedy Writing Award Nominee 2025) Alice India is back at Brighton Fringe with a brand new show about the funny in what we hide from each other. In this new work in progress show, Alice India wants to peel back the secrets we keep from each other and ourselves whether we mean to or not. Exploring what happens when we have no choice but to wear what is different about us on our sleeves and whether change ever really changes us. title: Stephen and Hamlet: Blind and Mad
description: Let’s face it, we’re all nuts. When you’re a middle-aged, blind obsessive compulsive, though, life can be particularly odd, not to mention confusing and more than a little rude. Having previously traipsed around Edinburgh, Hove, Plymouth and Brighton, London’s gentlest comedian, Stephen Portlock, accompanied by his impeccably well behaved dog Hamlet, and bringing a cuddly Minion in tow, returns to Brighton to leave residents and visitors bewildered, befuddled and bemused as he delivers a very ramshackle guide through the highways and byways of life’s weirdness, exploring his ambivalent feelings about identity politics, and musing on how we can just learn to be nicer to one-another. Sweet, sometimes filthy, often embarrassing and surprisingly often true, this is one sentimental journey you will definitely remember.

title: Abbie Murphy: Garage Girl (Work In Progress)
description: Run, come, gather round! A love letter to UK Garage and the iconic tunes that defined a generation. "Garage Girl" is for anyone who lived it, loved it, or found it through its revival — all while asking: was life really better then, or were we just young? Finalist of Not So New Comedian of the Year 2025, Abbie Murphy is “nothing short of excellent” (Deadline News) and “One to watch” (The List). Star of David Brent: Life on the Road, Cheaters and Breeders.

title: Phil Kay
description: Legendary Scottish comedian, Phil Kay, returns to Brighton Fringe for 2 nights only. His unforgettable style of live standup will leave you wondering what just happened, as his whirlwind-like humour rips through the room like a tornado, yet tenderly tickles your funny bones. Phil has been crashing onto stages since 1989 with all the energy and fervour of an illegal rave, the improvisation skills of a professional shoplifter, and the comedy timing of a showbiz giant. His crackpot stories, unpredictable asides, and maniacal stage presence set him apart from mainstream comedy circuits, earning him a core following of lifelong devotees. Come and be converted, this one is not-to-be-missed! 'An enthralling, edge-of-the-seat high wire act, when he delivers, the laughs are all the louder' (Guardian) 'It's a case of gripping the side of the seat and holding on' (The Herald) 'Fertile, feral, other-worldly and exploding with energy' (The Scotsman) 'A master of the improvised' (The Skinny)

title: Kate Cheka: The Last Funny Woman (Work In Progress)
description: Last year award-winning UK comedian Kate Cheka attempted to save the world, now it is time to burn it down. What does it mean to be a feminist while Western civilisation collapses around us? The Funny Women Award winner 2023, Chortle Best Newcomer Nominee 2024 and DIVA Magazine Queer Comedian of the Year 2025 ponders this and other things. Like should she have just married her teenage boyfriend and become a tradwife instead of trying to be a strong independent woman. Come help Kate figure it out. ''Political comedy may yet have found its new messiah'' (The Guardian)

title: Who The F*&$% is Madeleine?
description: 'Who the F*$% is Madeleine?' Not only Lily Allen's hit song, but the question Madeleine has grappled with through years of living abroad, navigating Irish and French culture, and messing up her romantic life fantastically. This is Madeleine's recount of misadventures to a soap opera degree of tea, and a love letter to the gays and girlies, for their supportive voice notes, loud karaoke sessions, and endless gig pig behaviour at live music events, showing her time and time again where the healthy love is. Madeleine Munford is a stand-up comedian and actress originally from New York, who is currently based in London, England. Known for her honest storytelling, with as sharp as a whip comedic timing and a dry wit, Madeleine is a rising star on the London comedy scene. Before her relocation, Madeleine performed as the resident MC and producer for Icarus Comedy Club and Friday Night Live. Madeleine has performed as the support act for David McSavage and Bobcat Goldthwait.

title: Camilla Borges: Be Not Afraid!
description: Camilla has a theory that whoever you played in your school Nativity shapes how your life turns out. She played Mary, so surely when she grew up she became the most important person in the world? That is not quite what happened... Now she is in her 30s and life isn’t quite shaping up to what she was promised. Maybe now if she goes back, she will be able to turn things around. *Queue Little Donkey* Mary is everything, but she is also just a girl. Through characters, games and lots (and lots) of audience interaction, Camilla re-tells the story of the Nativity from a slightly different perspective. Expect familiar faces, and a few surprising ones- there definitely was a Lobster present at the birth of Christ? She asks the important questions, such as: Did Mary shave her legs? Was Joseph the original cuck? And, what exactly is Myrrh?? Hopefully, by re-visiting Mary Camilla will finally be able to properly grow up and stop being afraid. A show of pure silliness.

title: Jamie Byrne's Delightful History of Cigarettes
description: In Britain today, cigarettes are threated in all directions. Vaping, smoking bans, some other third thing I haven't thought of yet: they all have it in for cigarettes. Please (PLEASE) join Jamie Byrne in learning about the history of cigarettes and why they are a cornerstone of our culture. You'll have fun along the way, and you just might learn something. Jamie is an award winning character comedian who has written for the BBC.
description_html:
In Britain today, cigarettes are threated in all directions. Vaping, smoking bans, some other third thing I haven't thought of yet: they all have it in for cigarettes. Please (PLEASE) join Jamie Byrne in learning about the history of cigarettes and why they are a cornerstone of our culture. You'll have fun along the way, and you just might learn something.

Jamie is an award winning character comedian who has written for the BBC.

title: The SMALL Stuff
description: Daniel Tizon offers a forensic dissection of daily life’s minor agonies, from dealing with a chatty new neighbour to accepting, aged eight, dancing wasn’t for him, concluding that the extroverted demands of flair, gestures and exaggerated facial expressions were a technical impossibility for his understated persona. He explores, with equal scrutiny, the fraught etiquette of the adult education college urinal, specifically the moment a classmate you’ve never spoken to in class attempts to initiate the breakthrough conversation while positioned next to you at the trough. Of all the places to finally bond, the gents’ feels like a questionable choice. It's all a social minefield.

title: James Pendleton: Soft Boy
description: James used to live in Russia. Just packed up and went. Now he's back and can't decide what to have for lunch. Max Turner Prize Semi-Finalist | Edinburgh Fringe 2024 | Sold Out Brighton Fringe 2025 The guy who navigated Moscow can't navigate his own relationship. Sharp, uncomfortable stand-up about when being emotionally intelligent just means you can explain exactly why you're fucked. Russia stories that make your life seem normal. Relationship material that hits like a personal attack. When did growing up become giving in? Work in progress.

title: Phenomonopoly: The Surreal Estate Game Presented by The Mother Wolf Club
description: Hosted by the pun-loving Minotaur, three audience members become contestants in this surreal estate game show, racing to write the most unhinged fridge magnet poem imaginable. Why? To stop the future of language from devolving into emoji soup, and to topple the evil Uncanny Valley Girl from her dairy-based dystopia: the Tower of Babybel. On their journey they’ll duel with fowl-mouthed giant punk chicken John Cooper Cluck, slow-dance with eel sexologist Pink Freud and his slippery-slick cousin Elvers Presley, and be serenaded by Hal—the anthropomorphic fire alarm with Broadway dreams and rage issues. Part interactive musical, part high-brow panto, 100% the weirdest entertainment of your life.

title: Wheel of Failure
description: A washed-up game-show host lost his big break in a lawsuit. His wife left. His kids hate him. Now he’s clinging to one last shot at redemption. With only his ten-year-old indentured sidekick, Orphan, and a cardboard cut-out of his ex-wife for support, he hosts a disastrously homemade game show packed with questionable challenges like Whose Drink Is Mine? and Wheel of Shit From My House. Created by real-life father/son duo William and Jimmy Kontos, this interactive comedy invites the audience to play along, compete for points, and chase victory—only to discover that in this world, success is impossible and failure is guaranteed. Absurd, dark, and painfully funny, this is a show about pride, desperation, family, and the terrifying hope that maybe, just maybe, this time it will all work out. (It won’t.)

title: Zoe Cousin: Paul Sykes is Back on the Family Patch (Work in Progress)
description: Paris. Panama City. Pontefract. Pyongyang. He’s been aaabart a bit. But now he’s back on the family patch, and it’s like he never went away. Join Wakefield’s infamous hardman Paul Sykes, on a journey about survival, self-discovery and being a big fish in a big pond (Wakefield is the 11th largest city in the UK, when you consider wider districts). You might laugh, you might cry, you will learn how to punch someone in the f***ing earhole. As seen on BBC3. BBC New Comedy Awards 2025 regional finalist. So You Think You’re Funny 2025 semi-finalist. Zoe also appeared on BBC1’s Bargain Hunt in 2021, where she made a total loss of £110. Paul has never been on Bargain Hunt, though he did have a tense clash with David Dickinson in 1998 over Paul’s claim to have come up with his catchphrase “cheap as chips”. Dickinson has refused to comment on the matter. “Quite simply one of the most exciting character comedians on the circuit! Everyone should make it their mission to see Zoe!” Chris Cantrill

title: Tiny Dancers Family Rave
description: Get ready for the ultimate daytime party where grown-ups and kids dance together to the best dance music ever made! Tiny Dancers Family Rave is the award-winning event that has been selling out legendary London venues for over five years. We are thrilled to bring our high-energy and infectious spirit to Caravanserai for Brighton Fringe. Join our resident DJ JOY ALARM for a journey through nostalgic hits that travel through all eras of dance music. Having graced the stage at many major UK festivals including Glastonbury, Boomtown and Isle of Wight, JOY ALARM breathes new life into the tunes you raved to back when sleepless nights meant "bottled beer" rather than "bottle feed". Expect a sensory wonderland for all ages: • Live DJs and MCing • Bubbles, Confetti Cannons, and Balloons • Creative Crafts and Face Painting • Huge audience participation moments Relive your clubbing days and introduce the next generation to the culture we love. It’s big tunes for parents and big fun for kids!

title: Caroline McEvoy: RAGIN’ (WORK IN PROGRESS)
description: RAGIN’ is the latest work-in-progress show from Northern Irish comedian and chronically nice person Caroline McEvoy. Following ‘an impressive debut’ (The Scotsman), Caroline is turning her attention inward. Why is she so angry all the time? And why can’t anyone tell? Themes include: female rage, Protestant repression, Chess.com, and wedding planning. You’d be ragin’ to miss it. Caroline's debut comedy hour Train Man received critical acclaim at Edinburgh Fringe in 2025 and was later listed in the Irish Times top shows to see at Dublin Fringe.

title: Losing the Plot(kin)
description: Two Plotkins. Two works in progress. Two Brighton Fringe favourites. One Hour. All Brand new material. Fanny Dent (Leicester Comedy Festival nominee 2024) and Simon Plotkin (Do The Thing) team up for a split bill of fresh, work-in-progress comedy. Expect bold new characters, ideas, jokes and beautifully controlled chaos. Fanny Dent is known for her comedy and character cabaret: Playful, theatrical and gloriously unpredictable, her previous show, 'The Burlesque Imposter' (★★★★) sold out fringe runs across the country. Her new material continues in that spirit. Simon Plotkin is a familiar face on the Brighton comedy scene. He is one third of the improvised musical hit 'Do The Thing', which enjoyed a sell-out run at Brighton Fringe last year. Simon will be testing new ideas and test-driving new material. What will they come up with? It'll be loose, lively and full surprises!

title: Tom Whiston: Night Mail (Work In Progress)
description: In the sorting room of a remote post office, a naughty 1940s postman passes time with pranks, letters and telegrams from the future, while waiting for news of the war. It's a fast-talking, absurdist, character comedy with elements of clowning, and packed with jokes, both sensical and non. Tim Whiston of the GPO (like his favourite singer: "G'Peter Ondre") plays games and pranks with the audience to pass the time, while checking his wireless for news of his sister's RAF squadron caught behind enemy lines. Alas, instead, it's a broadcast of The Pirates of Penzance - and not even one with a conductor he respects. Ultimately, the show is a commentary on the way in which we consume information in the modern era - either anxiety-inducing, distressing news from mainstream sources (Tim's wireless), or bizarre stream-of-consciousness absurdity, teeming out of social media (the telegram machine). How we cope - through jokes, through drugs, through despair - is up to us.

title: Calls From Castlemar
description: Calls for Castlemar explores the difficult dynamics between three young children's home residents with snappy dialogue, comedic levity, and youthful play. Themes of identity, belonging, forced family, and jealousy are explored in a world where adults just don't seem to care, let alone exist. Based on Canadian play Home for Boys, this story makes it way across the pond with altered narrative to suit a UK audience. It will appeal to those with or without nostalgia for the 90s, and the hearts of those who have ever lost or longed for someone important in their lives.
